channel.title: Game News Round-Up
channel.description: A daily curated digest of games-industry news.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 01/06/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 1 June 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Fable has been delayed to February 2027
  • Activision confirms Call of Duty Warzone is shutting down on last-gen PlayStation and Xbox when Modern Wafare 4 releases

Business

  • Balatro owner Playstack sold to subsidary of parent company of Fandom and Gamespot (Integrated Media Company)
    • Previous owner Turin told investors it had agreed to sell its 84.5 percent stake in the UK publisher to VantageCo, a direct subsidiary of IMC, for £112.4 million ($151 million)
  • NetEase Q1 Results
    • For the three months ending March 31, 2026:
      • Net revenues: RMB30.6 billion ($4.4 billion), up 6.1% year-on-year
      • Games and related value-added services net revenues: RMB25.7 billion ($3.7 billion), up 6.9% year-on-year
      • Online games revenue: approximately RMB25.1 billion, up around 7% year-on-year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Call of Duty Modern Warfare 4 announced. Skipping PS4/Xbox One but coming to PS5 / Xbox Series, PC and Switch 2. Campaign is set in Korea

Business

  • The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt has sold 65 million units
  • 007 First Light sells 1.5 million copies in the first 24 hours of release

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Valve raises Steam Deck OLED prices by over 40% due to higher component costs and logistical challenges
    • Steam Deck OLED 512GB: Now $789; CAD $1,129; €779; £649; AUD $1,199; PLN 3,279
    • Steam Deck OLED 1TB: Now $949 USD; CAD $1,349; €919; £779; AUD $1,429; PLN 3,879
  • The Witcher 3 is officially getting a third expansion, Songs of the Past

Free Games / Subscription

  • Donkey Kong 64 is coming to Nintendo Switch Online

Business

  • Pokémon game sales cross 515m units

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Destruction AllStars delisted from PS Store, servers to shutdown in November 2026. Multiplayer services taken offline immediately due to "ongoing technical issues"
  • CD Projekt announces Witcher anniversary live stream, amid reports new DLC is on the way. May 28 at 11am ET / 4pm BST / 5pm CEST

Free Games / Subscription

  • June’s PlayStation Plus ‘free’ Essential monthly games: Grounded Fully Yoked Edition, Nickelodeon All-Star Brawl 2, Warhammer 40,000: Darktide

Business

  • Take-Two Financial Results
    • For the three months ending March 31, 2026:
      • Net revenue: $1.7 billion, up 6% year-on-year
      • Net bookings: $1.58 billion, flat year-on-year
      • Net loss: $59.6 million, down 98% year-on-year
    • For the financial year ending March 31, 2026:
      • Net revenue: $6.2 billion, up 19% year-on-year
      • Net bookings: $6.72 billion, up 19% year-on-year
      • Net loss: $298.2 million, down 93% year-on-year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Epic announces Unreal Engine 6, which will run the ‘next era’ of Rocket League .
    • In 2024, Tim Sweeney said "UE6 = UE5 + Verse + rough deployment parity into Fortnite and into standalone products + metaverse economy + standards + ?? magic TBD."

Business

  • Star Citizen tops $1 billion crowdfunding milestone
  • Kenshi Sells 3 Million Units

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 22/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Sega is teasing the new Crazy Taxi with first social media post in 7 years
  • Bungie confirms it’s ending Destiny 2 updates

Business

  • Xbox announces hire of veteran analyst Matthew Ball as chief strategy officer
  • ‘Red Dead Redemption 2’ Becomes Third Best-Selling Game Ever Released, With 85 Million Copies Sold to Date
  • Subnautica 2 has sold 4 million copies in 5 days

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• PlayStation State of Play showcase including Marvel’s Wolverine announced. June 3rd at 10pm BST, 2pm PT, 5pm ET, 11pm CEST. Expected to last over an hour and will be shown at US cinemas (Alamo Drafthouse)
  • UK-centric gaming showcase, For One Night Only, announced, featuring IO Interactive and Criterion. Tuesday 29th July 2026 with a live studio audience.

Business

  • Quantic Dream's multiplayer game Spellcasters Chronicles is being shut down and the studio reorganised, but Star Wars Eclipse is unaffected
  • Ubisoft Financial Results
    • Full Year Numbers
      • Revenue: €1.4 billion (down 21.8% year-on-year)
      • Net bookings: €1.5 billion (down 17.4% year-on-year)
      • Digital net bookings: €1.33 billion (down 16.0% year-on-year)
      • Back-catalogue net bookings: €1.28 billion (down 1.1% year-on-year)
    • Three Months Ending March 31, 2026
      • Revenue: €419.5 million (down 47.3% year-on-year)
      • Net bookings: €415 million (down 54% year-on-year)
      • Back-catalogue net bookings: €243 million

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Fortnite is back on the Apple App Store in most regions as the protracted legal battle goes on
  • Valve claims Vampire Survivors (and the many copies that followed) are "Bullet Heaven" games

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass titles for the rest of May have been revealed

Business

  • Embracer announces plan to split into 2 companies, as Tomb Raider and LoTR spun off
    • Fellowship Entertainment is planned to be established in 2027 and will be an “IP-led” entertainment company built around the development, publishing, and licensing of games, with a single, centralized business plan.
      • The new company will take over stewardship of The Lord of the Rings IP, and game franchises such as Tomb Raider, Darksiders, Dead Island, Kingdom Come Deliverance, Metro, and Remnant.
    • Embracer says Fellowship will ‘more actively’ explore Deus Ex, TimeSplitters, Legacy of Kain and Saints Row partnerships (developed by external partners)
  • Far Far West sells over 1 million copies in three weeks, publisher Fireshine Games rejects generative AI, but allows use of AI in other tools (such as for code completion, co-pilot in Word .etc)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo announces Pictonico, a new mobile game that turns your photos into mini-games
  • Original Doom soundtrack to be inducted into US National Recording Registry. Composed by Bobby Prince, the 1993 score is the third video game soundtrack to join the registry

Free Games / Subscription

  • PlayStation Plus is going up in price for new members. £1/$1 for one month, £3/$3 for three-month option.

Rumors

  • PlayStation boss Hermen Hulst tells staff that single-player games won’t come to PC going forward

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• After a fan poll, Microsoft’s new games boss (Asha Sharma) is seemingly rebranding Xbox, to ‘XBOX’
  • Lego 2K Drive Set to be Delisted May 19th With Multiplayer Shutdown Scheduled for May 31st, 2027

Business

  • Subnautica 2 sells 2 million in 12 hours with over 600K concurrent players
  • Terraria marks 70 million sales and 15th anniversary with a promise to "continue beyond" its next big update
  • May 2026 Games Industry Role Appointments Round-up
    • Ex-Aardman exec Lorna Probert joins Diva Agency
    • Two Point Studios appoints Jo Koehler as COO
    • Pete Smith and Samantha Ebelthite have joined Keywords Studios as global client partners. In this role, Smith and Ebelthite will advise partners on the different services offered by Keywords.
    • Jam City co-founder Joshua Yguado steps down as CEO
  • Nexon Q1 Results
    • Revenue: ¥152.2 billion ($959.9 million, up 34% year-over-year)
    • Operating income: ¥58.2 billion ($367 million, up 40% year-over-year)
    • Net income: ¥57.2 billion ($360.7 million, 118% year-over-year)
  • Square Enix FY26 Results
    • Net sales: ¥297.6 billion ($1.8 billion, down 8.3% year-on-year)
    • Digital entertainment sales: ¥172.8 billion ($1.08 billion, down 16.3％ year-on-year)
    • Operating income: ¥54.7 billion ($344.9 million, up 34.9% year-on-year)
    • Profit attributable to owners of parent: ¥29.6 billion ($186.6 million, up 21.3% year-on-year)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 15/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 15 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Bobby Prince’s ‘Doom’ Soundtrack Inducted into Library of Congress

Business

  • Subnautica 2 has reached 1 million copies sold
  • Windrose passes 2 Million copies sold

Rumors

  • Amazon reportedly cancels Lord of the Rings MMO. Firm's head of games says it's working on a "new game experience" within the franchise
  • WWE 2K26 dev Visual Concepts is laying off staff. Unknown amount of employees affected

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is getting a real-life treasure hunt with a prize worth half a million dollars . The Treasure Hunt starts later this year and will last 2-5 years.
  • Valve's new Steam Controller Wilhelm screams if you drop it from a great enough height

Free Games / Subscription

  • PS Plus Premium and Extra games for May announced.
    • Star Wars Outlaws
    • Red Dead Redemption 2
    • Bramble: The Mountain King
    • The Thaumaturge | PS5
    • Flintlock: The Siege of Dawn
    • Broken Sword – Shadows of the Templar: Reforged
    • Enotria: The Last Song Standard Edition
    • Time Crisis (will feature all-new gyro aiming)

Business

  • Sensor Tower acquires AppMagic
  • Capcom Financial Results
    • Net sales: ¥195.6 billion ($1.2 billion, up 15.2% year-on-year)
    • Net income: ¥54.5 billion ($345.4 million, up 12.7% year-on-year)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Tekken boss Katsuhiro Harada has joined SNK
  • PlayStation exec Christian Svensson says next few years of unannounced games is ‘unbelievably’ positive: ‘There are no dire times ahead for the industry’
  • Fallout TV show season three casts Breaking Bad's Aaron Paul

Business

  • SEGA FY26 Results
    • Net sales: ¥487.5 billion ($3.09 billion, up 13.6% year-on-year)
    • Operating income: ¥47.1 billion ($299 million, down 2% year-on-year)
    • Ordinary income: ¥54.2 billion ($344 million, up 2% year-on-year)
  • Metroid Dread developer MercurySteam conducts layoffs. Unknown amount of people affected
  • Silent Hill f shipments and digital sales top two million

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• SEGA has canceled development of its ‘super game’, as it pivots away from live service games .
    • Over 100 developers who were working on free-to-play titles have already been transferred to Full Game development teams “focusing on the mainstay IPs”, according to the company.
    • New Crazy Taxi, Jet Set Radio, Golden Axe and SegaGaGa revivals are still in development even after super game has been cancelled.
  • Discord Nitro now includes a ‘starter edition’ of Xbox Game Pass
  • Nintendo is changing the name of My Nintendo Store later this month (May 27th). Renaming to "Nintendo Store".

Business

  • Shift Up says it will publish Stellar Blade 2 instead of Sony, wants to ‘reach a broad global audience from day one’ . Is also "actively exploring" further platform expansion for the first game.
  • Sony maps out how first-party PlayStation studios are utilising AI tools during development

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Sony lays out its AI plan for PlayStation: ‘We believe AI will unleash the creativity of our studios’ . SIE President and CEO Hideaki Nishino says AI will play a big role in the development of its games
  • Psychonauts, Kiln studio Double Fine votes to unionise
  • Atari acquires rights to first five Wizardry RPGs. Japanese publisher Drecom retains Wizardry trademark worldwide and ownership of Wizardry 6, 7, and 8
  • Forza Horizon 6 Leaks And Is Being Pirated On PC More Than A Week Before Release. Non-Encrypted Steam Build accidentally set live early.

Business

  • Sony Financial Results
    • Full Year Numbers
      • Net sales: ¥12.5 trillion ($79.7 billion, up 3.7% year-over-year)
      • Operating income: ¥1.4 trillion ($8.9 billion, up 13.4% year-over-year)
      • Game & Network Services net sales: ¥4.7 trillion ($29.9 billion, up 0.3% year-over-year)
      • Game & Network Services operating income: ¥463.3 billion ($2.9 billion, up 12%)
    • Q4
      • Net sales: ¥3.03 trillion ($19.3 billion, up 8.3% year-over-year)
      • Operating income: ¥163.5 billion ($1.04 billion, up 24% year-over-year)
      • Game & Network Services net sales: ¥1.02 trillion ($6.5 billion, down 2.8% year-over-year)
      • Game & Network Services operating income: ¥54.1 billion ($345.1 million, down 41.6% year-over-year)
  • Nintendo Financial Results
    • Net sales: ¥2.3 trillion ($14.6 billion, up 98.6%)
    • Operating profit: ¥360.1 billion ($2.2 billion, up 27.5%)
    • Ordinary profit: ¥542.2 billion ($3.4 billion, up 45.6%)
    • Gross profit: ¥909 billion ($5.7 billion, up 28%)
  • Gamble With Your Friends has sold 1 million copies in one week

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo’s Takashi Tezuka, director of classic Mario and Zelda games, is retiring
  • Resident Evil Requiem gets a free ‘fast-paced’ bonus mode, Leon Must Die Forever, and it’s available now

Business

  • Eve Online studio CCP Games is rebranding following its split from Crimson Desert’s Pearl Abyss. CCP Games is now "Fenris Creations"
  • Capcom: Sales of All-New IP PRAGMATA Top Two Million Units in 16 Days
  • 2K has laid off staff at 31st Union, the studio working on free-to-play shooter Project Ethos. Studio head has "never been more confident in the future of our game" but needed to "scale back our team to better align with where we are in development". Unknown amount of people affected.
  • Unity Q1 results
    • Revenue: $508 million (up 17% year-on-year)
    • Create Solutions revenue: $157 million (up 4% year-on-year)
    • Grow Solutions revenue: $352 million (up 23% year-on-year)
    • Net loss: $347 million (with a margin of 68%)
  • Sony Records New $560 Million Loss On Bungie Acquisition As Marathon Struggles And Destiny 2 Nosedives
  • Nintendo Switch 2 has sold 19.86 million units as of Q1 2026
    • Software sales
      • Mario Kart World - 14.7M
      • Pokemon Legends ZA - 12.8M (S1+S2)
      • DK Bananza - 4.52M
      • Pokopia - above 4M in 6 weeks
      • FireRed/LeafGreen - above 4M in 5 weeks
      • Tomodachi Life - 3.8M in 2 weeks
  • PS5 shipments top 93.7 million. 1.5 million units shipped during the three months ended March 31, 2026.
  • Nintendo raises prices for Nintendo products and services

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• New Star Fox game announced for Nintendo Switch 2. Releases June 25th 2026
    • This is the third remake of Star Fox 64 (fourth remake if you consider Star Fox 64 a remake of the original Star Fox)

Business

  • Founders of Gearbox Quebec announce new studio to "create original, premium games on our own terms". Ricochet Games working on "a co-op action-adventure game designed for a global audience".
    • The studio has been founded by Sébastien Caisse and Pierre-André Déry, who previously launched and lead Gearbox Studio Québec, alongside former Borderlands Creative Director Maxime Babin and former Gearbox Director of Creative Development Yanick Piché. Caisse and Déry departed Gearbox in 2024.
  • EA FY26 Results
    • For the three months ending March 31, 2026
      • Net revenue: $2.1 billion (up 12% year-on-year)
      • Net income: $461 million (up 81.5% year-on-year)
      • Net bookings: $1.86 billion (up 3.6% year-on-year)
    • For the 12 months ending March 31, 2026
      • Net revenue: $7.53 billion (up 1% year-on-year)
      • Net income: $887 million (down 20.9% year-on-year)
      • Net bookings: $8.03 billion (up 9% year-on-year)

Rumors

  • MindsEye studio Build A Rocket Boy reportedly lays off 170 more employees
    • This comes after two prior layoff rounds (March and last summer)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• IGN Entertainment releases internal data as public report, developed by Kantar and UC Berkeley
    • Among the games-specific insights of the report, highlights include:
      • 71% of audiences no longer purchase physical music and 62% no longer buy full-price games
      • Gen X respondents prioritised Google search to discover new games, while Millennials favoured 85% YouTube and Gen Z respondents prioritised social media
      • Millennial (38%) and Gen Z (42%) audiences are much more likely to buy games at full price, while Generation X lags far behind at 20%
      • Generation X favours single-player games and Generation Z prefers multiplayer titles to almost exactly the same degree, while Millennial players remain almost evenly split
      • Generation X and Millennials are more likely to return to games in order to complete or master them, while Gen Z are primarily motivated by new customisation or community content
      • Consumption of guides and game help varies by generation, with Gen X indexing higher on tips videos, Millennials on map tools and Generation Z on build guides
      • Generation X are highly sceptical of AI and more trusting of brand recognition, being 38% less likely to use AI for discovery and 44% less likely to believe that AI summaries are as good as human ones

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox details the next 13 Game Pass titles, including Forza Horizon 6, Mixtape and Subnautica 2

Business

  • Remedy says Control has now sold over 6 million lifetime copies and reaffirms Control Resonant is still "on track" for a 2026 release
  • Pearl Abyss sells CCP back to its CEO for less than half what it paid ($100 million), plus $20 million in crypto
  • Remedy Q1 Results
    • Revenue: €13.1 million (down 1.9%)
    • Operating profit: €1 million, down 23% compared to €1.3 million for the same quarter last year
  • Xbox no longer developing Copilot for consoles

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Lego reveals Sega Genesis / Mega Drive console. The Lego Sega Genesis Console will be available from June 1, 2026, priced at £34.99 / €39.99 / $39.99, and is made up of 479 pieces at a build measurement of 4 cm high, 6 in 16 cm wide, and 12 cm deep.
  • Microsoft’s Xbox Mode has released for Windows 11 PCs
  • Hero shooter Last Flag halts production just 2 weeks after launch, but vows to "make sure that the game doesn't disappear"
  • Full Metal Gear Solid 2 source code leaks online on 30th April. April 30th is a key date in the plot of Metal Gear Solid 2
  • US PlayStation players could be due payout as court agrees class-action settlement of $7.8 million.
    • The lawsuit is based upon the fact that Sony have been blocking third parties selling game codes, leading to an artifical inflation of PS digital game pricing.
      • Nintendo and Xbox allow third-party sites to sell game codes, so they are unaffected by this lawsuit.

Business

  • GameStop makes $55.5bn takeover offer for eBay
  • The Steam Controller sold out in 30 minutes
  • Roblox announces AI-driven upscaler, which it claims will convert Roblox titles to photorealistic graphics
  • Roblox Q1 Numbers
    • Revenue: $1.4 billion (up 39%)
    • Bookings: $1.7 billion (up 43%)
    • Net losses: $248 million compared to $216 million during the same quarter last year
  • Krafton Q1 Numbers
    • Revenue: $931 million (up 56.9%)
    • Operating profit: $381.3 million (up 22.8%)
    • Mobile revenue: $477 million
    • PC revenue: $247.1 million
    • Console revenue: $9.3 million
    • Other revenue: $197.6 million
    • PUBG franchise revenue: over $679 million, up 24% year-on-year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 01/05/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 1 May 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Nacon has rescheduled its postponed game showcase despite fears over its future. The company filed for Insolvency in late February
  • Rebellion’s British post-apocalyptic game Atomfall is being turned into a TV series
  • Star Wars: Galactic Racer confirms release date and $160 physical collector’s edition. Releases October 6th
  • Two computer giants of the 80s - the Spectrum and C64 - are being turned into pocket-sized clamshell handhelds by the makers of Evercade. Both machines are priced at £109.99 and launch on 15th October.

Business

  • Epic Games Store launches on iPhone in Japan
  • Ustwo Games to consider hiring more contractors to reduce development costs. "We'll have a core team and any growth will come through contractors, which is something I hate about the industry," says CEO
  • Digital Bros. acquires Wuchang: Fallen Feathers IP for $4.6m. The 2025 game has sold one million copies and generated around $35.1m in revenue
  • GameMaker incorporates Claude Code to enable AI-assisted workflows

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Starbreeze to capitalize on Roblox success with second game. Notoriety: A Payday Experience has surpassed 400m visits on Roblox and holds a 92% player approval rating

Free Games / Subscription

  • May’s PlayStation Plus Essential ‘free’ games: FC26, Wuchang: Fallen Feathers and Nine Sols

Business

  • Greedfall developer Spiders has shut down after Nacon recently filed for Insolvency
  • Microsoft Q3 Financial Results
    • For the three months ended March 31, 2026:
      • Revenue: $82.9 billion (up 18%)
      • More personal computing (incl. Xbox revenue): $13.2 billion (down 1%)
      • Operating income: $38.4 billion (up 20%)
      • Net income: $31.8 billion (up 23%)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Valve says Steam Deck 2 is still in development, acknowledges ongoing Steam Deck stock issues. "There's only so much we can do" about the ram shortage
  • Games Done Quick is coming to Europe with a special three-day programme at Gamescom 2026

Business

  • Capcom raises FY25 outlook following boost from Resident Evil: Requiem. The developer now expects net sales of ¥195.3 billion ($1.22 billion), up 2.8%, and net income of ¥54.5 billion ($342 million), up 6.9%, from the previous forecast

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Valve's new Steam Controller's price and release date revealed; £85. Launches 4th May
  • PowerWash Simulator 2 is getting a Star Wars level pack focused on original trilogy locations this summer
  • Hiring has begun for a new sequel, Tera 2, as Krafton eyes Unreal Engine 5 to secure a potential MMORPG hit
  • Rugrats: Retro Rewind Collection announced for PS5, Switch. Releases May 15th

Business

  • Assassin’s Creed Hexe has lost another director. Game Director Benoit Richer confirms his departure, weeks after Creative Director Clint Hocking's exit

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• ‘Their favourite games were already built with AI’: Google exec says almost every big studio uses AI, but not all disclose it
    • Google Cloud's Games boss cites Capcom as one major studio that uses AI tools
  • Final Fantasy XIV Online is coming to Switch 2 as new expansion Evercold revealed
    • Expansion to feature 24 person raid collaboration with Evangelion
  • Outrun, Streets of Rage, NiGHTS, and SGGG are just four of many series Sega promises to revisit / celebrate in 2026

Business

  • Resident Evil Requiem sales exceed 7 Million (in less than two months)
    • The previous RE game to reach this milestone the quickest was Resident Evil 4 (2023), which took 12 months and 1 week to reach 7 million
  • Atari has acquired retro emulation studio Implicit Conversions, which specialises in PS1 ports
  • Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 passes 8 million in sales
  • Bulletstorm dev People Can Fly acquires Cooldown Games
  • Gameworks and PerfCop are part of a new wave of tools for keeping an eye on game performance throughout development

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Xbox confirms it’s ‘reevaluating’ exclusivity as it shares future mission statement. Microsoft Gaming will be rebranded back to just "Xbox"
  • Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced releases July 9th for PS5, Xbox Series S/X and PC
  • Gang Of Dragon Trailer And Nagoshi Studio's YouTube Channel Vanish Amid Funding Crisis

Rumors

  • GOG Reddit account teasing a re-release of Freelancer

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Ecco the Dolphin Complete announced, containing the first two games and a new ‘contemporary’ game. The collection's development is being led by Ecco creator Ed Annuziata
  • Remedy releases Control on iPhone and iPad with touch controls, reworked gameplay systems, and ray tracing

Business

  • Nintendo has been sued by two players in the US, who say the company stands to unjustly profit from any tariff refunds it receives from the US government
  • Vampire Survivors’ developer is working on over 15 projects, including IP collaborations. Opening new studios in Italy and Japan
  • Koei Tecmo FY2026 profit forecast lifted from ¥37bn to ¥55.5bn, up 50%. Operating profit guidance rose from ¥31bn to ¥36bn due to better-than-expected Q4 game sales and investment gains
  • Dead by Daylight studio Behaviour Interactive confirms redundancies. Studio says "demand for mobile and casual external development projects has declined". Unknown amount of people affected.
  • Palia reaches 10 million players

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 22/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• MindsEye studio staff takes legal action, saying management installed ‘invasive’ surveillance software
  • Single-player spin-off Splatoon Raiders comes to Nintendo Switch 2 this summer. Releases July 23rd

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox is cutting Game Pass prices and removing launch-day Call of Duty games. Game Pass Ultimate drops from $29.99 to $22.99. Call of Duty games to be added roughly one year post-release

Business

  • Dragon Ball and Digimon producer Toei announces Toei Games, focusing on new IPs first
  • Sanrio establishes in-house gaming brand Sanrio Games. Hello Kitty creator plans to launch ten games over the next three years, starting with Sanrio Party Land later this year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Speakers announced for Summer Game Fest’s The Game Business Live event. EA boss Laura Miele, Naughty Dog co-founder Jason Rubin, and popular industry analyst Matthew Ball will appear at the LA event
  • The Assassin’s Creed: Black Flag Remake Will Be Revealed This Week (April 23rd, 9 am PT/12 pm ET / 5pm BST)
  • PlayStation legend Shuhei Yoshida says Jim Ryan fired him because he didn't listen to him
  • Sony begins rolling out age verification for PlayStation in UK and Ireland. Age verification will become mandatory from June 2026 for full access to communication and content sharing features

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox has revealed the next 10 titles coming to Game Pass, including Final Fantasy 5. 10 games will be removed from Game Pass next week

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Clair Obscur and Dispatch lead 2026 BAFTA Games Awards Winners. Other awards include Arc Raiders for Multiplayer and Death Stranding 2 for Artistic Achievement
  • Panic won't release Playdate titles that use certain forms of generative AI. Seems like generated code is the only form of generative AI allowed
  • Ludum Dare will officially end in October 2028. The long-running game jam has spawned a number of successful titles such as Hollow Knight and Inscryption.

Business

  • Iron Galaxy Studios announces further layoffs, unknown number of staff affected. Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 3+4 dev says "it's impossible to sustain our team size even after downsizing from last year"
  • PRAGMATA surpasses one million units sold in two days
  • Windrose has sold 1 million copies in 6 days

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Fortnite's Save the World mode is now available free-to-play. Peak concurrent player count in the past 24 hours exceeded 100k
  • Metro 2039 announced. Releasing on PS5, Xbox Series S/X and PC this winter
  • Phasmophobia x Alan Wake collaboration announced. Free limited time event starting May 12th

Business

  • Microsoft's Matt Booty details cross-studio collaborations (Blizzard assisting with Fable's cienmatics, Sea of Thieves team helping with Kiln .etc)
  • Roblox to pay over $12m to state of Nevada in child safety settlement. Additional protections to be implemented nationwide, including enhanced age verification for all users
  • Windrose has sold 500,000 copies in 48 hours

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Facepunch ends developer preview for game creation platform s&box; reopens publicly on Steam April 28th. Recently confirmed deal with Valve to allow games made within s&box to be sold standalone on Steam

Free Games / Subscription

  • PS Plus Premium and Extra games for April include The Crew Motorfest, Horizon Zero Dawn Remastered, Football Manager 26, Warriors: Abyss, Squirrel with a Gun, The Casting of Frank Stone, Monster Train and Wild Arms 4

Business

  • Crimson Desert Surpasses 5 Million Copies Sold Worldwide

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 15/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 15 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Graveyard Keeper's free giveaway made the publisher at least $250,000 in DLC sales on PC alone. Console has yet to be counted
  • Red Dead Online and GTA Online revenue data leaked from recent data breach
    • GTA Onlines's daily average revenue is $1.3 million ($9.5 million a week)
      • Of that $9.5m weekly average, PC makes $264,273 whereas PS5 makes $4.4m (and other consoles fill in the remaining cash)
    • GTA Online makes $498.8m a year.
    • Red Dead Online makes $507,193 on average per week ($26.4 million per year)

Business

  • Bloober Team expands leadership, as studio steps up to seven horror titles in active development. CEO says focus is "sustainable development, financial discipline, and long-term stability", not "reckless" growth that "solves problems through layoffs"
  • UK government begins rollout of £30m Games Growth Package. £28.5 million allocated through the UK Games fund, £1.5 million awarded to the London Games Festival over the next three years

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Bloodborne Getting R-Rated Animated Movie Adaptation From Sony. Produced by youtuber Jacksepticeye
  • Roblox unveils new subscription service Roblox Plus. Launching on April 30, Roblox Plus offers subscribers discounts on in-game items and avatars, as well as unspecified "exclusive" benefits for $5 per month.

Business

  • UKIE: UK games market reaches £8.7bn in 2025

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Rockstar confirms new data breach, after hacker group threatens: ‘Pay, or we leak’. Rockstar claims that "a limited amount of non-material company information" was accessed
  • Amazon Luna has stopped offering individual games and subscriptions from third-party stores. Players who previously purchased titles through Luna can continue to play them until June 10, at which point Luna will no longer support them. They will still be playable through the EA, Ubisoft and GOG stores they were redeemed on
  • After 20 years in pre-production limbo, a Metal Gear Solid movie is on the way. Zach Lipovsky and Adam Stein, the Canadian directing duo best known for Final Destination Bloodlines to direct.
  • The James Bond IP owner has opposed an attempt to trademark ’90s parody video game character James Pond
  • Ex-Bethesda Exec (Pete Hines) Says The Elder Scrolls 6 Maker Isn't Part Of Something 'Genuine' Or 'Authentic' At Microsoft

Business

  • Ex-MPG CEO Andy Norman joins CodeDev as Chief Operating Officer (COO). Will be working alongside other Ex-MPG CEO Rik Alexander (Chair of CodeDev) and CEO Myke Parrott

Rumors

  • Epic set to release Arc Raiders-like extraction shooter with Disney characters by the end of the year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Jon Gibson, the head of transformation at Keywords Studios, says: "Of the 500+ AI tools tested by a leading game dev tech provider, only ‘half a dozen’ help ‘in the right way"
  • Neill Blomkamp’s blockchain studio Gunzilla Games hasn’t paid some staff ‘for many months’, employees allege

Business

  • Konami's eFootball tops 1 billion downloads

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Cyberpunk 2077 gets a free PS5 Pro upgrade that brings PSSR and better ray tracing options

Business

  • Split Fiction developer Hazelight celebrates 50 million games sales. A Way Out, It Takes Two, and Split Fiction sold 13m, 30m, and 7m units, respectively
  • The developer behind Peak (Landfall) has launched a publishing arm called Evil Landfall
  • Fully remote, independent co-development collective (1oh1Designs) setup by group of industry creatives

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• As part of the new PlayStation Playerbase initative, PlayStation is hunting for "biggest fans" so it can scan their faces and put them in games, starting with Gran Turismo 7
  • Impossible Mission 3 is coming on May 18th, nearly 40 years after the second game in the iconic UK series. Will be available digitally and as a physical Commodore 64 cassete tape

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox has revealed 18 Game Pass titles for April, including Hades 2, Kiln and Vampire Crawlers

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• McDonalds Türkiye's new 'Archie' device stops you going AFK while you eat a Big Mac
  • Double Dragon and River City Randsom creator Yoshihisa Kishimoto has passed away at the age of 64

Business

  • Take-Two lays off AI team

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Nexon calls Arc Raiders a "Trojan Horse" for proving controversial AI tools can build triple-A hits with smaller teams
  • Brian Raffel, Co-Founder of Call of Duty Developer Raven Software, Retires After 36 Years at Studio

Business

  • Goat Simulator developer Coffee Stain has closed its mobile-focused Malmö studio
  • MechWarrior dev Piranha Games affected by redundancies. Unknown amount of employees affected
  • Sony Interactive Entertainment Acquires Cinemersive Labs (UK-based machine learning and computer vision company)

Rumors

  • The Last of Us multiplayer game was roughly 80% complete when it was cancelled, claims former director

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• US patent office revokes Nintendo’s patent on summoning characters to make them battle
  • VR platform Rec Room to shut down in June 2026. "We never quite figured out how to make Rec Room a sustainably profitable business," says developer
  • ProbablyMonsters is betting on an Xbox 360-esque bonanza of single-player AA kookiness. Chief marketing officer David Reid explains why diligent use of marketing spend is the secret to targeting the now much-reduced AA sector
  • Original Resident Evil, Resident Evil 2, Resident Evil 3: Nemesis, and Breath of Fire IV now available via Steam
  • Nvidia App adds 'Auto Shader Compilation' for faster load times in games. The Beta feature automatically recompiles shaders in the background after every driver update

Free Games / Subscription

  • April’s PlayStation Plus ‘free’ Essential monthly games have been confirmed. Lords of the Fallen, Tomb Raider I-III Remastered and Sword Art Online Fractured Daydraem

Business

  • Stellar Blade developer (Shift Up) acquires Resident Evil creator Shinji Mikami's new studio, Unbound
  • Krafton, Flyquest, Arbitrum invest $3.5M in Psychedelic Games' MOBA Golden Tides

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 01/04/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 1 April 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Evercade unveils its next-gen retro gaming handheld Nexus, with analogue sticks and Banjo-Kazooie 1&2 (with enhancements such as widescreen and twin stick support) bundled in. £169.99/€199.99/$199.99. Releases October
  • Filming on Amazon’s live-action Tomb Raider series has halted after lead star Sophie Turner gets injured

Other Notes

  • As you may be able to guess, April Fool's day is one of the hardest days to effectively do the Game News Round-Up as news stories require extra validation. Thanks for your patience!

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 31/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 31 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• This summer’s Xbox Games Showcase is confirmed for June, followed by a Gears of War E-Day Direct (Sunday, June 7 at 10am PT / 1pm ET / 6pm BST)

Business

  • Konami increases its starting salary in Japan by nearly 30%, increases base pay for the fifth year in a row. The company says it's "consistently investing in human capital"
  • Eidos-Montréal announces 124 job cuts; studio head David Anfossi departs after 19 years at developer
  • The list of people hit by recent Epic layoffs has been turned into a dedicated portal for recruiters

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• From April 2nd, PS5 console prices are being raised globally (base and digital console prices are rising by $100. PS5 Pro's price will rise $150)
  • Sniper Elite studio Rebellion reveals Alien Deathstorm, an action horror FPS. Releases in 2027
  • Krafton cancels F2P PUBG spin-off PUBG: Blindspot after just 2 months

Business

  • As Crimson Desert nears 5 million sales, Pearl Abyss CEO says it’s looking into a Switch 2 port

Rumors

  • The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time remake and new Star Fox game expected to release this year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Hades 2 is coming to PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series S/X on April 14th
  • Fox McCloud is officially appearing in The Super Mario Galaxy Movie
  • New RPG Maker Entry ("RPG Maker WITH") Announced with HD-2D Style Visual Shift. First entry to allow users to share their projects and assets on console.

Business

  • Meta Quest usage reaches "all-time high" in 2025 despite studio closures and layoffs. Over 100 titles generated more than $1 million in gross revenue, while in-app purchase sales grew by over 10%

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• After Tekken 8 Season 3 led to a Mostly Negative rating on Steam, Bandai Namco is working on an "emergency patch" to win fans back
  • Half-Life 2 writer Erik Wolpaw says some people at Valve are looking at AI tools, but he's not "worried about AI taking over creative writing" anytime soon
  • Jason Schreier: Exact budgets of video-game productions can be tough to corroborate but the numbers I've heard floating around AAA game dev these days are $300 million or more — sometimes much more! — which I think helps explain the current state of the industry

Business

  • Crimson Desert sales top 3 million
  • Team17 parent company Everplay Financial Results
    • Revenue: £166 million, flat year-on-year
    • Gross profit: £76.3 million, up 10% year-on-year
  • Four Nacon subsidiaries file for insolvency (Spiders, Kylotonn, Cyanide, and Nacon Tech)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 25/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Epic Games is laying off over 1,000 employees due to ‘Fortnite downturn’
    • Due to the layoffs, Fortnite Festival's Battle Stage (NOT the main Festival mode), Ballistic and Rocket Racing will be sunset

Business

  • Behaviour Interactive acquires 7 Days to Die developer "The Fun Pimps"
  • Sony closes first-party studio Dark Outlaw Games. Developer was founded by industry veteran Jason Blundell in March 2025. PlayStation is making other cuts, including mobile development. Approx 50 people affected.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Microsoft announces Xbox Partner Preview for this week, with Ryu Ga Gotoku's Stranger Than Heaven making a showing; Thursday, 26th March, 5pm UK / 10am Pacific / 1pm Eastern. It will all last for roughly 30 minutes
  • Roblox insists plans to start charging creators for brand integrations "is not about revenue at all". "It's about evolving our responsibility to our users, creators and brands"
  • Capcom says it will use generative AI to speed up production. It will not be using AI for "asset creation" but aims to use it to improve efficiency and productivity "including graphics, sound and programming"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Minecraft is officially getting a £50m UK theme park land, ‘Minecraft World’ - The Chessington World of Adventures Minecraft Park will open in 2027
  • Netflix’s live-action Assassin’s Creed series has started filming as cast is officially announced - The series will take place in Rome
  • ESRB will not adopt PEGI age-rating changes in the US as it "could be confusing"
  • Square Enix announced plans to integrate Google’s generative AI model “Gemini” into Dragon Quest X - Will act as personal conversational AI to help guide new players and act as a personal assistant

Business

  • Two more senior Xbox leaders (Lori Wright and Haiyan Zhang) exit Microsoft after serving the company for over 10+ years
  • CD Projekt sales rise 9% in 2025 (to 867 million PLN / $235.2 million, driven by Cyberpunk franchise
  • Crimson Desert has topped 2M sales within a day
  • Savvy Games Group to acquire Moonton Games for $6 billion - TikTok maker ByteDance paid $4 billion for Moonton in 2021

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Sony is shutting down one of the two PlayStation VR2 exclusives it published; Firewall Ultra's servers are shutting down on September 17th 2026 (rendering the game unplayable). Already delisted from storefronts. Developer First Contact Entertainment closed down in Jan 2024
  • Vampire Survivors' deck-building spin-off Vampire Crawlers releases April 21st on PS5, PC, Xbox Series S/X for £9.99/€9.99/$9.99/¥1200
  • Epic Games expands Unreal Editor for Fortnite (UEFN) with official Star Wars assets, templates, and gameplay systems for creators to use
  • Fortnite back on Google Play worldwide after Epic and Google settle

Business

  • Ubisoft ‘ends game development’ at Tom Clancy studio, Red Storm, resulting in 105 job losses

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• FBC: Firebreak just got its last major update, along with a price drop and free Friend’s Pass - “We have done engineering work to ensure we can sustain the upkeep of the relay servers when the player volume is lower.”
  • New Cyberpunk TCG becomes the most-funded TCG game in Kickstarter history - The $100K target was smashed within 5 minutes, and currently sits at £7.25 million
  • PlayStation is phasing out the "PlayStation Network" branding by September 2026. No functionality changes expected. Unclear what the new terminology will be
  • Cyberpunk 2077 arcade game, Cyberpunk 2077: Chrome Rush, trailer released

Business

  • Crystal Dynamics reduces workforce by 20 roles - fourth round of layoffs in 12 months

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• IO Interactive and MindsEye studio BARB officially end partnership, Hitman crossover DLC to be scrapped - BARB has assumed sole publishing responsibilities for Mindseye
  • Embark "mutually parts ways" with co-founder Rob Runesson following sexual misconduct allegations - Developer says "investigation did not substantiate the allegations" but it "found the situation unsustainable"
  • Starfield launches on PS5 on April 7, 2026

Free Games / Subscription

  • Pokémon XD: Gale of Darkness is available now on Nintendo Switch 2

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Cyberpunk 2077 PS5 Pro enhanced update coming soon
  • Nvidia has announced DLSS 5, the next generation of its image enhancement technology, which it claims is “the most significant breakthrough in computer graphics since the debut of real-time ray tracing”. Online reaction has been overwhelmingly negative, claiming that the tool creates "yassified, look-maxxing freaks"
  • PS5 Pro’s major PSSR update is rolling out to more games this week including the Silent Hill games, Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth and more
  • Nintendo released a new Handheld Mode Boost feature for Switch 2 that makes Switch 1 games use the "TV mode" performance modes in Switch 2's handheld mode

Business

  • Court tells Subnautica 2 publisher Krafton to reinstate fired CEO and let him handle its Early Access release. Judge ruled that Krafton terminated the game leads' contracts "without valid cause"
  • Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed Bin Salman’s EGDC firm has acquired 5% of Capcom - his public investment fund already bought 5% of the company in 2022

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Silent Hill and Slitterhead creator makes a recruitment call for his ‘new large-scale project’
  • Former Overwatch director Jeff Kaplan reveals The Legend of California, an open-world online Wild West FPS

Business

  • Warner Bros. Montréal devs report layoffs; While there's been no formal confirmation of the cuts as yet, several former WB developers from across all disciplines have announced on LinkedIn that they're now looking for work. Most report they finished on Friday, 13th March. Unknown amount of people affected
  • Resident Evil Requiem Sales Exceed 6 Million Units

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Games with loot boxes in them will be rated minimum PEGI 16, starting this summer. Online games with no blocking or reporting features will be rated PEGI 18
  • Tomb Raider 1-3 Remastered gets free challenge mode alongside mobile and Switch 2 release
  • RuneScape's monthly membership now costs as much as a World of Warcraft subscription as Jagex announces its second price hike in less than 2 years
  • The 7th Guest Remake announced. Seems to be a port of the VR version (without the VR). Coming soon to PS5, Xbox Series S/X, Nintendo Switch and PC

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Fortnite is making its original Save the World mode free-to-play
  • Microsoft quietly retires This is an Xbox marketing campaign

Free Games / Subscription

  • March PS Plus Game Catalog titles confirmed; Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2, EA Sports Madden NFL 26, Persona 5 Royal, Blasphemous 2, Metal Eden, Lord of the Rings: Return to Moria, Astroneer and Tekken Dark Resurrection

Business

  • Nintendo’s share price climbs 15% in two days following the success of Pokémon Pokopia; prior to this the share price had dropped 40% in three months
  • Silent Hill 2 Remake Tops 5 Million Players (includes sales + subscription services)
  • Global Sales of Pokémon Pokopia for Nintendo Switch 2 Surpass 2.2 Million in First Four Days

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Yoshi and the Mysterious Book will release on Thursday May 21st
  • Epic is increasing the price of Fortnite V-Bucks (and Fall Guys in-game currency) ‘to help pay the bills’ - initial reactions online have been overwhelmingly negative including calls to boycott Fortnite
  • Capcom confirms Resident Evil Requiem story expansion and mini-game
  • Bloober Team says it will reveal several new projects this year, including the ‘ambitious’ Project H - Project H will be developed by the team behind Cronos: A New Dawn
  • Rumours of a Fallout 3 remaster refuse to quiet down as new toy listings for still-unannounced project pop up in multiple places

Business

  • METAL GEAR SOLID Δ: SNAKE EATER Reaches 2 Million Global Sales
  • Original founders of Bossfight Entertainment re-launch studio as Bossfight - Developer was shuttered in 2024 by Netflix, which acquired the studio in 2022
  • Valve sued by The Performing Right Society for allegedly using its members' musical works "without permission" - Online reaction has claimed this to be a "Patent Troll" situation and Valve does not broadcast commerical music
  • Scopely hits $15 billion in lifetime revenue

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Shinji Mikami’s new studio, ‘Unbound’, is building a ‘AAA original IP’ for consoles and PC - The Resident Evil 4 director left Tango Gameworks in 2023
  • Nintendo releases final Mario Galaxy Movie trailer and confirms new cast members - Issa Rae as Honey Queen, Donald Glover as Yoshi and Luis Guzman as Wart

Business

  • EA lays off staff across Battlefield Studios, following last year's record-breaking Battlefield 6 debut - Unknown amount of employees affected
  • WarioWare director Goro Abe leaves Nintendo after 25+ years - Abe has taken up a professorial role at Osaka Electro-Communication University
  • Until Dawn Remake Developer Ballistic Moon Officially Dissolved

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Nagoshi Studio reportedly facing closure, after NetEase ‘pulls Gang of Dragon funding’ - Game was announced three months ago
  • Capcom quietly removes DRM system "Enigma" it added to 3-year-old Resident Evil 4 Remake last month following player backlash of performance issues it introduced

Business

  • Nintendo of America sues US government over tariffs

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Capcom has moved up Pragmata’s release. Originally scheduled for release on April 24, Pragmata will now arrive on PS5, Xbox Series, Nintendo Switch 2, and PC a week earlier, on April 17
  • Xbox confirms its next console will run PC games and is codenamed ‘Project Helix’
  • Suda and Swery’s Hotel Barcelona has received a free ‘major overhaul’ with Under New Management update
  • Embark Studios rushes to fix Arc Raiders Discord integration bug as "serious privacy and security violations" may have exposed private Discord DMs, friends data, more

Business

  • Pitchify launches new service to connect developers and publishers
  • Fortnite leaker actually worked for Epic Games, as a contractor, according to new lawsuit

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Assassin’s Creed boss provides updates on Hexe, Invictus, and Jade, confirms Black Flag remake - Plus AC Shadows updates are winding down and AC Unity is getting a 60fps patch this week
  • Final Fantasy 11 locks down yet another server as overpopulation continues to hit Final Fantasy 14's predecessor, over two decades after its original launch

Free Games / Subscription

  • Nintendo Switch Online's March update to the Classics library is all about Mario - Mario Tennis (Virtual Boy), Mario Clash (Virtual Boy) and Mario Vs Donkey Kong (GBA)

Business

  • Industry veteran, Fiona Cherback, launches indie-focused talent agency Rocket Game Talent - Former PlayStation and Xbox recruiter aims to provide AAA hiring expertise for small studios, startups, and indie teams
  • Build a Rocket Boy confirms more layoffs amid further claims of "organized espionage and corporate sabotage"
  • Good Games Group has bought the Humble and Firestoke back catalogues. Now, newly renamed as Balor Games, it wants to invest in triple-I
  • Paramount makes no reference to game studios in first WB call to investors

Rumors

  • PlayStation is reportedly ditching its PC release strategy, and most single-player first-party games will now be console exclusive
  • Unity Technologies will close its operations in France

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 4 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Highguard servers to go offline on March 12, 2026 - Game launched January 26th
  • The Unity Asset Store is ditching publishers based in China, Hong Kong, and Macau - Assets from creators in those regions will be delisted on March 31, 2026. Removal is the result of updated regional licensing, distribution, and compliance requirements for publishers in the Greater China region
  • Let It Die Is Going Offline After Nearly A Decade Of Live Service - will shift into an "offline version" in fall 2026

Free Games / Subscription

  • March Xbox Game Pass titles revealed - Final Fantasy III, Kingdom Come: Deliverance II, to a T, EA Sports F1 25, Planet of Lana II: Children of the Leaf, Construction Simulator, Cyberpunk 2077, Hollow Knight: Silksong and DreamWorks Gabby’s Dollhouse: Ready to Party

Business

  • Resident Evil Requiem surpasses 5 million units in 5 days
  • Killing Floor dev Tripwire Interactive lays off 23 employees (approx 20% of staff)

Rumors

  • Trump administration debating if to pressure Tencent to divest from US gaming studios

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Nacon delays game showcase after filing for insolvency: ‘We are choosing to focus our resources’
  • Nintendo announces Indie World showcase - Today (March 3) at 6am PT / 9am ET / 2pm GMT
  • Capcom Spotlight stream announced for March 5 featuring games: Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection, Pragmata, Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection, Mega Man Dual Override, Street Fighter 6

Business

  • Warner Bros. Discovery is "rebuilding its video game pipeline" after a "significant" 2025 - Warner Bros. Discovery does not provide disaggregated data for its game division, but across its full Studios segment (which includes gaming) revenue was down 14% year-on-year to $3.18 billion
  • Halfbrick Studios is cutting jobs in Australia - unknown amount of people affected

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/03/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 March 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Pokémon Winds & Waves revealed: New games coming in 2027 for Nintendo Switch 2
  • Sony confirms PS5 Pro upgrade (PSSR 2) rolled out with Resident Evil Requiem launch - roll out for other titles expected in March
  • Evercade's squeezing 14 Rare classics into a little handheld that's launching this June including Battletoads, Banjo Kazooie and more - Device is £49/$69/€59
  • Amazon shares first look at Ryan Hurst as Kratos in the God of War TV series - online reaction has likened the image to something from a parody movie like Scary Movie

Free Games / Subscription

  • Pokémon XD: Gale of Darkness comes to Nintendo Switch 2’s GameCube library this month
  • Netflix backs out of Warner Bros deal, declines to match Paramount Skydance's raised offer - unclear at this time how this will effect Warner Bros' gaming studios

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• RedOctane announce Guitar Hero successor Stage Tour, coming late 2026

Business

  • Assassin’s Creed: Hexe director, Clint Hocking, has left Ubisoft as part of franchise upheaval - Hocking previously helmed Splinter Cell: Chaos Theory, Far Cry 2 and Watch Dogs: Legion
  • Splash Damage acquires Scum dev Gamepires from Jagex - Splash Damage CFO Ben Hopkinson appointed CEO of combined joint venture, with financial backing from Emona Capital
  • Riot Games confirms layoffs within publishing division - 12 roles affected across its publishing teams

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• New York sues Valve over loot box-driven gambling

Free Games / Subscription

  • March’s PlayStation Plus Essential monthly games - PGA Tour 2K25, Monster Hunter Rise, Slime Rancher 2, The Elder Scrolls Online

Business

  • Amazon Game Studios ends publishing agreement with Maverick Games - Maverick Games was founded in 2022 by several of the core team behind the Forza Horizon series, including creative director Mike Brown. Development of Maverick's unannounced driving game "continues to progress as strongly as planned"
  • Skate developer Full Circle impacted by layoffs - Number of employees affected unknown as EA studio "reshapes to better support Skate's long-term future"
  • Nacon files for insolvency, says it’s ‘assessing all possible solutions’ to continue running - the company's majority shareholder was unable to repay its bond loan

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 25/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Marvel’s Wolverine release date revealed - September 15, 2026 for PS5
  • New mobile Overwatch game; Overwatch Rush, announced

Business

  • Ex-Radical Entertainment, Hothead Games devs form new studio - "New Radical Games". Spearheaded by CEO Ian Wilkinson and COO Tim Bennison
  • Kingdom Come: Deliverance director Daniel Vávra is stepping away from game development - the writer and designer will instead helm efforts to produce a Kingdom Come movie

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Amazon is shutting down another game: Former Lionhead devs’ (Glowmade) King of Meat - the comedy platformer will close six months after it launched
  • Nintendo announces 80-minute Treehouse stream showcasing Mario Wonder Switch 2 and Pokémon Pokopia - Tuesday, February 24 at 2pm PT / 5pm ET / 10pm GMT
  • Nier creator Yoko Taro (and composer Keiichi Okabe) and working on a new series of Evangelion

Business

  • Matthew Ball's State of Video Gaming in 2026

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Ubisoft’s CEO breaks silence, says the company has ‘multiple’ Far Cry and Assassin’s Creed games coming

Business

  • Nier: Automata Crosses 10 Million Copies Sold Alongside Mysterious 'To Be Continued' Tease
  • Final Fantasy VII (1997) has sold over 15.5 million units worldwide
  • Embark Studios CEO Patrick Söderlund has been named Executive Chairman of Nexon

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Pokémon Presents confirmed for next week - Friday, February 27 at 6:00 a.m. PST / 2:00 p.m. GMT / 3:00 p.m. CET on the official Pokémon YouTube and Twitch channels
  • Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen coming to Nintendo Switch next week (will be available after the upcoming Pokemon Presents event), but not as part of Switch Online
  • Slay the Spire 2 early access trailer released - Game launches in early access March 5th
  • Nintendo surprises fans with a Switch 2 Edition of Xenoblade Chronicles X, out now - Physical version coming April 16. Players who already own the Switch 1 version of Definitive Edition can upgrade for $5
  • Phil Spencer Retiring, Sarah Bond Out, Matt Booty Promoted as Microsoft AI Exec Asha Sharma Named New Xbox Boss

Business

  • Sony is shutting down Bluepoint, developer of Demon’s Souls remake
  • Ubisoft confirms layoffs at Toronto studio, but insists Splinter Cell’s remake is still coming - around 40 people affected

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Warframe for Android has launched globally
  • Destiny 2: Shadow and Order Delayed Until June 9th
  • REPLACED Release Date Shifts To April 14th
  • Kristen Bell Joins Sonic the Hedgehog 4 movie as Amy Rose
  • Highguard Dev Speaks Out After Website Outage Sparks Shutdown Rumors - Website is being "Transferred and simplified"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Classic shooter Unreal Tournament 2004 is now free as fans revive and release it with Epic's blessing

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox has announced the rest of February’s Game Pass titles
    • Aerial_Knight’s DropShot (Cloud, Xbox Series S/X, Handheld, and PC) – Available now
    • Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora (Cloud, Xbox Series S/X, Handheld, and PC) – Available now
    • Death Howl (Cloud, Xbox Series S/X, Handheld, and PC) – February 19
    • EA Sports College Football 26 (Cloud and Xbox Series S/X) – February 19
    • The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt – Complete Edition (Cloud and Console) – February 19
    • TCG Card Shop Simulator (Game Preview) (Cloud, Xbox Series S/X, Handheld, and PC)
    • Dice A Million (PC) – February 25
    • Towerborne (Full Game Release) (Console, Handheld, and PC)
    • Final Fantasy III (Cloud, Xbox Series S/X, and PC) – March 3
    • Kingdom Come: Deliverance II (Cloud, Xbox Series S/X, and PC)
  • Nintendo’s Virtual Boy app is now available on Switch Online, with seven games at launch
    • 3D Tetris
    • Galactic Pinball
    • Golf
    • The Mansion of Innsmouth
    • Red Alarm
    • Teleroboxer
    • Virtual Boy Wario Land

Business

  • Hasbro shutters internal video game studio Atomic Arcade - The North Carolina studio had been developing a title based on the G.I. Joe franchise. The studio is gone but Hasbro has said that the project (dubbed "Untitled Snake Eyes Project") has not been cancelled
  • Mewgenics has reached 1 million copies sold

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Metal Gear's Solid Snake joins the roster of Rainbow Six Siege
  • Call of Duty: Warzone Mobile servers to shut down in April 17th 2026 - unrelated to Call of Duty Mobile

Business

  • Embracer Financial Results
    • For the three months ending December 31, 2025
      • Net sales: SEK 5.1 billion ($569.2 million), down 26% year-on-year, including:
        • PC/console games: SEK 1.9 billion ($212 million), down 11%
        • Mobile games: SEK 566 million ($63.1 million), down 66%
      • Net profit: SEK 477 million ($53.1 million, compared to net profit of SEK 806 million / $89.9 million during the same period last year)
    • For the nine months ending December 31, 2025:
      • Net sales: SEK 11.9 billion ($1.3 billion), down 26% year-on-year, including:
        • PC/console games: SEK 5 billion ($557 million), down 22%
        • Mobile games: SEK 1.6 billion ($178 million), down 63%
      • Net loss: SEK 20 million ($2.2 million, compared to net loss of SEK 1.5 billion / $167.4 million during the same period last year)
  • NetEase revenue grows 6.9% to $16.1bn in 2025 - Publisher's games segment rose 10.1% following higher sales of Fantasy Westward Journey Online, Where Winds Meet, and Marvel Rivals

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Hideki Sato, designer of all Sega’s consoles, has died at age 77
  • Shutaro Iida, director of Bloodstained, has died at the age of 52
  • Clair Obscur’s soundtrack is breaking records - Released by Laced Records, the Expedition 33 original soundtrack has become the first game score to reach No. 1 on the Official Independent Albums Chart (UK), the Official Physical Albums Chart (France, UK), and the Official Vinyl Albums Chart (France, UK)
  • Capcom drops PPV price of upcoming Street Fighter 6 tournament stream from $40 to $10 following player backlash - Unrestricted co-streaming will also be allowed for the group stages

Business

  • Ubisoft Financial Results
    • For the nine months ending December 31, 2025:
      • Revenue: €976.2 million (down 1.4% year-on-year)
      • Net bookings: €1.1 billion (up 18% year-on-year)
      • Digital net bookings: €941.7 million (up 20% year-on-year)
      • Back-catalogue net bookings: €1.03 billion (up 36.2% year-on-year)
    • For the three months ending December 31, 2025:
      • Net bookings: €338 million (up 12% year-on-year)
      • Digital net bookings: €297 million (up 10.7% year-on-year)
  • Sega records $200m impairment write-down for Rovio during Q3
    • For the nine months ended December 31, 2025:
      • Net sales: ¥335.2 billion ($2.1 billion, up 4% year-on-year)
      • Operating income: ¥19.8 billion ($129.2 million, down 54.6% year-on-year)
      • Ordinary income: ¥23.8 billion ($155.3 million, down 51.8% year-on-year)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Metal Gear Solid Master Collection 2 Announced - Contains Metal Gear Solid 4, Metal Gear Solid: Peace Walker and the Game Boy exclusive Metal Gear Solid: Ghost Babel. Releases August 27th for PS5, Xbox, Nintendo Switch / 2 and PC
  • Silent Hill: Townfall is based in Scotland, is first person and releases in 2026
  • Bungie is holding an ‘open preview weekend’ for Marathon later this month - Sever Slam preview weekend will run from February 26 to March 2 on PlayStation 5, Xbox, and PC, and feature “an introduction to Marathon’s gameplay”, with some content locked for the full release on March 5
  • Legacy of Kain is getting a Defiance remaster, and a new 2D action game - Defiance launches March 3. Legacy of Kain Ascending releases March 31st. Defiance's deluxe edition comes with scrapped demo of cancelled sequel Dark Prophecy
  • New Saros trailer addresses Returnal complaints head-on with a permanent progression system
  • Rayman: 30th Anniversary Edition is officially coming this week, courtesy of Digital Eclipse - Comes with six version of Rayman 1 (including SNES prototype). Releases today
  • PlayStation shadow drops God of War 2D spin-off and confirms a Trilogy remake - God of War: Sons of Sparta is available now. Trilogy remake is in early development
  • John Wick game starring Keanu Reeves revealed during State of Play - Developed by Saber Interactive
  • Castlevania: Belmont’s Curse is a new Castlevania game coming in 2026 - developed by Dead Cells and The Rogue Prince of Persia - releasing in 2026
  • A new Dead or Alive game is in development, while Dead or Alive 6: Last Round is coming in June
  • Ghost of Yotei’s Legends multiplayer mode is coming next month - The co-op multiplayer mode will be free to all players and feature three modes
  • Death Stranding 2 is coming to PC next month - March 19
  • PlayStation adventure Kena: Bridge of Spirits is getting a PS5 and PC sequel this year - Kena: Scars of Kosmora will release for PS5 and PC this year
  • Helldivers movie lands Minecraft and Street Fighter actor Jason Momoa

Free Games / Subscription

  • February PS Plus Game Catalog titles confirmed
    • Marvel’s Spider-Man 2
    • Test Drive Unlimited Solar Crown
    • Neva
    • Season: A Letter to the Future
    • Monster Hunter Stories 2: Wings of Ruin
    • Monster Hunter Stories
    • Venba
    • Echoes of the End: Enhanced Edition
    • Rugby 25
    • PlayStation Plus Premium
      • Disney Pixar Wall-E

Business

  • Kingdom Come: Deliverance II surpasses 5 million sales within first year

Rumors

  • GTFO developer 10 Chambers lays off staff following co-founder departing after a decade (to go indie)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Highguard Developer Wildlight Entertainment Confirms Layoffs At The Studio - One of the staff members affected claims the majority of the studio is impacted, official statement claims a core team remains to continue working on the game
  • At least 1,200 Ubisoft workers strike in response to recent restructuring
  • Playstation State of Play tonight / Silent Hill Transmission afterwards - State of Play starts at 10pm UK time

Business

  • Unity Financial Results
    • Revenue: $503 million (up 10% year-on-year)
    • Create Solutions revenue: $165 million (up 8% year-on-year)
    • Grow Solutions revenue: $338 million (up 11% year-on-year)
    • Net loss: $89 million (with a margin of 18%)
  • Arc Raiders has sold 14 million units and has 6 million weekly active users

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Silent Hill Transmission set for February 12 (4pm PT / 7pm ET) featuring Silent Hill: Townfall

Business

  • Supercell revenue declines 4% to €2.65bn in 2025 - Headcount grew 30% reaching 890 employees globally, developer contributed €220 million to Finnish economy
  • Square Enix revenues continue to decline over past nine months to $1.3bn
    • Net sales: ¥215.4 billion ($1.3 billion, down 13.3%)
    • Profit: ¥25.6 billion ($163.4 million, up 3.6%)
    • Digital entertainment net sales: ¥112.3 billion ($717 million, down 23.7%)
  • Mattel to acquire full ownership of Mattel163 from NetEase - Acquisition of mobile studio will "significantly enhance Mattel's capabilities in self-publishing"
  • Mewgenics Sells Over 150k Copies in First 6 Hours; "This Is Beating Expectations by Quite a Lot" - Made back its development budget after three hours
  • Lies of P and the Overture DLC have surpassed 4 million units sold combined

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• PlayStation announces a new hour-long State of Play coming later this week - Thursday, February 12 at 2pm PT / 5pm ET / 10pm GMT

Business

  • Riot Games says it’s ‘downsizing’ the 2XKO development team, after a disappointing launch - Approximately 80 employees laid off
  • Remedy Financial Results
    • Q4 2025
      • Revenue: €17 million (up 46.3%)
      • Operating profit: €0.7 million (compared to operating loss of €1.4 million in 2024)
    • FY 2025
      • Revenue: €59.5 million (up 17%)
      • Operating loss: €14.9 million (compared to €4.3 million in 2024)
  • Control developer Remedy names former EA exec, Jean-Charles Gaudechon, as new CEO

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Hideo Kojima no longer attending GDC Festival of Gaming 2026 - Members of Kojima Productions will still be presenting talks during the event. No reason given
  • Mindseye Co-CEO Mark Gerhard alleges €1 Million was spent in sabotaging the game, will persue legal action

Business

  • Roblox Financial Results
    • Q4
      • Revenue: $1.4 billion, up 43% year-on-year
      • Bookings: $2.2 billion, up 63% year-on-year
      • Net losses: $318 million, compared to $221 million in the same quarter last year
    • Full-year
      • Revenue: $4.9 billion, up 36% year-on-year
      • Bookings: $6.8 billion, up 55% year-on-year
      • Net losses: $1.07 billion, compared to $940.6 million last year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Namco’s last major arcade Ridge Racer game, Rave Racer, is getting a home version for the first time - coming to PS4/5, Switch/2 and Xbox Series S/X,
  • PlayStation and Guerrilla announce ‘tactical co-op’ Horizon game, Horizon Hunters Gathering
  • Super Bomberman Collection has been announced for Switch and Switch 2, is available now. The compilation includes seven games including unreleased localised editions of some titles
  • Fallout 4 and Indiana Jones have been confirmed for the Switch 2 - Indiana Jones and the Great Circle will arrive on Nintendo's platform on 12th May. Fallout 4: Anniversary Edition, meanwhile, will be available on the eShop from 24th February
  • Baldur's Gate to be adapted into HBO series - Last of Us showrunner Craig Mazin to helm series, which takes place after the events of Baldur's Gate 3
  • MachineGames asking more staff to move to full-time office work - "We know that we are a lot more efficient and a lot more collaborative in general when we're actually in the office together"

Business

  • Sony Q3 Results
    • For the nine months ended December 31, 2025
      • Sales: ¥9.4 trillion ($59.8 billion, up 2.3%)
      • Operating income: ¥1.3 trillion ($8.2 billion, up 21%)
    • Games & network services
      • Sales: ¥3.66 trillion ($23.2 billion, up 1.3%)
      • Operating income: ¥409.1 billion ($2.6 billion, up 27%)
  • No Rest For The Wicked sells 1.5 million copies
  • UK co-development house Sumo Digital announces redundancies

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Valve confirms Steam Machine and Steam Frame price / release details have been delayed by exploding component prices - still planned for release for the first half of this year
  • Overwatch 2 is being renamed back to just "Overwatch", adding 10 new heroes, and coming to Switch 2, in major revamp
  • Nintendo Direct Partner showcase today (6am PT / 9am ET / 2pm GMT) - will last for roughly 30 minutes
  • Poker Night at the Inventory re-releasing March 5th for Steam, PS4 and Nintendo Switch

Business

  • Sony says Ghost of Yotei sales are outperforming Tsushima

item.content:encoded:
• Players buying third-party games on the Epic Games Store will soon unlock Fortnite cosmetics based on those games - Capcomn and Mihoyo are among the studios already signed up for crossover content
  • "The launcher sucks, let's call it what it is," Epic Game Store boss (Steven Allison) says, promising a year of big improvements to speed, new "forum-type" social features, more

Free Games / Subscription

    • Final Fantasy II (Cloud, Xbox Series S/X, and PC) – Now Available
