    • Net loss: $92.9 million (compared to $125.2 million loss in the year-ago quarter)
    • Total net bookings: $1.76 billion (up 28% year-on-year)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• The original Alone in the Dark trilogy is currently free on GOG - The trilogy is part of the GOG preservation program
  • IGN Fan Fest Returns in 2026, promising over 80 exclusive reveals (and appearances from over 50 games) - 25th February and 26th February

Business

  • Switch is officially Nintendo’s best-selling console ever, overtaking DS - Switch has sold 155.37 million units, DS has sold 154.02 million units
  • UK retailer Game is closing its remaining standalone stores after entering administration (three stores affected) - concession stores and website are unaffected
  • Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds blasts past 1m copies sold, and Sega is expecting another million "within this fiscal year". The game currently has not met SEGA's sales expectations

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/02/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 February 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Google rolls out prototype of Project Genie, an AI tool which generates time limited (60 second) "playable" worlds - Game company stock prices temporarily affected
  • Escape from Tarkov announces first major crossover event; Escape from Tarkov x Escape from Duckov
  • Austin Wintory wins first Grammy for 'Best Score Soundtrack For Video Games And Other Interactive Media' for Sword of the Sea
  • TEKKEN 8 - Season 3 Trailer reveals Kunimitsu, Bob and Roger Jr. and more

Business

  • Ashes of Creation's leadership team quits "in protest" as director accuses board of decisions he could "not ethically agree with or carry out" - Remaining staff claim they are now facing mass layoffs
  • Cairn has reached 200k copies sold

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo reveals Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream Release Date - April 16th
  • Bloober Team tease new game. Fans speculate could be either a new IP or a Rule of Rose remake

Business

  • Paradox operating profit dropped 162% year-on-year as it writes down Vampire: The Masquerade Bloodlines 2
    • For the three months ending December 31, 2025
      • Revenue: SEK 874.8 million ($98.7 million), up 23% year-on-year
      • Operating profit: -SEK245.4 million (-$27.7 million), down 162% year-on-year
    • For the 12 months ending December 31, 2025
      • Sales: SEK 2.2 billion ($248.3 million), level year-on-year
      • Operating profit: SEK 146 million ($16.5 million), an 80% decrease year-on-year
  • Over 1M units of Silent Hill games were sold in the last quarter. Silent Hill series has now sold over 14M units worldwide. Metal Gear Solid franchise sales are up 0.4M QoQ, now at 65.5M total

Rumors

  • UK retailer GAME is going into administration again

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Indie dev who turned to social media to lengthen their game’s credits ended up taking 300 hours to add 60,000 names - The developer promised to add anyone who liked their tweet, expecting 100 to reply
  • A seven-game retro compilation of Ren & Stimpy games has been announced - Ren & Stimpy: Happy, Happy, Joy, Joy Collection is coming to Switch, PS5 and PC
  • Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream Direct today (6am PST / 2pm GMT / 11pm JST)

Free Games / Subscription

  • February’s PlayStation Plus Essential monthly games have been confirmed - Ace Combat 7: Skies Unknown, Subnautica: Below Zero, Undisputed and Ultros

Business

  • QA staff report layoffs at NetEase Games Montréal
  • Ubisoft unions call for four-day strike: "It is time for management to understand that they cannot do whatever they want"
  • Jack Emmert returns to Cryptic Studios as CEO - MMO developer has "reassumed responsibility" for its titles, including Star Trek Online, following divestment from Embracer
  • comcept shuts down - Studio was founded by Keiji Inafune in December 2010
  • Xbox FY26 Q2
    • Total Gaming Revenue (-9%)
    • Content and Services Revenue (-5%)
    • Hardware Revenue (-32%)

Rumors

  • Amazon Game Studios boss Christoph Hartmann (formerly head of 2K) is leaving the company as Amazon continues its retreat from the PC and console video-game space in favor of its cloud gaming service Luna

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• GOG job posting for Senior Software Engineer mentions Linux support for GOG Galaxy - "Linux is the next frontier"

Free Games / Subscription

  • 2 unreleased Virtual Boy games are coming to Nintendo Switch as part of the NSO Expansion Pack - Zero Racer and D-Hopper will be playable for the first time

Business

  • UK tribunal rules £656m Valve market rigging lawsuit can proceed
  • Capcom Financial Results
    • Net sales: ¥115.3 billion ($748.8 million), up 29.8% year-on-year
    • Operating income: ¥54.3 billion ($352.6 million), up 75.1% year-on-year
    • Net income: ¥38.8 billion ($251.9 million), up 68.6% year-on-year
  • Chinese mobile publisher Yodo1 rolls out data platform IPVerse - The service is designed to allow developers and property holders to figure out what the best partnerships are

Rumors

  • Walmart could have leaked Nintendo Switch Online’s next GameCube games as two unannounced Gamecube titles are pictured on its website - Metroid Prime 2 and Pikmin 2
  • Ubisoft Employee Claims He’s Placed on Unpaid Suspension for Criticising Return-to-Office Policy

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Blizzard announces four showcases over the next two weeks for World of Warcraft, Overwatch, Hearthstone and Diablo
  • A Nintendo Direct dedicated to Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream is coming this week - Thursday, January 29 at 6am PT / 9am ET / 2pm GMT
  • Final Fantasy 7 Remake director explains why the third game will still use Unreal Engine 4
  • Stop Killing Games' EU initiative clears almost 1.3m signatures, prepares for official submission to European Commission next month
  • Game Awards final game Highguard releases to "Mostly Negative" Steam reviews

Business

  • UK Appeals court state RuneScape gold counts as property and can be stolen, in $700k bombshell case
  • Hyrule Warriors: Age of Imprisonment sales top 1m in three months
  • Ubisoft launches voluntary redundancy process to cut 200 jobs from Paris head office

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Mario Galaxy Movie release date brought forward in global alignment - The movie will now release April 1st in many major markets. Releases April 24th in Japan
  • Vampire Survivors' highly anticipated deck-building spin-off is getting an Xbox and Steam demo soon - Demo releases 23rd February

Business

  • Roblox claims Bruno Mars Steal a Brainrot performance drew a record 12.8 million concurrent users, issued 5.4 million brainrot collectibles

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Double Fine reveals Kiln, a pottery-based multiplayer party brawler - Kiln is due for release later this year on Xbox Series S/X, PS5 and PC, and will be available on Xbox Game Pass on the day of release
  • Xbox’s big Fable reveal confirms ‘truly’ open world, and Xbox and PlayStation release this Autumn
  • Pokémon studio Game Freak gives a new look at its PS5, Xbox and PC action RPG Beast of Reincarnation
  • Forza Horizon 6 first gameplay details revealed and release date confirmed - Releases May 19, with a Premium Edition offering early access starting on May 15. PS5 version launches later this year
  • Unity launches Walmart SDK to "seamlessly integrate products" into games

Business

  • Battlefield outsold Call of Duty for the first time ever last year in the US, as Black Ops 7 dropped to 5th place
  • Ubisoft shares drop 33% following its ‘major company reset’ announcement - The company's share price has dropped 95% in total over the past eight years
  • RuneScape tops $4 billion in lifetime revenue

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 22/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Pokémon is coming to Universal Studios - A permanent Pokémon attraction will de but in Japan, followed by the US. No word if there are plans for a Pokémon attraction at Universal Bedford
  • Donkey Kong Country Returns HD gets a free update which improves Switch 2 visuals and adds Dixie Kong

Business

  • Ubisoft details ‘major company reset’: 6 games canned, 7 delayed, and 2 studios closed. Prince of Persia Sands of Time remake cancelled. Ubisoft asking all staff to return to office full-time to "boost the collective performance for AAA". Changes will cause additional billion-euro loss in fiscal 2026 but "lay foundations for a return to midterm growth"
  • Layoffs Hit Payday 3 Developer Starbreeze

Rumors

  • Beyond Good & Evil 2 Survives Ubisoft’s Project Purge

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Life is Strange: Reunion officially announced, will conclude the Max and Chloe saga - Releases March 26th for PS5, Xbox Series S/X and PC

Free Games / Subscription

Business

  • Doug Bowser joins Hasbro’s board, fresh from retiring as Nintendo of America boss
  • Scotland aims to boost games sector to more than $1bn by 2030 - Scottish Game Action Plan looking to build nation into a "games supercluster" with a phased approach
  • Tainted Grail: The Fall of Avalon has sold 1 million copies

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Ubisoft is updating three classic Far Cry games to run at 60fps on modern platforms - Far Cry 3, Far Cry 3 Blood Dragon and Far Cry Primal will receive the updates January 21st
  • Marathon releases March 5th

Business

  • Ex-Assassin's Creed boss Marc-Alexis Côté sues Ubisoft after his surprise and "disguised dismissal" - Seeks CAD $1.3m (£698k) for a mixture of lost severance pay and "moral damages", and for Ubisoft to lift Côté's non-compete clause
  • Hill Climb Racing Tops 2 billion downloads

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Valve has ‘significantly’ rewritten Steam’s rules for how developers must disclose AI use
  • Viral Roblox hit Steal A Brainrot is now a big enough deal it's getting its first celebrity concert, from Bruno Mars
  • PUBG maker Krafton has 26 games in the pipeline as it continues hunt for "big franchise IP" - 12 of these titles are set to launch within the next two years

Business

  • Ace Combat 7 has sold over 7m copies, seven years after its launch
  • Arknights Endfield passes 35m pre-registered players ahead of launch this month
  • Saudi PIF moving $12bn of games firm shares to subsidiary Savvy - It will ultimately own the fund's stakes in companies such as Nintendo, Take-Two and Square Enix

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Amazon Games confirms MMO New World has a year left before it goes offline permanently
  • Sega announces Sonic 35th anniversary plans to ‘celebrate Sonic’s legacy’
  • Amazon has greenlit a Fallout reality show where contestants live in a vault for a ‘huge cash prize’ - "Fallout Shelter" is currently looking for contestants
  • Yakuza Kiwami 3 continues the series’ retro tradition by including 12 playable Game Gear titles
  • Sony wiped over 1,000 shovelware games off the PlayStation store without warning, all from a single developer (ThiGames)
  • Amazon's God of War series has found its Kratos; Ryan Hurst - Along with his live action credits, which include as Gerry Bertier in Remember the Titans and Opie Winston in Sons of Anarchy, Hurst also portrayed Thor in 2022's God of War Ragnarök

Business

  • DRAGON BALL Z: KAKAROT has sold over 10 million copies worldwide
  • Scopely's Monopoly Go is the fastest mobile game in history to hit $6bn revenue

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 15/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 15 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• As Anthem shuts down, the game’s executive producer (Mark Darrah) has released a nearly four-hour post-mortem
  • Animal Crossing New Horizons 3.0 and Switch 2 Upgrade now available
  • Multiple Ecco the Dolphin games officially in development
  • Resident Evil Showcase set for today (2pm PT / 10pm GMT) - promising closer look at Resident Evil Requiem

Free Games / Subscription

  • PS Plus Extra and Premium games for January 20th
    • Resident Evil Village
    • Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth
    • Expeditions: A MudRunner Game
    • A Quiet Place: The Road Ahead
    • Darkest Dungeon 2
    • The Exit 8
    • Art of Rally
    • A Little to the Left
    • Ridge Racer (PlayStation Plus Premium)

Business

  • Ubisoft confirms 29 jobs lost at Abu Dhabi mobile studio - "Difficult decision to restructure" lead to cancellation of some projects to focus on free-to-play MMO Growtopia

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• ‘You don’t want to wrestle Goliath’: GOG bosses say the aim is not to defeat Steam, but better it in certain areas - "Certainly we can do more for classic retro and modern classics"
  • Judge denies request from fired Rockstar employees to be paid while legal case is ongoing - the staff failed to show a "pretty good chance of success" in proving they were fired for starting a union, the judge said
  • Rockstar launches official marketplace for mods - Acquired from Cfx (creators of FiveM and RedM). Some mods are free, while bundles can cost several hundred dollars

Business

  • Meta has closed several studios, including ‘Splosion Man studio Twisted Pixel and Asgard Wrath’s Sanzaru Games
  • Ubisoft hits The Division and Avatar studio, Massive Entertainment, with layoffs - Around 55 roles could be affected; studio remains focused on future on The Division
  • Arc Raiders has sold 2.4m copies in 2026 so far
  • Larry ‘Major Nelson’ has recently been laid off by Unity

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Media Molecule co-founder and Bullfrog veterans on board for Peter Molyneux’s ‘final’ god game, Masters of Albion - Releases April 22nd on Steam
  • The first Lego Pokémon sets have been revealed, including a $650 Charizard, Blastoise and Venusaur statue
  • Palworld is getting an official card game this summer - releases July 30th
  • Resident Evil Showcase planned for Jan 15th, 2025 at 2pm PST

Business

  • Detroit: Become Human surpasses 15 million units sold worldwide
  • ARC Raiders sales top 12.4 million

Rumors

  • A poster for The Division: Definitive Edition has reportedly appeared at a Ubisoft event in Japan

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Following various AI controversies, Larian will not use generative AI for Divinity concept art - "That way, there can be no discussion about the origin of the art"
  • Awesome Games Done Quick 2026 closes with £1.8m raised for Prevent Cancer Foundation
  • Fortnite Now Lets Creators Publish Islands With In-Game Transactions - The move by Epic Games has sparked concerns that user-created games will turn into pay-to-win experiences

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• South Park is coming to Fortnite this week - Free mini battle pass focused on South Park, Cartmanland, mech suit skins and more
  • Xbox Developer Direct coming later this month featuring Fable, Forza and Game Freak - Jan 22, will include first in-depth look into Fable
  • Xbox and Obsidian’s Avowed is coming to PS5 - PS5 version will release alongside Avowed’s anniversary update on February 17, along with a New Game Plus mode, new races, new weapon types, a Photo Mode, and more

Free Games / Subscription

  • Fire Emblem: Path of Radiance has been added to Nintendo Switch Online

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Netflix confirms The Witcher series will end this year
  • This Week In Video Games news editor Patrick Dane has died in a car accident in Hampshire, England
  • Facial Age Checks Now Required to Chat on Roblox

Business

  • Ubisoft has closed its Halifax studio and claims the decision is unrelated to recent unionization - 70 roles affected

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Sony AI patent will see PlayStation games play themselves when players are stuck
  • Stranger Things' final battle was directly influenced by Baldur's Gate 3
  • Sigourney Weaver and Jason Isaacs cast in Amazon's Tomb Raider series

Free Games / Subscription

    • Brews & Bastards (Consoles, PC, and cloud) - Available now
    • Little Nightmares: Enhanced Edition (Xbox Series S/X, PC, and Cloud) - Available now
    • Atomfall (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC) – 7th January

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds is getting Pac-Man content later this week
  • Former ZeniMax Online boss confirms he quit following Microsoft axe - Project Blackbird was cancelled as part of Microsoft's 2025 cuts
  • Sega co-founder and arcade founding father, David Rosen, dies age 95
  • Xbox veteran Albert Penello has passed away - The Microsoft alumnus died aged 53 after a battle with cancer

Business

  • Final Fantasy 9 character designer, Toshiyuki Itahana, quits Square Enix
  • Final Fantasy Tactics: The Ivalice Chronicles shipments and digital sales top one million - Achieved in three months

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/01/2026
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 January 2026.
item.content:encoded:
• 2025 Steam Award winners announced
    • Game of the Year: Hollow Knight: Silksong
    • VR Game of the Year: The Midnight Walk
    • Labor of Love Award: Baldur's Gate 3

    • Most Innovative Gameplay Award: Arc Raiders

Free Games / Subscription

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/12/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 December 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Game Awards 2025 Sets Record With 171 Million Livestreams Worldwide
  • Sony vs Tencent ‘Horizon Clone’ Lawsuit Over Light of Motiram Ends in Settlement, Game Delisted From Storefronts
  • 2013's Tomb Raider reboot coming to mobile devices next year - Feb 12th for iOS and Android

Business

  • Final Fantasy Pixel Remaster series sales top six million
  • Horses Sells Over 18k Copies, Pays Back Loans and Royalties Despite Removal From Steam and Epic
  • Hogwarts Legacy tops 40 million units sold - Milestone does not include free copies given away via Epic Games Store
  • FIFA officially announces new game with Netflix and little-known studio; California-based studio, Delphi Interactive
  • After putting its entire staff on redundancy notice, Wreckreation studio Three Fields turns to Patreon for help

Rumors

  • Unity reportedly introduces $250K+ "minimum contribution" for its biggest customers - This minimum commitment amount is determined based on your gross revenue from the previous 12 months

Other Notes

  • That's a wrap on another year of the Game News Round-Up! I want to thank each and everyone of you for constantly tuning in, emoji reacting and supporting the effort! It's crazy to think this started almost 6 years ago and has been going strong daily since!
  • From all of us that help bring the Game News Round-Up to your virtual doorstep, please have a fantastic holiday break and we'll see you next year!

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/12/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 December 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Brian Fleming, co-founder of Ghost of Yotei studio Sucker Punch, is leaving the studio - Studio will be run by Jason Connell and Adrian Bentley
  • Tencent invests in ‘hyperreal’ bodycam shooter that became internet sensation - Unrecord has entered full production and expanded its development team

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/12/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 December 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Bungie finally confirms a Marathon release date and $40 price point - March 2026 for PS5, Xbox Series S/X, and PC at the target price of $39.99/€39.99/£34.99
  • Team Cherry casually unveils first "big" expansion for Hollow Knight: Silksong titled Sea of Sorrow - and it's completely free. Coming 2026
  • Megabonk gets spooky update just in time for the festive season, with new map, character and more

Business

  • Ubisoft acquires upcoming MOBA March of Giants from Amazon Game Studios, rehires former Ubisoft Montreal dev team
  • Hollow Knight: Silksong sells over 7m copies in three months
  • Where Winds Meet tops 15 million players in one month
  • Dambuster Studios' financial statements are out (as of March 2025) The company employed 194 employees Dead Island 3 is in development The current predicted release window for the project is Q1/2 2028

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 15/12/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 15 December 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• French President publicly congratulates Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, calling the Game of The Year win "a historic first"
  • "The scripts are incredible, and very funny" - Sigourney Weaver confirms she will play Wallace in live-action Tomb Raider series
  • Co-CEO of Lies of P maker Neowiz says Korea's government is helping indie boom - But the executive says that securing funding has become harder for studios in the region
  • Sir Nick Clegg joins Hiro Capital as general partner to new scale-up VC fund, Hiro III - The new fund is focused on "spatial AI, robotics, longevity, games, and space and defence"
  • New Tomb Raider Games Recast Lara Croft With A Gaming Veteran - Wilton Regan is known for her roles as Samantha Traynor in Mass Effect 3 and the female Inquisitor in Dragon Age: Inquisition. She also played Aya in Assassin's Creed Origins

item.title: Game News Round-Up Special Edition — The Game Awards 2025 - 12/12/2025
item.description: Special edition games-industry round-up: The Game Awards 2025 (12 December 2025).
item.content:encoded:
• Game Award Winners
    • Game of the Year - Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
    • Best Game Direction - Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
    • Best Adaptation - The Last of Us: Season 2
    • Best Narrative - Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
    • Best Art Direction - Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
    • Best Score And Music - Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
    • Best Audio Design - Battlefield 6
    • Best Performance - Jennifer English (Clair Obscur: Expedition 33)
    • Innovation In Accessibility - Doom: The Dark Ages
    • Games for Impact - South of Midnight
    • Best Ongoing Game - No Man’s Sky
    • Best Community Support - Baldur’s Gate 3
    • Best Independent Game - Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
    • Best Debut Indie Game - Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
    • Best Mobile Game - Umamusume: Pretty Derby
    • Best VR/AR Game - The Midnight Walk
    • Best Action Game - Hades II
    • Best Action Adventure Game - Hollow Knight: Silksong
    • Best RPG - Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
    • Best Fighting Game - Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves
    • Best Family Game - Donkey Kong Bananza
    • Best Sim/Strategy Game - Final Fantasy Tactics - The Ivalice Chronicles
    • Best Sports/Racing - Mario Kart World
    • Best Multiplayer - Arc Raiders
    • Most Anticipated Game - Grand Theft Auto VI
    • Content Creator of the Year - MoistCr1TiKaL
    • Best Esports Game - Counter-Strike 2
    • Best Esports Athlete - Chovy
    • Best Esports Team - Team Vitality
    • Players’ Voice - Wuthering Waves

Announcement Highlights

  • A new Mega Man game is coming in 2027 - Mega Man Dual Override
  • Star Wars: Galactic Racer is the debut title from Fuse Games, the studio founded by former Criterion leads
  • Star Wars: Fate of the Old Republic Revealed with First Trailer - Directed by Casey Hudson, the director of the original Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic and the Mass Effect trilogy
  • PlayStation 5 exclusive Saros has been delayed until April 20th 2026
  • Soma is getting a "spiritual successor" from the same studio, and it's set in a grim moon hotel on the edge of reality - titled Ontos. Releases 2026
  • Total War: Warhammer 40,000 Announced From Creative Assembly - Stars David Harbour
  • 007 First Light trailer unveils villain played by Lenny Kravitz
  • Mario + Rabbids creator's next game spoofs Bloodborne and more, in a mascot platformer parody of the games industry starring a badger - titled Bradley the Badger and stars Evan Peters
  • The Game Awards’ mysterious statue was for a new Divinity game
  • Coven of the Chicken Foot is the debut game from Uncharted and Last of Us director Bruce Straley’s new studio
  • Capcom officially reveals that Leon Kennedy will be in Resident Evil Requiem - The game is split into two. Grace's part is survival horror and Leon's is action
  • A new trailer for Exodus shows more of Wizards of the Coast’s sci-fi action RPG starring Matthew McConaughey
  • Control Resonant, the sequel to Control, has been announced for next year - Story follows Dylan Faden, the brother of the first game's protagonist
  • The debut game from Yakuza creator Toshihiro Nagoshi’s new studio is Gang of Dragon
  • Two new Tomb Raider games, a remake (Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis) and a sequel (Tomb Raider: Catalyst), have been revealed. Remake is due 2026, Sequel is due 2027
  • Hogwarts Legacy is free on the Epic Games Store for the next week
  • No Law, the next game from the team behind The Ascent, is a UE5 cyberpunk FPS
  • The Game Awards 2025 Debuts Upcoming Shooter From Apex Legends Creators - Highguard launches Jan 26th

Other News

  • Veteran voice actor and voice of Ratchet & Clank's Captain Qwark (among many other beloved characters), Jim Ward, has died aged 66
  • Tencent sells Battalion maker Bulkhead to newly-formed consortium backed by Team 17 owner
  • Tencent exercising greater control in companies like Techland, Funcom and Sumo - “We 100% stay away from creative intervention. However, creative people do not always know how to do finance or production or hiring”

item.content:encoded:
• PlayStation Store has accidentally revealed that a Resident Evil veteran will be coming to Requiem
  • Netflix says Warner Bros' games arm is "relatively minor in the grand scheme of things"
  • Call of Duty team: We will no longer do back-to-back releases of Modern Warfare or Black Ops games

  • Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced has been listed on PEGI’s website

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/12/2025

item.content:encoded:
• The ‘future of Tomb Raider’ will be revealed at The Game Awards

Business

  • Tekken boss Katsuhiro Harada says he’s leaving Bandai Namco this month - Harada says Tekken's 30th Anniversary is the "most fitting moment" to leave

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/12/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 December 2025.
item.content:encoded:

  • Fallout co-creator Tim Cain's rejoined Obsidian full-time to work on a mystery game, but plans to keep his YouTube chats going

Business

  • PS5 reigns supreme over Black Friday, accounting for 62% of all console sales. Nintendo Switch 2 was 23% of all hardware sales - Data from NielsenIQ and GSD
  • iam8bit sues Skybound Games for $4m, claiming breach of contract and theft of designs - iam8bit also alleges Skybound used the former's trade secrets to cut it out of a Stray deal
  • Elden Ring Nightreign's The Forsaken Hollows DLC has shipped over 2 million units worldwide
  • Netflix has agreed to buy Warner Bros, including game developers behind Mortal Kombat, Hogwarts Legacy - If approved, Netflix will own Rocksteady, TT Games, Netherrealm Studios and more

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/12/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 December 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Cary-Hiroyuki Tagawa Dies: ‘Mortal Kombat, ‘Last Emperor’ & ‘Man In The High Castle’ Actor Was 75
  • Carmageddon Rogue Shift announced - General reaction has been "this is not Carmageddon"
  • PlayStation is publishing the first game by JJ Abrams’ Bad Robot Games, a co-op shooter directed by Left 4 Dead’s creator - The game is currently in development for PS5 and PC

Business

  • Goat Simulator creators reveal new studio Feeble Minds - The Coffee Stain co-founders are working on a "four player co-op soulslike roguelike" called Serpent’s Gaze
  • Wreckreation maker Three Fields Entertainment puts whole studio on redundancy notice - "Without the enthusiasm or financial support from our publisher to continue development, we simply cannot sustain the studio in its current form"
  • Sega says it will use AI in game development, but only in ‘appropriate use cases’ - SEGA is aware that AI "can face strong resistance in creative areas"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/12/2025

  • British retro gaming journalist Nick Thorpe has died unexpectedly aged 38
  • EA makes eight new accessibility patents free to all - Technologies include grapple assist, speech and audio generation tools, and improvements to its text and size contrast tool Fonttik
