item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/12/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 December 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Monkey Island creator Ron Gilbert cancels 2D pixel art Zelda-like RPG after struggling to secure funding - "The deals that publishers were offering were just horrible."
  • The Game Award's Player Choice Round 1 is live - vote now!

Business

  • Japanese game developers face ridiculously high font license fees following US acquisition of major domestic provider. Live-service games to take the biggest blow - The old LETS plan allowed developers to integrate high-quality Japanese fonts into their game builds for roughly 60,000 yen a year (around USD $380). Under the new Monotype structure (which no longer has local pricing for Japan), a plan that permits game integration is reported to cost $20,500 per year. Even more problematic for developers, the contract includes a 25,000-user cap for applications using provided fonts, which, as Game*Spark points out, is completely unrealistic for most commercial titles nowadays

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 01/12/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 1 December 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Metal Gear’s producer says he’s undecided on future remakes, MGS4 would be ‘real challenge’
  • MindsEye dev tries to resuscitate its flailing action game with a new "free starter pack"
  • Where Winds Meet players are tricking AI-powered NPCs into giving them rewards by using the 'Solid Snake method', a conversation style where you simply repeat the last few words from the other person as a question. An example would be: "I want a burger." "You want a burger?"
  • Fortnite Zero Hour Live Event Draws 10.5 Million Players, As Godzilla, King Kong, Iron Man and KPop Demon Hunters Unite For Avengers: Endgame-Style Finale

Business

  • Phil Spencer says Xbox's Game Pass cloud hours are up 45% YoY, making gaming "more accessible than ever" - "Console players are streaming 45% more on console and 24% more on other devices"
  • Resident Evil Survival Unit surpasses 2m downloads in 10 days
  • Where Winds Meet Surpasses 9 Million Players; Mobile Version Launches December 12

Rumors

  • Capcom is reportedly working on a new Dead Rising sequel starring Frank West

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Beyond Words is a roguelike word game by a new studio formed by David Doak and Steve Ellis (of GoldenEye and TimeSplitters fame) - demo now available on Steam, release planned for early 2026 for PS5, Xbox Series S/X, Switch and PC
  • Gil Canizes' Dotu is now available on Steam
  • A "lost chapter" of Kill Bill is premiering in Fortnite later this week, over 20 years after the original film - The Lost Chapter: Yuki's Revenge, will premiere in Fortnite on the 30th November at 2pm, ET / 11am PT / 7pm GMT / 8pm CET. It's an animated movie made in-engine, directed and written by Quentin Tarantino
  • Beleaguered free-to-play Halo x Portal FPS Splitgate 2 set to re-launch next month

Business

  • Paradox writes down $37 million of Vampire: The Masquerade Bloodlines 2 dev costs
  • Nintendo is acquiring Bandai Namco’s Singapore studio, will rename it Nintendo Studios Singapore
  • With just 17% of Sega's recent sales coming from new titles, the company suggests "definitive editions" could be putting players off Day One purchases

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• World of Warcraft: Midnight launches March 2nd, 2026
  • s&box is now open-source - built on-top of Source 2 which remains closed source / with Valve
  • A new Grasshopper Direct presentation takes place next week, featuring Romeo is a Dead Man - It's the third presentation from Suda51's Grasshopper Manufacture studio. 5pm BST / 8PM EST December 4th

Free Games / Subscription

  • December is getting five (more than the usual 3) PlayStation Plus Essential monthly games - Lego Horizon Adventures, The Outlast Trials, Killing Floor 3, Neon White and Synduality: Echo of Ada. Available December 2nd

Business

  • Cyberpunk 2077 has now sold 35 million copies, hitting the milestone faster than The Witcher 3 did
  • Coatsink to lay off half its staff due to "challenging co-development market" - up to 60 roles affected
  • Splash Damage places entire studio into consultation ahead of redundancies - All staff members affected, after UK studio stalwart was purchased by private equity in September

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Tekken boss Harada says he’s ‘quite interested’ in Capcom’s decision to charge for Street Fighter tournament streams - says the big money of fighting tournaments isn't reflected in game revenue
  • Roblox boss says problem of predators is an "opportunity" for the platform, in disastrous interview following latest child safety measures
  • Yet another Nier Automata crossover has been announced, this time in Final Fantasy 7 Ever Crisis

Free Games / Subscription

  • Nintendo Classics Update for Switch: Battletoads (NES), Ninja Gaiden II: The Dark Sword of Chaos (NES), Bionic Commando (GB) and Kid Icarus: Of Myths and Monsters (GB)

Business

  • Embracer Group divests Arc Games and Cryptic Studios, while several key rights are retained within the group

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 25/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• The Steam Awards 2025 nominations are active
  • Far Cry Getting TV Adaptation From Rob Mac and Alien Earth Creator Noah Hawley
  • Assassin's Creed Shadows Attack on Titan Crossover Is Now Available — But Free Quest Only Available For a Month

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Call of Duty, OD Actor, Udo Kier, has passed away
  • Sandfall Interactive and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 sweep Golden Joystick Awards 2025 - French developer won seven awards, including Studio of the Year and Game of the Year
  • Don't forget to vote in The Game Awards
  • Fan game TimeSplitters Rewind releases after a 13 year wait
  • FBC: Firebreak's Rogue Protocol update is delayed to January as it "still needs a bit more time"

Business

  • Ubisoft's Tencent deal goes through - The French publisher confirms a €1.16 billion investment in Vantage Studios for 26.32% stake
  • Ubisoft announces H1 earnings
    • Overall net bookings: €772.4 million (up 20.3% year-on-year)
    • Digital net bookings: €685.8 million (up 30.2% year-on-year)
    • Back-catalogue net bookings: €741.4 million (up 50% year-on-year)
    • IFRS operating income: -€120.2 million (vs -€271.8 million YoY)
    • Non-IFRS operating income: €27.1 million (vs -€252.1 million YoY)
    • IFRS operating net income: -€161.3 million (vs -€246.5 million YoY)
    • Non-IFRS operating net income: -€37.0 million (vs -€208.1 million YoY)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Pokémon TCG Pocket wins Google Play's Best Game of the Year award
  • Great Ormond Street Hospital is looking for a Gamer in Residence - Role aims to improve patient care, minimise trauma, and ensure that every young person has someone to game with
  • Silent Hill 2 Remake surprise-launches on Xbox Series S/X
  • Eminem will hire Agent 47 to kill Slim Shady in Hitman’s next Elusive Target mission - Releases December 1st
  • Vampire Crawlers, a first-person deck building Vampire Survivors spin-off, is coming next year - Vampire Crawlers is coming in 2026 to Xbox Series S/X, PlayStation 5, Nintendo Switch 2, and PC, and will be available day one with Xbox Game Pass. A sequel to the original game is also in development
  • New 007 First Light trailer confirms Bond’s car is an Aston Martin Valhalla
  • Dave the Diver is finally on Xbox, and it’s available now
  • BAFTA Games Concert confirms UK city dates and adds Fallout, Returnal, and Helldivers

Business

  • Ubisoft confirms €1.16 billion Tencent investment in new subsidiary is 'imminent'
    • Ubisoft said Q2 net bookings exceeded expectations after increasing by 39 percent year-on-year to €490.9 million—beating guidance of around €450 million.
    • Ubisoft also confirmed its cost reduction initiatives remain "on track" and has resulted in the exit of 1,500 employees over the past 12 months.
  • Unity signs 'multi-title development partnership' with 2K Games
    • Unity will be 'co-developing' the Nintendo Switch 2 port of PGA Tour 2K25
  • Resident Evil Survival Unit tops 1 million downloads in 24 hours

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Fortnite Chapter 6 Finale Event Trailer Teases Godzilla and Kill Bill Appearances as Chapter Seven Looms - Live event takes place November 29 at 11 a.m. PT / 2 p.m. ET
    • As part of the season, it seems that the "lost chapter" of Kill Bill titled "Yuki's Revenge" (which details the destruction of the Pussy Wagon) will be played out in some form in-game.
  • Epic and Unity announce ‘unique’ partnership - Unity Ddevelopers will be able to publish their games into Fortnite from next year

Free Games / Subscription

  • Games leaving PlayStation Plus soon
    • Battlefield 2042
    • Grand Theft Auto 3 The Definitive Edition
    • Arcade Paradise VR
    • Sonic Frontiers
    • Forspoken
    • Star Wars: Tales from Galaxy’s Edge Enhanced Edition
    • Firefighting Simulator: The Squad
    • Surviving Mars
    • Star Trek Bridge Crew

Business

  • Dispatch has sold two million copies
  • Just Cause Dev Avalanche Closes Liverpool Studio And Lays Off Workers - 31 developers are impacted
  • Dragon's Dogma 2 sells 4m copies

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Valve accidentally ruins a game's (Planet Centauri) sales again after apologizing for ruining its sales the first time
    • Originally, Valve screwed up sending out notifications to anyone who had wishlisted Planet Centauri when it came out of early access. As a consolation prize Valve offered the devs the opportunity to put their game on the front page in the “daily deals” section and gave them the option to select from some dates. The devs selected November 12th. This also happened to be the announcement day of the new Steam Machine, Steam Frame, and Steam Controller. The entire front page of the store got squished down to make way for the big announcement, essentially knocking Planet Centauri accidentally out of the way.
  • ‘I don’t think we qualify’: Megabonk dev withdraws nomination from The Game Awards. Developer claims Megabonk doesn't count as a "debut" indie game as “I’ve made games in the past under different studio names, so Megabonk is not my debut game”
  • Nier Director Yoko Taro assures players he’s been working on projects, but they keep getting cancelled

Free Games / Subscription

  • More Xbox Game Pass Games Coming
    • Arriving:
      • Moonlighter 2: The Endless Vault (Game Preview) (PC) – 19th November
      • Kulebra and the Souls of Limbo (Cloud, Console, and PC) – 19th November
      • Revenge of the Savage Planet (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – 19th November
      • Monsters are Coming! Rock & Road (Handheld & PC) – 20th November
      • The Crew Motorfest (Cloud, Console, and PC) – 20th November
      • Banishers: Ghosts of New Eden (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – 25th November
      • Kill It With Fire! 2 (Cloud, Console, and PC) – 25th November
      • Marvel Cosmic Invasion (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC) – 1st December
      • Lost Records: Bloom & Rage (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – 2nd December
    • Leaving (November 30th)
      • Barbie Project Friendship
      • Lords of the Fallen
      • Octopath Traveler
      • Octopath Traveler 2
      • SteamWorld Build

Business

  • Bungie settles Destiny 2 copyright lawsuit with writer in undisclosed settlement. If no further objections are made within 60 days, the court will presume "all parties have firmly agreed upon a compromise"
  • Farming Simulator 25 surpasses four million copies sold
  • Riot sells Hytale IP and Hypixel brand back to original co-founder

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Rebecca Heineman, creator of Bard’s Tale 3, Dragon Wars and the person who ported Doom to the 3DO, has passed
  • The Game Awards 2025 Nominees Announced - Game of the Year is going to be either Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, Death Stranding 2, Donkey Kong Bananza, Hades II, Hollow Knight: Silksong or Kingdom Come: Deliverance II
  • Xbox has announced a Partner Preview showcase for later this week - Thursday, November 20 at 10am PT / 1pm ET / 6m GMT
  • Helldivers studio CEO says people ‘jump to extreme takes’ when debating AI
  • The first official photos from The Legend of Zelda live-action movie have been published

Business

  • Paradox Interactive parts ways with Cities: Skylines studio after over 15 years
  • Improbable founder Herman Narula (who threatened to leave the UK over tax plans for wealth creators) has seen cumulative losses (almost £425 million) at his SoftBank-backed company

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Rebecca Heineman, creator of Bard’s Tale 3, Dragon Wars, has been diagnosed with adenocarcinoma and is in a fight for her life
  • Roblox rolls out next phase of IP licensing platform - Any eligible IP holder can offer content for use on Roblox after platform debuted with a select number of partners earlier this year
  • Over 200 Rockstar North staff sign letter demanding "immediate reinstatement" of fired colleagues - Independent Workers' Union of Great Britain says letter has been handed to senior management at developer

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Red Dead Redemption is officially coming to Switch 2, PS5, Xbox Series S/X and mobile next month. Netflix subscribers will also be able to download the mobile versions as part of their subscription
  • Fallout Season 2’s full trailer is out now, as Fallout 76 becomes free via Amazon Prime - Series returns December 17th
  • Kojima Productions has announced Death Stranding Isolations, an animated series coming to Disney+ in 2027 - The new series is seperate from the previously announced animated film Death Stranding Mosquito
  • Silent Hill F update adds ‘Casual’ difficulty and lets players skip part of a New Game+ playthrough

Free Games / Subscription

  • Five classic Blizzard games have been added to Xbox Retro Classics (powered by Antstream) - The Lost Vikings, Rock & Roll Racing, Blackthorne, Lost Vikings 2: Norse by Norsewest, RPM Racing

Business

  • Tomb Raider developer Crystal Dynamics announces yet more layoffs - Around 30 people affected
  • Ubisoft delays H1 FY25-26 results and temporarily halts trading of shares - Delayed results will be issued "in the coming days". Ubisoft's shares have plummeted 95.51% in five years, and are down 49.44% year-on-year
  • PUBG creator Brendan "PlayerUnknown" Greene is planning a new multiplayer FPS. The current plan for it is 100 player vs 100 player fights

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Valve announces a new Steam ‘console’, controller and VR headset - All releasing early 2026
  • The next Ratchet & Clank game is a free-to-play multiplayer shooter for mobile - Ratchet and Clank: Ranger Rumble
  • Sony and NCSoft Announce Horizon Steel Frontiers, a New Mobile-First MMORPG Set in Guerrilla's Horizon Universe
  • Infinity Nikki’s Infold believes there’s ‘untapped demand’ for non-violent big budget games
  • Nintendo reveals the first trailer for The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, with Brie Larson joining the cast. Bowser Jr will be played by Benny Safdie, the filmmaker and actor who wrote and directed The Smashing Machine and Uncut Gems
  • Valve will soon support Android games on Steam

Free Games / Subscription

  • November’s PS Plus Catalog games - Drop November 18th
    • GTAV, Thank Goodness You’re Here, Still Wakes the Deep, Pacific Drive, Insurgency: Sandstorm, The Talos Principle 2, Monster Jam Showdown, and MotoGP 25

Business

  • Sega is raising Japanese employees’ salaries again, three years after its previous increase - Approx 10% increase
  • 2K, Riot, Epic execs found marketing network Games Growth Guild - "Game developers have solved incredible technical challenges, but marketing and distribution remain the hardest problems"
  • Digiphile is a new charitable discovery platform from a group of Humble Bundle veterans
  • Nexon Q3 results
    • Revenue: ¥118.7 billion ($767 million, down 12% year-on-year)
    • Operating income: ¥37.5 billion ($242.3 million, down 27% year-on-year)
    • Net income: ¥38.2 billion ($247 million, up 41% year-on-year)
  • Sharkmob confirms "difficult but necessary" restructuring, unknown number of jobs at risk

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Elden Ring Nightreign DLC, The Forsaken Hollows, is coming next month - Releases December 4, and will include additional Nightfarers, Bosses, and more
  • An official Death Stranding 2 exoskeleton is being released in collaboration with Kojima Productions
  • PokéPark Kanto, the first permanent Pokémon theme park, will open in February - Opens February 5, 2026
  • ‘We’re here for the tough love’: Skate studio acknowledges that players have been having issues since launch
  • Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds is getting another three free characters; NiGHTS, Super Monkey Ball's AiAi and Tangle/Whisper from a Sonic comic
  • Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls is getting a second closed beta, with Ghost Rider and Spider-Man playable and this time, no interruptions - It'll be available starting the 5th of December, and will run interrupted for 72-hours
  • The Game Awards 2025 to stream on Amazon Prime

Business

  • Unity Third Quarter Financial Results
    • Revenue: $471 million (up 5% year-on-year)
    • Create Solutions revenue: $152 million (up 3% year-on-year)
    • Grow Solutions revenue: $318 million (up 6% year-on-year)
    • Net loss: $127 million (with a margin of 27%).
  • Sony Game & Network Services Financial Results
    • H1 (six months ended September 30, 2025)
      • Net sales: ¥2.1 trillion ($13.6 billion, up 5.2% year-on-year)
      • Operating income: ¥268.3 billion ($1.7 billion, up 31.3% year-on-year)
    • Q2 (three months ended September 30, 2025)
      • Net sales: ¥1.11 trillion ($7.19 billion, up 3.7% year-on-year)
      • Operating income: ¥120.4 billion ($780 billion, down 13.2% year-on-year)

Rumors

  • Valve expected to make an announcement today; believed to be Steam Frame / Steam Controller announcement

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo Direct focused on The Super Mario Galaxy Movie - Wednesday, November 12
  • PlayStation State of Play announced for today; focused on Japan and Asian games (will be broadcast in Japanese with subtitles) - 2pm PT/ 5pm ET / 10pm UK Time. 40 Minutes of information

Business

  • Ghost of Yotei sold 3.3 million copies in a month, Sony says
  • On its 5th birthday, PS5 sales remain virtually neck-and-neck with PS4 - PS5 clears 84 million units; two million behind PS4 at the same stage, going into its sixth holiday season

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Clair Obscur snubbed in Grammy video game soundtrack nominations - Games nominated in the video game category this year include Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora – Secrets of the Spires (Pinar Toprak), Helldivers 2 (Wilbert Roget), Indiana Jones And The Great Circle (Gordy Haab), Star Wars Outlaws: Wild Card & A Pirate’s Fortune (Cody Matthew Johnson & Wilbert Roget, II), and Sword of the Sea (Austin Wintory)
  • Arc Raiders breaks another concurrent record as player count soars; 400,000
  • FromSoft's parent company reveals we'll get Elden Ring Nightreign DLC by March, Nightreign "performing well beyond initial expectations"
  • Turns out Halo Infinite isn't infinite at all as Halo Studios ends active development after just four years

Business

  • Stalker 2 dev strikes down copycat game Misery with DMCA claim - Misery dev responds by posting a screed of pejorative slurs, prompting a follow-up apology
  • Red Dead Redemption 2 is now the fourth-biggest-selling game of all time, having sold 79 million copies since it debuted in 2018
  • Take-Two Interactive second quarter financial results
    • Net revenue: $1.77 billion (up 31% year-on-year)
    • Net loss: $133.9 million (compared to $365.5 million loss in the year-ago quarter)
    • Total net bookings: $1.96 billion (up 33% year-on-year)
  • Roblox Corp sued by State of Texas for allegedly platforming pedophiles

Rumors

  • Silent Hill 2 Remake release date leaks via Microsoft Store (21st November), all but confirming the Xbox version is coming soon

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2 version of Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds gets a release date and Switch upgrade path pricing - Thursday, December 4. $10 upgrade from Switch 1 version ($5 for the first week)
  • Yogscast Games announces two new roguelikes, Infinity Sweeper and The Gate Must Stand - Both games will be released in the first half of 2026
  • Ichiban Kasuga from Like a Dragon joins Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds for free, with a themed festival this weekend
  • Square Enix says it wants generative AI to be doing 70% of its QA and debugging by the end of 2027
  • The Game Awards' Future Class program remains on ice

Business

  • Marvel 1943: Rise of Hydra has received another delay ‘beyond early 2026’ - Game was previously set to release in 2025, and now no longer has a release window
  • Grand Theft Auto 6 has been delayed again to the end of 2026 - new release date is November 19th, 2026
  • Square Enix announces more layoffs as part of US and Europe restructuring - "nearly all areas" of Square Enix's western business affected by latest cuts, it tells staff. Wording highlights that it is focused on the publishing duties of the western teams. One person affected in Europe said that nearly 140 people were told they were “at risk” in the London office alone
  • Yet another NetEase studio has closed, this time former Watch Dogs devs’ Bad Brain

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• An official limited-time island based on Disneyland is coming to Fortnite today - The island, which is titled Disneyland Game Rush, launches at 8am PT / 11am ET / 4pm GMT. Players will be able to find it by either searching for its name or using its specific island code, which is 4617-4819-8826. The island will open with a central hub, from which players can visit seven mini-games, each based on a different Disneyland ride
  • Marvel Games says it’ll continue to work with Spider-Man and Wolverine studio Insomniac ‘for many years to come’
  • Nintendo says it plans a ‘consistent release’ of new films following the Super Mario Galaxy and Zelda movies - Nintendo says it is "building a framework" for future movies
  • Football Manager 26 launches on Steam with "mostly negative" reviews

Business

  • Rockstar claims dismissals were due to information leaks, not union busting - Says it "took action against a small number of individuals" for "distributing and discussing confidential information"
  • Krafton posts "record-high cumulative" performance in Q3 2025 - Revenue was up 21% year-on-year to 870.6 billion KRW ($604.2 million), with operating profit also up by 7.5% to 348.8 billion KRW ($241.9 million) across the same period. It took the company's year-to-date operating profit past KRW 1 trillion for the first time
  • Google and Epic strike a deal that could open up Android to third-party stores - the deal would apply globally and could cut Google's fees to as low at 9%
  • Fortnite's Simpsons crossover caused a spike in new players, and tempted back record numbers of returning gamers, too
  • Sumo Group-owned outsourcing studio Atomhawk closes Canadian branch

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• US patent office director orders re-examination of Nintendo’s patent on summoning characters to make them battle
  • NetEase’s closed studio, T-Minus Zero Entertainment, is back from the dead - name has been acquired by staff. "Reimagined" studio to relaunch as a production company

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass Games for November
    • Arriving
      • Dead Static Drive (Cloud, Console, PC) – November 5
      • Sniper Elite Resistance (Cloud, Console, PC) – November 5
      • Egging On (Cloud, PC, Xbox Series S/X) – November 6
      • Whiskerwood (PC) – November 6
      • Voidtrain (Cloud, PC, Xbox Series S/X) – November 7
      • Great God Grove (Cloud, PC, Xbox Series S/X) – November 11
      • Lara Croft and the Temple of Osiris (Cloud, Console, PC)
      • Pigeon Simulator (Cloud, PC, Xbox Series S/X) – November 11
      • Relic Hunters Legend (Cloud, Console, PC) – November 12
      • Winter Burrow (Cloud, Console, PC) – November 12
      • Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 (Cloud, Console, PC) – November 14
    • Leaving (November 15th)
      • Blacksmith Master
      • Football Manager 24
      • Football Manager 24 Console Edition
      • Frostpunk
      • Spirittea
      • STALKER 2: Heart of Chernobyl

Business

  • Sega Sammy's fiscal year ending March 2025
    • Net sales: ¥428.9 billion ($2.79 billion, down 8.5% year-on-year)
    • Operating income: ¥48.1 billion ($313 million, down 16.8% year-on-year)
    • Persona 5 series tops 13 million sales
  • Nintendo Financial Results
    • Net sales: ¥1.1 trillion ($7.2 billion, up 110% year-on-year)
    • Operating profit: ¥145.1 billion ($950 million, up 19.5% year-on-year)
    • Ordinary profit: ¥236 billion ($1.5 billion, up 60.5% year-on-year)
    • Hardware sales (incl. Switch and Switch 2): 12.25 million units
    • Software sales (incl. Switch and Switch 2): 82.18 million units

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 4 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Olympics ends Esports plans with Saudi Arabia after just one year; The IOC says it decided to end the announced partnership following a review
  • The original Saints Row’s design director says he’s been ‘asked to create a pitch’ for a potential prequel

Business

  • Nintendo Switch 2’s phenomenal launch continues as it clears 10.36 million units – more than PS4 and PS5
  • Another NetEase Games studio is shutting down; Fantastic Pixel Castle was founded in 2023 by a Warcraft veteran to create a fantasy MMO
  • Mario Kart World sold 9.57 Million copies in 4 months. Donkey Kong Bananza has sold 3.49 million units
  • Dispatch has sold 1 Million copies in 10 days

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/11/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 November 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• The Mortuary Assistant dev unveils its next horror project, and its a Paranormal Activity game; Paranormal Activity: Threshold
  • Arc Raider is "committed to skill and fun, not unfair advantages" and is refunding gear lost to cheaters - even if you didn't report them

Business

  • Nintendo wins lawsuit against player who kept streaming pirated Switch games and taunted ‘I can do this all day’ - Jesse Keighin told Nintendo's legal team: "You might run a corporation, I run the streets"
  • Roblox Q3 results
    • Revenue: $1.35 billion, up 48% year-on-year
    • Bookings: $1.92 billion, up 70% year-on-year
    • Net losses: $257 million, compared to $240 million during the same quarter last year
    • Average Daily Active Users (DAUs): 151.5 million, up 70% year-on-year
    • Hours engaged: 39.6 billion, up 91% year-on-year
    • Average monthly unique payers (MUPs): 35.8 million, up 88% year-on-year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 31/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 31 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Fox Hunt, the online multiplayer mode for Metal Gear Solid Delta: Snake Eater, is available now - the 12-player online multiplayer mode offers two different game types
  • Animal Crossing: New Horizons is getting a Nintendo Switch 2 Edition ($5 upgrade) and a huge free update that will add Lego furniture and playable NES and Game Boy games

Business

  • Nintendo has been denied a patent in Japan for a Pokémon-like capturing mechanic
  • Fall Guys veterans launch new studio Panic Stations to make multiplayer co-op games - Planning to ship multiple games per year, recruiting now
  • Heart Machine lays off more staff for the second time this month, two weeks before launch
  • Don't Nod H1 revenues rise to €7m, announces partnership with Netflix - Lost Records: Bloom & Rage "remained under expectations", leading to write-down of €13.1 million
  • Xbox Q1 hardware sales down 29% while third-party content experiences "better-than-expected performance"
    • For the three months ended September 30, 2025:
      • Revenue: $77.7 billion (up 18% year-on-year)
      • Net income: $27.7 billion (up 12% year-on-year)
      • More Personal Computing (incl. Xbox) revenue: $13.8 billion (up 4% year-on-year)

Rumors

  • Amazon’s Lord of the Rings MMO has reportedly also been cancelled

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Resident Evil Requiem is getting a Switch 2 Pro Controller, Amiibo, and coming to Fortnite - Players who purchase Reqiuem through the Epic Game Store will receive Fortnite content
  • Take-Two’s CEO doesn’t think a Grand Theft Auto built with AI would be very good - Strauss Zelnick believes AI's usefor games is limited because it lacks creativity
  • Nintendo is releasing a Zelda Breath of the Wild vinyl box set in the West to gauge the demand for physical soundtracks there - Soundtrack will be released in 2-LP and 8-LP editions. Collaboration with Laced Records
  • ‘We’re still figuring it out’: John Romero hasn’t given up on his axed Xbox shooter - hints that it is about 50% completed and has costed roughly $50 million to develop to this point

Free Games / Subscription

  • November’s PlayStation Plus Essential games confirmed - Stray, WRC 24 and Totally Accurate Battle Simulator

Business

  • Games London launches new accelerator program to help UK studios develop own IP - The two month training program will help studios develop IP
  • Capcom H1 2025 Results (six months ended September 30, 2025)
    • Net sales: ¥81.2 billion ($531.4 million), up 43.9% year-on-year
    • Operating profit: ¥81.1 billion ($530.7 million), up 89.8% year-on-year
    • Ordinary profit: ¥36.5 billion ($238.9 million), up 76.5% year-on-year
    • Net income: ¥27.5 billion ($179.9 million), up 80.1% year-on-year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Amazon ends new content updates for New World: Aeternum following massive layoffs, says 'servers will be live through 2026'
  • Apple has been appointed to The Game Awards’ Advisory Board
  • The original Splinter Cell and Hitman are among 16 new games added to GOG’s Preservation Program

Business

  • MultiVersus directors form new studio (Airlock Games) in response to ‘complexity of AAA games’ - their first title is a 16-bit space horror game
  • Remedy "not satisfied" with Q3 results - Developer records operating loss of €16.4 million, revenue down 32% to €12.2 million
  • Former Lionsgate Games creative director (Amanda Kruse) joins Blumhouse Games

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Capcom insists it will still charge to watch Street Fighter tournament streams, but is still preparing pricing details - It was previously announced that fans would have to pay up to $40 to watch two tournament finals
  • Forza Motorsport is taking a back seat as Xbox focuses on games releasing sooner, Phil Spencer says - "We understand the reaction many people had when we scaled back Turn 10 studios", the Xbox executive said
  • After 12 years of development, Fan-made Timesplitters Rewind finally has a release date; November 23rd

Business

  • Netflix shutters Squid Game: Unleashed developer Boss Fight Entertainment - The streaming giant recently explained it will be adopting a 'less is more' approach to its video game business
  • Escape From Duckov has reached 2 million units sold in less than 2 weeks

Rumors

  • Amazon plans to cut 30,000 corporate jobs across core departments (including video-games)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• GDC lowers ticket prices, streamlines pass types in Festival of Gaming 'reimagination'
  • Halo: Campaign Evolved is coming next year for Xbox Series, PC and PS5. Campaign only remake in Unreal Engine 5. Online feedback has been mixed, with many claiming Microsoft "hired that man" - a jab at the visual identity of the game based on amateur fan "remasters" on YouTube that don't focus on visual cohesion (dubbed the "Nintendo Hire That Man" trend)
  • Fallout 4 is getting an ‘Anniversary Edition’ and coming to Nintendo Switch 2 - Anniversary Edition bundles the 10-year-old RPG with its six official add-ons, in addition to over 150 pieces of ‘Creation Club’ game mods. Will be available digitally on Xbox One, Series S/X, PS4/5, PC and Switch 2 in 2026

Business

  • Roblox's Steal A Brainrot creator sues copycat Fortnite Creative game, Stealing Brainrots - Creator wants actual damages, punitive damages, attorney fees, and a permanent injunction. Steal The Brainrot, another Steal A Brainrot clone also available on Epic's platform, is officially licensed and therefore does not form part of this lawsuit
  • Pokemon TCG Pocket has eclipsed 150 million downloads
  • Pokémon Legends: Z-A sold 5.8 million copies in its first week, making it the fifth best Pokémon launch

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Fallout 4 is getting an ‘Anniversary Edition’ and coming to Nintendo Switch 2 - releases November 10 for Xbox One / Series S/X, PS4/5 and Nintendo Switch 2
  • Assassin’s Creed Shadows has been officially confirmed for Nintendo Switch 2 - releases December 2nd
  • After numerous delays, Sonic Rumble finally has a release date - PC / mobile November 5th

Business

  • PUBG and Subnautica publisher Krafton says it’s transforming into an ‘AI-first’ company
  • Ubisoft is asking staff at The Division studio Massive Entertainment to volunteer to be laid off
  • Pokémon Legends: Z-A sells 5.8m units during launch week

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• ‘Generative entertainment company’ Wolf Games has signed a partnership with NBCUniversal, which will see the release of generative AI games based on its film and television IP
  • 2 years after release, Nintendo is updating Pikmin 4 with new content, including a Photo Mode

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass Games for October
    • Arriving
      • Commandoes Origins (Cloud, console, and PC)
      • Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024 (Cloud, Xbox Series S/X, and PC)
      • PowerWash Simulator 2 (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – 23rd October
      • Bounty Star (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – 23rd October
      • Super Fantasy Kingdom (PC) – 24th October
      • Halls of Torment (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – 28th October
      • The Outer Worlds 2 (Cloud and Xbox Series S/X) – 29th October
      • 1000xResist (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – 4th November
      • Football Manager 26 (PC) – 4th November
      • Football Manager 26 Console (Cloud, Console) – 4th November
    • Leaving (October 31st)
      • Jusant (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Metal Slug Tactics (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Return to Monkey Island (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Business

  • Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds Surpasses 1 Million Copies Sold. Player stats include over 74.5 million races, 31.5 million Grand Prix and over 13 million ranked matches
  • Trials studio Ubisoft RedLynx is laying off up to 60 staff as it refocuses on ‘small screens’
  • Gamescom Asia reports record 206,149 visitors

Rumors

  • Xbox is more expensive for developers too, as Microsoft bumps up dev kit cost from $1500 to $2000 - a 30 percent increase in cost. Reason given is "macroeconomic developments"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 22/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• The Sims Mobile to be sunset in January 2026 - Game has been delisted on app stores, will no longer be updated
  • Nintendo’s second Kirby Air Riders Direct is this week, and will last an hour - Sakurai had already presented a 47 minute Kirby Air Riders Direct in August
  • Halo co-creator says he’s ‘disappointed’ he and some former colleagues weren’t credited for their work on Battlefield 6 - Ridgeline Games "built the foundation" before EA closed it last year, Marcus Lehto said

Business

  • Remedy CEO Tero Virtala steps down after nine years - Markus Mäki acts as interim CEO
  • 12 Welsh game studios secure $775,000 in funding to help develop "innovative games and immersive experiences" - Creative Wales' Games Development Fund aims to support devs to "achieve self-sustaining growth"
  • Escape From Duckov waddles into the extraction shooter space with over 200,000 concurrent players
  • Rovio is laying off 36 staff, says Angry Birds Dream Blast has ‘not been performing as expected’

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Next-gen Xbox confirmed in development, following rumours Microsoft would stop making consoles
  • Sonic Team boss wants more year-long celebrations of Sonic characters, following the Year of Shadow

Business

  • Mediatonic founder launches new group of studios under the banner Skeleton Crew - Skeleton Crew will consist of a slate of new companies, plus the existing teams at Payload, Brightrock and Mythwright
  • Ball X Pit sells 300,000 units in 5 days

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Battlefield 6 has become the biggest launch in series history, selling 7 million in three days - Over 172 million matches were played during the game's first weekend
  • Sleeping Dogs movie back on track as Marvel star Simu Liu reveals the draft script is "done"
  • Football Manager 26 will finally include official FIFA licensing for the first time, with a special International Management update for the World Cup

Business

  • ‘I did not walk away’: Former Assassin’s Creed boss says he didn’t resign, he was ‘asked to step aside’ by Ubisoft

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Quantic Dream’s first multiplayer game is Spellcasters Chronicles, a 3v3 strategy action game
  • McDonald’s Japan is selling Street Fighter themed burgers
  • Marvel Rivals adds PvE Marvel Zombies mode - Starts October 23rd

Business

  • 2K appoints Ben Brinkman as 31st Union studio head - Brinkman to start role on October 20
  • EA confirms Battlefield 6 is the best-selling entry in the series, with over 7m copies sold and 172m online matches played
  • Silent Hill 2 remake shipments and digital sales top 2.5 million - Silent Hill series surpasses 10 million units shipped

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Ninja Gaiden, Dead or Alive Legend Tomonobu Itagaki Has Passed Away
  • Sonic the Hedgehog has been added as a sidekick on GPS navigation app Waze
  • Capcom ‘took a lot of convincing’ to bring the original Resident Evil games to GOG, exec says - Capcom felt the remakes were the main way to play Resident Evil 1-3 in the modern era

Free Games / Subscription

  • October’s PlayStation Plus Game Catalogue
    • Premium / Extra
      • Silent Hill 2 (PS5)
      • Until Dawn (PS5)
      • V Rising (PS5)
      • Yakuza: Like a Dragon (PS4/5)
      • Poppy Playtime: Chapter 1 (PS4/5)
      • As Dusk Falls (PS4/5)
      • Wizard with a Gun (PS5)
    • Classics
      • Tekken 3 (PS1)

Business

  • Sound director Dan Forden, best known as Mortal Kombat’s ‘Toasty’ guy, has retired after 37 years

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 15/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 15 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Summer Game Fest confirms 2026 date and venue change - Next year's Summer Games Fest will take place in Hollywood's Dolby Theatre on Friday June 5th 2026
  • Dissidia Duellum Final Fantasy is a mobile 3v3 battle game which brings Final Fantasy characters into modern Tokyo - Set for release in 2026

Business

  • Banjo-Kazooie director Gregg Mayles confirms Rare exit after 36 years
  • Following the cancellation of its live service God of War, Sony’s Bluepoint is hiring for a third-person action game
  • Owlcat Games unveils free educational resource hub for game industry professionals
  • Assassin's Creed franchise lead Marc-Alexis Côté leaves Ubisoft following formation of Tencent subsidiary

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Arrowhead is delaying Helldivers 2 updates to fix its performance problems: 'We're focusing way more on addressing these issues to make sure this sort of thing doesn't happen again'
  • Lego Party and Digital Eclipse’s Tetris Time Warp will soon be available to stream on Netflix - They're two of five streamable party games coming to the service this holiday season

Business

  • NetEase divests Fantastic Pixel Castle as studio hunts for new publisher to avoid closure

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• New Dissidia Final Fantasy is coming to iOS and Android. More info due October 14th
  • A new ToeJam & Earl game is ‘in the planning stage’, co-creator reveals - movie is currently in search of a director
  • Genshin Impact's new UGC system is almost here – here's what to expect when Miliastra Wonderland launches later this month
  • Mortal Kombat 3 movie confirmed before the release of Mortal Kombat 2

Business

  • Halo art director leaves after 17 years with cryptic message suggesting it was on bad terms - "Stay strong, take evidence when necessary and find where you belong"
  • Remedy issues profit warning with FBC: Firebreak treading water - Remedy said the impairment has no effect on its cash position

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Mark Cerny triggers PS6 speculation by discussing AMD tech coming to ‘a future console in a few years’ time’
  • Nintendo says its ‘Close to You’ Pikmin videos were Nintendo Pictures short films, rather than a game or movie teaser
  • The Minecraft DLC pack is now available for Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds - First of six paid guest DLC packs planned for the game

Business

  • Kimberley Turner and Mark South appointed Double Eleven co-CEOs
  • Development ends on Hyper Light Breaker as studio Heart Machine says it’s making layoffs - The Hyper Light Drifter prequel has had mixed reviews whilst in Early Access

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• This year’s Fortnitemares event in Fortnite adds Jason (Friday 13th), Scooby-Doo, Ghostface, Huggy Wuggy from Poppy Playtime and Doja Cat
  • Chucky, Jason Voorhees and Predator are coming to Call of Duty: Black Ops 6 and Warzone

Free Games / Subscription

  • Three Nintendo NSO SNES games are added to NSO on October 10th; Bubsy, Mario & Wario and Fatal Fury Special
  • Xbox reveals October’s Game Pass games
    • Baldur’s Gate and Baldur’s Gate II: Enhanced Editions (PC) – October 9
    • The Casting of Frank Stone (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – October 14
    • Ball x Pit (Cloud, Console, and PC) – October 15
    • The Grinch: Christmas Adventures (Cloud, Console, and PC) – October 15
    • Eternal Strands (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – October 15
    • He Is Coming (Game Preview) (PC) – October 15
    • Ninja Gaiden 2 Black (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – October 15
    • Pax Dei (PC) – October 16
    • Keeper (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – October 17
    • Evil West (Cloud, Console, and PC) – October 21
    • Ninja Gaiden 4 (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – October 21

Business

  • Laid-off devs of cancelled ZeniMax MMO Project Blackbird form new studio 'with no outside investors and full creative control,' and they're calling it Sackbird
  • Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 tops 5 million sales worldwide in 5 months

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Silent Hill f has been added to GOG; one of the "earliest" third party AAA releases the platform has seen to date
  • PowerWash Simulator 2 launches later this month - 23rd October for PC, PS5, Switch 2, Xbox Series S/X
  • Elon Musk says his xAI company will release a ‘great AI-generated game’ by the end of 2026
  • Nintendo posts mysterious ‘Close to You’ animated short film - unclear if it relates to a film or game project or is a one-off showcase

Free Games / Subscription

  • Alan Wake 2 is available on PlayStation Plus

Business

  • Epic sues Fortnite fans who used over 20,000 bots to get paid tens of thousands of dollars - accused of falsifying the popularity of their islands, Epic Games says
  • Breakout hit Megabonk sells more than a million copies on Steam
  • Nintendo is seeking $4.5 million in damages from a Reddit moderator it says widely pirated Switch games

Rumors

  • PlayStation Plus is reportedly adding Silent Hill 2 Remake. If rumour is true, expected to be added October 21st

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• PlayStation cancels remaining ‘PlayStation: The Concert’ 2025 tour dates in the US

Business

  • Sega’s ‘Sonic the Hedgehog’ Sets Multiyear Licensing Deal With International Olympic Committee
  • Skate already has more than 15 million players less than 3 weeks after launching in early access - Season 1 drops today at 6pm UK time
  • Metal: Hellsinger studio closed as part of Funcom cuts
  • Indie slot machine roguelite CloverPit tops 500,000 sales in just 8 days

Rumors

  • Xbox's dedicated handheld was cancelled because AMD required 10 million units

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Developers rush to update games after serious Unity vulnerability discovered
  • ‘There are no easy solutions’: Helldivers 2 dev explains why PC version needs 3x more storage than consoles
  • Street Fighter 6 director says he was ‘shocked’ at Capcom’s decision to charge fans $40 to watch tournament final streams
  • Kingmakers delayed indefinitely as developer needs "more time before we feel good about charging money for it"

Business

  • As Xbox raises Game Pass prices, former FTC chair says it warned Activision deal would ‘harm gamers’
  • Nintendo denies lobbying over genAI, but stresses it will continue fighting IP infringement "whether AI is involved or not"
  • Winners of the UK Best Places To Work Awards 2025 revealed
  • Epic Games launches webshop service **on PC and mobile

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• House of Golf VR uses mixed reality to let players create mini-golf courses anywhere
  • Sega reveals its limited-edition trainer collaboration with Puma and they're far cooler than I expected
  • FIFA lives on in a mascot-focused five-a-side football game, coming next year - FIFA Heroes will be available in 2026

Free Games / Subscription

  • Amazon Prime Gaming’s 14 ‘free’ games
    • DragonStrike (GOG) - Available now
    • Tormented Souls (Amazon Games App) - Available now
    • XCOM 2 (GOG) - October 9
    • Vampire: The Masquerade – Reckoning of New York (Amazon Games App) - October 9
    • Empty Shell (GOG) - October 16
    • Fallout: New Vegas Ultimate Edition (GOG) - October 16
    • True Fear: Forsaken Souls Part 1 (GOG) - October 16
    • Hellslave (GOG) - October 23
    • True Fear: Forsaken Souls Part 2 (GOG) - October 23
    • Lost & Found Agency: Collector’s Edition (Legacy Games) - October 23
    • Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition (GOG) - October 23
    • You Will Die Here Tonight (GOG) - October 30
    • Werewolf: The Apocalypse – Heart of the Forest (Amazon Games App) - October 30
    • Halloween Stories: Horror Movie Collector’s Edition (Amazon Games App) - October 30

Business

  • Starbreeze has cancelled its Dungeons & Dragons game and is laying off 44 staff as it focuses on Payday - Project Baxter
  • GLAAD launches new program to mentor early-career LGBTQ+ developers
  • Lucid Games to open new office in Liverpool, UK
  • After layoffs, cancellations, and controversy, ZA/UM UK staff unionise as the Workers' Alliance
  • Mastered secures funding to support senior talent at UK games studios
  • Read the ESA's new guidance for storefront accessibility tags

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Xbox Game Pass is increasing in price by 50%
  • Rock Band 4 will be delisted this weekend, with its DLC to follow
  • Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 multiplayer and zombies changes detailed in depth by Treyarch
  • Namco’s first ever video game from 1978 is getting its first re-release this week - Gee Bee
  • Ghost of Yotei’s day one patch improves environmental detail and improves ray tracing
  • Moonlighter 2 delayed to November due to overstuffed October release schedule
  • Man pleads guilty to sending death threats to Epic Games
  • Amazon has ‘completely redesigned’ Luna with party games including an AI Snoop Dogg courtroom title
  • Everyone is going to be golden, as KPop Demon Hunters heads to Fortnite with a game mode, outfits, and more

Business

  • Ubisoft’s Tencent-backed subsidiary starts operation today, is named Vantage Studios
  • Former TT Games boss has launched a new studio to work on a Peter Pan game
  • Dune Awakening developer Funcom suffers layoffs after biggest release in studio history
  • BioWare's future under EA in question, studio veteran warns, if it makes "the kind of games that this new company isn't interested in making anymore"
  • EA says there will be no "immediate changes" to its workforce following leveraged buyout
  • Hasbro opens new Wizards of the Coast development hub in Montreal

Rumors

  • It looks like Prototype could be coming back after a Steam update added new credits - An update to the Steam version of Prototype has added new credits and references to Ubisoft Connect, which could point to a re-release of the game.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 01/10/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 1 October 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Battlefield 6’s free Season 1 roadmap revealed: new maps, modes and weapons incoming
  • A ‘deep dive’ on the future of Halo will be held next month
  • Social justice advocacy groups slam Roblox for "silencing important voices" with new parental controls
  • Capcom will not "guarantee" Monster Hunter games can run on Windows 10 after October 14
  • Terminator 2: No Fate delayed to November due to "ongoing trade and tariff changes"
  • Hades 2 reaches over 112,000 concurrent players on Steam

Business

  • Avalanche is closing its Liverpool studio following Contraband’s cancellation

Rumors

  • Sony is reportedly set to release an updated PS5 DualSense controller with a removable battery

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Epic pulls controversial Peacemaker dance emote from Fortnite following big twist in the TV show's latest episode
  • Borderlands 4 details first paid DLC plans as 2K continues to struggle with PC performance issues - Mad Ellie and the Vault of the Damned
  • Sony celebrates PlayStation anniversary with a new photography book that costs £95 (or £245 if you're fancy) - a photography book of never before seen prototypes and concepts.
  • SNK launches its new Premium Selection label with a PC port of Real Bout Fatal Fury 2
  • UK retailers are heavily discounting 1TB PS5 Slim consoles ahead of the 825GB version’s arrival
  • ’90s FPS Blood is being re-remastered by Nightdive Studios, coming to consoles for the first time

Business

  • EA confirms that it will be acquired for $55 billion by investors, including Saudi Arabia’s PIF
  • Nintendo Announces New Singapore-Based Establishment
  • Silent Hill F sold 1 million copies faster than the Silent Hill 2 remake, Konami says

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• The ROG Xbox Ally and Ally X price has finally been confirmed
  • U.S. Silent Hill f fans slam pitiful Steelbook allocations as missing pre-order gifts pop up on pricey eBay auctions
  • Pokémon TCG Pocket’s next set will include a special card in every pack

Business

  • EA reportedly in ‘advanced talks’ over $50bn buyout with investors, including Saudi’s PIF
  • The campaign against predatory in-game practices takes a step forward in Brazil, as President Lula bans loot boxes targeted at under-18s
  • GDC founder says the event is "rapidly becoming a dinosaur"

Rumors

  • Nintendo's own Zurich Pop Con display reveals five Lego games are getting Switch 2 ports

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo is opening a pop-up store in London next month
  • Forza Horizon 6 has officially been announced, and will be set in Japan
  • Final Fantasy 7 Remake director: players should accept Switch 2 Game-Key Cards because the issue is speed, not cost
  • Pokémon TCG Pocket's next (limited time) booster pack will let you collect rare cards you might have missed - Deluxe Pack: ex
  • After Genshin Impact and Honkai Star Rail, gacha master HoYoverse set its sights on the Animal Crossing-like cosy sim genre
  • Starbreeze admits it "dropped the ball" over stealth price increase to DLC bundle

Business

  • Doug Bowser to retire, Devon Pritchard to succeed as Nintendo of America president
  • Developers report lengthy payment delays on itch.io
  • Splash Damage breaks away from Tencent
  • Microsoft has 'ceased and disabled' Azure services to the Israeli Ministry of Defense
  • Unity forms AI Council to 'accelerate AI product Innovation'

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 25/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Here's everything announced in PlayStation's latest State of Play showcase
    • Housemarque's Returnal follow-up, Saros - March 20th, 2026
    • ZA/UM's Zero Parades: For Dead Spies - 2026

  • Mobile game Monster Hunter Outlanders gets a gameplay trailer as Closed Beta sign-ups begin
  • Nintendo’s first new mobile game in years is a surprise Fire Emblem spin-off - Fire Emblem Shadows is available for mobile today
  • Valve rolls out new streamlined Steam storefront with enhanced search tools
  • Nintendo Switch 2 wins special industry award at Japan Game Awards - Metaphor: ReFantazio, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, and the
  • PlayStation Store were among the other winners
  • Starbreeze Entertainment launches Payday 2 subscription service on Steam
  • Pragmata gets a new gameplay trailer, but still no release date

Free Games / Subscription

  • PS Plus games for October
    • Alan Wake 2
    • Cocoon
    • Goat Simulator 3
    • The Last of Us Part 2 Remastered
    • Tekken 3
    • Soul Calibur 3
    • Lara Croft Tomb Raider Anniversary

Business

  • Former AppLovin leaders establish casual mobile studio Cheer
  • Code Coven reveals first Aurora Accelerator and Agency cohort
  • Costco stops selling Xbox consoles in UK and US
  • Scottish Game Developers Association opens applications for Games Accelerator programme
  • Larian Studio's Tara Saunders confirmed to continue as BAFTA Games committee chair
  • Embracer CEO states that while AI is a "powerful" tool, "human authorship is final"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Metaphor: ReFantazio crowned best game at Japan Game Awards
  • Yakuza 0 Director’s Cut, Yakuza Kiwami and Yakuza Kiwami 2 are coming to PS5 and Xbox Series X/S
  • Yakuza Kiwami 3, a remake of Yakuza 3, has been officially announced
  • Yakuza Kiwami 3 will include Yakuza 3 Gaiden: Dark Ties, a brand new spin-off starring antagonist Mine
  • Days before release, Borderlands 4 is indefinitely delayed on Switch 2
  • Annapurna Interactive marks its Tokyo Game Show debut by announcing three new games - D-topia, People of Note and Demi and the Fractured Dream
  • 35 minute PlayStation State of Play confirmed, including Housemarque’s Saros - September 24 at 2pm PT / 5pm ET / 11pm CEST
  • PlayStation trophies will now unlock the ability to purchase real-life merchandise of some franchises
  • Pokémon Trading Card Game Pocket art gallery coming to Rome
  • "Thousands" banned in Fortnite as new Delulu proximity voice chat mode fuels hate speech

Business

  • Former ProbablyMonsters workers raise alarm about mismanagement at studio
  • Sonic musician's rights lawsuit against Sega dismissed, song remains in "limbo"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Palworld: Palfarm is a new farming spin-off coming from Pocketpair
  • Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 multiplayer reveal confirms 18 launch maps and more
  • Unyielder, a 1v1 boss rush FPS from Shueisha Games, comes to PC next week
  • Pokemon is holding a pop-up shop in London’s Natural History Museum next year
  • Fortnite launches new Daft Punk Experience
  • Borderlands 4 amasses over 2.5m players since launch

Business

  • Xbox has made "largest investment in Game Pass to date" this year, countering recent criticism of subscription service's value
  • Roblox appoints Vlad Loktev as chief creator ecosystem officer to "strengthen relationships with the creator community"
  • Game Developer Conference rebrands as GDC Festival of Gaming: "the industry is changing and so are we"
  • Void Interactive ends "relationship" with Ready or Not community manager over Charlie Kirk comments
  • Video Games Europe and the EGDF launch Pixels to Policy campaign
  • Playstack revenue increases 52% YoY in the first half of 2025
  • Midwest Games raises $2 million to support its 'publishing as a service' model

Kojima News Round-Up

  • Kojima Productions and Niantic Spatial are working on an AR walking game
  • New looks revealed for Death Stranding animated and live-action movies
  • Kojima reveals visual for PlayStation stealth game Physint, announces 3 cast members
  • ‘Bold, unique, and unmistakably Kojima’: Kojima Productions reveals new look at Xbox-published ‘OD’

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 22/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Lenovo has cancelled some pre-orders of its $1,100 Legion Go 2, saying it’s ‘substantially exceeded expectations
  • Microsoft is raising Xbox console prices in the US again, five months after its last price increase - The price of Xbox Series X and S consoles in the US increased by $100 and $80 respectively
  • Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds’ final DLC racer is another classic video game character - Megaman
  • Valve to end Steam support for 32-bit versions of Windows 10 OS

Business

  • Helldivers 2 ranks at No.4 on revenue charts for August 2025 following Xbox launch

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• EA Play members can start playing EA Sports FC 26 today, ahead of Early Access starting at midnight
  • Football Manager 26 adds the women's game for the first time, with official Barclays Women's Super League inclusion
  • Hollow Knight: Silksong devs admit it has "moments of steep difficulty" but also a "higher level of freedom" to avoid getting stonewalled - "We allow you to go way off the path."
  • Studio of former Disco Elysium staff rename forthcoming detective RPG and shift from familiar isometric perspective as rivalry continues - Now called Tangerine Antarctic.
  • Steam, Discord, Twitch, and Reddit CEOs called to appear in Congress after Charlie Kirk shooting

Free Games / Subscription

  • Two more Game Boy Advance games are coming to Switch Online’s Nintendo Classics library next week - September 25.
    • Klonoa: Empire of Dreams (Switch/Switch2)
    • Mr Driller 2 (Switch/Switch2)

Business

  • Infinity Festival to celebrate Innovators in the Age this October
  • Tencent accuses Sony of seeking a "monopoly on genre conventions" as it responds to Light of Motiram copyright lawsuit
  • BAFTA shares $375,000 in grants to "86 talented creatives" to launch their careers in the UK and U.S.
  • Epic Games to allow Fortnite developers to sell in-game items
  • UKIE launches Interactive Entertainment Scotland
  • IGDA board of directors appoints new chair, additional executive appointments

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• WWE 2K25’s Attitude Era DLC pack arrives today, along with an extra paid expansion adding Goldberg and Nikki Bella
  • Lasombra and Toreador Clans added to Vampire: The Masquerade – Bloodlines 2 base game following fan backlash to original premium DLC plans
  • Another Dragon Quest RPG has been announced, as Square Enix capitalises on renewed series success - Dragon Quest Smash/Grow is a roguelite RPG coming to mobile devices globally next year.
  • Netflix’s Splinter Cell animated series gets full trailer ahead of October premiere
  • New Elder Scrolls Online co-lead says studio 'really doesn't use' generative AI
  • Randy Pitchford calls Borderlands 4 "pretty damn optimal" amidst PC performance complaints

Free Games / Subscription

  • Games leaving PS Plus next month
    • Battlefield 1 (PS4)
    • Ghostbusters: Spirits Unleashed (PS4, PS5)
    • Tour de France 2023 (PS4, PS5)
    • The Last Clockwinder (PSVR2)

Business

  • Founders of Subnautica 2 studio Unknown Worlds accuse parent company Krafton of "changing story mid-litigation"
  • Nine Dots Studio's action-RPG Outward tops 3 million players

Rumors

  • Battlefield 6 Battle Royale Will Reportedly Release on October 28, 2025
  • Assassin’s Creed Black Flag remake is reportedly adding extra content and RPG mechanics, aiming for a March release - Remake not yet confirmed/revealed
  • Sega’s next Yakuza game reportedly leaked on Like A Dragon website

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Palworld will leave Early Access in 2026 with a ‘massive update’ planned for version 1.0
  • New PS5 system update finally makes using a DualSense controller on multiple devices far less hassle
  • Asset marketplace Fab is now accessible in the Epic Games Launcher
  • Godot 4.5 ushers in accessibility features, including screen reader support
  • Valheim coming to PlayStation in 2026
  • Hollow Knight: Silksong's second patch detailed, but don't expect any balance tweaks - Mostly *bug *fixes

Free Games / Subscription

  • XBox Game Pass's remaining games
    • New releases
      • Roadcraft (Xbox Series X/S, Cloud) – available today on Game Pass Ultimate / Standard
      • Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 3 (Console) – September 17, now on Standard
      • For the King 2 (Console) – September 17, now on Standard
      • Overthrown (Xbox Series X/S) – September 17, now on Standard
      • Deep Rock Galactic: Survivor (Xbox Series X/S, PC, Cloud) – September 17, Game Pass Ultimate / PC
      • Frostpunk 2 (Xbox Series X/S, Cloud) – September 18, Game Pass Ultimate
      • Wobbly Life (Xbox Series X/S, PC, Cloud) – September 18, Game Pass Ultimate / Standard / PC
      • Hades (Console, PC, Cloud) – September 19, Game Pass Ultimate / Standard / PC
      • Endless Legend 2 (PC) – September 22, Game Pass Ultimate / PC
      • Sworn (Xbox Series X/S, PC, Cloud) – September 23, Game Pass Ultimate / PC
      • Peppa Pig: World Adventures (Xbox Series X/S, PC, Cloud) – September 25, Game Pass Ultimate / Standard / PC
      • Visions of Mana (Xbox Series X/S, PC, Cloud) – September 25, Game Pass Ultimate / Standard / PC
      • Lara Croft and the Guardian of Light (Console, PC, Cloud) – September 30, Game Pass Ultimate / Standard / PC
      • Sopa: Tale of the Stolen Potato (Console, PC, Cloud) – October 7, Game Pass Ultimate / PC
    • Coming to Game Pass Core
      • Cities: Skyline: Remastered – October 1
      • Disney Dreamlight Valley – October 1
      • Warhammer 40,000: Darktide – October 1
    • Leaving on September 30
      • Ninja Gaiden Sigma (Console, PC, Cloud)
      • Ninja Gaiden Sigma 2 (Console, PC, Cloud)
      • Ninja Gaiden 3: Razor’s Edge (Console, PC, Cloud)
      • Terra Invicta (PC)

Business

  • Hollow Knight: Silksong is reportedly already about a third of the way to the original's total sales, with Steam leading the charge
  • Tanglewood Games calls for more support to develop game hubs in the North and across the UK
  • Trade body calls for stronger tax credits to support "disadvantaged" UK industry
  • Valve updates Steam to make it easier for devs to assess how discounts impact sales
  • Video game TV adaptations boost player numbers 140% on average, says new report
  • As PlayStation physical game sales drop, it's no wonder Sony is pushing digital-only consoles - Dropped 6% of sales back in 2020
  • Darrell Gallagher leaves Xbox following The Initiative’s closure

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo’s latest Amiibo figures push the boundaries of size and price - The just-revealed Kirby Air Riders Amiibo will cost $50
  • Gearbox Software addresses concerns of spyware in Borderlands 4 - Some fans raised concerns over Take-Two Interactive's terms of service
  • The Witcher Season 4 to premiere on October 30, 2025

Business

  • Valve no longer permits games with "mature themes" to release in early access
  • Vampire Survivors’ developer created publisher to "share the luck" with other indie studios- Luca Galante says Poncle wants to "give something back to the indie community" with its publishing arm, Poncle Presents

Rumors

  • Insomniac’s Wolverine is reportedly coming next year, followed by a Venom spin-off

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 15/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 15 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Yoshi and the Mysterious Book unveiled to celebrate Super Mario Bros 40th anniversary - Coming Spring 2026
  • After selling 1.4m copies on Steam, Stardew Valley-inspired Dinkum hits Nintendo Switch this November 6th
  • The Super Mario Galaxy games are coming to Switch consoles (apparently not emulated this time) in £60 double pack - Releases 2nd October
  • The Final Fantasy 7 Remake ‘trilogy’ will also release for Switch 2 and Xbox - Unclear if Part 3 will be a timed PlayStation exclusive
  • Nintendo Switch is getting a Virtual Boy accessory and Switch Online games - The physical Virtual Boy accessory (either the cardboard accessory or full Virtual Boy recreation accessory) will be required to play
  • The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is Nintendo's next big transmedia swing

Business

  • Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 quietly tops 4.4 million in sales

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Ubisoft confirms Far Cry will ‘more predominantly push multiplayer’ in future
  • Elden Ring Nightreign gets a free ‘high difficulty mode’ called Deep of Night
  • BAFTA Games in Concert announced, celebrating 20 years of award-winning music - UK and worldwide tour is confirmed for 2026
  • Don't forget there is a Nintendo Direct today at 2PM UK time

Business

  • French Lara Croft taking legal action against Aspyr for AI-generated voice use in Tomb Raider remasters
  • QA firm Testronic secures "significant" new investment as it restructures worldwide operations

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Acclaim marks its return by announcing 9 new indie games
  • Sony empowers parents with new PlayStation Family app
  • Nintendo Direct announced - September 12 at 9am ET / 2pm BST

Free Games / Subscription

  • September’s PlayStation Plus Game Catalogue update
    • Extra / Premium
      • WWE 2K25 (PS4/5)
      • Persona 5 Tactica (PS4/5)
      • Green Hell PS4/5)
      • Fate/Samurai Remnant (PS4/5)
      • Crow Country (PS4/5)
      • The Invincible (PS5)
      • Conscript (PS5)
    • Premium
      • Legacy of Kain: Defiance (PS2)

Business

  • UK games industry trade body UKIE has partnered with Tencent, the Department for Business and Trade, and delivery partner Player One Consulting, for the second year of its Video Games Growth Programme, designed "to help ambitious studios across the UK scale both creatively and commercially."
  • Ubisoft is cutting jobs within its publishing team, due to the formation of the new Tencent-backed subsidiary. 9 roles affected

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Beloved Japanese racehorse that inspired Umamusume: Pretty Derby character has passed away - Haru Urara was 29. Over her race career, Haru ran in 113 races, losing every single one of them
  • Codemasters’ old Dirt and Grid titles lose online services on March 16th, 2026 - Games to be affected are Dirt Showdown, Dirt 3, Grid Autosport and Grid 2

Business

  • Overwatch GM Walter Kong steps up as SVP head of development, live games/mobile - Despite the promotion, Kong says he will remain leading Overwatch's "overall strategy" but will be expanding that remit to encompass Hearthstone, Diablo Immortal, and Warcraft Rumble, too
  • Dead Island 2 reaches 20m players

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Former Bethesda exec Pete Hines claims subscription services are undercutting developers
  • Nintendo wins $2 Million settlement against Switch modchip seller who previously denied wrongdoing

Business

  • Ready or Not passes 13m copies sold across platforms - 3m on console
  • Roblox kicks off Roblox Developers Conference 2025 with a "suite of innovations," including an 8.5% boost to creator revenue - the company also unveiled its new generation of "fully functional 4D objects with AI"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• ‘Skate’ soundtrack confirms Royel Otis, Wallows and more than 90 other names
  • Epic finally revokes fraudulent V-Buck purchases on Xbox, but a "malfunctioning" refund system only causes more confusion - "It was our fault that we didn’t update the V-Bucks balance immediately," Fortnite developer admits
  • Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines 2 to refund all PS5 Premium Editions following backlash over paywalled clans
  • Despite layoffs, Build a Rocket Boy releases MindsEye Update 4 and promises it's "committed" to enhancing the gameplay experience
  • Indiana Jones and the Great Circle update adds new difficulty, improved hair and more

Business

  • Valve trademarks "Steam Frame". Announcement expected soon, expectation is VR hardware (potentially the "shipping name" for the Deckard prototype)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Roblox says all players who communicate in-game will need an age check by the end of the year - The platform is also launching new systems which limit communication between adult and child players
  • Second season of Netflix's Tomb Raider adaptation will also be its last - Arrives on Netflix December 11th
  • Marvel Rivals Season 4 Trailer Reveals PS4 Release Date as NetEase Teases New Heroes Daredevil and Angela - Season 4: Heart of the Dragon will launch on 12th September
  • Amazon's live-action Tomb Raider series has found its Lara Croft - Sophie Turner, known to many as Game of Thrones' Sansa Stark
  • Hollow Knight: Silksong launch breaks global game storefronts

Business

  • IO Interactive CEO says MindsEye disaster means it ‘remains to be seen’ whether it’ll publish other studios’ games again - "Well, that was definitely tough, right?" Hakan Abrak says
  • 2K confirms a "staff reduction" at Civilization and XCOM developer Firaxis - unclear at this time how many developers have been affected
  • Metal Gear Solid Delta: Snake Eater shipments and digital sales top one million - milestone reached on first day of release

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 4 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• 007: First Light to release on March 27, 2026 - PS5, Xbox Series S/X, Nintendo Switch 2 and PC

Free Games / Subscription

  • Forsaken 64 becomes the 40th Nintendo 64 game to be added to Switch Online

Business

  • Dreamhaven affected by unspecified number of layoffs
  • Aonic acquires research tech firm Prime Insights for $250m+

Rumors

  • PS5 Digital Edition consoles in Europe are reportedly getting a quiet storage downgrade - From 1TB to 825GB, console will cost the same price

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Call of Duty movie officially in the works at Paramount, TV show to possibly follow

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass titles for September include Hollow Knight: Silksong and RoadCraft
    • Arriving
      • I Am Your Beast (Cloud, PC and Xbox Series S/X) – Available today
      • Nine Sols (Xbox Series S/X) – September 3 on Game Pass Standard
      • Hollow Knight: Silksong (Cloud, PC and Console) – September 4
      • Cataclismo (PC) – September 4
      • Paw Patrol World (Cloud, PC and Console) – September 10
      • RoadCraft (Cloud and Xbox Series S/X) – September 16
    • Leaving (15th September)
      • Wargroove 2
      • We Love Katamari Reroll+ Royal Reverie
      • All You Need Is Help

Business

  • Saudi firm acquires co-ownership of Evo fighting game tournaments - The long-running fighting game tournaments are now co-owned by PIF-backed Qiddiya

Rumors

  • Take-Two was reportedly in talks to fund and publish Perfect Dark reboot - Deal allegedly fell through due to companies involved being "unable to come to terms over long-term ownership" of IP

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Kojima Productions to show ‘future project’s during Tokyo Game Show week this month - Celebrating 10 years with an anniversary event. ‘Beyond the Strand’ will take place on Tuesday, September 23 – two days before TGS 2025 – at Tokyo’s Roppongi Hills TOHO Cinema
  • A PlayStation State of Play focusing on 007 First Light is coming this week - September 3 at 11am PT / 7pm BST / 8pm CEST
  • The Cyberpunk 2077 team is seeking "brave netrunners" for something on 4th September, but won't say what it is
  • Left 4 Dead creator Mike Booth has announced he is working on a new four-player co-op shooter
  • Escape from Tarkov is coming to Steam

Business

  • Rare veteran Louise O'Connor lands new role at Xbox Game Studios (Chief of Staff) after Everwild cancellation
  • Daniel Drapeau, recently the game director on Fairgames at Haven Studios, has announced his new role as a creative director over at Warner Bros Montreal - The move adds further doubt to the status of Fairgames, the live-service shooter published by Sony, following the departure of studio founder Jade Raymond
  • Gears of War: Reloaded surpasses 1m players - Gears of War: Ultimate Edition multiplatform remaster launched on August 26, 2025
  • Nintendo rebrands transmedia division from Warpstar to Nintendo Stars - The firm said it was "strengthening" the "ancillary use" of films featuring Nintendo IP to encompass live events, merchandise, and more

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 01/09/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 1 September 2025.
item.content:encoded:
