• 8 weeks before release, Mortal Kombat 2 (the movie) has been delayed 7 months - Mortal Kombat 2 will now release on May 15, 2026. Rumored reason is "movies typically don’t perform well at the box office in October"
  • Yooka-Replaylee confirmed for October at $30, additional 30% discount for original owners
  • Steam now requires age verification in the UK for mature game pages
  • Capcom confirms Pragmata isn’t a secret Mega Man game
  • Stardew Valley will be getting another update - no release date given at this time
  • Honkai Nexus Anima revealed, a spin-off creature collector from Genshin Impact studio - Those interested can sign up for the test via the official website, with the recruitment period lasting from now until 12th September
  • F2P RPG Duet Night Abyss abandons gacha mechanics just weeks before launch in response to "consistent" player "dissatisfaction"

Business

  • Curveball Leisure launches full-service publishing division - Curveball Games aims to fund "unique" titles from indie to AA helmed by senior publishing manager Ed Gregory, formerly of PlayStation and PQube
  • CD Projekt H1 profit rises 4.3% to $121m
  • NetEase shuts down Rich Vogel-led T-Minus Zero Entertainment

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Unreal Engine 5 performance issues are mainly due to devs not optimising properly, Epic CEO Tim Sweeney says - Not enough studios optimise for less powerful hardware early in the development process, he claims
  • Metal Gear Solid Delta Snake Eater issues acknowledged by Konami, patch on the way

Business

  • 75.4% of Cyberpunk 2077’s Nintendo Switch 2 sales were physical
  • Thunderful Group shareholders approve $5.3 million Atari acquisition

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Side and Razer announce 'human-in-the-loop' AI playtests - The tool uses AI to analyze data from user playtests, and the information is then sent to QA analysts. The companies claim this will lead to 'more testers and more playtest hours' at the 'same or lower cost' than traditional playtests

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox is bringing Cloud Gaming to lower Game Pass tiers, in addition to PC games
  • PlayStation Plus’s September games include a 3D platformer and two indie hits - Psychonauts 2, Stardew Valley and Viewfinder

Business

  • Atari has acquired five Ubisoft games (Child of Eden, Grow Home, Cold Fear, I Am Alive, Grow Up) and will re-release and ‘evolve’ them
  • Crystal Dynamics lays off "a number of talented colleagues" - unclear how many have been affected at this time
  • Square Enix shutters two ten-year-old mobile games in Japan - Final Fantasy Brave Exvius and Dragon Quest of the Stars
  • Part of the team behind EA's cancelled Black Panther game joins Wizards of the Coast - Led by Monolith Productions veteran Michael de Plater, the group of 15 ex-Cliffhanger Games employees will be working on a new title

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Return to Silent Hill ("Silent Hill 2"-based) Movie gets First Teaser Trailer
  • Iconic game publisher Acclaim marks its return with a showcase next month - Showcase will take place on September 10 at 11.30am PS / 2.30pm ET / 7.30pm BST
  • Skate’s Early Access date has finally been confirmed - September 16 for PS4/5, Xbox One / Series S / X, PC

Business

  • PlayStation CEO doesn't want first party studios to "play it safe" but "fail early and cheaply"
  • New games industry body launches in India - Indian Game Publishers and Developers Association (IGPDA) was founded by nine companies, including Nazara Technologies and Tara Gaming
  • Developer Rec Room lays off 'about half' its staff
  • Evil Empire tells devs to avoid early access unless their project is 90 percent complete

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Dead or Alive 6 director is leaving Bandai Namco, after working on Tekken 8
  • The Division 2: Survivors has been announced, coming in 2026 - Survivors is described as a "Survival Extraction" experience
  • Gamescom Opening Night Live 2025 audience reaches over 72m viewers

Business

  • 357,000 visitors attended Gamescom 2025 - More than 34,000 trade visitors and 1,568 exhibitors participated in the event
  • Devcom 2025 attendance up 6% from 2024 - Conference featured 390 speakers, a rise of 30% from the previous year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 22/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Mortal Kombat: Legacy Kollection adds the lowest rated game in franchise history - Mortal Kombat Mythologies: Sub Zero and Mortal Kombat: Special Forces
  • Hollow Knight: Silksong release date announced - September 4, 2025 for PS4/PS5, Xbox One / Series S/X, Nintendo Switch / Switch 2

Business

  • Devcom to rebrand as Gamescom Dev
  • Pete Parsons leaves Bungie, Justin Truman steps in as CEO

Rumors

  • The setting for the next Forza Horizon game has seemingly leaked via a car company - If true, Forza Horizon's next entry would be based in Japan

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Xbox ROG Ally line coming in October with Handheld Compatibility Program - Both handhelds will be released on October 16. Details about pricing and pre-orders are coming soon, according to Microsoft.
  • Halloween Revealed by Friday the 13th: The Game Developer Illfonic - Includes Single Player Mode
  • Infinity Nikki Is Getting a Stardew Valley Collab in September (September 1st)
  • Final Fantasy 14's Yoshi-P Says There May Be 'Good News' in the Future About FF14 on Nintendo Switch 2

Business

  • Unknown Worlds sues former leadership team for breach of employment and "fiduciary duty of care" - Founders are also accused of downloading tens of thousands of company documents before their termination
  • Peak Sells 10 Million Copies

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Black Forest Games producer Dennis Schiefer has passed away
  • Second season of Fallout TV series to premiere on December 17, 2025 - New trailer showcases New Vegas and Deathclaw
  • Crunchyroll reveals first footage of Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice anime - Sekiro: No Defeat releases next year
  • Remedy launches new streaming series "looking back on the games we made" - Series kicks off with Control on August 27
  • Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 is getting a four-player co-op campaign and more - Multiplayer and zombies news is coming at Call of Duty Next on September 30th. Game releases November 14th
  • Helldivers 2 x Halo ODST Legendary warbond releases August 26th
  • Hollow Knight: Silksong announcent due Thursday 21st August (7:30 AM PT | 10:30 AM ET | 3:30 PM UK)
  • Vampire: The Masquerade Bloodlines 2 finally gets a release date, locks two of its clans behind $22 DLC
  • Wukong follow-up Black Myth: Zhong Kui revealed at Gamescom
  • Ghost of Yotei is getting Legends multiplayer mode next year
  • Monster Hunter Wilds and Final Fantasy 14 are officially crossing over - Both games will get content to celebrate
  • Monkey Island and Maniac Mansion veteran Ron Gilbert shows his next game, Death by Scrolling
  • Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War 4 has a new developer (King Art Games), promises ‘return to roots’
  • Indiana Jones and the Great Circle is coming to Nintendo Switch 2 next year
  • TT Games reveals Skywalker Saga-style Lego Batman game, Legacy of the Dark Knight - Features Matt Berry as Bane
  • Sonic and Pac-Man’s next games will have crossover DLC featuring each other
  • Atari is releasing a brand new Bubsy game; Bubsy 4D

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass August line-up
    • Arriving:
      • Blacksmith Master (Game Preview) (PC) – Available Today
      • Void/Breaker (PC) – Available Today
      • Goat Simulator Remastered (Xbox Series S/X) – August 20
      • Persona 4 Golden (Cloud, Console, and PC) – August 20
      • Herdling (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – August 21
      • Gears of War: Reloaded (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – August 26
      • Dragon Age: The Veilguard (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – August 28

Business

  • Starbreeze says PUBG collab was its 'single largest revenue contributor' in Q2
  • Cloud Chamber lays off "unspecified" number of staff as Rod Fergusson is parachuted in to lead troubled BioShock 4 production

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• A new Nintendo Direct focused on Kirby Air Riders is happening today - 8am PT / 11am ET / 2pm BST
  • Gamescom Opening Night Live today - 7pm BST
  • Shenmue 3 is getting an Enhanced edition, including Xbox and Nintendo versions for the first time - PS5, Xbox Series S/X, PC and "Nintendo"

Business

  • Puzzle & Dragons studio says a former executive embezzled $2.3 million of company funds - Gungho's already under-fire CEO will take a pay cut for three months
  • Peak sold 1.7m copies last week

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• EA's upcoming Skate will have 100 songs on its soundtrack for early access (full song list TBD)
  • Gamescom Opening Night Live will be broadcast on Tuesday, August 19 at 7pm UK (BST)
  • The Settlers 2 is coming to Amiga after nearly 30 years, and is officially licensed by Ubisoft

Business

  • Nexon Q2 Financial Results
    • Revenue: ¥118.9 billion ($804.7 million, down 3% year-over-year)
    • Operating income: ¥37.7 billion ($255.2 million, down 17% year-over-year)
    • Net income: ¥16.8 billion ($113.7 million, down 58% year-over-year)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 15/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 15 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Gamescom exec says it was never in competition with E3, has a responsibility to step up now it’s gone
  • Krafton claim former Subnautica 2 leads have "resorted to litigation to demand a payday they haven't earned"

Business

  • Nexon Q2 Results
    • Revenue: ¥118.9 billion ($804.7 million, down 3% year-over-year)
    • Operating income: ¥37.7 billion ($255.2 million, down 17% year-over-year)
    • Net income: ¥16.8 billion ($113.7 million, down 58% year-over-year)
  • Embracer will deploy 'targeted cost initiatives' and AI tech to unlock more value

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Football Manager 26 announces comeback with first tease of in-game Unity engine footage
  • Namco’s 1996 arcade racer Aqua Jet gets its first ever home release this week
  • PUBG to end support for last-gen consoles from November 13th

Free Games / Subscription

  • PlayStation Plus Game Catalogue for August
    • Extra and Premium:
      • Mortal Kombat 1 | PS5
      • Marvel’s Spider-Man | PS4/5
      • Sword of the Sea | PS5
      • Earth Defense Force 6 | PS4/5
      • Unicorn Overlord | PS4/5
      • Atelier Ryza 3 Alchemist of the End & the Secret Key | PS4/5
      • Indika | PS5
      • Harold Halibut | PS5
      • Coral Island | PS5
    • Premium
      • PS1 Resident Evil 2 | PS4/5
      • PS1 Resident Evil 3: Nemesis | PS4/5
  • Chibi-Robo is the next GameCube title coming to Switch 2’s Nintendo Classics library

Business

  • Minecraft console dev 4J opens new Edinburgh studio as work continues on its Minecraft rival Reforj
  • Undisputed developer Steel City Interactive opens second studio in Leamington Spa, UK
  • Starbreeze appoints former EA exec Jonas Skantz as Payday's general manager
  • Square Enix Financial Results
    • Net sales: ¥59.2 billion ($401 million), down 15.2% year-on-year
    • Profit: ¥4.8 billion ($32.5 million), down 54.8% year-on-year
    • Digital Entertainment net sales: ¥32.9 billion ($223 million), down 25% year-on-year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• New Helldivers 2 Xbox trailer teases Halo 3 ODST collab
  • This year’s Gamescom will have more than 1,500 exhibitors for the first time - The show floor will also take up a record high of 233,000 square metres
  • Senua’s Saga: Hellblade 2 Enhanced out now on PS5, with a free update for Xbox and PC
  • Epic says Fortnite and the Epic Games Store will come to iOS in Australia, following court ruling
  • Remedy was ‘unsatisfied’ with FBC: Firebreak’s launch sales but says it has ‘a solid game to build on’

Business

  • John Davison (senior vice president and publisher) departs from IGN Entertainment
  • Remedy Financial Results
    • Q2 Revenue: €16.9 million (up 63.5%)
    • Q2 Operating loss: €0.5 million (compared to operating loss of €3.2 million for same quarter last year)
    • H1 2025 Revenue: €30.3 million (up 43.4%)
    • H1 2025 Operating profit: €0.8 million (compared to operating loss of €5.3 million for same quarter last year)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Dragon Age trilogy remaster was pitched to EA, but "they basically seem to be against free money" says series veteran
  • Microsoft will "continue to support Forza Motorsport and Forza Horizon 5," despite Turn 10 cuts
  • Roblox bans and threatens to sue “predator hunter” YouTuber Schlep

Business

  • Bandai Namco Q1 Results
    • Net sales: ¥300 billion ($2 billion), up 7.1% YoY
    • Operating profit: ¥51.9 billion ($399 million), up 17.9% YoY
    • Ordinary profit: ¥54.6 billion ($369 million), up 11.4% YoY
    • The Mobile Suit Gundam IP has seen a massive 81.2% increase in sales YoY
  • Date Everything sells 500,000 copies
  • Sega Q1 Results
    • Net sales: ¥81 billion ($548 million, down 22.7%)
    • Operating loss: ¥519 million ($3.5 million, compared to operating income of $130 million during same period last year)
  • Mortal Kombat 1 Sells Over 6.2 Million Units

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Tencent quietly updates "slavish Horizon clone" Light of Motiram's Steam page; removed, edited, and/or replaced key art, just days after Sony filed a copyright lawsuit

Business

  • Diablo series head Rod Fergusson leaves Blizzard after five years
  • Bithell Games is making ‘the majority’ of its full-time staff redundant - 11 staff have been impacted

Rumors

  • A Far Cry TV show that "doesn't shy away from the darkest, most absurd parts of humanity" is reportedly in development at FX - Ubisoft may have prematurely posted about the project on its website

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Microsoft has stopped development on Avalanche Studios’ Contraband
  • Sony CFO says Marathon is expected to release before March, and Bungie’s independence is ‘getting lighter’ with the eventual aim to completely intergrate into PlayStation Studios
  • Digital Foundry Leaves IGN (who owns Eurogamer), Now Fully Independent
  • Hexen + Heretic by Nightdive Studios shadowdropped for PC (Steam / GoG), Switch, Xbox Game Pass and PlayStation

Business

  • Sony Vice President, Sadahiko Hayakawa: "We are moving away from a hardware centric business model to a platform business that expands the community and increases engagement."
  • Sony CFO says its live service shift is ‘not entirely going smoothly’ but pledges to carry on and learn from mistakes
  • Sony's PlayStation 5 has sold 80.3 million units as it closes on in on fifth anniversary
  • Konami stocks hit record highs thanks to eFootball and new Momotaro Dentetsu game. The Momotaro Dentetsu series is one of the company's strongest IPs, with sales totalling 18.9 million, almost double that of its horror franchise, Silent Hill, which has sold 11.7m copies since it launched in March 1999
  • Take Two Financial Numbers (for the three months ending June 30, 2025)
    • Net revenue: $1.5 billion (up 12% year-on-year)
    • Net loss: $12 million (compared to $262 million loss in the year-ago quarter)
    • Total net bookings: $1.42 billion (up 16% year-on-year)
  • Sony has released its financial results for Q1 2025 (for the three months ended June 30th, 2025)
    • Overall:
      • Sales income: ¥2.62 trillion ($17.8 billion), up 2% YoY
      • Operating income (after tariff impact): ¥340 billion ($2.3 billion), up 36% YoY (before tariff impact)
    • Games & Network Services
      • Sales income: ¥937 billion ($6.4 billion), up 8%
      • Operating income: ¥148 billion ($1 billion), up 127% YoY
  • Unity Financial Results
    • Revenue: $441 million (down 2% year-on-year)
    • Create Solutions revenue: $154 million (up 2% year-on-year)
    • Grow Solutions revenue: $287 million (down 4% year-on-year)
    • Net loss: $107 million (with a margin of 24%)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Obituary: Marvel's Spider-Man 2 accessibility engineer Patrick "Thor" Gass has passed away - Gass, a US Army veteran, previously worked at Raven Software as a UI engineer on the Call of Duty series
  • Video Game History Foundation expands its preservation efforts by making "one of the first American" games magazines available for free; Computer Entertainer

Business

  • RedOctane Games is a new studio dedicated to "advancing the rhythm game genre" - Founded by seasoned developers from the rhythm gaming world, who have worked on Guitar Hero and DJ Hero, alongside new talent, RedOctane Games is "focused solely on advancing the rhythm game genre" while "combining the past with the future."
  • Vintage Story developers claim itch.io has withheld payments for three months

Rumors

  • Microsoft Has Reportedly Sold Less Than 30 Million Consoles This Generation (Xbox Series S and X)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Fortnite Chapter 6 Season 4 trailer reveals Power Rangers and Halo collaboration - Chapter 6 Season 4 starts August 7th and is called Shock 'n Awesome
  • Nintendo will hold an Indie World live stream on Thursday, August 7 at 6am PT / 9pm ET / 2pm BST / 3pm CEST
  • Peak developers would rather you pirate its game than play Roblox "microtransaction-riddled ripoff slop" called Cliff
  • Genshin Impact PS4 is being discontinued - users are being urged to "move on" to Genshin Impact PS5

Free Games / Subscription

  • August’s PlayStation Plus Essential titles are now available; Lies of P, Day Z and My Hero One's Justice 2
  • Xbox Game Pass titles for August
    • Arriving:
      • Rain World (Cloud, Console, and PC) – available now
      • Citizen Sleeper 2: Starward Vector (Xbox Series S/X) – August 6
      • Lonely Mountains: Snow Riders (Xbox Series S/X) – August 6
      • MechWarrior 5: Clans (Xbox Series S/X) – August 6
      • Orcs Must Die! Deathtrap (Xbox Series S/X) – August 6
      • Assassin’s Creed Mirage (Cloud, Console and PC) – August 7
      • Aliens: Fireteam Elite (Cloud, Console, and PC) – August 12
      • 9 Kings (PC) – August 14
    • Leaving August 15th:
      • Anthem (Console and PC)
      • Farming Simulator 2 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Persona 3 Reload (Cloud, Console, and PC)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Evercade is getting a 12-game Rare compilation - The cartridge, which is priced at £19.99 / $29.99, will consist of 12 games mainly covering the studio’s early years, including some games released under its previous incarnation Ultimate: Play the Game. Rare Colllection 1 releases in November and is priced at £19.99 / $29.99
  • Tekken 8’s final Season 2 DLC fighter is Miary Zo, a brand new character from Madagascar
  • Mastercard insists it has not "required restrictions of any" game on Steam or itch.io, but Valve claims otherwise
  • One million people claim GOG's FreedomToBuy games in protest of ongoing NSFW game censorship row

Business

  • Call of Duty workers at Raven secure first union contract with Microsoft - Three years after Activision Blizzard first officially recognized the union, the Game Workers Alliance

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 4 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• GUILTY GEAR -STRIVE- Season Pass 4's Lucy (from Cyberpunk Edgerunners) overview trailer released - releases August 21st
  • New VIRTUA FIGHTER project video released showcasing combat
  • Armor King showcased for Tekken 8 Season 2 DLC - Launches October 13th for Season 2 Character Pass owners and October 16th for all users
  • MARVEL Tōkon: Fighting Souls PS5 Closed Beta announced - September 5th - 7th
  • Battlefield 6's UGC is powered by Godot
  • Gothic Classic (Gothic 1,2 and 3) announced for Xbox One / Series S / X, PS4/5

Business

  • BioShock 4’s leadership replaced as 2K insists it’s ‘committed to delivering a great game’ - director and studio head affected after the game reportedly failed an internal development review

Rumors

  • Bioshock 1 remake shelved

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 01/08/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 1 August 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Namco’s 2002 platformer Pac-Man World 2 is getting a remake - Pac-Man World 2 Re-Pac is coming to Switch/2, PS4/5, Xbox Series S/X and PC on September 26
  • A brand new Katamari game is coming to PC and consoles in October - Once Upon A Katamari is the first new non-mobile Katamari game in 14 years. Switch, PS5, Xbox Series S/X and PC on October 24 as a digital-only release
  • A new HD-2D RPG from Square Enix (The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales) has been announced for Switch 2
  • Square Enix announces Octopath Traveler 0 - set for release on Switch and Switch 2 on December 4
  • Monster Hunter Stories 3 announced by Capcom, is coming to Switch 2 next year
  • Bloober's The Medium will be adapted to film, courtesy of It and Annabelle writer-producer Gary Dauberman

Business

  • Nintendo Switch 2 has sold 5.82 million units in Q1 and 8.67 million games in total. Mario Kart World sold nearly as many copies as the console - 5.63 million units
  • Xbox content and services revenue up 13% year-on-year
    • For the three months ended June 30, 2025:
      • Revenue: $76.4 billion (up 18% year-on-year)
      • Net income: $27.2 billion (up 24% year-on-year)
      • More Personal Computing (incl. Xbox) revenue: $13.5 billion (up 9% year-on-year)
  • Capcom Q1 Results
    • For the three months ended June 30, 2025:
      • Net sales: ¥45.5 billion ($303 million), up 53.7% year-on-year
      • Operating profit: ¥24.6 billion ($164 million), up 90.8% year-on-year
      • Ordinary profit: ¥22.9 billion ($153 million), up 69.7% year-on-year
      • Net income: ¥17.2 billion ($115 million), up 72.8% year-on-year
  • Roblox Q1 Results
    • Three months ended June 2025 (Q1 2025):
      • Revenue: $1.08 billion (up 21% year-on-year)
      • Bookings: $1.44 billion (up 51% year-on-year)
      • Net losses: $278.4 million
    • Highlights:
      • Average Daily Active Users (“DAUs”) were 111.8 million, up 41% year-over-year.
      • Hours engaged were 27.4 billion, up 58% year-over-year
      • Average monthly unique payers were 23.4 million, up 42% year-over-year, and average bookings per monthly unique payer was $20.48
      • Average bookings per DAU (“ABPDAU”) was $12.86, up 7% year-over-year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 31/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 31 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• The next Nintendo Direct Partner Showcase happens today - 6am PT / 9am ET / 2pm BST
  • Pokémon Company removes two TCG Pocket card designs and ‘deeply apologises’ following claims of plagiarism

Business

  • Ready or Not tops 2m console sales in 2 weeks
  • UK game tech company JECO secures $1.3 million in pre-seed investment - Simon Barratt, JECO describes itself as an "industry first" B2B tech toolkit which allows developers "to increase performance whilst reducing production costs, timelines, and carbon consumption by adopting an automated suite of optimization tools for all software, on any device."

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Mario Kart World’s latest update makes CPU opponents easier - The version 1.2.0 patch also tells players when they've collected everything in Free Roam mode
  • EA CEO says it’s ‘all in as a company’ on Battlefield 6, is building a platform rather than a product

Free Games / Subscription

  • PlayStation Plus Essential monthly games for August 5th - Lies of P, Day Z and My Hero One's Justice 2

Business

  • Atari has agreed to acquire Thunderful for around €4.5 million - Atari will own around 82% of Thunderful shares, subject to shareholder approval next month
  • Warner Bros. is hiring (for what appears to be) another live service game
  • Guerrilla's Horizon franchise has sold over 38m copies worldwide
  • IGN launches Gaming Trends platform, shedding light on how mobile, Roblox, and more are reshaping the industry - The initial 2025 Gaming Trends report focuses on six key areas, including mobile gaming, user-generated content, and the trend for nostalgia
  • Krafton reports record revenue, buoyed by PUBG and Inzoi
    • Revenues rose to ₩1.54 trillion ($1.1 billion), an increase of 11.5% on the same period in 2024.
    • Profits were up 9.5% to ₩703 billion ($505 million).
    • Mobile accounted for the lion's share of revenue at ₩960 billion ($690 million), with PC revenues at ₩543 billion ($390 million), and console revenues at just ₩33 billion ($23 million)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Sony’s PlayStation 5 fight stick is called FlexStrike, is coming in 2026
  • Xbox just leaked Mortal Kombat: Legacy Kollection's release date - According to the now removed dates on the Xbox App, Mortal Kombat: Legacy Kollection releases September 29th / September 30th (depending on location in the world)

Free Games / Subscription

  • Mario Paint has been added to Switch Online’s SNES library, supports Switch 2 mouse controls - Switch owners can also play by plugging in a USB Mouse

Business

  • The publisher and developer of mobile hit Puzzle & Dragons (GungHo) says it’s considering a shareholder request for an extraordinary general meeting to oust company president and CEO Kazuki Morishita - Angry shareholders previously called out GungHo for increasing its president's pay whilst profits plummet
  • Sony sues Tencent for "slavish clone" of its "valuable" Horizon franchise - Sony claims Tencent asked to license the IP and when refused, pressed ahead with a "knock-off" version of its own
  • Free-to-play FIFA-licensed mobile game (FIFA Rivals) tops 1m downloads since its launch last month - 5m matches, 16m goals

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Wolfenstein is the next game series to get an adaptation for TV by Amazon MGM Studios
  • Team Fortress 2's summer update is here, including 10 new community-created maps, the summer 2025 Cosmetic Case - which contains 23 new community-contributed items - four new community-contributed taunts, and 14 new community-created Unusual effects
  • Weeks before release, Dying Light: The Beast has been delayed - The spin-off will now release on September 19, instead of August 22, the developer announced on Friday

Business

  • The Elder Scrolls 4: Oblivion Remastered has welcomed nine million players since its release
  • Krafton acquires Last Epoch studio, Eleventh Hour Games
  • Former DreadXP and Critical Reflex alum Ted Hentschke has teamed up with Jakub Bałuszek-Sobolewski to form Black Lantern Collective, a new publisher dedicated to horror games

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 25/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• The Final Fantasy Tactics remake has been rebuilt from scratch due to source code being overwritten - Square Enix has confirmed that the original code for Final Fantasy Tactics no longer exists
  • Pokémon Trading Card Pocket gets new Johto-themed packs alongside trading update
  • Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 6LP vinyl box set, 8 CD set, and a double LP announced by Laced Records
  • Like Steam before it, Itch.io has hidden its entire NSFW games library, as payment providers pressure stores
  • Lego Game Boy finally revealed after months of leaks - releases October 1, cost $59.99, and will include 421 pieces. Set includes two Lego Game Boy cartridges, one for The Legend of Zelda: Link’s Awakening, and one for Super Mario Land

Business

  • Elden Ring Nightreign has sold 5 million units, Shadow of the Erdtree 10 million
  • Hasbro's recent Magic the Gathering Final Fantasy crossover card set made $200m sales in one day
  • Sony has acquired 2.5% of Elden Ring publisher Bandai Namco in a ‘strategic partnership’
  • Nintendo Switch 2 set a US launch month record at 1.6 million units - 82% of Nintendo Switch 2 buyer during June also acquired Mario Kart World

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Bethesda veteran and 'father of the Elder Scrolls' Julian LeFay dies, aged 59 - Following news earlier in the week that he left the games industry due to cancer
  • Sony’s new Power Saver mode will ‘scale back performance’ of supported PlayStation 5 titles - Alongside the Power Save mode, the latest beta firmware will also allow players to pair a DualSense controller with up to 4 devices at once
  • Microsoft has reversed plans to charge $80 for The Outer Worlds 2 - the Obisidian sequel will now launch for $70 due to "current market conditions" according to Microsoft

Business

  • Scottish Silent Hill developer NoCode has changed its name to Screen Burn: ‘We have coders now’
  • Fortnite's return to iOS in the UK "uncertain", as Epic accuses UK regulator of deprioritising store competition
  • Sandsoft shutters two internal studios, impacting 65 jobs in Riyadh and Barcelona
  • Rematch reaches five million players one month after launch

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• EA confirms it will reveal Battlefield 6 this week - reveal trailer July 24th
  • Pokémon Champions: New details revealed for Switch and mobile battling game that is similar to Pokémon Stadium
  • Saber Interactive is working on a single-player Hellraiser game for PS5, Xbox, and PC
  • Surprise Switch / mobile game Pokémon Friends is out now - "Plush-filled puzzle game packed with charming challenges"
  • Splitgate 2 ‘unlaunches,’ studio to cut staff ahead of 2026 rerelease - Suffered from 96% player drop. This is the second round of layoffs since June with an unknown number of employees affected. Game is going back to Beta
  • Critical Role working on its first video game, which will be set in Exandria - Dispatch is currently set to release later in 2025

Business

  • Supermassive has delayed Directive 8020 amid a round of layoffs - around 36 employees affected
  • Ubisoft to restructure as "Creative Houses" as Q1 25-26 financials come in "lower-than-expected"
    • For the first quarter of 2025-26 financial year ending June 30, 2025
      • Revenue: €310.8 million ($365m, down 3.9% year-on-year)
      • Net bookings: €281.6 million ($331m, down 2.9%)
      • Digital net bookings: €250.2 million ($294m, down 2.7%)
      • Back-catalogue net bookings: €260.4 million ($306m, up 4.4%)
  • Side, a support studio that has lent services to games like the Silent Hill 2 remake, Kingdom Come Deliverance II, and Baldur's Gate 3, has laid off 'less than 10' staff

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 22/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Tomb Raider composer faces 16 months in jail over Covid loan fraud - Peter Connelly provided music for Tomb Raider: The Last Revelation, Chronicles, and The Angel of Darkness
  • Atari announces special console and Atari 50 DLC for Pac-Man’s anniversary - DLC includes Pac-Man 2600, Galaga, Dig Dug, Xevious, Galaxian, and more
  • The Pokémon Company will hold a Pokémon Presents presentation today - 6 a.m. PT / 9 a.m. ET / 2pm UK Time

Business

  • Still Wakes the Deep studio The Chinese Room regains independence, ‘working on 2 original IPs’ - Layoffs were confirmed as part of its split from Sumo Digital

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• 12 years later, Grand Theft Auto V has been cleared for release in Saudi Arabia - made possible in part due to the introduction of a 21+ age rating
  • Microsoft quietly removes ability to purchase movies and TV shows on Xbox and the Microsoft Store - previous purchased content still viewable for now
  • Bethesda veteran Julian LeFay, 'father of The Elder Scrolls', departs game development due to cancer
  • Smartphone racer Mario Kart Tour gets first new content in years to celebrate Switch 2's Mario Kart World

Business

  • Ready or Not hits 1m console sales in under four days

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• The Mortal Kombat 2 movie trailer is here, featuring Karl Urban’s Johnny Cage - Film due for release October 24th
  • Nintendo is doing another test via the "Nintendo Switch Online Playtest Program" - starts on July 21st and is run on a first-come-first-served system. Wording is unclear but it seems to be a playtest of the same experience from October last year
  • YouTuber raided by police for reviewing retro gaming handhelds - Once Were Nerd faces a maximum fine of around $17,000 and up to three years in prison. The issue stems from promoting (via review, not from endorsement) "retro" devices that contain thousands of game roms
  • Ubisoft and Netflix greenlight Assassin's Creed live-action series
  • FFXIV to stop support for Windows 10 on October 14, 2025. This is due to Microsoft ending security updates for the OS. The game will still boot and play on Windows 10, though it will no longer be an officially supported version
  • Saber Interactive Is Teasing Something With Spooky Vibes For Later This Month - Online consensus believes the game to be Hellraiser related

Free Games / Subscription

  • Civilization 6 Premium Edition is currently free to claim on the Epic Games Store

Business

  • Former German development studio Artex brought back to life as a publisher after 25 years - shift from developing games to supporting "young studios and start-ups"
  • Co-dev specialists Virtuos announces layoffs affecting 7% of workforce - Around 270 employees in Asia and Europe are affected
  • PlayStation Antitrust Settlement Rejected Over Lack of Clarity

Rumors

  • Marvel's Blade budget is 94m euros according to France's Committee of Finance

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• 7 months after Cyberpunk 2077’s ‘last’ update, yet more new content has been revealed - AutoDrive, self-driving Delamain cabs, Mac port and more
  • Sam Lake and Laura Bailey join cast of Surgent Studios’ Dead Take - Cast includes Matthew Mercer, Travis Willingham, CDawgVA, Jane Perry, Alanah Pearce, Ben Starr and Neil Newbon
  • Zelda and Link’s movie actors revealed for The Legend of Zelda live-action movie; 16-year-old British actor Benjamin Evan Ainsworth, best known as the voice of Pinocchio in Disney’s live-action remake, will take on the role of Link. Zelda will be played by 21-year-old English actor Bo Bragason, who is known for her roles in the BBC One series Three Girls and The Jetty, and the Disney+ series Renegade Nell
  • Pokémon TCG Pocket finally receives much-needed trading overhaul - update will take place on 29th July

Business

  • Ubisoft names Yves Guillemot’s son Charlie Guillemot as co-CEO of new Tencent-backed subsidiary
  • Steam introduces vague new rules banning 'certain kinds of adult content' to appease credit card and payment processor companies - Online feedback has been extremely negative with people pointing out banks should not be able to dictate what games a person should be allowed / not allowed to purchase and play
  • Metaphor: ReFantazio has sold 2m units worldwide, Atlus officially reveals
  • Sega, Netflix and others will let Roblox creators monetize their major franchises

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• John Wick Hex is being delisted (removed from sale) on July 17th
  • Tony Hawk’s Pro Skater 3 + 4 dev says it’s removing a cheeky Guitar Hero reference and ‘investigating’ how it happened - Easter egg was a Guitar Hero World Tour-style box was placed prominently in a bin. Neversoft (developers of classic THPS) were the developers of Guitar Hero World Tour. Fans have taken the easter egg as a mean spirited jab at Neversoft
  • Free-to-play MOBA Predecessor follows Fortnite with return to original Paragon map Agora
  • Cyberpunk 2077's delayed 2.3 update is being revealed today
  • Uma Musume: Pretty Derby players are sending massive amounts of ryegrass to a retired Japanese race horse. Haru Urara, depicted as a lighthearted pink-haired horse girl in Uma Musame: Pretty Derby, didn't win a single race IRL, which is reflected in-game with career goals focused on gaining fans rather than taking home trophies. The character in-game has quickly become a fan-favourite and fans have been showing their appreciation by sending the real horse ryegrass

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass titles for July
    • Arriving:
      • High On Life (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • RoboCop: Rogue City (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – July 17
      • My Friendly Neighborhood (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 17
      • Back to the Dawn (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 18
      • Abiotic Factor (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – July 22
      • Wheel World (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – July 23
      • Wuchang: Fallen Feathers (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – July 24
      • Grounded 2 (Game Preview) (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – July 29
      • Farming Simulator 25 (Cloud, Console, and PC) – August 1
    • Leaving:
      • Gigantic (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Kunitsu-Gami: Path of the Goddess (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Turnip Boy Robs a Bank (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Business

  • Epic Games confirms legal action against Fortnite cheat seller and DDoS cyber attacker:

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 15/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 15 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• FBI says it's now seized "multiple" ROM piracy sites, claims downloads resulted in $170m losses in just three months
  • Tony Hawk’s Pro Skater 3 + 4 locks SpongeBob and TMNT Easter Eggs behind $70 Deluxe Edition
  • Warhammer 40K: Dawn of War Definitive Edition and Space Marine 1 re-release coming this year

Business

  • Way of the Samurai studio Acquire Corp and Red Dunes Games announce collaboration on multiple new IPs
  • Xbox graphics department lead puts up hiring post on LinkedIn, uses AI graphics in post

Rumors

  • A Nintendo Direct is scheduled for later this month, after Donkey Kong Bananza releases

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Summer Games Done Quick 2025 raises £1.8m for Doctors Without Borders

Business

  • Lords of the Fallen surpasses 5.5m players

Rumors

  • Forza Horizon 5 Sold 2 Million Units in One Month on PS5
  • An untitled Donkey Kong film has been recently registered at the U.S Copyright Office by Universal Pictures and Nintendo

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• GameStop is auctioning off the staple and stapler that damaged a customer’s Nintendo Switch 2 - the damaged Switch 2, which Gamestop has repaired, is also part of the auction
  • Sea of Thieves is getting multi-ship private servers and paid custom servers "early" next year
  • Nintendo is discontinuing Switch Game Vouchers - Existing vouchers will continue to work after the termination date in early 2026

Business

  • SAG-AFTRA approves 2025 Video Game Agreement, officially concluding its strike
  • Krafton addresses leadership changes at Unknown Worlds, former executives file lawsuit against publisher - Firm claims "absence of core leadership resulted in repeated confusion in direction" resulting in delay of Subnautica 2

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Astro Bot’s award winning soundtrack is coming soon to vinyl, and it’s available for pre-order now
  • Stalker 2 is coming to PS5 later this year with PS5 Pro enhancements
  • Limited Run’s SNES release of Doom is available for pre-order now, alongside a new SNES controller - This version of the game includes “impossible graphics tricks”, according to Limited Run, as well as levels missing from the original game. Other new features include support for circle-strafing and the ability for monsters to respawn on Nightmare difficulty. Controller comes with rumble support and Limited Run is open-sourcing support for it
  • Dead by Daylight details its Walking Dead collaboration as public test build goes live
  • Live-service mech shooter Steel Hunters will shut down in October, announced just three months after it entered early access

Free Games / Subscription

  • PlayStation Plus games for July
    • Extra and Premium
    • Cyberpunk 2077
    • Abiotic Factor
    • Banishers: Ghosts of New Eden
    • Bluey: The Videogame
    • Planet Zoo
    • Risk of Rain 2
    • Tropico 6
    • Premium
    • Twisted Metal 3
    • Twisted Metal 4

Business

  • UK Video Games Council confirms inaugural members - The new organization will "play a vital role in representing the views of the sector" to UK government
    • The following members will join joint chairs Jason Kingsley CBE, CEO of Rebellion Games, and Nick Button-Brown, chair of Outright Games, on the inaugural council:
      • Jason Kingsley CBE, CEO and Creative Director, Rebellion (Co-Chair)
      • Nick Button-Brown, Chair, Outright Games (Co-Chair)
      • Emily Bailey, Green-BIT
      • Saad Choudri, Miniclip
      • Charu Desodt, Interior:Night
      • Kirsty Rigden, Futurlab
      • Dave Gould, T2
      • Chris Van Der Kuyl, 4J Studios
      • Donna Orlowski, Chucklefish
      • Nick Poole OBE, CEO, Ukie
      • Tara Saunders, Larian
      • Maria Sayans, ustwo Games
      • Tim Varney, Microsoft
      • Dr Richard Wilson OBE, CEO, TIGA

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• You can now play The Last of Us Part 2 in yet another way, as Naughty Dog releases free "Chronological" mode
  • A Ghost of Yotei State of Play featuring ‘nearly 20 minutes’ of gameplay is coming this week - July 10 at 2pm PT / 5pm ET / 10pm BST / 11pm CEST
  • Capcom cancels Monster Hunter lecture as ‘staff face death threats from players’ over the game’s performance issues - unfortunately the talk was titled "Monster Hunter: Wilds runs smoothly! Everything you need to know about optimization"
  • Animal Crossing Crocs collaboration announced - releases 26th August

Free Games / Subscription

  • XCOM dev’s highly rated Marvel’s Midnight Suns is ‘free’ to claim for a limited time for Prime Gaming users

Business

  • Epic Games settles antitrust lawsuit with Samsung - "We are grateful that Samsung will address Epic’s concerns," said Epic CEO

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Unity promises strong AI copyright 'guardrails' after employee conjures Mickey Mouse on stream
  • Stop Killing Games' proposals for game preservation "prohibitively expensive", major publishers insist - as EU petition surpasses 1m signatures
  • Valve blocks mod on Mount and Blade: Warband in South Korea following request from government - Mod was a "modern depiction of the Gwangju Democratisation Movement" that "distorted historical facts", says South Korean government

Business

  • Romero Games disputes claims it has "completely closed" - The studio says it is "doing everything in our power" to remain open
  • Square Enix has invested in a Danish indie studio BetaDwarf to fund its ‘dream game’ Vault Breakers - Publisher says it is 'always looking' for exciting indie titles to support

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Cyberpunk: Edgerunners is officially getting a second season
  • A Resident Evil mobile strategy game, Resident Evil Survival Unit, will be revealed July 10th - co-developed by Joycity (3on3 FreeStyle Rebound) and Aniplex (Demon Slayer -The Hinokami Chronicles 2) for Capcom. Kingdom Hearts’ co-creator Shinji Hashimoto is involved
  • Destiny: Rising, the free-to-play mobile version of Bungie's long-running sci-fi shooter series, has been given a release date; 28th August for iOS and Android - Collaboration between Bungie and NetEase, described as a "shared world" adventure set in an "alternate Destiny timeline, exploring a narrative in the post-Dark Age era"

Business

  • Double Eleven sister studio Cast Iron Games has opened its doors in Wakefield - focusing on co-development and porting
  • Stockholm-based Toca Boca lays off unknown number of employees

Rumors

  • Around 300 Build A Rocket Boy employees reportedly receive "at-risk of redundancy" emails following layoffs announcement

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 4 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• PlayStation hit Helldivers 2 is officially coming to Xbox with crossplay with all three platforms - releases August 26th
  • EA to sunset Anthem in January 2026 - Devs NOT affected by layoffs

Business

  • John Romero’s next FPS has lost funding in the wake of the Microsoft cuts - A Romero Games employee has claimed the “whole studio” is being let go, with many advertising on LinkedIn that they’ve lost their jobs
  • ZeniMax Online Studios head Matt Firor to depart after 18 years
  • G/O Media sells Kotaku to digital publishing company Keleops - G/O Media winding down operations following Keleops acquisition of Kotaku

Rumors

  • Dying Light developer Techland has reportedly cancelled two games - losses of 130 million Polish złoty (approximately 37.3 million USD)
  • 2K mobile studio Cat Daddy Games is making layoffs

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Neil Druckmann is stepping away from The Last of Us TV show to ‘transition complete focus’ on game development
  • Blizzard is giving up on its Warcraft mobile game (Warcraft Rumble) amid layoffs - will no longer be developing new content for the free-to-play mobile strategy game, and instead focus on "regular, systemic in-game events and bug fixes."
  • Nexus Mods is getting age verification for UK and EU users, weeks after changing owners
  • Rockstar releases surprise Red Dead Online update, despite previously ending major support - The update is known as Strange Tales of the West, includes four missions based on supernatural elements
  • Dave the Diver director says there’s ‘nothing indie’ about it, and he didn’t apply for its controversial Game Awards nod
  • Mark Cerny says PS5 Pro will get its major ‘replacement for the current PSSR’ upscaler next year - AMD's FSR 4 will replace PSSR

Business

  • ZeniMax Online Studios boss confirms departure amidst rumours of Blackbird project cancellation
  • Xbox closes The Initiative and cancels its Perfect Dark reboot
  • Three former Ubisoft execs receive suspended prison sentences at harassment trial
  • 9,100+ employees affected by layoffs, studio closures and project cancellations across Microsoft
    • Whilst an official list has not been released, it is believed that AT LEAST The Initiative (closed), Rare, Zenimax, King, Raven Software, High Moon, Sledgehammer Games, Halo Studios, Turn 10 (nearly 50% of staff), Xbox’s user research team (nearly 50% of staff) and Undead Labs are amongst the affected studios.
  • Rare's Everwild has been cancelled

Rumors

  • Banjo-Kazooie, Sea of Thieves director, Gregg Mayles, leaves Rare after 35 years, following Everwild cancellation
  • Return to Moria developer Free Range Games lays off 80 people - entire development team

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Sega’s Tokyo flagship store opens later this month in Japan
  • Nintendo confirms Donkey Kong Bananza’s developer is ‘the same team as Super Mario Odyssey’ - But Nintendo won't confirm DK's Director or any key personnel

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass titles for July
    • Arriving:
      • Little Nightmares II (Cloud, Console, and PC) - July 1
      • Rise of the Tomb Raider (Cloud, Console, and PC) - July 1
      • Legend of Mana (Console) – 2nd July
      • Trials of Mana (Console) – 2nd July
      • Ultimate Chicken Horse (Cloud, Console, and PC) - 3rd July
      • The Ascent (Cloud, Console, and PC) – 8th July
      • Minami Lane (Cloud, Console, and PC) – 9th July
      • Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 3 + 4 (Cloud, Console, and PC) – 11th July
      • High On Life (Cloud, Console, and PC) – 15th July
    • Leaving (July 15th):
      • Flock (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Mafia Definitive Edition (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Magical Delicacy (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Tchia (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • The Callisto Protocol (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • The Case of the Golden Idol (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Business

  • Krafton replaces the leadership of Unknown Worlds (Subnautica 2) with Striking Distance head Steve Papoutsis

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 01/07/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 1 July 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Halo Studios teases fans ‘won’t want to miss’ upcoming game event - Takes place in Seattle on October 24, ahead of the franchise’s 25th anniversary next year
  • Nintendo announces OG Switch price increases in Canada, amid US trade tension - New pricing information will be shared on the Nintendo Canada website on August 1
  • Hideo Kojima says he personally inserted all his "Hideo" cameos in Death Stranding 2 - Kojima has joked that his staff pretend not to know what he’s asking them to do when it comes to "Kojima" references, so he adds them himself

Business

  • Don't Nod confirms layoffs at its Montreal studio
  • FTC reopens refunds for players affected by unwanted purchases in Fortnite - Agency issued over $126 million in compensation last week, deadline for submitting a claim is July 9, 2025

Rumors

  • Assassin’s Creed Black Flag actor says fans ‘may have to beat the game again’ as remake rumors mount
  • Black Shamrock laid off 11 employees

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Bandai Namco announces Summer Showcase featuring new game reveal - July 2 at 8pm UK time
  • Sonic Adventure director says don’t expect remakes or remasters anytime soon
  • A Short Hike dev halts work on unfinished RPG, saying, "I might never finish this, so I'm releasing the demo!"
  • Fans slam The Alters after discovering evidence of undisclosed gen AI in images, text, and translation
  • National Videogame Museum to feature at World Expo 2025
  • A year after that PSN fiasco, Helldivers 2's long-anticipated review bomb cape is finally here
  • Battlefield’s latest leaked gameplay video shows 15 minutes of a new mode - Return of the Rush game mode
  • Dave the Diver delays In the Jungle DLC but sure knows how to soften the blow with these other announcements - In the Jungle DLC has been delayed to 2026, but the Godzilla DLC pack is free again

Free Games / Subscription

  • Nintendo Switch Online + Expansion Pass game
    • Super Mario Strikers - July 3

Business

  • Riot Games opens the door to betting sponsorships in professional League of Legends and Valorant
  • Obituary: Unity senior program manager Andy Brammall has passed away

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• First Resident Evil Requiem gameplay revealed, showing first and third-person views
  • Capcom reveals Pragmata’s unique puzzle-shooting gameplay in a new video
  • Amico releases some of its games on Switch and Steam, insists a console is still coming - The company sold its Intellivision branding to Atari last year
  • Monster Hunter Wilds' next update detailed as Steam reviews continue Overwhelmingly Negative plunge - Lagiacrus and Seregios added
  • Street Fighter Movie: Jason Momoa cast as Blanka, 50 Cent as Balrog

Business

  • Intel begins another round of layoffs, "roughly 107 employees" reportedly affected
  • Sony faces Dutch lawsuit over "artificially high PlayStation prices" - A consumer group in the Netherlands says that Dutch consumers pay too much for digital games on the PlayStation Store
  • Peak sells 2m units in nine days
  • Lost Records developer Don't Nod cuts a "non-insignificant chunk" of jobs - Roles impacted include QA, artists, and level designers
  • Epic Games successfully sues Fortnite cheat for $175,000
  • Game Dev Heroes co-founder Alex Boucher awarded MBE
  • FBC: Firebreak surpasses 1m players - Remedy's first self published co-op title launched on June 17, 2025
  • Square "collapsed" after Final Fantasy creator Hironobu Sakaguchi left, says composer Nobuo Uematsu
  • Steam's fourth most played game [Bongo Cat] doesn't really make any money, and its developer says it's better off that way
  • Sonic joins McLaren Racing in historic partnership with Sega
  • Chris Avellone joins former Quantic Dream writer at Republic Games to work on ambitious project inspired by "golden-age of RPGs"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• The latest Humble Bundle offers the Mafia and BioShock trilogies for $18
  • Everybody’s Golf Hot Shots gets release date, pre-ordering unlocks playable Pac-Man - September 5. Players will still be able to unlock him later through “normal progression”
  • WWE 2K25’s second DLC is out today, adding three NBA stars and two other giants - Shaquille O’Neal, Tyrese Haliburton, Jalen Brunson, The Great Khali, and Abyss
  • Xbox confirms Hellblade 2 Enhanced’s new features, release date and PS5 upgrade for the first game
  • PlayStation maintains that it still won’t release first-party games on PS Plus on day one
  • Netflix is delisting 20+ mobile games next month, including Hades, Braid and Monument Valley
  • Nintendo Museum in Japan is getting an art gallery extension
  • Two worlds collide at last as Palworld: Tides of Terraria launches today

Free Games / Subscription

  • PS+ games for July
    • Diablo 4 (PS5, PS4) - July 1
    • Jusant (PS5) - July 1
    • King of Fighters 15 (PS5, PS4) - July 1

Business

  • Little Nightmares franchise celebrates 20m unit sales
  • UKIE on what the government's Creative Industries Sector Plan means for the UK games industry

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 25/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Drag x Drive release date confirmed for Nintendo Switch 2 - August 14
  • Xbox’s surprise Meta Quest 3S VR headset is out now – but supply is ‘extremely limited’ - Available from today for $400 / £380
  • The UK leg of the Death Stranding world tour takes place next week - Tickets are on sale now
  • BitSummit, Japan’s leading indie game expo, reveals its full line-up for this year’s show - 120 indie games from Japan and around the world will be playable at the event
  • Little Nightmares 3 Release Date Announced - October 10, 2025
  • Jurassic World Evolution 3 is ditching its AI-generated art after "some initial feedback"
  • [UPDATE] Patch approved - Limited Run Games requests Nintendo temporarily pull Gex Trilogy from Switch 2 eShop following compatibility issues

Business

  • Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 back in Top 10 for revenue a month after release

Rumors

  • [Rumor] Xbox is reportedly planning another round of major layoffs next week - The job cuts are part of a company-wide reorganisation, sources say

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Minecraft-like Hytale cancelled after ten years in development
  • Horizon Zero Dawn developer not replacing actors with AI, says Aloy voice actress - This comes after footage leaked showing a Sony employee engaging with an on-screen AI version of the Horizon lead in March

Business

  • Peak sells 1m copies in six days
  • UK launches new $40.5m fund to support British game development
  • Dune: Awakening tops 1m players, becoming Funcom's "fastest-selling game ever"
  • MindsEye developer confirms layoffs

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Disco Elysium's reimagined mobile version is out soon - First two chapters are free
  • House of the Dead 2: Remake release date announced - Available on Steam, GOG and Nintendo Switch on 7th August
  • Metroid Prime 4 ad on London Underground erroneously claims the game is ‘out now’.

Business

  • Splitgate 2 developer 1047 Games lays off "small group of valued staff" - Co-founders say they will not draw a salary until "the next phase of the project"
  • The DLC for Lies of P, Lies of P: Overture, sells 3m copies
  • Rematch reaches 1 million players.
  • Still Wakes The Deep developer The Chinese Room has seemingly made a small number of layoffs
  • Sega accidentally revealed sales numbers
    • Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth – 1.66m
    • Like a Dragon: The Man Who Erased Their Name – 0.96m
    • Persona 3 Reload – 2.07m
    • Sonic Superstars – 2.43m
    • Sonic Frontiers – 4.57m
    • Total War: WARHAMMER III – 2.34m
    • Shin Megami Tensei V (Including Vengeance) – 2.11m
    • Yakuza: Like a Dragon – 2.86m
    • Persona 5 Royal (Including Remaster version) – 7.25m
    • Team Sonic Racing – 3.50m
    • Total War: THREE KINGDOMS – 3.21m

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Limited Run has asked Nintendo to pull Gex Trilogy from the eShop as it doesn’t work on Switch 2
  • Elden Ring Nightreign is getting remixed bosses in a free update
  • After a rocky start, Gears of War Reloaded’s beta is being extended
  • Resident Evil Requiem, Pragmata, and more to appear in Capcom Showcase next week - June 26 at 11PM BST

Business

  • Third-party Switch 2 game sales have started off slow, with one publisher selling ‘below our lowest estimates'
  • Playdead co-founder says the studio is suing him, accuses it of trying to erase his involvement in Limbo and Inside
  • Embracer has reduced its workforce by over 1800 people in the past year - Revealed in annual report.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Hades 2 gets fresh early access update as development finally approaches full release
  • Support for Beat Saber on PS4 and PS5 ends, shining a light on PSVR2's lack of success
  • Nintendo reveals new Donkey Kong Bananza gameplay info, including Pauline reveal
  • Death Stranding animated film confirmed: Original story in the works from Raised by Wolves writer
  • Christophe Gans' Silent Hill 2 movie adaptation gets a release date - 23rd January 2026
  • Skate reboot "just months away" from launch, as playtests open up to all who register before end of June

Free Games / Subscription

  • Sega games for iOS and Android made free before being delisted
    • Crazy Taxi Classic
    • Golden Axe Classics
    • Shining Force Classics
    • Sonic CD Classic
    • Sonic the Hedgehog 4 Episode II
    • Streets of Rage Classic
    • Streets of Rage 2 Classic
    • Super Monkey Ball: Sakura
    • Virtua Tennis Challenge

Business

  • Warner Bros. Games promotes three studio heads as it focuses on four key properties - Harry Potter, Mortal Kombat, DC Comics, and Game of Thrones
  • Developers at Ubisoft Halifax have filed to unionise
  • Aggro Crab and Landfall 'side hustle' Peak has sold 100,000 copies in 24 hours
  • A Roblox game about growing vegetables is seemingly now bigger than Fortnite - 16m concurrent users
    • Editor note: for context, PUBG holds the Steam record for most concurrent players... at 3.3m.

Rumors

  • PS5’s Stellar Blade is reportedly coming to Nintendo Switch 2
  • Tencent says report about acquisition interest in Nexon is untrue
  • Microsoft cutting ‘thousands more jobs’ after just laying off 6,000 people

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Arcade golf series Golden Tee is getting a retro compilation by Digital Eclipse - Features eight games
  • Lego Fortnite Expeditions is a mission-based PvE mode where you're up against an evil mask maker - Launches today
  • Bungie's Marathon reboot delayed indefinitely in response to "passionate" fan feedback
  • Microsoft says it's working on next-gen Xbox "consoles" you'll play "in your living room and in your hands" - As AMD partnership announced
  • After five tumultuous years, Ubisoft's Prince of Persia: Sands of Time remake is now "deep" in development - Currently due next year.
  • Dark And Darker Is In Trouble Again, As It's Removed From Another Platform - Will be removed from the libraries of Epic Game Store customers on November 1st

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass titles for June
    • FBC: Firebreak (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series X|S) - June 18
    • Crash Bandicoot 4: It’s About Time (Console and PC) - June 18
    • Lost in Random: The Eternal Die (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series X|S) - June 18
    • Star Trucker (Xbox Series X|S) – June 18
    • Wildfrost (Console) – June 18
    • Rematch (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series X|S) – June 19
    • Volcano Princess (Cloud, Console, and PC) – June 24
    • Against the Storm (Cloud and Console) – June 26
    • Warcraft I: Remastered (PC) – June 26
    • Warcraft II: Remastered (PC) – June 26
    • Warcraft III: Reforged (PC) – June 26
    • Call of Duty: WWII (Console and PC) – June 30
    • Little Nightmares II (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 1
    • Rise of the Tomb Raider (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 1
  • Amazon Prime Gaming
    • Dungeon of the Endless: Definitive Edition (Amazon Games App) - June 18
    • Saints Row 2 (GOG Code) - June 18
    • Saints Row IV: Re-Elected (GOG Code) - June 18
    • Star Wars: Rebellion (GOG Code) - June 18
    • TOEM (GOG Code) - June 18
    • Tomb Raider I-III Remastered (GOG Code) - June 18

Business

  • Studio Camelia shuts down a year after raising €300,000 on Kickstarter to fund JRPG - The studio's now-shelved title, Alzara Radiant Echoes, became one of the most-backed Kickstarter projects of 2024.
  • Turkish studio Bigger Games nets $25 million to expand flagship mobile title - he studio is hoping to support mobile puzzler Kitchen Masters
  • Fallout Shelter reaches over 230m downloads across all platforms
  • Nexus Mods founder to step away as ownership of the site "changes hands"

Rumors

  • Nexon reportedly considering acquisition bid from Tencent

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Borderlands 4 won’t cost $80 after all - Gearbox confirms $70 price point
  • A Nintendo Direct is coming this week, focusing on Donkey Kong Bananza - Wednesday, June 18th, 14:00 UK time

Business

  • NBA signs another "multiyear partnership" with 2K
  • Stellar Blade sells 3m copies

Rumors

  • Another Legacy of Kain game is seemingly coming to PS5 soon - Legacy of Kain: Defiance has been rated for PS5 and PS4

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Sony quietly removes PC sale restrictions in dozens of countries for four huge PlayStation games - Games returned to sale include God of War Ragnarök, The Last of Us Part 2 Remastered, Marvel's Spider-Man 2, and yes... Helldivers 2.
  • Nintendo fans are compiling a master list of what games run better - or worse - on Switch 2
  • PlayStation Studios boss confident Marathon won't repeat the mistakes of Concord
  • Nier: Automata creators deny characters "were problematic overseas" and blame mistranslated subtitle for censorship rumours

Business

  • 2025's Summer Game Fest is its biggest to date, with viewer numbers up 89% on last year's show - The livestream topped 50m views collectively
  • SAG-AFTRA proposed AI protections will let performers send their digital replicas on strike - A tentative agreement proposed by the union will also require game studios to secure informed consent from performers when using AI.
  • Sony says it will continue to adjust PS Plus pricing as more players choose higher tiers - CEO Hideaki Nishino says subscribers are “increasingly choosing” higher tiers despite price increases
  • Nintendo Switch 2 sets all-time launch record for hardware in the US, beating PS4 - Also biggest launch ever in Japan
  • Sony confirms PS5 has more monthly players than PS4 for the first time - Last year Sony said PS4 and PS5 users were a 50/50 split

Rumors

  • Pre-orders for the Xbox Rog Ally will reportedly begin in August, with launch expected two months later

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Konami confirms it’s working on a Silent Hill 1 remake with Bloober Team - The Silent Hill 2 remake developer is working on a second game
  • Metal Gear Solid 3 remake reveals Xbox-only Konami crossover - The Xbox Bomberman game will replace the original’s Ape Escape version
  • A final, unannounced game by the Metal Slug team is getting a Switch release 20 years after it was cancelled - Navinosuke: The Yo-Kai Buster was a GPS-based game in development on Game Boy Advance
  • Silent Hill f gets development deep dive at Konami game showcase
  • MindsEye Reveals June Roadmap to Address Bugs & Performance Issues
  • Ratchet & Clank: Rift Apart designer reveals hidden mini-game that’s gone undiscovered for four years
  • Metal Gear Solid Delta multiplayer mode revealed - An original online multiplayer mode, Fox Hunt.

Business

  • TIGA welcomes UK Spending Review's focus on creative industries, but emphasizes "importance" of UK Games Fund
  • Nice Plans Studios raises $3m in funding round - Investment will be utilised to support further development of 3v3 shooter Ricochet Squad
  • Cobra Mobile shuts down after 20 years
  • Microsoft shares best practices for devs looking to support ROG Xbox Ally handheld
  • RuneScape developer accused of ‘catering to American conservatism’ by rolling back Pride Month events
  • Nintendo Switch Online Services offline due to Google Outage - Service already back
  • The Outer Worlds 2 Game Director says they aren’t responsible for $80 Price - Brandon Adle points blame at Xbox for price Increase
  • Stellar Blade becomes the second PlayStation published game to pass 100,000 concurrent players on Steam - It surpassed Ghost of Tsushima, God of War and Spider-Man

Rumors

  • First-Party Xbox Handheld “essentially cancelled”, it’s claimed

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Dune Awakening hits 142,000 concurrent players during full launch, smashing past record

Free Games / Subscription

  • PlayStation Plus titles for June
    • FBC: Firebreak (PS5) - June 17
    • Battlefield 2042 (PS5, PS4) - June 17
    • Five Nights at Freddy’s: Help Wanted 2 (PS5) - June 17
    • The Hunter: Call of the Wild (PS4) - June 17
    • We Love Katamari Reroll + Royal Reverie (PS5, PS4) - June 17
    • Eiyuden Chronicles: Hundred Heroes (PS5, PS4) - June 17
    • Train Sim World 5 (PS5, PS4) - June 17
    • Endless Dungeon (PS5, PS4) - June 17
    • Deus Ex: The Conspiracy (PS5, PS4) [Classic] - June 17

Business

  • SAG-AFTRA formally calls off industrial action after almost a year of strikes - Details of the agreement will be released at a later time
  • Street Fighter 6 sells five million copies, on track to be best-selling Street Fighter ever
  • Days Gone studio Sony Bend announces layoffs following live-service cancellation - reported to be 30% of their staff, around 40 people

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Splatoon Raiders revealed for Nintendo Switch 2 - The first Splatoon spin-off revealed alongside Splatoon 3 update
  • Cult ‘rock vs EDM’ action adventure No Straight Roads is getting a sequel - Coming to PC and consoles next year
  • Apple announces new games platform launching later this year

Business

  • Nintendo Switch 2 sells 3.5 million in 4 days, likely the biggest console launch ever
  • Embracer to replace CEO who oversaw turbulent acquisition spree, and mass layoffs - Lars Wingefors will be replaced as CEO
  • SAG-AFTRA reaches tentative agreement with major games companies to end strike
  • Subway Surfers Blast developer Outplay Entertainment has laid off 21 workers
  • Roblox appoints Naveen Chopra as CFO
  • Multiplayer game and live streaming gaming platform Noice shuts down

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• The Zelda movie has already been delayed (to May 7, 2027) due to production issues
  • Konami is holding its own showcase later this week - called Konami Press Start and will take place on Thursday June 12 at 6am PT / 9am ET / 2pm BST
  • Game Maker's Sketchbook is selling art from Indiana Jones, Marvel Rivals and more for good causes - Prints purchased benefit the non-profits AIAS Foundation and Day of the Devs
  • Ubisoft trial witnesses testify to "being tied to a chair, forced to do handstands, and subjected to constant comments about sex" - HR allegedly told one woman "don't make a big thing of it"
  • Star Trek Beyond writer Doug Jung announced as showrunner for Amazon's Mass Effect series - Jung will also serve as executive producer alongside series writer Dan Casey

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds adds Minecraft, Persona, Hatsune Miku and Like a Dragon crossover characters
  • Resident Evil 9 is officially ‘Resident Evil Requiem’ - Releases February 27th on Xbox Series S/X, PC and PS5 - 9 in Japanese is "kyuu", which is how Q is read due to no Q in the Japanese alphabet (RE:Q is yet another pun for the series)
  • Xbox branded Asus Ally X announced
  • Fighting Game Invincible VS Announced With A Trailer Full Of Gory Superhero Battles - Coming to Xbox Series S/X, PlayStation 5, and PC in 2026
  • Puppet-fronted comedy fighter Felt That: Boxing announced at Summer Game Fest
  • Bloober's Cronos: The New Dawn gets release window - "Fall 2025" for Xbox Series S/X, PS5, and PC
  • Ninja Gaiden 4 (PlatinumGames + Team Ninja) gets October 21st release date for Xbox Series S/X, PC and PS5
  • A new co-op adventure Lego Voyagers lets players ‘experience what it feels like to be a Lego brick’ - For PS4/5, Xbox Series S/X, Switch and PC. No release date given
  • Monument Valley 3 is coming to PS4/5, Xbox Series S/X, Nintendo Switch and PC July 22nd
  • High On Life 2 has been announced for Xbox Series S/X, PC and PS5 with winter release
  • Super Meat Boy 3D is coming early 2026 for Xbox Series S/X, PS5 and PC
  • Pokémon developer Game Freak revealed a new IP (Beast of Reincarnation) at the Xbox showcase - expected to release 2026
  • InXile’s first-person steampunk RPG Clockwork Revolution gets a 5-minute gameplay trailer
  • Grounded 2 will release via Xbox Game Preview July 29th
  • Persona 4 remake (Persona 4 Revival) has officially been confirmed - coming soon to Xbox Series S/X, PS5 and PC
  • Final Fantasy 7 Remake Intergrade and Final Fantasy 16 Announced for Xbox - Final Fantasy 16 out now, Remake Intergrade releases "this winter"
  • The next Call of Duty game is Black Ops 7
  • Indiana Jones and the Great Circle’s first major DLC is set in Rome - The Order of the Giants releases September 4th
  • The Outer Worlds 2 is officially Xbox’s first $80 game - A Premium Edition, which includes early access and a DLC pass, will release for $100
  • Keeper, a new game from Double Fine, announced for Xbox and PC with an October 17th release date
  • Princess Peach’s voice actor of 18 years confirms she’s been replaced
  • Bandai Namco unveils Code Vein 2 at Summer Game Fest - 2026 release date for Xbox Series S/X, PS5 and PC
  • Jurassic World Evolution 3 is coming in October with ‘a comprehensive dinosaur breeding system’ - Releases October 21st for Xbox Series S/X and PC
  • Square Enix reveals Killer Inn, a 24-player online murder mystery game
  • 15 years later, the Scott Pilgrim game is getting a sequel (Scott Pilgrim EX), featuring a brand-new story from Bryan-Lee O'Mally - planned for release in 2026 for consoles and PC
  • Hitman’s latest elusive target is Le Chiffre from 007 Casino Royale, played by Mads Mikkelsen - Available in Hitman World of Assassination from June 6th - July 6th
  • Brass Lion Entertainment has announced Wu-Tang: Rise of the Deceiver, a co-op action RPG featuring music from the acclaimed hip-hop group
  • Like a Dragon studio’s latest game is called Stranger Than Heaven

Business

  • Dune Awakening neared 100k concurrent players on day one of its special edition launch
  • Gearbox publishes statement to confirm its games do not contain "spyware" as players slam new EULA

Rumors

  • Tencent reportedly acquired a 15.75% stake in Arrowhead Game Studios last year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Summer Games Fest returns tonight 5p ET / 2p PT / 10p BST / 11p CEST / 6a JST
  • CD Projekt isn’t done with Cyberpunk 2077, announces another patch incoming (after a previous announcement for the second time that no more patches were planned)

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass titles for June
    • Baldur’s Gate Enhanced Edition and Baldur’s Gate II: Enhanced Edition (Cloud and Console) – June 5
    • Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine – Master Crafted Edition (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – June 10
    • Barbie Project Friendship (Cloud, Console, and PC) – June 11
    • Kingdom: Two Crowns (PC) – June 11
    • EA Sports FC 25 (Cloud, Console, and PC) via EA Play – June 12
    • The Alters (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – June 13
    • FBC: Firebreak (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – June 17
    • Crash Bandicoot 4: It’s About Time (Console and PC) – June 17
    • Lost in Random: The Eternal Die (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – June 17

Business

  • Scottish mobile developer Outplay Entertainment cuts 15% of staff to "align operations with current business realities" - more than 20 roles affected

Rumors

  • Red Dead Redemption 2 reportedly getting next gen update alongside Switch 2 port

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Arc System Works is making a Marvel fighting game - Marvel Tokon Fighting Souls. Coming 2026
  • 007 First Light trailer shows off IO’s James Bond action game for the first time - Game focuses on a young James Bond
  • Astro Bot is getting more levels, and the Astro Bot DualSense is coming back - DLC brings the games planet count up to 90
  • Nioh 3 announced for 2026, with a demo available today
  • Mortal Kombat: Legacy Kollection is coming later this year - Includes Arcade, Console and Handheld versions of Mortal Kombat 1-4 (and MK Advance)
  • Final Fantasy Tactics remaster; Final Fantasy Tactics The Ivalice Chronicles, announced for September 30th release
  • New Pragmata trailer announces it’s coming 2026
  • Lumines Arise announced with "Fall 2025" release date
  • Fortnite Switch 2 improvements detailed, including mouse support and enhanced visuals
  • There will be no Ubisoft Forward this year, for the first time since it started
  • Street Fighter 6’s latest major update includes four playable NES games in the Gallery Section; Bionic Commando, Street Fighter 2010: The Final Fight, Mighty Final Fight and Mega Man 3
  • A Phasmophobia film is in the works at Blumhouse
  • Suda 51's next game is the ultra-violent Romeo is a Dead Man
  • Silent Hill f will be "more action-oriented" than Silent Hill 2 remake, coming September 25th

Free Games / Subscription

  • More PS Plus classic games announced
    • Myst – June
    • Riven – June
    • Deus Ex – June 17
    • Twisted Metal 3 & 4 – July 15
    • Resident Evil 2 & 3 – Summer

Business

  • Jagex sheds unspecified number of "non-game dev and non-player-facing" roles as it shuts down Project Zanaris
  • Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 developer Warhorse Studios is opening a new office in Brno

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 4 June 2025.
