item.content:encoded:
• PlayStation’s next State of Play will take place today - 40 minutes of "news and updates on must-play games coming to PS5" - 2pm PT / 5pm ET / 10pm BST
  • State of Unreal 2025: MetaHuman, UEFN, RealityScan 2.0, and Unreal Engine 5.6 "major performance upgrades to empower teams to build large-scale open worlds that run smoothly on current-gen hardware" - Witcher 4 content shown at 60fps with raytracing on base PS5

Business

  • MindsEye studio execs depart Build a Rocket Boy, one week before game's debut - Chief Legal Officer and Chief Financial Officer have left
  • Zynga shuts down Torchlight 3 developer (Echtra Games) four years after its acquisition
  • Scopely officially closes $3.5 billion acquisition of Niantic's games business - Deal closed May 29th
  • Elden Ring: Nightreign passes 3.5m players

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Epic Games' State of Unreal livestream today at 9:30am ET / 6:30am PT / 2:30pm BST
    • Expected news about animation features in Unreal Engine, CD Projekt's collaboartions with Epic (Engine features, The Witcher IV) and Fortnite
  • IO Interactive announces its first showcase featuring Hitman, 007 First Light and MindsEye - IOI Showcase streams June 6th
  • Former The Sims producer Tim LeTourneau has died

Business

  • 6 Welsh game developers secure $1.1m Welsh Government funding to develop their pilots
  • Tango Gameworks unveils new branding and confirms it's working on a new action game
  • Wizards of the Coast announces "exclusive publishing agreement" for Dungeons & Dragons games with Giant Skull

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/06/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 June 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Dynasty Warriors producer says the series still can’t be called a success in the West
    • Latest game in the series, Dynasty Warriors: Origins, has sold more than 1 million copies worldwide

Business

  • People Can Fly suspends two projects, announces layoffs
    • Project Gemini (Square Enix) and Bifrost (VR) cancelled
    • Unknown number of employees affected
  • ZeniMax Workers United reaches tentative agreement with Microsoft
    • QA developers to receive 13.5% wage increase, new minimum salaries and protections against arbitrary dismissal and grievance procedures
  • MultiVersus officially closed and delisted
  • Playtonic confirms layoffs: ‘The landscape is shifting, and with it, so must we’
    • Unknown number of employees affected.
  • Elden Ring Nightreign sells 2 million copies on day one, as a patch for easier solo play is announced

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Summer Game Fest 2025 announces 60+ partners - Airs live on Friday June 6th at 5p ET / 2p PT / 9p GMT

Free Games / Subscription

  • Free on Epic Game Store (June 5 at 11am ET / 4pm BST)
    • Limbo
    • Tiny Tina's Wonderlands

Business

  • Atomic Heart surpasses 10m players

Rumors

  • Xbox's first-party handheld has been sidelined (for now), as Microsoft doubles down on 'Kennan,' (third party device) and Windows 11 PC gaming handheld optimization

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Cyberpunk 2 (formally "Project Orion") is now in preproduction, CD Projekt says
  • A Death Stranding anime is in the works, Hideo Kojima confirms

Business

  • EA has cancelled its Black Panther game and closed its studio Cliffhanger Games
  • Amplitude Studios raises over $13 million in Series A funding
  • Cyberpunk 2077: Phantom Liberty Has Sold 10 Million Copies
  • The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt has sold 60 million copies

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo Switch 2 is already in some users’ hands, but a mandatory update means they can’t be played

Free Games / Subscription

  • PlayStation Plus Essential monthly games for June
    • Essential (Monthly Games)
      • NBA 2K25
      • Alone in the Dark
      • Bomb Rush Cyberfunk
      • Destiny 2: The Final Shape
    • Extra / Premium
      • Another Crab’s Treasure
      • Skull and Bones
      • Destiny 2: Legacy Collection
      • Grand Theft Auto III: The Definitive Edition

      • Riven

Business

  • Apple makes first ever game developer acquisition, but insists it’s not the start of a trend (RAC7, the two-person developer behind Apple Arcade’s 2019 hit Sneaky Sasquatch)
  • Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 hits 3.3m sales 33 days after release
  • Pokémon software nears 500m units sold
  • Niantic spin-off Niantic Spatial continues layoffs with 'small number' of additional cuts - another 5 employees affected

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• WRC rights return to Nacon, after EA’s Codemasters ends rally game development - Nacon planning "Complete Reboot"
  • Ex Machina director Alex Garland to helm Elden Ring movie for A24
  • Balatro's PlayStation Platinum trophy has only been obtained by 0.1% of players, and Mark Cerny is one of them
  • NetherRealm ends Mortal Kombat 1 support as focus shifts to "the next project" - no additional DLC characters or story chapters planned

Business

  • Capcom strengthens game development muscle with new Osaka base
  • Fantasy Life I: The Girl Who Steals Time hits 500,000 sales in three days

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Metal Gear Solid Δ: Delta Intro Video released

Free Games / Subscription

  • Sifu, Deliver At All Costs and Gigapocalypse are the next free Epic Game Store games

Business

  • Years later, The FTC has finally dropped its fight against Microsoft’s Activision Blizzard deal
  • Embracer intends to spin off Coffee Stain Group by the end of 2025, with remaining business rebranded as Fellowship Entertainment
  • Embracer Q4 / FY Results
    • Q4 (3 months ended March 31, 2025)
      • Net sales: SEK 5.4 billion ($560.5 million, down 6% year-on-year)
      • PC/Console games: SEK 3 billion ($311.4 million, down 2% year-on-year)
      • Mobile: SEK 943 million ($97.9 million, down 31% year-on-year)
      • Entertainment & services: SEK 1.3 billion ($134.9 million, up 9% year-on-year)
    • Full-year (12 months ended March 31, 2025)
      • Net sales: SEK 22.3 billion ($2.32 billion, down 18% year-on-year)
      • PC/Console games: SEK 1.5 billion ($155.7 million, down 27% year-on-year)
      • Mobile: SEK 5.3 billion ($550 million, down 9% year-on-year)
      • Entertainment & services: SEK 6.5 billion ($674.6 million, down 7% year-on-year)
  • NetEase games segment revenue grows 12.1% to $3.3bn in Q1
    • Overall revenue for the firm increased 7.4% year-on-year to RMB 28.8 billion ($4 billion), while profit grew by 8.6% year-on-year to RMB 18.5 billion ($2.5 billion).
    • The Chinese games firm attributed the increase for its gaming segment to online games, which represented approximately 97.5% of revenue compared to 95.2% to the same period last year.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 22/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Former Xbox console exclusive Senua‛s Saga: Hellblade II is heading to PS5
  • Sony confirms it’s ending its PlayStation Stars loyalty program
  • Michael Bay Skibidi Toilet adaptation officially in production
  • Cyberpunk 2077 sequel features second city (in addition to Night City) that's like "Chicago gone wrong", says series creator

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox and Antstream Arcade have launched a new Retro Classics service on Game Pass including such titles as Commando, Grand Prix, Kaboom!, Mech Warrior 2: 31st Century Combat, and Pitfall
  • Here're the games leaving PlayStation Plus Extra and Premium in June
    • Monster Hunter Rise
    • Rogue Legacy 2
    • Inscryption
    • After Us
    • Kayak VR: Mirage
    • Avicii Invector

Business

  • King (of Activision King Blizzard Microsoft) appoints Todd Green as president following Tjodolf Sommestad's departure
  • Ubisoft CEO Yves Guillemot and HR director Marie Derain will be summoned to court in Ubisoft harassment trial to highlight the alleged "systemic nature of harassment" at Ubisoft
  • Doom: The Dark Ages reaches 3m players

Rumors

  • Looks like Ubisoft is giving Rayman an "AAA" revival after last year's "exploration phase"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Fortnite is back on the US App Store, after a 5-year court battle
  • New ‘prestige’ physical game label Editions (from "Lost In Cult") promises to ‘reinvent what it means to collect games’
    • First three releases are Immortality (PS5 limited to 1,500), The Excavation of Hob’s Barrow (Switch limited to 3,000) and Thank Goodness You’re Here! (PS5 limited to 1,500, and Switch limited to 3,000)
  • Stellar Blade 2 is official and seemingly not too far away - due to arrive before the end of next year
  • Clash of Clans latest video game to get the Netflix animated treatment

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass gets two more games today; Firefighting Simulator: The Squad and Police Simulator: Patrol Officers

Business

  • Human Fall Flat publisher Curve Games has been acquired by Nazara Technologies for £21.6m
    • The Indian tech company owns sports media site Sportskeeda and early learning app Kiddopia
  • Revenge of the Savage Planet clocks up 1m players less than 2 weeks after launch

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• SAG-AFTRA challenges Epic Games over Darth Vader AI in Fortnite
    • Epic worked with the James Earl Jones estate on the AI voice
    • SAG-AFTRA argues that the tool still takes work away from other actors who could’ve voiced the character in his video game form.
    • SAG-AFTRA: “Unfortunately, they did so without providing any notice of their intent to do this and without bargaining with us over appropriate terms. As such, we have filed an unfair labor practice charge with the NLRB against Llama Productions.”
  • Infinity Nikki developer apologises after poorly received update, promises compensation and more

Business

  • Final Fantasy 11 was going to shut down last year but fans have kept it alive, director says
    • MMO is still getting updates 23 years later

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Fortnite now lets you chat with Darth Vader using generative AI speech
  • A year after release, Activision is winding down Call of Duty: Warzone Mobile after it has "not met expectations"

Business

  • Bungie pulls Marathon gameplay after art plagiarism storm, and say it’s reviewing all art

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Epic CEO says Apple still hasn’t approved its Fortnite iOS submission, suggests it’s obstructing the process
  • Hideo Kojima says he’s left staff a ‘USB stick of game ideas’ for after he dies
  • Seminal idle game Cookie Clicker is coming to PS4/5, Xbox, Switch on May 22nd
  • Persona 5: The Phantom X will release globally on 26th June and will be available across iOS, Android and PC via Steam
  • Death Stranding 2 getting world tour to celebrate its release

Free Games / Subscription

  • Killer Instinct Gold becomes the 39th Nintendo 64 game to be added to Switch Online

Business

  • Jade Raymond has left Haven Studios, the PlayStation-owned studio she founded
  • EA pushes office return, largely phasing out remote roles
  • Take Two Results
    • Q4
      • Net revenue: $1.583 billion (up 12% year-over year)
      • Net loss: $3.73 billion (compared to a $2.9 billion loss in the year-ago quarter)
      • Total net bookings: $1.582 billion (up 13% year-over-year)
    • Full Year
      • Net revenue: $5.63 billion (up 5% year-over-year)
      • Net loss: $4.48 billion (compared to a $3.74 billion loss in the last fiscal year)
      • Total net bookings: $5.65 billion (up 6% year-over-year)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 May 2025.

    • The game is not being delisted or made unplayable, Gun Interactive stresses
  • Marvel 1943: Rise of Hydra has been delayed to "early" 2026

Business

  • Capcom Financial Results
    • For the 12 months ended March 31, 2025:
      • Net sales: ¥169.6 billion ($1.15 billion, up 11.3% year-over-year)
      • Net income: ¥48.45 billion ($328 million, up 11.7% year-over-year)
    • Eighth consecutive year of record results, 12th consecutive year of operating profit growth

Rumors

  • Accessibility leader Mark Barlet of AbleGamers accused of employee abuse and waste of charity's funds

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Fallout Renewed for Season 3 Ahead of Season 2 Premiere in December
  • Silent Hill 2 is getting a £125 complete vinyl soundtrack with discs the colour of putrid meat - Joint venture from Laced Records and Konami

Business

  • Sega FY2025 Results
    • Net sales: ¥428.9 billion ($2.89 billion, down 8.5% year-on-year)
    • Operating income: ¥48.1 billion ($323 million, down 16.8% year-on-year)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Giant Bomb has been sold by Fandom (to long-time staff members Jeff Grubb, Jeff Bakalar, Dan Ryckert, Mike Minotti and Jan Ochoa), will continue independently
  • Bam Margera is officially returning to Tony Hawk’s Pro Skater 3 + 4 as secret skater after Tony Hawk demands inclusion
  • Sega announces latest Virtua Fighter is coming to Switch 2, PS5, and Xbox
  • Dead by Daylight celebrates ninth anniversary with Five Nights At Freddy's and The Witcher collaborations
    • Springtrap, the FNAF villain, will be the game's next Killer - played by Matthew Lillard

Business

  • 200 Overwatch devs at Activision Blizzard vote to unionise (joining the Communications Workers of America (CWA)
  • Sumo Group announces leadership changes, co-founders step down
    • Carl Cavers, Paul Porter, and Darren Mills retiring, COO Gary Dunn and CFO Andy Stewart appointed as co-CEOs

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Gex Trilogy gets release date (PS5, Xbox Series S/X, Switch and PC on June 16), as well as a $200 collector’s edition with a 3-foot inflatable doll
  • Mafia: The Old Country release date (August 8) and $50 price point confirmed
  • Paradox announces Europa Universalis 5

Business

  • Tripledot Studios acquires AppLovin's mobile games business for $800 million
    • The deal means Tripledot will acquire 10 studios, helping it to 'significantly expand its operations' in key markets like the US and Asia.
    • The deal is half cash and half equity, with AppLovin becoming a minority shareholder of Tripledot Studios. Tripledot expects the deal to close in summer 2025, pending regulator approval.
  • Mobile studio Fuse Games receives $7m investment
  • Nintendo Financial Results
    • For the full year ended March 31, 2025
      • Net sales: ¥1.2 trillion ($8.3 billion, down 30.3%)
      • Operating profit: ¥282.5 billion ($1.9 billion, down 46.6%)
      • Ordinary profit: ¥372.3 billion ($2.5 billion, down 45.3%)
      • Gross profit: ¥710.1 billion ($4.9 billion, down 25.5%)
    • Forecast for the full year
      • Net sales: ¥1.9 trillion ($13.1 billion, up 63.1%)
      • Operating profit: ¥320 billion ($2.2 billion, up 13.3%)
      • Ordinary profit: ¥380 billion ($2.6 billion, up 2.1%)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Hideo Kojima says he has a concept for Death Stranding 3 – but says someone else will have to make it
  • Rockstar reiterates that the latest GTA 6 trailer was captured on PS5 and consisted of "equal parts gameplay and cutscenes"

Business

  • Sony announces new studio TeamLFG including Bungie, Fortnite and Roblox veterans
  • Unity Q1 Results
    • Revenue: $435 million (down 6% year-on-year)
    • Create Solutions revenue: $150 million (down 8% year-on-year)
    • Grow Solutions revenue: $285 million (down 4% year-on-year)
    • Net loss: $78 million (with a margin of 18%)
  • EA Q4 / FY Results
    • Q4 Results:
      • Revenue: $1.8 billion (up 5.8% YOY)
      • Net income: $254 million (up 39.5% YOY)
      • Net bookings: $1.8 billion (up 8% YOY)
    • FY Results:
      • Revenue: $7.4 billion (down 1.3% YOY)
      • Net income: $1.1 billion (down 8% YOY)
      • Net bookings: $7.3 billion (down 1% YOY)

Rumors

  • Rumors are starting to circulate amongst online circles that Build a Rocket Boy's "Everywhere" is being quietly cancelled in favour of MindsEye.
    • Message on BARB discord has ended the builders beta. All mentions of Everywhere have been removed from BARB's website and terms of service. everywhere.game now redirects to mindseye.game
    • It is understood that "Arcadia", the content editor for Everywhere, will now be used to make UGC for MindsEye exclusively.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Grand Theft Auto 6’s second trailer released

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass games for May
    • Arriving:
      • Dragon Ball Xenoverse 2 (Cloud, Console, and PC) – May 7
      • Dungeons of Hinterberg (Console) – May 7
      • Flintlock: The Siege of Dawn (Xbox Series S/X) – May 7
      • Metal Slug Tactics (Console) – May 7
      • Revenge of the Savage Planet (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – May 8
      • Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Mutants Unleashed (Cloud, Console, and PC) – May 8
      • Warhammer: Vermintide 2 (Cloud and Console) – May 13
      • DOOM: The Dark Ages (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – May 15
      • Kulebra and the Souls of Limbo (Cloud, Console, and PC) – May 16
      • Firefighting Simulator: The Squad (Cloud, Console, and PC) – May 20
      • Police Simulator: Patrol Officers (Cloud, Console, and PC) – May 20
    • Leaving (May 15th):
      • Brothers: A Tale of Two Sons (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Chants of Sennaar (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Dune: Spice Wars (Game Preview) (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Hauntii (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • The Big Con (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Business

  • Split Fiction surpasses 4m copies sold
  • Roblox Q1 Results
    • Revenue: $1.04 billion, up 29% year-on-year
    • Bookings: $1.2 billion, up 31% year-on-year
    • Net losses: $215.4 million compared to $270.8 million during the same quarter last year
  • Starbreeze to acquire Payday 3 publishing rights from Embracer subsidiary Plaion

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• A new Ecco the Dolphin is coming, as well as remasters of the first two games
  • Gears of War: Reloaded is officially coming August 26th to PlayStation 5, Xbox Series S/X, and PC
  • Grand Theft Auto 6 release date has been announced - May 26, 2026

Business

  • Monopoly Go surpasses $5bn in two years

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Future of Giant Bomb website in doubt following prominent staff departures - Brand will undergo a "strategic realignment" according to owner Fandom
  • Saber confirms it’s delisting Evil Dead: The Game but says it plans to keep the servers online

Business

  • Ubisoft hints more Prince of Persia on the way as The Lost Crown passes 2m players
  • Polygon has been sold to Valnet, with the majority of staff laid off
  • Microsoft is raising prices on Xbox consoles, with games set to hit $80 this year
    • Most notably, the price of Xbox Series X and Series S consoles has been increased by $100 and $80 respectively in the US.
    • The UK appears to be one of the countries affected least by the increase – while Xbox Series S has risen by £50, Series X has only seen a £20 increase

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 01/05/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 1 May 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Fortnite returning to US App Store next week. Will return to worldwide app store if "Apple extends the court's friction-free, Apple-tax-free framework worldwide"
  • Sega’s 32-player PC and mobile game Sonic Rumble has been delayed again - originally set to release May 8, no updated release date given

Free Games / Subscription

  • PS+ May Games: Ark: Survival Ascended, Warhammer 40,000: Boltgun and Balatro

Business

  • Top PlatinumGames designer (Takahisa Taura) quits studio, starts new game company Eel Game Studio
  • Xbox Q3 Results
    • Xbox gaming revenue: Up 5% year-over-year.
    • Xbox content & services (Game Pass, first-party releases): Up 8%.
    • Game Pass subscriptions: Increased (exact numbers not disclosed) – last confirmed at 34M in February.
    • Console Sales: Down 6% year-on-year.
  • Remedy Q1 Results
    • Revenue: €13.4 million (up 24.1%)
    • Operating profit: €1.3 million (compared to operating loss of €2.1 million for same quarter last year)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Borderlands 4 release date moves forward (originally slated for release on 23rd September - will now launch on 12th September)
  • Far Cry 4 gets surprise PS5 patch adding 60fps support
  • A Minecraft Movie is leaning into the memes with sing-along sing/shout/meme screenings - Titled "Block Party Edition" screenings

Business

  • Up to 400 workers have been laid off as EA culls two Respawn game projects
  • Krafton posts "record-high quarterly sales performance" in Q1 2025
    • Sales up 41.6% quarter-over-quarter (QoQ), and 31.3% year-over-year (YoY)
    • Operating profit: 112.2% QoQ, and 47.3% YoY

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Bulletstorm studio confirms Project Maverick is a Gears of War game
    • Project Maverick was first reported back in June 2023. At this time, it was said People Can Fly's Project Maverick would be fully funded by Microsoft with a budget of $30m-50m, and based on one of the Xbox maker's existing properties.
  • Ex-PlayStation narrative director starts Until Dawn film petition to credit game writers
  • A collection of materials spanning three decades of Volition company history has been donated to the National Museum of Play

Business

  • Elden Ring sales pass 30m
  • Russian government seizes former Wargaming studio Lesta for supporting Ukraine - Lesta and Wargaming accused of "extremist activities"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Classic Capcom RPG Breath of Fire 4 returns to PC as part of GOG's Preservation Program - Plus Realms of the Haunting and more

Business

  • Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 sells 1m copies in three days
  • Resident Evil 4 Remake becomes fastest game in the series to sell 10m
  • The Elder Scrolls 4: Oblivion Remastered has clocked up over 4 million players in less than 5 days

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 25/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Remedy’s FBC: Firebreak gets a release date (June 17), will be on Xbox Game Pass and PS Plus on day one
  • Switch 2 Edition upgrades vary in cost: Nintendo UK reveals differing prices for first-party upgrades
  • Steam to launch new tags to help disabled players "search for games based on accessibility features"

Free Games / Subscription

  • Several PlayStation exclusives are leaving PS Plus on PlayStation 5 soon
    • Grand Theft Auto 5
    • Moto GP24
    • The Sims 4 Island Living Expansion Pack
    • Resistance: Fall of Man
    • Resistance 2
    • Walkabout Mini Golf
    • Synth Riders
    • Ghostbusters: Rise of the Ghost Lord Frozen Empire
    • Before Your Eyes
    • The Walking Dead: Saints and Sinners
    • The Walking Dead: Saints and Sinners Chapter 2
    • Lego Marvel Super Heroes 2
    • Stranded Alien Dawn
    • The Lego Movie 2 Video Game
    • Ghostrunner
    • Ghost
    • Payday 2 Crimewave Edition
    • Bloodstained: Ritual of the Night
    • Journey to the Savage Planet
    • Portal Knights
    • Enter the Gungeon
    • Batman Arkham Knight
    • Infamous Second Son

Business

  • NGO files complaint against Ubisoft for data protection concerns and forcing "always online" connections - "If the behaviour is illegal in the physical sphere, it should be illegal in the digital one as well"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Ghost of Yōtei (PlayStation 5) releases October 2
  • PS5’s classic PlayStation themes are coming back today - Expected to remain permanently
  • Instead of shutting down mod remaking Oblivion in Skyrim, Bethesda gave the Skyblivion mod team Oblivion Remastered keys

Business

  • Phasmophobia hits 3 million sales on console

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion Remastered is available now for Xbox, PC, PS5 and Game Pass
  • An Out Run movie is being made by Michael Bay and Sydney Sweeney
  • Fortnite is getting its first full Star Wars season next month, featuring Darth Jar Jar
  • Steve's Lava Chicken song from A Minecraft Movie goes viral, enters UK chart
    • 34-second song is chart's shortest ever track

Business

  • Devolver Digital reveals which IPs and platform have made the most money as it shares its future strategy
    • Third-party release Cult of the Lamb is the publisher’s most successful IP, with total lifetime revenue of more than $90 million.
      • This is followed by numerous first-party IP, including Astroneer ($80 million), Stronghold ($50 million across 9 games) and Serious Sam ($45 million across 11 games).
    • According to the slides, “gamers are spending more time on known IPs as opposed to new IPs”, and Devolver plans to respond to this by “leaning further into our own popular IPs in a variety of ways”.
  • Square Enix, Capcom, Taito, and Sega commit to "archiving past development materials"
  • AppLovin cuts another 97 jobs, including CEO of subsidiary studio Machine Zone
    • It has filed seven WARN filings since June 2022; 120 jobs were impacted in November and 126 in October
  • Dwarf Fortress hits 1 million sales on Steam
  • Devolver cuts 40 percent of workforce at Reigns developer Nerial

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Xbox’s ‘stream your own game’ feature on console is now available to all Game Pass Ultimate members
  • Among Us 3D release date announced - May 6th
  • A Minecraft Movie surpasses $550m globally

Business

  • Panic has paid out over $1 million to Playdate developers on Catalog

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Dune: Awakening delayed for last minute changes from beta testers
  • Metal Gear in Concert coming to London later this year
    • Two performances of the show will take place on Saturday, November 1 – a matinee performance at 2.30pm and an evening performance at 7.30pm.

Free Games / Subscription

  • April Xbox Game Pass games
    • Arriving
      • Grand Theft Auto V – April 15
      • Neon White (Console) – April 16
      • SpongeBob SquarePants: The Patrick Star Game (Cloud, Console, and PC) – April 16
      • Crime Scene Cleaner (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – April 17
      • Tempopo (Cloud, Console, and PC) – April 17
      • Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – April 24
      • Towerborne (Game Preview) (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – April 29
      • Far Cry 4 (Cloud, Console, and PC) – April 30
      • Anno 1800 (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – May 1
      • Call of Duty: Modern Warfare II (Cloud, Console, and PC) – May 1
      • Dredge (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – May 6
    • Leaving (April 30th)
      • Have a Nice Death (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Kona II Brume (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Sniper Elite 5 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • The Last Case of Benedict Fox (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Thirsty Suitors (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • The Rewinder (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Business

  • Niantic lays off 68 workers following Scopely acquisition
    • Employees were notified on March 20, 2025, layoffs effective from May 20

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 15/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 15 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• ‘Zero Company’ is the next Star Wars game from Respawn
    • It’s being created through a production collaboration with Bit Reactor, which is a new studio formed by veterans of XCOM and Civilization studio Firaxis Games. Respawn is producing the game while Bit Reactor leads on the development
    • Full reveal on April 19th

Free Games / Subscription

  • Metro’s 15-Year anniversary celebrated with free Metro 2033 Redux offer
    • Available for free on Steam, GOG and Xbox until April 16 at 5 PM CET / 9 AM PT

Rumors

  • Oblivion Remake planned to be shadowdropped on the week of the 21st of April

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• PS5 price to rise in Europe, Australia and New Zealand
    • The price hikes are effective immediately and affect the digital edition of a base PS5 in the UK and Europe, and both the digital and standard versions in Australia and New Zealand. The UK price rises by £40, the European price rises by €50.
    • PS5 Pro is not impacted by the price increases
    • Sony is decreasing the price of its PS5 disc drives to £69.99 / €79.99 / AUD $124.95 / NZD $139.95
  • Chaotic match-modifying shooter FragPunk gets new console release date after last month's delay - 29th April

Free Games / Subscription

  • Nintendo Switch Online adds three more Mega Drive classics; Streets of Rage, Super Thunder Blade, ESWAT: City Under Siege

Business

  • Matt Karch, CEO and co-founder of Saber Interactive, donates $5 million for game industry institute at University of Texas

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• David Fincher and Romain Chassaing direct Xbox advert starring CGI rats
  • Netflix's Devil May Cry animated series gets season 2 order one week after debut
  • The Xbox Games Showcase returns on June 8
  • The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild's Nintendo Switch 2 Edition Won't Include the DLC

Business

  • A group of former World of Warcraft, Fortnite, and Baldur's Gate 3 devs have joined forces to create a new anime-style multiplayer RPG in bid to create 'most accessible UGC open world' ever - "Tomo: Endless Blue"

Rumors

  • Amazon Tomb Raider series from Phoebe Waller-Bridge reportedly "dead"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Vampire Survivors forced to abandon PlayStation cross-save due to undisclosed legal complications
    • Cross-Save will still be coming to Xbox, Switch, PC and Mobile

Free Games / Subscription

  • PlayStation Plus Extra and Premium games for April
    • Extra / Premium
      • Blue Prince (PS5) – April 10
      • EA Sports PGA Tour (PS5) – April 10
      • Hogwarts Legacy (PS4/5) – April 15
      • Lost Records: Bloom & Rage Tape 2 (PS5) – April 15
      • Battlefield 1 (PS4) – April 15
      • PlateUp! (PS4/5) – April 15
    • Premium (Classics)
      • Alone in the Dark 2 (PS1) – April 15
      • War of the Monsters (PS2) – April 15

Business

  • New developer Sirius Studio announced, with Bloodborne and Demon’s Souls producer (Teruyuki Toriyama) as co-founder
  • Star Citizen has now raised over $800m, with still no release in sight
  • The Molasses Flood has been absorbed into CD Projekt Red
    • The dev team will continue working on The Witcher multiplayer spin-off 'Project Sirius' within CDPR proper, but the studio itself has been dissolved

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Astro Bot wins Best Game at the BAFTA Games Awards
  • Hades 2 will be a Nintendo Switch console exclusive at launch
  • Battle Royale Sonic Rumble’s release date has been revealed (May 8th)
  • PlayStation: The Concert is reportedly cancelling numerous tour dates and delaying others
    • So far UK shows are unaffected

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo eShop music won't be returning for Switch 2
    • Nowadays many of the titles listed on the eShop have videos accompanying them, which showcase gameplay to potential customers, and Nintendo "didn't want to intrude on that"
  • Unreleased Retro Studios Nintendo Switch game, Ridge Racer prototype and more leak online
  • Bungie's Marathon is finally getting a reveal this week - 12th April at 6pm UK time (10am PDT)
  • Sabrina Carpenter is the "Icon" of Season 8 of Fortnite Festival - Starts April 8

Business

  • Nintendo taking 'variable' approach to pricing with Switch 2 titles

Rumors

  • Michael Sarnoski reportedly attached to direct Death Stranding movie
    • Sarnoski is known for directing A Quiet Place: Day One and Pig, is currently filming The Death of Robin Hood

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• "Game development legend" Overwatch art director Bill Petras has unexpectedly died
  • Zenless Zone Zero is coming to Xbox, More Info in April 11th Livestream

Business

  • Nintendo delays Switch 2 pre-orders in the US due to Trump tariffs
    • Nintendo has said the launch date will remain the same in the US
  • Twin Sails Interactive becomes independent from Asmodee Group
    • "Our time with Asmodee provided us with invaluable experience," says CEO Nicolas Godement

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 4 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Marvel Rivals Season 2 Features Emma Frost & Ultron
  • Narayana Johnson (River Boy), composer for the Cult of the Lamb OST, has passed away

Business

  • "Australia's largest studio" PlaySide announces restructure and job cuts
    • The studio recorded revenue of $40.9m (AUD$64.6m) earlier this year. For FY25, revenue had expected to be between $39.3m-$43.1m (AUD$62-68m), but this has since been revised downward to $31.7m-$34.2m (AUD$50-54m).
    • The studio said the restructure and redundancies will save the firm $2.5m-$3.1m (AUD$4-$5m), although the restructure itself will initially cost an additional $954,000 (AUD$1.5m). It's thought the cuts will primarily impact its work-for-hire and "non-project specific" workforce.
  • Ubisoft Leamington has officially closed its doors

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo Switch 2 battery life revealed
    • Nintendo's next console will house a Lithium ion battery, with a battery capacity of 5220 mAh. Meanwhile, its battery life is "Approx. 2 - 6.5 hours". Nintendo notes this is just an "estimate" life span, and adds "the battery life will depend on the games you play and usage conditions".
    • Switch Lite has 3-7 hours battery life, Switch consoles that start with the serial number XA (release model) had 2.5 - 6.5 hours
  • Zelda Breath of the Wild and Tears of the Kingdom will have achievements on Nintendo Switch 2
    • In-app achievements will be exclusive to the Nintendo Switch 2 versions of the games
    • These achievements track general stats such as weapons collected, the number of shrines completed, and other in-game metrics. Another achievement system, medals, will be awarded for reaching certain in-game milestones.
    • Players will be able to track their achievements against their friends, and other players in the Nintendo app.
  • FromSoftware is making a multiplayer Switch 2 exclusive called The Duskbloods
    • PvPvE multiplayer title directed by Hidetaka Miyazaki, seemingly heavily inspired by Bloodborne
    • Coming in 2026
  • Switch 2 Details TL;DR
    • $449.99 / £395.99 for base console, $499.99 / £429.99 for Mario Kart World bundle. Japan has a region locked "cheaper" variant
    • 4K support, HDR, Joy-Con with mouse controls, built in microphone, webcam support, 120hz screen
    • Certain games will get paid Switch 2 upgrades (Breath of the Wilds, Tears of the Kingdom ,etc), though some will get free upgrades. Non-upgraded games are backwards compatible
    • Gamecube games are available on NSO, exclusive to Switch 2. Games on the service will include The Legend of Zelda Wind Waker, Soul Calibur 2, F-Zero GX, Super Mario Sunshine, -Luigi's Mansion, Super Mario Strikers, Pokemon XD: Gale of Darkness, and more
    • Showcase game "Nintendo Switch 2: Welcome Tour" is a paid digital title
    • Default game price seems to start around $80
    • Switch 2 available June 5th
  • Upcoming Switch 2 First Party Games
    • Mario Kart World
    • Donkey Kong Bananza
    • Metroid Prime™ 4: Beyond – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition
    • Kirby Air Riders
    • Drag x Drive
    • Nintendo Switch 2 Welcome Tour
    • The Duskbloods
  • 46 third-party games have been confirmed for Switch 2, with 17 coming on launch day
    • Third Party Launch Games
      • Arcade Archives 2: Ridge Racer
      • Bravely Default Flying Fairy: HD Remaster
      • Civilization VII
      • Cyberpunk 2077: Ultimate Edition

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass April
    • Arriving
      • Borderlands 3 Ultimate Edition (Cloud, Console, and PC) – April 3
      • All You Need is Help (Console) – April 3
      • Still Wakes the Deep (Xbox Series S/X) – April 3

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo Switch 2 Direct today at 6 AM PT/9 AM ET / 2 PM UK
    • The Nintendo Switch 2 Direct will last for “approximately 60 minutes,” according to Nintendo
  • Konami are selling a limited time White Pillow from Silent Hill 2 (not an April Fool's)
    • For those that have played the game; an odd choice of merchandise
  • Evercade is releasing a Neo Geo cartridge and handheld
    • The cartridge, which will be named Neo Geo Arcade 1, contains six Neo Geo arcade games and will be released in July at $24.99 / €24.99 / £19.99. Games are Metal Slug, King of Fighters 2000, Shock Troopers, Megician Lord, Sengoku and Ironclad

Business

  • Atomfall tops 1.5m players
  • Krafton acquires $14m controlling stake in Indian studio Nautilus Mobile
  • Death Stranding passes 20m players

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 01/04/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 1 April 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Official Final Fantasy 9 25th anniversary website ignites remake hopes once again

Business

  • Former EA exec Adolf Kristjansson appointed Starbreeze CEO
    • Kristjansson replaces current interim CEO, Mats Juhl, who resumes his role as CFO
  • Monster Hunter Wilds smashes past 10 million sold, sets new record for Capcom
    • It's now the 6th best-selling Capcom game ever
  • Eidos Montreal is laying off 75 staff, saying it doesn’t ‘have the capacity’ to reallocate them
    • On LinkedIn, said it had to let the staff go because one of its ‘mandates’ was ending

Other Notes

  • April Fools can be a very "dangerous" time for game news. As is tradition with the Game News Round-Up, news stories covered on April 1st - 2nd are usually intentionally a little less than the usual amount. We know that people come to the Game News for facts they can rely on, so as per our usual promises - we try to vet every story a little closer on April 1st - 2nd to hopefully filter out any of the silly jokes and memes. Sometimes some stories slip through and we'll be sure to highlight if this is case. As per usual, thanks for support the Game News Round-Up!

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 31/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 31 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• MindsEye, new thriller from former GTA producer and Hitman publisher, gets release date; 10th June 2025, across PS5, Xbox Series S/X, and PC
    • MindsEye will be available as a standalone game seperate to the Everywhere platform for £54.99 / $59,99 / €59.99.

Business

  • SAG-AFTRA firms up support for student devs and game jam participants
    • The Student Interactive Waiver Agreement and the Game Jam Waiver Agreement aims to "provide video game developers at every stage of their careers the opportunity to work with union performers, even as the video game strike continues".
  • Myst studio Cyan Worlds lays off half its team
  • UK National Videogame Museum appoints new trustees
    • Trustees include Ukie chief executive Nick Poole and Sony Interactive Entertainment senior director John Booth
  • Square Enix to shut down War of Visions: Final Fantasy Brave Exvius in May 2025
    • Mobile title launched in March 2020, will end service due to difficulty maintaining "a satisfactory app experience"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo announces new app that will deliver daily information and content and "functions like a daily calendar"
  • Classic PSP rhythm adventures Patapon 1 & 2 are shaking off their PlayStation exclusivity with PS5, Nintendo Switch and PC collection PATAPON 1+2 Replay published by Bandai Namco
  • Metroid Prime 4: Beyond gameplay trailer shows Samus’ new psychic abilities
  • Nintendo announces Rhythm Heaven Groove for Nintendo Switch
    • Coming 2026
  • Nintendo reveals major Switch and Switch 2 digital games shakeup with ‘Virtual Game Cards’
    • Users can opt-out and remain with the original system currently in place
    • This system allows users to locally share their games with other devices / family members for up to two weeks at a time.
  • Nintendo’s Tomodachi Life is getting a sequel next year

Free Games / Subscription

  • Epic Games Store's latest free games revealed
    • Cat Quest and Neko Ghost, Jump!

Business

  • Crystal Dynamics lays off 17 employees
  • Tencent invests $1.25bn in new Ubisoft subsidiary focused on Assassin's Creed, Far Cry, and Rainbow Six
    • The new subsidiary has been granted a "worldwide, exclusive, irrevocable, perpetual license" in respect to the IPs listed above, and includes teams developing those franchises in Montréal, Quebec, Sherbrooke, Saguenay, Barcelona, and Sofia, although it will be headquartered in France and "100% owned" by Ubisoft.
    • Through a "formal and competitive process," Ubisoft selected Tencent to invest a minority stake of €1.16bn ($1.25bn) to focus on expanding those games' "truly evergreen and multiplatform ecosystems."
  • PowerWash Simulator tops 17m players

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• InZOI developer is removing Denuvo DRM from the game following player backlash
    • Removing Denuvo increases the risk of piracy but opens up customization, InZoi's director says
    • Essentially disabled Denuvo as it may affect modding opportunities
  • A new Nintendo Direct focusing on the original Switch is coming today:
    • 7am PT / 10am ET / 2pm GMT
    • Direct is focused on Switch 1 games. Switch 2 specific Nintendo Direct is planned for April 2nd at 6am PT / 9am ET / 2pm BST / 3pm CEST
  • Ark: Aquatica developer shifts blame for disastrous AI trailer onto marketing team

Free Games / Subscription

  • PlayStation Plus Essential games for April
    • RoboCop: Rogue City (PS5)
    • The Texas Chainsaw Massacre (PS4/5)
    • Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth – Hacker’s Memory (PS4)

Business

  • CD Projekt 2024 Financial Results
    • The developer reported a 17.9% decrease in sales to PLN 985 million ($254 million) compared to PLN 1.2 billion ($310.4 million) in 2023.
    • Its net profit dropped by 2.3% from PLN 481.1 million ($124 million) the previous year to PLN 469.8 million ($121.5 million).
    • Cyberpunk 2077 and its expansion Phantom Liberty were the main drivers of sales, generating almost PLN 600 million ($155.1 million) in 2024.
    • As of November 2024, sales of Cyberpunk 2077 topped 30 million copies while Phantom Liberty sold over eight million units.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Japanese shrine aided by Ghost of Tsushima fans announces tourist ban due to "unforgivable act of a grave and unforgivable act of disrespect committed by foreigner(s)"
    • All tourists - domestic and foreign - are banned
  • Game Informer rescued by blockchain firm, with ‘entire team’ reunited
    • In an announcement posted on its revived website on Tuesday, Game Informer said it would be returning in both digital and print, with “the entire team” that was working together during its closure reunited.
  • Sims rival InZOI has become the most wishlisted game on Steam
    • As the game’s Early Access release on March 28 nears, it’s seen a surge in popularity, causing it to overtake the previous most wishlisted game, Hollow Knight: Silksong.
    • The game has also been followed on Steam by more than 50,000 followers in the past week, bringing its total to around 240,000.
  • Witcher and Cyberpunk’s CD Projekt is teaming up with Scopely for an IP adaptation
    • The partnership was announced during CD Projekt‘s earnings call on Tuesday. No details were announced, nor was the specific IP that the game will use.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 25/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Update: Silent Hill f's Australia non-classification removed from Australian Classification Board website
    • Upcoming game was previously refused classification, but has since been removed
    • An error on a self-reporting tool has been blamed for the early non-classification
  • Sony confirms Indiana Jones PS5 date
    • April 17, 2025

Business

  • Geoff Keighley and Christopher Dring announce new publication The Game Business
    • The B2B site will have a physical event alongside Summer Game Fest
    • “Geoff may be backing it, but TGB is not part of The Game Awards (despite the acronym). This is a separate entity that I’m responsible for. But Geoff’s support does mean we can go faster, I can make most of it free, and it means we can put on our first event: The Game Business Live, which takes place alongside Summer Game Fest in June.”

Rumors

  • Warner Bros was working on a Mario Kart-like crossover game

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• 27 more classic PC games have joined the GOG Preservation Program, including Silent Hill 4
    • The preservation program is GOG's commitment that games within the program continue to run on newer and modern and hardware "forever"
    • With Silent Hill 4's introduction to the program, GOG have altered the game to fix bugs from the original PC version, such as missing hauntings (that were present on the PS2 version)

Free Games / Subscription

  • Nintendo Switch Online adding four more SNES games
    • Nobunaga's Ambition
    • Nobunaga's Ambition: Lord of Darkness
    • Romance of the Three Kingdoms 4: Wall of Fire
    • Uncharted Water: New Horizons

Business

  • ESA launches Accessible Games Initiative and unveils 24 "tags" for gaming storefronts and product pages
    • EA, Google, Nintendo of America, Ubisoft, Amazon Games, Riot Games, Square Enix and Warner Bros Games have all pledged support
  • Silent Hill f has been refused classification in Australia
    • Without any changes, this means Silent Hill f will be not able to be sold in Australia
  • Assassin's Creed Shadows tops 2m players, "surpassing" launches of Origins and Odyssey

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Phil Spencer says more movies and TV shows based on Xbox games are on the way
  • RollerCoaster Tycoon 3 is now available on Xbox and PS5
  • Nightdive's long-awaited System Shock 2 remaster finally arrives June 26th for PlayStation, Xbox, Switch, and PC
  • McDonald's limited-edition £8 Minecraft Movie menu includes collectibles for adults

Free Games / Subscription

  • Epic Games Store's latest free PC and mobile games are here
    • 20th March - 27th March
      • Jurassic World Evolution 2 (PC)
      • Super Meat Boy Forever (iOS/Android)
      • Eastern Exorcist (iOS/Android)

Business

  • Assassin's Creed Shadows passes 1m players on launch day

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Xbox’s nunchuk-like Adaptive Joystick is now available, priced at $30
    • The controller is aimed at players with limited mobility, and is compatible with Xbox consoles or PC
  • Palworld’s latest update includes crossplay, Photo mode and other new features
    • Available now
  • Analogue’s Nintendo 64 reimagining, Analogue 3D, has been delayed
    • The $250 console has slipped to July

Free Games / Subscription

  • Eight games leave PlayStation Plus next month
    • Animal Well
    • Tales of Kenzera: ZAU
    • Nour: Play with Your Food
    • Deliver Us Mars
    • Miasma Chronicles
    • Stray Blade
    • Kena: Bridge of Spirits
    • Slay the Spire

Business

  • A new industry-wide union has been launched for all American and Canadian developers
    • United Videogame Workers-CWA (UVW-CWA) will launch at GDC this week
  • ID@Xbox has paid indie devs more than $5bn since 2013
    • Program saw over 1,000 games launch last year, including Balatro and Phasmophobia

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Croc: Legend of the Gobbos remaster gets a PC and console release date
    • 2nd April for PS4/5, Xbox One / Series S/X, Switch and PC (via GOG)
  • Snail Games announces ARK: Survival Evolved DLC "Acquatica", with controversial AI trailer

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass lineup for the rest of March
    • Arriving
      • Octopath Traveler II (Series S/X) – March 19 - Standard
      • Train Sim World 5 (Console) – March 19 - Standard
      • Mythwrecked: Ambrosia Island (Cloud, Console, and PC) – March 20 - Ultimate, Standard
      • Blizzard Arcade Collection (Console and PC) – March 25 - Ultimate, Standard
      • Atomfall (Cloud, Console, and PC) – March 27 - Ultimate
    • Leaving (March 31st)
      • MLB The Show 24
      • Lil Gator Game
      • Hot Wheels Unleashed 2
      • Open Roads
      • Yakuza 0
      • Yakuza Kiwami
      • Yakuza Kiwami 2
      • Yakuza Like a Dragon
      • The Lamplighter’s League
      • Monster Hunter Rise

Business

  • Unity claims 79 percent of developers are 'feeling positive' about generative AI
    • The annual Unity Gaming Report, based on a Clint survey with 300 respondents (150 based in North America, 60 in EMEA, 60 in APAC, and 30 in South America), claims the vast majority of developers are upbeat about the encroachment of generative AI tools on the game industry.
    • It states that 31 percent of developers are feeling 'extremely positive' about the technology, with an additional 48 percent feeling 'somewhat positive.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Nightdive announces a port of "I Have No Mouth, And I Must Scream" to consoles
    • Releases March 27th for PS4/5, Xbox One / Series S/X and Nintendo Switch

Business

  • PlayStation has formed new studio Dark Outlaw Games with Call of Duty Zombies lead Jason Blundell

Rumors

  • Resident Evil 9 To Be A Major Reinvention On Par With RE4 & RE7, Will Feature A Different Playstyle

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Respawn’s next Star Wars game confirmed for reveal
    • Star Wars "turn-based tactics" strategy game will be revealed at celebration on April 19th
  • Star Wars: Hunters to be sunset later this year
    • Season 5 of Star Wars: Hunters will be extended by three weeks, beginning on March 25, 2025.
    • The final update for all platforms, including Switch, iOS, and Android, will be on April 15, 2025. In-game purchases will also be disabled from this date.
  • UK: You can now order CEX from JustEat

Business

  • PlayStation is partnering with People Can Fly on a PlayStation game franchise
    • People Can Fly is building a prototype based on a beloved PlayStation IP
    • No official further details given
  • Final Fantasy series has now sold over 200m copies worldwide
    • Final Fantasy Pixel Remaster sells over 5m copies alone
  • Split Fiction reaches 2m sales in one week

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 3 is officially in development
    • Commenting on the announcement, Focus Entertainment deputy CEO Jon Bert reiterated that Space Marine 2 remains the focus for now, with more content to come for that game before Space Marine 3 is released.
  • Microsoft is introducing an Xbox AI that helps players finish games
    • Copilot for Gaming will provide situation-specific tips during gameplay
  • PowerWash Simulator 2 is coming this year to PS5, Xbox Series and PC
    • The game isn't currently planned for Nintendo consoles

Business

  • Spectre Divide developer Mountaintop to shutter just six months post-launch
    • CEO says the studio is "out of funding to support the game" and "will close doors at the end of this week"
    • It's unclear how many people have been impacted by the closure, but LinkedIn states the company employs between 51-200 people.
  • Prytania Media sues NetEase for defamation, blames it for studio collapse
    • The lawsuit—filed on January 3, 2025—seeks $400 million in damages and blames NetEase executives for allegedly leaking confidential financial information to other investors, purportedly in retaliation of the Strains' efforts to help the Chinese conglomerate comply with American regulations.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Obituary: Voice actor Dave Mallow has passed away at age 76
    • Throughout his voice acting career, Mallow voiced characters in games like Street Fighter, Resistance 3, and Call of Duty
  • Obituary: Thomas Lee, EA alum and strategic advisor, has died
    • Lee previously worked on Final Fantasy IX and Wing Commander III before taking on managerial and producer positions at Nexon America and Stealth CrossMedia.

Free Games / Subscription

  • PS Plus Premium and Extra games for March
    • PlayStation Plus Premium and Extra:
      • UFC 5 (PS5)
      • Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown (PS4/5)
      • Captain Tsubasa: Rise of New Champions (PS4)
      • Mobile Suit Gundam Battle Operation Code Fairy (PS4/5)
      • Bang-On Balls: Chronicles (PS4/5)
      • You Suck at Parking (PS4/5)
      • Syberia – The World Before (PS4, PS5)
    • PlayStation Plus Premium:
      • Armored Core (PS4/5)
      • Armored Core: Project Phantasma (PS4/5)
      • Armored Core: Master of Arena (PS4/5)
      • Arcade Paradise VR (PSVR2)

Business

  • Live-service game engine Pragma nets $12.75 million in funding
  • Virtuos establishes new South Korea studio
  • Moon Studios' Ori series tops 15m sales
  • Pokémon Go and the rest of Niantic’s game business is being sold to Monopoly Go maker Scopely for $3.5 billion

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Disco Elysium studio ZA/UM has announced its next game, espionage RPG Project C4
    • Project C4 is a "mind-warping" espionage RPG

Free Games / Subscription

  • Apple Arcade is getting brand new Katamari and Space Invaders games next month
    • Katamari Damacy Rolling Live will be released on April 3rd (Exclusive to Apple Arcade)
    • Space Invaders Infinity Gene Evolve will also be released on April 3rd (This too will be exclusive to Apple Arcade)
    • The third new game coming to Apple Arcade next month is Puffies, a puzzle game which “reimagines classic jigsaw puzzles with the nostalgic charm of puffy stickers”.
    • Joining the three new games are three ‘+’ versions of existing games – RollerCoaster Tycoon Classic+, The Game of Life 2+ and Sesame Street Mecha Builders+.
  • Enter The Gungeon is back on Xbox Game Pass

Business

  • Ori developer Moon Studios goes ‘fully independent’ post Private Division, will finish No Rest For the Wicked without Take-Two
    • Take-Two had retained the rights to No Rest For The Wicked after selling off its Private Division label
  • Lego is starting to bring its game development in-house, key exec says
  • The Outlast Trials clocks up 3 million players since launch

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Something ‘very similar’ to FSR 4 is coming to PS5 Pro in 2026
    • Cerny spoke in the wake of news that Sony and AMD have partnered on the Project Amethyst initiative, which is focused on delivering AMD’s equivalent to Nvidia‘s DLSS. This technology is similar to Sony’s own PSSR tech which is a machine-learning based upscaler.
    • “The neural network (and training recipe) in FSR 4’s upscaler are the first results of the Amethyst collaboration and results are excellent, it’s a more advanced approach that can exceed the crispness of PSSR. I’m very proud of the work of the joint team!”
  • Call of Duty Warzone’s iconic Verdansk map returns next month, Activision confirms
    • Verdansk returns to Call of Duty: Warzone on April 3
  • A new Lego Mario Kart set aimed at adults has been announced
    • The set, which consist of 1,972 pieces, is called Lego Mario Kart: Mario & Standard Kart and is rated 18+ due to the complexity of the build.
    • Launches May 15th
  • Silent Hill Transmission Set for March 13 (3pm PT / 6pm ET / 10PM GMT) - to Feature Silent Hill f

Business

  • Split Fiction crosses massive sales milestone in just 48 hours (1 million copies sold)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Death Stranding 2 releases June 26th
    • An orchestral world tour is planned for the end of the year with 16 locations all over the world planned.
  • The Pokémon Company and Honda have produced a life-sized concept motorbike based on a Pokemon
  • Resident Evil movie reboot snapped up by Sony, and it already has a release date
    • Releases 18th September 2026

Business

  • Hidden Variable sues Skullgirls publisher Autumn Games for $1.2m in unpaid payments
    • HVS says it is "no longer involved" with Skullgirls Mobile, 2nd Encore, or "other Skullgirls IP-related efforts"
  • Respawn lays off staff, cancels another unannounced game
    • Respawn has cancelled its third unannounced project in three years.
    • Unconfirmed amount of staff affected. It is claimed only a "small" number are affected, though a former executive assistant has claimed their project was gutted.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• RoboCop: Rogue City is getting a standalone expansion and you can play as pre-Robocop Alex Murphy
    • Unfinished Business launches this summer on Xbox Series S/X, PlayStation 5, and PC
  • Fortnite maker suing prolific thief who stole and sold player accounts on an industrial scale
    • Epic is seeking unspecified damages from Illinois Fortnite fan Isaac "i5aac" Strock for fraud, copyright infringement and unjust enrichment.
    • Strock is named in a legal filing dated 27th February, which Epic Games has also publicised via a recent blog post - its latest move to name and shame those who target Fortnite with unscrupulous practices.
  • Sony is leasing PS5, PS5 Pro and PS Portal consoles to UK players for a monthly fee
    • The leasing plan, which has been branded Flex, is a partnership between PlayStation and leasing firm Raylo.
    • As the FAQ states, players don’t own their device when they lease it through Raylo. When the lease ends, they have the choice to return their console, continue paying monthly or contact Raylo to discuss potentially buying it outright.
  • Jackbox’s party game library is coming to your living room via a new smart TV app
    • The games will be initially be free with ads, but a monthly subscription is planned

Business

  • Dark and Darker pulled again, this time from Epic Games Store, as legal woes continue
    • Studio "working to understand the exact reasoning behind this removal".
  • CCP hires former bank economist to 'legitimize' EVE Frontier's in-game economy
    • Economist Stefán Þórarinsson has been brought onto CCP to help EVE Frontier achieve its dreams of having a 'truly open financial system within a virtual world.'

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• NetEase last-minute console delays console versions of hero shooter FragPunk
    • While the game will still be released for PC on March 6, the PS5 and Xbox Series S/X versions have been indefinitely delayed.
    • Announcing the delay on X, the FragPunk team said it had encountered “unexpected technical challenges in optimization and adaptation” during the console compliance testing process.
  • Obituary: Legacy of Kain writer Jim Curry has died
    • Along with Legacy of Kain: Soul Reaver, Curry wrote Akuji: The Heartless and handled marketing for the Gex games

Business

  • Sega promises fewer new games but deeper investments in Sonic, Atlus, and Like a Dragon
    • SEGA plans to "reinforce" its core studios through hiring and mergers & acquisitions
  • 20-year-old browser game Urban Dead to shut down as result of UK's new online safety law
  • Translation studio Transperfect acquires support dev Technicolor Games
    • As part of Transperfect's game division, Technicolor will 'reinforce its reputation as a premier provider of art, animation, and VFX services to the global video game industry.'

Rumors

  • Until Dawn remake studio Ballistic Moon "effectively closed"
    • Roughly 40 employees were let go in October, with remaining staff kept on to provide post-launch support for Ballistic's Until Dawn remake. However, it's claimed these developers - equating to around 20 employees - lost their jobs in December, leaving only the studio's founders and "possibly" a handful of employees "at most". Reportedly, Ballistic's entire PR, marketing, and development teams are now gone.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Grand Theft Auto 5’s Enhanced edition is now available for PC
    • Free GTA5 upgrade introduces features from Series S/X and PS5 versions alongside other modern PC enhancements such as ambient occlusion and global illumination
  • Activision unveils Tony Hawk’s Pro Skater 3 + 4 remaster for consoles and PC
    • It is believed from the marketing wording that Tony Hawk Pro Skater 4 has been changed to be "two minute run" gameplay akin to THPS 1-3, causing a lot of controversy online
    • Doom Slayer, once an unlockable character in the original, is now only available with the digital deluxe edition
    • Releases July 11th for Xbox One / Series S/X, PS4/5, Nintendo Switch and PC (Three day early access with digital deluxe)
    • Will be day one game pass release

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass reveals March lineup
    • Monster Train (Cloud, Console, and PC) – out today on Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass, Game Pass Standard.
    • Galacticare (Xbox Series S/X) – March 5. Now with Game Pass Standard.
    • One Lonely Outpost (Cloud, Console, and PC) – March 6 with Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass.
    • Enter the Gungeon (Cloud, Console, and PC) – March 11 with Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass, Game Pass Standard.
    • Mullet Madjack (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – March 13 with Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass.
    • 33 Immortals (Game Preview) (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – March 18 with Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass.

Business

  • Iconic game publisher Acclaim has been "resurrected", with wrestler Jeff Jarrett as advisor
    • Led by CEO Alex Josef, the former founder and CEO of Graffiti Games (Turnip Boy Commits Tax Evasion).
    • In a statement, the publisher says its initial goals are “to support indie developers and reignite classic franchises”, providing funding, marketing and PC support to indie studios.
  • More layoffs at PlayStation following live-service cancellations
    • The layoffs come amid a restructuring of Sony's US studios, as an unknown number of staff from the San Diego-based Visual Arts studio will be let go this week.
  • Jagex appoints new CEO Jon Bellamy
    • Present CEO, Phil Mansell, steps down after eight years
  • Tencent acquires additional shares in FromSoftware parent company, Kadokawa
    • Bought 1.11%, bringing its total shareholding ratio to 7.97%.
  • Monster Hunter Wilds sells 8m units in three days
  • PTW rebrands as Side to begin 'new era' of support services
    • As explained by comments from chief revenue officer Kaley Hurst: "This rebrand is the amalgamation of years of organic and inorganic growth, strategic planning for the future, and the realization that we are stronger together – in both brand and values."

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 4 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Static Recompilation for Xbox 360 titles now possible
    • A sonic fan was annoyed they couldn't play Sonic Unleashed on PC so built the system within 6 months
    • Sonic Unleashed has been used to prove out the system
    • Non-technical explanation: Opens the door for native PC ports of Xbox 360 games, akin to N64 Recompiled (which was used to make Zelda 64: Recompiled). A lot of manual work and technical knowledge of recompilation is still required but this is a huge moment for the game preservation community.
  • Black Ops 6 Season 3 and Warzone Season 3 have been delayed
    • Season 3 was widely expected to kick off on March 20, but it will now launch two weeks later than previously anticipated.
    • Activision announced last August that it planned to bring back Warzone’s original map, Verdansk, in the spring of 2025
  • Activision appears to be gauging interest in new Guitar Hero, Call of Duty and Crash Bandicoot games using AI generated adverts for non-existent titles
    • Guitar Hero Mobile, Call of Duty: Zombie Defender and Crash Bandicoot Brawl
    • Use of "terrible" AI has drawn a lot of criticism

Business

  • Secret Mode splits from Sumo Group to become an independent publisher
    • Secret Mode is the UK publisher behind games including Still Wakes the Deep and A Little to the Left
  • Rockstar acquires studio founded by the director of LA Noire and The Getaway
    • Rockstar Games acquires Video Games Deluxe, now known as Rockstar Australia
    • Sydney-based developer recently worked on upgrades to GTA: The Trilogy – Definitive Edition
  • Smash Bros creator Masahiro Sakurai honored by Japanese government for pioneering game design tutorials

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/03/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 March 2025.
item.content:encoded:
Free Games / Subscription

  • Games leaving Xbox Game Pass (March 15)
    • Evil West
    • Lies of P
    • Yakuza 5 Remastered
    • Yakuza 6: The Song of Life
    • No More Heroes 3
    • SpongBob SquarePants: Battle for Bikini Bottom
    • MLB The Show 24
    • Solar Ash

Business

  • Pokémon TCG Pocket has passed 100 million downloads
  • Monster Hunter Wilds hits nearly 1 million concurrent Steam players, despite mixed reviews on performance
    • Less than half of the Steam reviews for Monster Hunter Wilds are currently positive
  • Warner Bros games revenue dropped 29% during Q4 2024
    • Firm also addressed recent restructure in a letter to shareholders, says it expects to "propel games division back to profit in 2025"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Gibson and CRKD are releasing new Les Paul guitar controllers
    • The new guitar controllers work with Fortnite Festival and some legacy Rock Band and Guitar Hero games
  • Gex Trilogy trailer confirms a summer release window
    • The compliation consists of all three GEX games
  • Indie studio introduces a unique temperature-based pricing system for its snowman game
    • A Good Snowman is Hard to Build is a puzzle game that was originally released in 2015, and is usually priced at $14.99.
    • However, to celebrate the game’s 10th anniversary, the game’s price will change every hour on itch.io depending on the current temperature in London, according to weather forecast website OpenWeather.
  • Games being added to the GOG Preservation Program
    • The program is a commitment from GOG to ensure each game remains playable on modern PCs for as long as possible.
      • Lego Batman: The Video Game
      • Lego Batman 2: DC Super Heroes
      • Lego Batman 3: Beyond Gotham
      • Lego City Undercover (new to GOG)
      • Lego Indiana Jones: The Original Adventures
      • Lego Indiana Jones 2: The Adventure Continues
      • The Lego Movie Video Game (new to GOG)
      • The Lego Movie 2 Video Game (new to GOG)
      • The Lego Ninjago Video Game (new to GOG)
      • Lego Pirates of the Caribbean: The Video Game
      • Lego The Lord of the Rings
      • FEAR Platinum ("by next month" "to ensure Molonith's legacy")
  • A new 2D game based on Terminator 2 (Terminator 2D: No Fate) is coming in September
    • PS4/5, Xbox One /S/X, Switch, and PC (via Steam and the Epic Games Store) on September 5
  • Pokémon Champions announced for Nintendo Switch and mobile
    • The new battle-focused game is being developed by Pokémon Works and Game Freak
    • The game will connect to Pokémon Home and will be compatible with Pokémon Scarlet and Violet
  • Palworld studio is giving developers the day off to play Monster Hunter Wilds

Free Games / Subscription

  • For the first time, Nintendo is delisting a game (Super Formation Soccer, SNES) from the Switch Online retro library

Business

  • Metro dev 4A Games rebrands to Reburn with new game reveal
    • Reburn is pivoting away from Metro to a new IP, the sci-fi shooter La Quimera
  • EA deploys various Command & Conquer games' source code to the masses
    • Command & Conquer: Red Alert, Command & Conquer: Generals' Zero Hour expansion, Command & Conquer: Renegade, and a remastered collection of the first two games. Mod support for those games—and Command & Conquer: Kane's Wrath and Command & Conquer: Red Alert 3—has also been included.
    • Shared under GPL v3 license. Game files must be provided and are not included as part of the license.
  • Tekken 8 sales punch up to 3 million copies in first year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Nacon Connect digital showcase to reveal ‘secret projects’ in March
    • March 6 at 10am PT / 1pm ET / 6pm GMT / 7pm CET
  • Sonic Rumble: Sega’s free-to-play 32-player Sonic game has been delayed until spring
    • The game was soft launched last year and is currently available in ‘pre-launch’ in 44 countries, but Sega had previously said it would eventually get a global launch everywhere else (including the UK and US) in the winter. Now is set for Spring

Free Games / Subscription

  • PS+ Essential Games (Available from March 4)
    • Dragon Age: The Veilguard (PS5)
    • Sonic Colors: Ultimate (PS4)
    • Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Cowabunga Collection (PS4/5)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Steam Next Fest has launched, offering players ‘thousands of demos’
    • Runs until March 3rd
  • Tony Hawk’s Pro Skater 3 + 4 has been rated in Singapore
    • Has been rated for Nintendo Switch, PC, PS4/5, Xbox One, and Xbox Series consoles
    • Reveal expected March 4th
  • Xbox has officially delayed Fable to 2026

Free Games / Subscription

  • The Nintendo Music app has just added its 40th soundtrack
    • Super Mario Bros 2 Soundtrack

Business

  • Warner Bros. is closing three game studios and canceling Wonder Woman
    • Monolith Productions, Player First Games and Warner Bros. Games San Diego affected
    • Warner Bros Games Division is focusing on Harry Potter, Mortal Kombat, DC and Game of Thrones
  • Konami and Bloober Team are working on a new game following the success of Silent Hill 2
    • "Project H" and "Project G" are believed to be Silent Hill 1 and Silent Hill 3 - though no official confirmation the new project is Silent Hill based has been confirmed at this time. "Project G" was started towards the end of development of Silent Hill 2
  • Amazon Appstore to close on Android in August 2025
    • The company said it wants to focus on supporting the Appstore for its own devices as "that's where the majority of our customers currently engage with it."

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 25/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Retro publisher Retro-Bit admits 16-bit re-releases contain ‘uncredited’ fan translations
    • The publisher also said that future releases have been placed on hold so it can further investigate the situation and ensure they won’t suffer the same issue.
  • Mortal Kombat 1 reveals T-1000 gameplay ahead of March DLC release
    • The T-1000 and Madam Bo Kameo will join Mortal Kombat 1's Khaos Reigns expansion March 18th
    • T-1000's fatalty references the chase scene from Terminator 2
    • Robert Patrick reprises his role for the T-1000

Free Games / Subscription

  • Balatro shadowdropped on Xbox Game Pass

Business

  • Surgeon Simulator studio Bossa is making layoffs as it ‘scales back’ operations
    • In a statement posted on LinkedIn, studio co-founder and co-CEO Henrique Olifiers said the company was the latest to fail to successfully adapt to what he called “a fundamental transformation of the games industry” over the past couple of years.
    • “Once Lost Skies is established and enjoying live operations, we’ll reshape into small teams working independently on novel game ideas brought to the players as early as possible”
    • At the end of 2023, Bossa laid off approximately one-third of its staff. This reportedly saw 19 of 59 employees let go from the studio, which was founded in 2010 in London but is now fully remote.
  • Forte Labs-owned Phoenix Labs shutters Dauntless just weeks after laying off the "majority" of its staff
    • Phoenix Labs is sunsetting Dauntless after a six-year run on May 29
    • In recent years, Phoenix Labs conducted several waves of layoffs, including over 100 employees in May 2024. Weeks ago, the developer announced it'd laid off "a majority" of its remaining staff.
  • Balatro wins formal appeal to reclassify poker game as PEGI 12
    • "On the basis of this appeal, the PEGI Experts Group will develop a more granular set of classification criteria."
  • Dovetail Games appoints Gemma Brown and Rob O'Farrell as Co-CEOs
  • $112m of Hasbro's $472m 2024 digital gaming revenue came from Monopoly Go alone
    • Hasbro's gaming business saw its digital and licensed gaming revenue increase by 22% year-over-year to $471.7 million. $112 million alone of that came from Scopely's Monopoly Go.
    • Overall, however, Hasbro's full-year revenues fell by 17% YoY to $4.1 billion, which Hasbro attributes to the eOne divestiture.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• A game based on cult anime Samurai Pizza Cats is in development
    • Samurai Pizza Cats: Blast From the Past is coming to “all major platforms” in 2026, with a Steam page already up.
    • Set to be published by Red Dunes Games, the game is being developed by Dutch studio Blast Zero, best known for the anime inspired beat ’em up Jitsu Squad.
  • A new Tony Hawk game reveal is being teased in Black Ops 6
    • A sign in the new Grind map seems to suggest an announcement is coming "03.04.25”
    • Comes after a rumour that THPS 3+4 is due to release soon

Business

  • Marvel Rivals surpasses 40m players
  • Pacific Drive hits 1m copies sold

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Pokémon Presents has been announced for next week
    • February 27 at 2 PM UTC/GMT, 6 AM PT, and 9 AM ET
  • Limited Run Games says two of its recent NES releases could damage the console
