    • Last year the manufacturer released physical versions of Rugrats: Adventures in Gameland and Piopow, both of which arrive as an NES cartridge designed to be played on actual NES hardware.
    • Issues with voltage regulation
    • This isn’t the first time Limited Run has had to apologise for the quality of the games it’s been releasing for retro consoles. Last year the company apologised for using burnt CD-R discs instead of pressed discs for a re-release of 3DO horror game D.

Business

  • War Robots reaches $1bn in lifetime revenue
    • More than 4.7 million users played the Pixonic mobile shooter monthly in 2024
  • Dutch consumer foundation sues Sony for overpricing digital PlayStation games
    • The Dutch consumer foundation Massaschade & Consument alleges Sony prices digital PlayStation games at nearly double the cost of physical versions
  • Mouthwashing sales top 500K copies ahead of incoming console ports
  • Konami raises base salaries of Japanese workers for fourth year in a row
    • The developer hopes the pay increase for its full-time workers in Japan 'creates a stable and rewarding environment for our employees.'
    • The raise increases pay by ¥5,000 (or $32) per month–or ¥60,000 ($395) per year.
  • Over 32 million players have played Palworld in its first year, developer says

Rumors

  • Pokémon Go developer Niantic close to selling games for $3.5bn to Saudi-owned Monopoly Go maker Scopely
  • Netflix has reportedly laid off ‘multiple workers’ at Oxenfree developer Night School Studio

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Epic Games sues Fortnite cheater, takes back winnings and forces them to post humiliating public apology video as a stark warning to others
    • In a statement, Epic Games said it sued Bamford when it emerged he had shared his account with a friend to qualify for the FNCS 2023 Global Championship - Fortnite's biggest tournament, which that year held a $4m prize pool.
    • "We took legal action against a player who shared their account to unfairly qualify for FNCS, now they're banned from Fortnite tournaments forever and their undeserved prize money was donated to charity." Epic Games wrote, in a message posted to its Fortnite Competitive social media account.
  • Nintendo is ending its My Nintendo Gold Points Switch eShop rewards scheme
    • Players will no longer be able to earn Gold points when buying games on the Switch eshop after March 25, 2025

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass latest February titles announced
    • Arriving:
      • Avowed - 18th February (Ultimate, PC)
      • EA Sports F1 24 - 20th February (Ultimate, PC)
      • Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader - 20th February (Standard, Ultimate, PC)
      • Watch Dogs: Legion - 20th February (Standard, Ultimate, PC)
    • Leaving (Feb 28th):
      • F1 22 (Console and PC) - EA Play
      • Gris (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Maneater (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • PAW Patrol World (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Space Engineers (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Warhammer 40,000: Boltgun (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Wo Long Fallen Dynasty (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Yakuza 3 Remastered (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Yakuza 4 Remastered (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • 10 games leaving PlayStation Plus in March
    • Monster Energy Supercross - The Official Videogame 6
    • Resident Evil 3
    • Dragon Ball Z: Kakarot
    • Phoenix Wright: Ace Attorney Trilogy
    • JoJo's Bizarre Adventure: All-Star Battle R
    • Life is Strange: True Colors
    • Life is Strange 2
    • Final Fantasy Type-0 HD
    • Street Fighter 5
    • Mortal Kombat 11

Business

  • Marvel Rivals team hit by layoffs
    • It is believed that six people were affected; the entire Seattle team / North American division. Team members in China were not affected.
    • Various news outlets have been misreporting this story, making out Thaddeus Sasser was the overall director of Marvel Rivals. That is not the case, Guangyun 'Guangguang' Chen is the creative director.
    • The Seattle team were tasked with R&D tasks, not active development on the game.
    • Whilst most game news outlets are pointing to Marvel Rivals success as "why this should not have happened" - this does not seem related to the positive reaction of Marvel Rivals. Instead, it is believe this is part of NetEase’s overseas investments and studios. The firm has pulled funding from a number of overseas teams over the past year.
    • Whilst unconfirmed at this time, it is claimed that Trump's tariffs on China are playing a large part of NetEase moving away from American teams.
  • Toast Interactive cuts majority of staff, closes office
    • Toast Interactive was founded in 2016, and is known for its VR titles Richie's Plank Experience and Max Mustard.
    • When asked whether Richie's Plank Experience is still coming to PSVR2, the firm said it was "99% sure it'll happen."

Rumors

  • Mystery Tony Hawk remaster now mentioned by pro skater involved in the game, even though Activision hasn't announced it yet
    • Game is believed to be Tony Hawk Pro Skater 3+4, expected to release on Xbox, PC and PS5 in the summer.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Ubisoft announces Rainbow Six Siege X, the "biggest transformation in the game's history"
    • A full reveal will follow in March, on Thursday 13th at 5pm UK time

Business

  • Avalanche founder’s new studio Liquid Swords makes layoffs but will ‘continue to work on new IP’
    • Liquid Swords was established in 2020 by Sundberg, who was previously the founder, owner and creative director at Avalanche, which is best known as the developer of the Just Cause franchise.
    • When the indie studio was announced in 2022, it said it was planning to grow to a 100-strong team in 2023.
    • Unclear how many people have been affected
  • Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 tops 2m copies sold in less than two weeks
  • Nexon 2024 Fiscal Year Financial Results
    • Revenue: ¥446.2 billion ($2.94 billion), up 5% year-over-year
    • Operating income: ¥124.2 billion ($817 million), down 8% year-over-year
    • Net income: ¥134.8 billion ($887 million), up 91% year-over-year

Rumors

  • GTA 6: Rockstar is reportedly planning Roblox/Fortnite-style user-generated content
    • Rockstar has already met with top creators regarding GTA 6, it's claimed
    • In 2023 Rockstar acquired CFX.RE, the group behind some of GTA 5 and Red Dead Redemption 2’s most popular modding tools

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• N64 cult classic platformer Glover gets console release date
    • Courtesy of publisher and developer QUByte Interactive, Glover will release across PS4/5, Xbox One/Series S/X, and Nintendo Switch later this month on 27th February.
    • No visual or gameplay enhancements planned
  • Influential Dishonored and Half-Life 2 art lead Viktor Antonov has died

Business

  • Embracer Financial Results
    • For the three months ending December 31, 2024
      • Net sales: SEK 7.4 billion ($680 million), down 3% year-on-year, including:
      • PC/console games: SEK 2.6 billion ($242 million), down 23% year-on-year
      • Mobile games: SEK 1.6 billion ($149 million), up 2% year-on-year
      • Net profit: SEK 960 million ($89 million, compared to net loss of SEK 1.8 billion / $167 million during the same period last year)
    • For the nine months ending December 31, 2024:
      • Net sales: SEK 16.9 billion ($1.5 billion), down 22% year-on-year including:
      • PC/console games: SEK 7.3 billion ($679 million), down 35% year-on-year
      • Mobile games: SEK 4.4 billion ($409 million), down 3% year-on-year
      • Net loss: SEK 1.3 billion ($121 million, compared to net loss of SEK 1.4 billion / $130 million during the same period last year)
  • Chinese video game company Papergames (Infinity Nikki, Love & Deepspace) generated nearly $1 billion in 2024
    • Epic Games Store hits 295 million users: <https://www.eurogamer.net/epic-games-store-has-more-pc-players-than-ever-295-million-in-fact>

Rumors

  • 10:10 Games cuts 20 jobs after "complete commercial and critical failure" of debut game, Funko Fusion
    • Sources intimate say no meaningful severance support has been made available, with only "the bare minimum" entitlement offered, as well as payment of unused holiday. However, this, some speculate, is why colleagues were pressured into using up all their leave ahead of the announcement.
    • Some colleagues were apparently notified that their jobs were at risk whilst on leave. In some instances, these staff felt obliged to attend meetings about their future at the studio whilst on holiday, even if they were away in different timezones.
    • Whilst unconfirmed, it's thought around 20 people have been impacted by the cuts

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Astro Bot free update adds new levels, bots, and a PS5 Pro boost
    • The five levels will appear on a weekly basis as follows:
      • Feb 13: Tick-Tock Shock
      • Feb 20: Thrust or Bust
      • Feb 27: Cock-A-Doodle-Doom
      • Mar 6: Hard to Bear
      • Mar 13: Armored Hardcore
  • Overwatch 2 is bringing loot boxes back, two years after they were removed from the series
    • The loot boxes will be available from weekly and event rewards. Players will be able to earn "1 Legendary Loot box in the Free Battle Pass, and an additional 2 Legendary Loot boxes in the Premium Battle Pass". The boxes themselves will allow players to earn various cosmetic pieces, such as skins and emotes.

Business

  • Square Enix has shut down the iOS version of Final Fantasy Crystal Chronicles due to an ongoing bug
    • Players had reported they were unable to access additional content purchased for the iOS game, which the publisher has stated was due to changes made to the in-app purchases model.
    • "Further investigation revealed that we are unable to completely fix the bug and implement the new changes, making it unlikely to resume service for the game," a notice from Square Enix reads. "Thus, we must regretfully announce that we will end support for the iOS version of Final Fantasy Crystal Chronicles."
    • Square Enix has confirmed the other versions of the game remain unaffected.
  • Ubisoft Financial Results
    • For the nine months ending December 31, 2024
      • Revenue: €990 million (down 31.4% year-on-year)
      • Net bookings: €944 million (down 34.8%)
      • Digital net bookings: €784 million (down 33.8%)
      • Back-catalogue net bookings: €762.3 million (down 27.7%)
    • For the three months ending December 31, 2024
      • Net bookings: €301.8 million
      • Digital net bookings: €257.4 million
  • Thunderful Gothenberg rebrands as Stormteller Games
  • MTG closes $620m acquisition of Plarium
    • Raid: Shadow Legends developer to be fully consolidated into the firm

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• PlayStation says it’s planning to release more family games like Astro Bot
    • Sony says the platformer's awards success validates its plan to expand in the family genre
  • Xbox’s Metal Gear Solid Delta replaces Ape Escape monkeys with Bomberman
  • Saros, the next game from Housemarque, is out next year
    • The studio behind Returnal is back with a new IP
  • Borderlands 4 release date confirmed
    • The shooter RPG will be released on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series S/X, and PC on September 23
    • A special State of Play presentation dedicated solely to Borderlands 4 will be coming this Spring, though no specific date was confirmed.
  • Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds gets its first gameplay trailer
    • Includes different racing styles from previous sonic racing games, such as the hoverboards from Sonic Riders.
    • Sega says the game will feature “iconic characters from the Sonic the Hedgehog and Sega universes”, confirming that the game won’t be solely restricted to Sonic characters like the previously released Team Sonic Racing
  • Sega shows trailer for Shinobi: Art of Vengeance, out later this year
    • Releases August 29, 2023 on PS4/5, Xbox One / Series S/X, and Steam, with Switch and physical versions following later
  • Elden Ring Nightrein, From Software’s spin-off multiplayer game, has been given a release date of May 30, 2025
  • MindsEye launches Summer 2025 on PS5
  • Lies of P: Overture launches Summer 2025
  • Days Gone Remastered announced for PC and PS5 for release April 25th
    • If you owned Days Gone on PS4, or already on PC, you'll have the option to upgrade to this new version for $10 (UK price TBA).

Free Games / Subscription

  • February’s PlayStation Plus Game Catalog games available from February 18th
    • Extra and Premium
      • Star Wars Jedi: Survivor (PS4/5)
      • TopSpin 2K25 (PS4/5)
      • Lost Records: Bloom & Rage – Tape 1 (PS5)
      • SaGa Frontier Remastered (PS4)
      • Somerville (PS4/5)
      • Tin Hearts (PS4/5)
      • Mordhau (PS4/5)
    • Classics
      • Patapon 3 (PS4/5)
      • Dropship: United Peace Force (PS4/5)
  • All three Armored Core PS1 games are coming to the PS Plus Premium Classics Catalog
    • No release date given

Business

  • PlayStation 5 enjoys best ever holiday period, reaching 75 million shipments
    • PS5 shipments are now neck-and-neck with PS4
  • ‘Change is never easy’: Minecraft studio Mojang announces studio head switch
    • Microsoft Gaming‘s Kayleen Walters will take over at Mojang. Currently, Walters is vice president and head of franchise development at Microsoft’s gaming division.
    • Kayleen will be based out of Microsoft's US HQ
  • Alan Wake 2 has sold more than 2 million copies, Remedy is now making royalties from it
    • Control 2 has now also entered full production Remedy says
  • One Piece Pirate Warriors 4 tops 4 million sales worldwide
  • Remedy Financial Results
    • Q4 2024
      • Revenue: €11.7 million (up 13.1%)
      • Operating loss: €1.4 million (compared to operating loss of €12.8 million in 2023)
    • FY 2024
      • Revenue: €33.9 million (up 49.3%)
      • Operating loss: €4.3 million (compared to operating loss of €28.6 million in 2023)
  • Relic Entertainment outlines new business strategy
    • Relic CEO Justin Dowdeswell said the studio will focus on three areas: a commitment to existing games, working on "smaller-scope" titles, and revisiting older franchises via remasters, re-releases, or new projects.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Royal Shakespeare Company collaborating on first-ever video game adaptation with former GTA developer
    • The Royal Shakespeare Company is working on its first-ever video game, an adaptation of Macbeth set in modern day Iran
    • Navid Khonsari previously worked at GTA developer Rockstar as cinematic director on GTA 3, Vice City and San Andreas, as well as the Max Payne games. After leaving the company he co-founded iNK Stories with his wife Vassiliki Khonsari and together they gained notoriety for their previous BAFTA-nominated game 1979 Revolution: Black Friday set in the Iranian Revolution.
  • Sony announces PlayStation State of Play for today
    • 10pm UK time (5pm ET / 11pm CET) on YouTube and Twitch
    • It will last for over 40 minutes and will focus on "news and updates on great games coming to PS5" from studios around the world

Business

  • Koei Tecmo appoints new CEO Hisashi Koinuma as co-founders Yoichi and Keiko Erikawa step back
  • Krafton reports "all-time highs" in sales and operating profits in 2024
    • Full-year revenue increased by 41.8 percent year-on-year to KRW 2.7 trillion ($1.85 billion). Operating profit rose by 54 percent year-on-year to KRW 1.2 trillion ($825.7 million).
    • PUBG has never been more lucrative, publisher says
  • Nintendo's smartphone apps have topped 900 million downloads
  • Paradox's year-end report touts 'strong' 2024 output despite revenue decline
    • Fourth quarter revenue amounted to 709.1 million SEK (or $64.8 million), down 29 percent year-over-year. For the entire January 1-December 31, 2024 period, revenue was 2.2 billion SEK ($201 million), down 17 percent compared to 2023 in the same period.
    • The Swedish publisher fared better with its operating profit, which increased by 19 percent (to 395.3 million SEK) in the fourth quarter and 10 percent (721.4 million SEK) for the full year
  • Roblox revenues rose 29% during 2024
    • Q4
      • Revenue: $988.2 million, up 32% year-on-year
      • Bookings: $1.36 billion, up 21% year-on-year
      • Net losses: $218.4 million, compared to $325 million in the same quarter last year
    • Full-year
      • Revenue: $3.6 billion, up 29% year-on-year
      • Bookings: $4.36 billion, up 24% year-on-year
      • Net losses: $935.5 million, compared to $1.16 billion last year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Marvel Rivals rolls back controversial mid-season rank reset hours after announcement
    • Last night, it was announced in 10 days that players would have a rank reset part-way through the current season of Marvel Rivals.
    • After extremely negative feedback, Netease has rolled back the decision mere hours after the announcement.
    • Instead of a rank reset, players will have to to play 10 matches to redeem the rewards for their rank following the mid-season update.

Business

  • Valve reminds devs they can't use mobile-like in-game paid advertising on Steam
    • Developers cannot use paid advertising as a business model for their game, such as requiring players to engage with ads before playing, or locking gameplay behind ads
  • Ghost-hunting sim Phasmophobia hits 2m console sales, and 22m sales overall
  • Square Enix financial earnings for the nine months ending December 31, 2024
    • Net sales: ¥248.5 billion ($1.6 billion, down 3.5% year-on-year)
    • Profit: ¥24.7 billion ($161.9 million, down 7.7% year-on-year)
    • Digital entertainment net sales: ¥160.3 billion ($1.05 billion, down 10.7% year-on-year)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• PSN was down for almost 24 hours globally over the weekend
    • PS+ members will be given 5 days of PS+ as an apology
    • No official reason was given outside of "operational issues"

Free Games / Subscription

  • Wario Land 4 is coming to Switch Online’s GBA library this week
    • Feb 14th

Business

  • Sega Financial Results
    • For the nine months ended December 31, 2024:
      • Net sales: ¥322.3 billion ($2.1 billion, down 8.1% year-on-year)
      • Operating income: ¥43.7 billion ($287.9 million, down 20.8% year-on-year)
      • Ordinary income: ¥49.4 billion ($325.4 million, down 13.9% year-on-year)
  • Sonic X Shadow Generations has sold 2 million copies
  • Roblox sues 'rule-breaking' third-party marketplace PlayerAuctions
    • The developer is taking the marketplace to task for "infringing on [our] property, disrupting the virtual economy, and interfering with contractual relationships."
    • Farmville creator Zynga previously sued PlayerAuctions in 2010 on similar grounds. A settlement between Zynga and the site was reached for an undisclosed sum the following year, and games from the developer have not appeared on PlayerAuctions since.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Football Manager 25 cancelled, as Sega shifts focus to FM26
    • Refunds offered for anyone who pre-ordered twice-delayed project
  • E3 organiser ESA launches new event called ‘iicon’
    • The "Interactive innovation conference" has support from Nintendo, Microsoft, Sony and others
    • The conference will take place in Las Vegas between April 27-30 next year
    • The ESA says the event will be an opportunity for attendees to gain “exclusive access to visionary keynotes, engaging discussions and workshops, and elite networking opportunities with executives shaping the future of entertainment, business and technology”.
  • Capcom is ending service for Resident Evil Village’s pack-in multiplayer game (Resident Evil Re:Verse)
    • The multiplayer game will be removed from stores on March 3, alongside the removal of the game’s DLC.
    • On June 29, the game will no longer be playable. The game will also no longer be included when purchasing digital copies of Resident Evil Village. It appears that players will still be able to download the application once the service has ended, however, it won’t be functional.

Business

  • GTA 5 has now sold 210m copies
    • 5m copies over the last three months of 2024, up until 31st December
    • Red Dead Redemption 2 has sold 70 million copies, up 3 million units on the previous quarter
  • Roblox shares drop by 20% as platform misses target active users
    • For the fourth quarter (ending 31st December), the number of daily active users fell by four percent to 85.3 million compared to the previous quarter
    • Overall player engagement also declined by 9.7 percent in the same period to 18.7bn hours.
    • Bookings, the company's measure of sales, totalled $1.36bn (£1.09bn), also falling shy of its forecast of $1.37bn(£1.1bn).
  • Take-Two Q3 FY2025 Results
    • GAAP Net revenue: $1.36 billion, slightly down from $1.37 billion last year
    • GAAP Net loss: $125.2 million, increased from $91.6 million last year
    • Total net bookings: $1.37 billion, a 3% year-over-year increase
  • Blind Squirrel Games acquires Distributed Development
    • The new team will initially focus on co-development
  • nDreams establishes new internal studio, shutters two others
    • New studio Compass to be led by industry veteran Callum Godfrey following closure of nDreams Studio Orbital and nDreams Studio
  • Paradox acquires Haemimont Games
    • Publisher says acquisition is "a further step in [its] strategic focus on growing in the management games genre"

Rumors

  • Hi-Rez lays off devs, ends development for three games
    • Unclear how many would be affected (at least 30 according the rumour)
    • Rumour states that Smite 1, Rogue Company, and Paladins are ending development to prioritize developing Smite 2
  • Bandai Namco has reportedly reduced its Japanese workforce by over 100 in the last year
    • A filing on Japan’s online pension database shows that between April 2024 and February 2025, 117 staff have left the company. This doesn’t clarify if those who left the company did so voluntarily or not.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Steam adds warning flagging potentially abandoned early access games
  • Wreckfest 2 hits Steam Early Access in March
    • The PC version of Wreckfest 2 will receive a Steam Early Access release on March 20, 2025

Business

  • Kingdom Come Deliverance 2 tops 1m sales in 24 hours
  • ProbablyMonsters initiates fresh round of job cuts
    • Company declines to comment as scale of cuts remains unknown
  • Killer Instinct studio Iron Galaxy has laid off 66 employees
    • Rumbleverse maker says job cuts are "last resort" measure "to enable long-term survival"
  • UK government rejects calls to prohibit disabling of live service games
    • While it acknowledged the concerns expressed in the petition and said it would continue to monitor the situation, the government essentially suggested existing regulations offer sufficient consumer protection.

Rumors

  • 31st Union president Michael Condrey has reportedly been fired by 2K
    • Call of Duty veteran Michael Condrey has reportedly been fired as the head of 2K Games studio 31st Union after its debut project, free-to-play hero shooter Project Ethos, recieved a muted reponse on its reveal toward the end of last year
    • 2K remains "very committed" to 31st Union's debut game

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Capcom shares new Onimusha details and confirms a PS2 remaster is coming
  • Xbox confirms it’s bringing two real-time strategy games to PS5 this year
    • Age of Mythology: Retold (March 4) and Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition (Spring) are coming to PS5

Free Games / Subscription

  • February’s PlayStation Plus Essential titles are now available, including Payday 3
    • Payday 3 (PS5), High on Life (PS4/5) and Pac-Man World Re-Pac (PS4/5) now available

Business

  • Dragon Ball: Sparking! Zero tops 5m global sales
    • Netflix cancels six game launches as it "adjusts" portfolio to better match "what members like": <https://www.gamesindustry.biz/netflix-cancels-six-game-launches-as-it-adjusts-portfolio-to-better-match-what-members-like>
    • This means Compass Point: West, Thirsty Suitors, Tale of the Shire: A Lord of the Rings Game, Don't Starve Together, Lab Rat, and Rotwood will now no longer join the streamer's gaming library.
  • Hideki Kamiya video reveals five more leads have quit Platinum, including Bayonetta 3’s director
    • In addition to Kamiya, the “former PlatinumGames directors’ drinking party” was attended by Bayonetta Origins director Abebe Tinari, Bayonetta 3 director Yusuke Miyata, Astral Chain director Takahisa Taura, Metal Gear Rising director Kenji Saito, and Anarchy Reigns and Resident Evil character designer Masaki Yamanaka.
  • Super Mario Party Jamboree marked the series’ biggest launch with 6m shipped

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 4 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• A new Xbox indie games showcase is coming this month
    • The next ID@Xbox event will take place on February 24 at 10am PT / 1pm ET / 6pm BST
  • Need for Speed is on hold, but EA says it ‘will return in new and interesting ways’
    • Need for Speed studio Criterion is currently fully focused on Battlefield
  • ‘Let’s build Battlefield together’: EA shares pre-alpha Battlefield gameplay alongside testing details
    • “Battlefield Labs is a place for us to test concepts and experiences we’re excited about with you, our players,” according to EA. “We want our community to play a key role in the future of Battlefield and this is an opportunity for many of you to do just that.”
    • Players can register now for the chance to participate, with initial invites set to be distributed to a few thousand players in Europe and North America. Over time, EA plans to open testing up to other territories and tens of thousands more users.

Business

  • Sumo Group announces layoffs at Sumo Digital
    • Unclear how many people will be affected
    • Spurred on by a move away from their own IP work, focusing fully on "development services for partners"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/02/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 February 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Pokemon TCG Pocket’s Trading feature is changing after fan backlash
    • “The item requirements and restrictions implemented for the trading feature were designed to prevent abuse from bots and other prohibited actions using multiple accounts. Our goal was to balance the game while preserving the fun of collecting cards that are core to the Pokemon TCG pocket experience."
    • "Going forward, we also plan to offer multiple ways to obtain trade tokens including through event distributions.”
    • Unclear what other changes will be made outside of offering more trade tokens
  • The Sims and The Sims 2 have been officially re-released, with expansions and DLC
    • All previously released DLC and expansion packs are included, with one exception; IKEA Home Stuff
    • Both games have been updated to run on Windows 10 and 11, and have an updated rendering engine.
  • Shang-Chi star Simu Liu says his dream is a Sleeping Dogs movie then a ‘sequel game’
    • The Marvel star says he's "working with the rights holders" to make a Sleeping Dogs film happen

Free Games / Subscription

  • Ridge Racer 64 is now on Switch Online, marking its first ever re-release

Business

  • Fallout: New Vegas’s lead writer has re-joined Obsidian, but insists it’s not for a sequel
    • New Vegas writer John Gonzalez is Obsidian's newest Creative Director
  • Konami has released its financial results for the nine months ending December 31, 2024
    • Revenue: ¥310.8 billion ($2 billion, up 22.8% year-on-year)
    • Business profit: ¥87.1 billion ($560 million, up 38.7% year-on-year)
    • Operating profit: ¥86.7 billion ($557 million, up 45.5% year-on-year)
    • Digital Entertainment
      • Revenue: ¥228.8 billion ($1.4 billion, up 32% year-on-year)
      • Business profit: ¥80.8 billion ($519.7 million, up 44.6% year-on-year)
  • MultiVersus will shutdown on May 30 after disappointing performance

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 31/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 31 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Forza Horizon 5 is coming to PS5 this Spring
    • The port is being handled by Panic Button (in partnership with Playground Games and Turn 10 Studios, and will contain all the content available in the Xbox and PC versions of the game.
  • Devil May Cry's animated Netflix series gets April release date, Limp Bizkit opener
    • Coming April 3rd
  • Summer Game Fest dates confirmed
    • The event will kick off with the annual Summer Game Fest livestream on Friday 6th June at 10pm UK time (5pm Eastern / 2pm Pacific)
  • Capcom games showcase coming next week, with a Monster Hunter Wilds stream right after
    • 10pm GMT/2pm PT next Tuesday, 4th February
    • First up is Capcom's Spotlight showcase. This lasts around 20 minutes and covers three titles on the publisher's slate: Onimusha Way of the Sword, Capcom Fighting Collection 2, and Marvel vs. Capcom Fighting Collection Arcade Classics.
  • WWE 2K is returning to mobile this year as a Netflix exclusive
    • Available Fall 2025
  • PlayStation’s 30th anniversary PS5 themes are leaving, but Sony says they’ll be back
    • Themes based on previous PlayStation consoles will be removed after January 31st (today)

Business

  • Prince of Persia: Lost Crown sold 1.3 million copies in its first year
    • Despite sales seemingly picking up for Prince of Persia: Lost Crown, the team that made it was disbanded after the game's single expansion came out.
  • Dr Disrespect’s Midnight Society is shutting down and its FPS is canceled
    • The studio is closing months as it split from Guy 'Dr. Disrespect' Beahm, who had been accused of sending sexually explicit messages to a minor

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Marvel Snap dev says it has a new publisher to ensure TikTok shutdown ‘never happens again’
    • New publisher is U.S.-based Skystone Games
  • Quantic Dream’s Beyond: Two Souls is getting a TV series adaptation
    • Elliot Page's Pageboy Productions has acquired the rights to make a Beyond: Two Souls TV Show
  • Sony announces plan to shift away from offering PS4 games through PlayStation Plus
    • Starting in 2026, "PS4 games will no longer be a key benefit" of the monthly PlayStation Plus line-up
    • This move won’t affect the PS4 monthly games that players have already redeemed from PlayStation Plus, which will still be accessible as long as they remain a member.
    • Roughly tracks with the same timeframe of PS3 games becoming deprecated from the service
  • Sony is dropping the PSN account requirement on PC, but having one will now unlock in-game content
    • However, Sony will now be offering in-game content unlocks to incentivize players to use a PSN account, which also offer players trophies and friend management

Free Games / Subscription

  • February’s PlayStation Plus Essential games have been revealed
    • Payday 3 (PS5), High on Life (PS4/5) and Pac-Man World Re-Pac (PS4/5)

Business

  • Capcom financial results
    • For the nine months ended December 31, 2024:
      • Net sales: ¥88.9 billion ($572.7 million), down 16% year-on-year
      • Operating income: ¥31 billion ($199.9 million), down 35% year-on-year
      • Net income: ¥23 billion ($148.2 million), down 33% year-on-year
  • BioWare's "agile, focused" approach sees jobs cut and others redeployed across "EA teams"
    • Mass Effect team "doesn't require support from the full studio"
  • Xbox revenue fell 7% in Q2 as hardware sales dropped 29%
    • Xbox content and services revenue increased 2% year-over-year, fueled by growth in Game Pass.
    • Indiana Jones and the Great Circle has attracted over four million players since its release in December.

Rumors

  • Bend Studio cuts contractors following live-service game cancelation
    • With its project scrapped, a former Bend contractor alleges the studio has ended all ongoing contracts, save for a 'select few' who were given full-time roles.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Dino Crisis 1 & 2 getting enhanced PC re-releases courtesy of GOG
    • DRM-free and out now
    • Their arrival coincides with the launch of GOG's new Dreamlist tool - a "next generation" version of its Community Wishlist - intended to show IP owners community interest in old games and help bring them back to life
  • Bethesda sends well wishes to veteran actor Wes Johnson, an "instrumental part of our games"
    • Fan donations pour in after voice artist hospitalised
  • Destiny 2 Heresy will feature a Star Wars collaboration
    • It will include three new armor ornaments (one for each Guardian class) “each inspired by the soldiers of the Galactic Empire”, plus a number of Star Wars-inspired accessories, emotes and finishers.
    • The Star Wars content will launch on February 4 alongside Destiny 2: Heresy, the game’s third Episode and the final epilogue of the Light and Darkness Saga.
  • Age of Empires 3 Definitive Edition DLC has been cancelled
  • Super Monkey Ball Banana Rumble adds the Crewmate from Among Us in new DLC
    • DLC out now

Business

  • Silent Hill 2 remake shipments and digital sales top two million
    • New milestone achieved three months after release
  • Midwest Games welcomes former Bungie exec Elizabeth Howard to spearhead 'shadow publishing' initiative
    • Midwest claims its new full-service model will address a "critical gap in the market" by offering scaled support, pricing, and service timelines while allowing developers to retain IP and revenue rights. Midwest says developers will also have "full say over publishing decisions."
  • Hideaki Nishino named sole CEO of Sony Interactive Entertainment
    • Hermen Hulst will continue leading the studio business group, CFO Hiroki Totoki promoted to Sony Corporate CEO
  • Staff take to LinkedIn to reveal "mass layoffs" at Moon Active
    • Redundancies primarily affect Lithuanian office, although staff from Poland, Armenia, Ukraine, Türkiye, and Israel also impacted
    • 20 roles apparently affected
  • Fast Travel Games cuts 30 jobs as it braces for a "more uncertain year ahead for VR games"
    • Redundancies will impact all areas of its business, including publishing, marketing, admin, and studio staff

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Xbox boss Spencer says Series S compatibility requirement isn’t going away
    • No plans to allow the release of Series X exclusive games
  • Xbox has enlisted a veteran Gears of War studio to co-develop the next series entry
    • Gears of War: Judgement studio People Can Fly is co-developing Gears of War: E-Day

Business

  • Ubisoft announces studio closure as it lays off 185 staff
    • Founded in 2002 as FreeStyleGames by a group of former Rare and Codemasters veterans, Ubisoft Leamington had most recently worked as a support studio on the Tom Clancy's The Division series, and also assisted development on games such as Star Wars Outlaws, Skull and Bones and Far Cry 5.
    • A small number of Leamington staff will be retained under remote contracts. Additionally, Ubisoft offices in Düsseldorf (formerly Blue Byte), Stockholm and the Newcastle-based Ubisoft Reflections will be downsized.
  • Dauntless and Fae Farm studio Phoenix Labs lays off "majority" of employees
    • The developer has faced multiple rounds of layoffs since its acquisition by blockchain company Forte Labs in 2023, starting with job cuts impacting 9 percent of its workforce in May that year. A further 34 layoffs followed seven months later, with an additional 140 jobs being cut last May. Layoffs have continued into 2025.
    • The studio says it'll share more about the fate of Dauntless and Fae Farm "in the coming weeks"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Tetsuhisa Seko, Nippon Ichi (NIS) president, has died at age 54

Business

  • Ex-Gearbox Publishing devs form new publisher Cooldown Games
    • Co-founder and CEO Steve Gibson said the plan is as it was at Gearbox: bring "promising projects" to the game market and continue working with his fellow alums after Gearbox Software was sold off to Take-Two last year
    • Helping Gibson start up the new company are chief officers Matt Charles (product), Erick Cobbs (business), Jackson Hardesty (operations), Jeff Skal (marketing), Jimmy Sieben (technology), and Brad Schliesser (revenue).
    • Cooldown's overall aim is to publish titles "where you don’t feel like you’re falling behind if you don’t play it enough." Skal noted the ongoing struggles with live-service and ongoing games trying to vie for players' time when they already have "forever games" set in stone. Cooldown-published titles, he reasoned, can serve as good in-between games instead of titles that "ask more of us than we can give."
  • Savvy Games hires Nika Nour as SVP and head of international business development
    • Nour will oversee international partnerships, provide advice on mergers, acquisitions, and investments
  • Annapurna appoints ex-Netflix exec Leanne Loombe as head of games
    • Loombe will oversee the company's publishing, development, and creative segments

Rumors

  • PlayStation Productions’ next movie project is Resident Evil
    • The next movie based on Capcom’s popular survival popular video game series will supposedly be directed by Zach Cregger, the actor and writer who helmed 2022 horror film Barbarian
    • Major studios are currently said to be bidding for the new film produced by PlayStation Productions

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Doom: The Dark Ages will let players change game speed and other difficulty sliders
    • The game will not have a multiplayer mode
  • Ninja Gaiden 2 Black, a remaster of the Xbox 360 game, is out today
    • Remaster was developed using Unreal Engine 5
    • Out now for Xbox Series S/X, PS5 and Steam
    • Available on Xbox Game Pass
  • Ninja Gaiden 4 is in development at Team Ninja and PlatinumGames
    • Fall 2025 on Xbox Series S/X, PS5, and PC. The game will also be available on Xbox Game Pass on day one
  • Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 won’t be a PS6 exclusive, producer promises
    • Yoshinori Kitase says FF7 part 3 has been in development for nine months
    • It should be noted that Kitase’s comments don’t necessarily mean Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 won’t be exclusive to PlayStation consoles when it’s released, simply that it won’t be exclusive to PS6.

Free Games / Subscription

  • Three more SNES games have been added to Switch Online
    • Fatal Fury 2, Super Ninja Boy and Sutte Hakkun

Business

  • Pocketpair launches publishing arm, partners with Surgent Studios for first title
    • Surgent Studios to release standalone horror game in late 2025, unrelated to Tales of Kenzera universe
  • Warner Bros. Games boss David Haddad departs following rocky period for publisher
  • Team17 Group rebrands to Everplay
    • To reduce confusion, Team17 (the publisher) is renaming to Everplay. Team17 (the developer) will keep the Team17 name.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
Free Games / Subscription

  • PlayStation Plus games leaving on the 18th February
    • The Outer Worlds: Spacer's Choice Edition
    • Tales of Arise
    • Tales of Zestiria
    • Tales of Symphonia Remastered
    • Tales of Vesperia
    • Outriders
    • Scarlet Nexus
    • Bulletstorm: Full Clip Edition

Business

  • Insomniac Games founder and president Ted Price is retiring
    • Ted Price will leave the games industry in March, over 30 years after founding Insomniac
  • Resident Evil 2 remake has sold fewer than 10,000 copies on iOS, AppMagic estimates suggest
  • Reflector confirms layoffs as Unknown 9: Awakening failed to meet "expectations"
    • Unclear at this time how many are affected
  • Virtuos acquires three studios to 'significantly augment' development support capabilities
    • The three studios significantly beef up Virtuos' headcount as it focuses on becoming the 'biggest external game developer globally.'

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 22/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Sonic the Hedgehog 4 movie release date confirmed by Paramount
    • Sonic the Hedgehog 4 will hit theaters on March 19, 2027

Free Games / Subscription

  • January’s upcoming Xbox Game Pass titles include four more day one releases
    • Arriving
      • Lonely Mountain: Snow Riders (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – Today
      • Flock (Console) – January 22
      • Gigantic: Rampage Edition (Cloud, Console, and PC) – January 22
      • Kunitsu-Gami: Path of the Goddess (Console) – January 22
      • Magical Delicacy (Console) – January 22
      • Tchia (Xbox Series S/X) – January 22
      • The Case of the Golden Idol (Console) – January 22
      • Starbound (Cloud and Console) – January 22
      • Eternal Strands (Cloud, Console, and PC) – January 28
      • Orcs Must Die! Deathtrap (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – January 28
      • Shady Part of Me (Cloud, Console, and PC) – January 29
      • Sniper Elite: Resistance (Cloud, Console, and PC) – January 30
      • Citizen Sleeper 2: Starward Vector (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – January 31
      • Far Cry New Dawn (Cloud, Console, and PC) – February 4
    • Leaving (Jan 31st)
      • Anuchard (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Broforce Forever (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Darkest Dungeon (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Death’s Door (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Maquette (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Serious Sam: Siberian Mayhem (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Business

  • Mortal Kombat 1 has sold over 5 million copies
  • Owlcat games' Warhammer 40K: Rogue Trader sells 1 million copies
  • Bohemia Interactive's revenue jumps 34% this financial year to $60.8m
    • 99% of the largest Czech game studio's "4.85m copies of games and DLC" came from sales overseas across PC, Nintendo Switch, PlayStation, and Xbox systems, but Bohemia stopped short of disaggregating that figure further.
    • 42% of all sales were made in the US, followed by the UK and Germany at 7% and 6% of the market share, respectively. There has also been "significant growth" in Ukraine and in Brazil.
  • Balatro passes 5m copies sold as developer implores fans to play more indie games

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• EA Origin to shut down in April 2025
    • EA Origin will shut down on April 17, 2025, as a result of Microsoft no longer supporting 32-bit software.
    • Players that use Origin will need to download and install the EA app, available from EA's website, which requires a 64-bit version of Windows.
  • Days Gone studio says it 'still plans on creating cool sh*t' after Sony pulls plug on latest game
  • Tekken boss says he just wants to "meet more people" as fans spot LinkedIn post looking for work
    • Katsuhiro Harada claims he is not actually looking for work but uses the feature to network and make new friends in the industry.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Call of Duty: Black Ops 6 bans another 136,000 cheaters
  • Tomb Raider 4 - 6 Remastered restores unused dialogue (and adds QOL features) to Angel of Darkness
    • Tomb Raider 4 - 6 Remastered will restore Kurtis Trent's ability to wield the Chirugai in-game. He will also be able to project a psychic shield around himself, shoot around corners while making his way about in stealth mode, and sprint like Lara does.
    • The remaster team has restored some unused voice lines from Lara Croft and Kurtis. These lines will be able to provide players with "more context" about what is going on, which should help them "better understand the story".
  • Pokémon TCG Pocket gets trading later this month, but rules will limit what you can swap
    • Trades can only be done between friends and only with cards of the same rarity
    • The Pokémon Company also stated that only certain cards from both the Genetic Apex and Mythical Island booster packs will be available to trade. "We are planning to continue to expand the selection of booster packs that you can trade from," it said.
    • Cards with a rarity of 1-4 diamond and 1 star can be traded. This excludes both Ex cards (generally the rarest of all) and Promo cards found in events.

Business

  • Poppy Playtime developer Mob Entertainment is suing Google for failing to remove "scam" apps from the Google Play store
    • Poppy Playtime's creators accuse Daigo Game 2020 Inc. of releasing "scam" apps intentionally advertised with names like Poppy Playtime: Chapter 3 and Poppy Playtime: Chapter 4 well before the instalments had been officially released for mobile devices.
  • Genshin Impact maker agrees to pay $20m in FTC case accusing it of violating children's privacy and deceiving players
    • Allegedly hid true cost of rare loot box items
  • Dragon Age: The Veilguard game director Corinne Busche leaves BioWare
  • Winking Studios announces proposed acquisition of Mineloader
    • The total consideration amounts to £16.3 million, comprising an upfront payment of £14.7 million and a deferred balance dependent on performance over the next five years.
    • The proposal is expected to be completed in the second quarter of 2025.
    • Mineloader is a shanghai-based outsourcing studio responsible for projects such as Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora, Star Wars Outlaws and various other AAA titles.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Here’s where you can play Switch 2 in your city [Nintendo Switch 2 Pre-Release Tour]
    • North America
      • New York – April 4-6
      • Los Angeles – April 11-13
      • Dallas – April 25-27
      • Toronto – April 25-27
    • Europe
      • Paris – April 4-6
      • London – April 11-13
      • Milan – April 25-27
      • Berlin – April 25-27
      • Madrid – May 9-11
      • Amsterdam – May 9-11
    • Oceania
      • Melbourne – May 10-11
    • Asia
      • Tokyo (Makuhari) – April 26-27
      • Seoul – May 31 – June 1
      • Hong Kong – TBD
      • Taipei – TBD
  • Nintendo patent attorney clarifies company's zero-tolerance position on emulation
    • Nintendo's stance: Emulation is legal, circumventing copy protection / game verification and using console bios / frontends is not (copyrighted material)

Business

  • Soul Assembly cuts 15% of workforce
    • Soul Assembly is a UK VR/XR Game Studio know for Just Dance VR and various Warhammer 40K licensed titles
    • Employees across its QA, marketing, and animation departments have been affected.

Rumors

  • Sony cancels two more live service games, including one based on God of War
    • Bluepoint were working on a live service God of War game, Bend were working on an unannounced live service game

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Animal Crossing collaboration coming to Sea Life aquariums across the UK
    • The event will allow guests to "discover incredible marine life alongside some of the game's characters" as they explore the 'Sea Life Islands', meet Tom Nook and Isabelle, and collect character stamps.
    • There will also be a fossil dig to reveal the ancestors of marine life, as well as a search for Gulliver who washed up on the island's beaches.
    • Locations:
      • Sea Life Manchester: 8th Feb – 9th March 2025
      • Sea Life Birmingham: 12th April - 5th May 2025
      • Sea Life Brighton: 24th May - 15th June 2025
      • Sea Life London: 30th June - 27th July 2025
      • Sea Life Chessington World of Adventures: 1st – 31st August 2025
  • Nintendo under fire once again for omitting original devs from remaster credits
    • Retro Studios once again excluded from credits, this time from Donkey Kong Country Returns HD
  • Nintendo Switch 2 Announced
    • Nintendo Direct planned for 02/04/2025

Free Games / Subscription

  • January’s PlayStation Plus Game Catalog and Classics titles announced
    • PlayStation Plus Extra and Premium | Game Catalog
      • God of War Ragnarök | PS4/5
      • Like a Dragon Gaiden: The Man Who Erased His Name | PS4/5
      • Atlas Fallen: Reign Of Sand | PS5
      • SD Gundam Battle Alliance | PS4/5
      • Sayonara Wild Hearts | PS4
      • ANNO: Mutationem | PS4/5
      • Orcs Must Die 3 | PS4/5
      • Citizen Sleeper | PS4/5
      • Poker Club | PS4
    • PlayStation Plus Premium | PS VR2 & Classics
      • Indiana Jones and the Staff of Kings | PS4/5
      • Medievil 2 | PS4, PS5

Business

  • Microsoft makes layoffs across several divisions including gaming
    • A spokesperson for the company reportedly said the layoffs were small in number but didn’t confirm a figure.
    • The latest round of layoffs are separate from performance-based job cuts Microsoft made across departments last week.
  • Baldur's Gate 3 exceeds 100m mod downloads

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 15/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 15 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Suicide Squad’s final content update has been released
    • Rocksteady ends major support for Suicide Squad less than a year after its release
  • The Sims 4 marking series' 25th anniversary with "huge" free update adding 70+ new items
    • Plus, MySims bundle coming to PC

Free Games / Subscription

  • A new Xbox Game Pass day one release has been confirmed
    • Lonely Mountains: Snow Riders will be available at launch next week (Jan 21st)

Business

  • GOG bolsters game preservation efforts by joining European game archivist organisation
    • Says this is "a significant step in bridging the private sector with cultural organisations"
  • Space Ape Games launches offshoot studio NextBeat
    • NextBeat will handle the BeatStar franchise and spin out 'more experiences, games and apps that showcase the inspiration and creativity of our artist partners.'

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Sega trademarks could point to the return of another classic series; Ecco the Dolphin
    • The publisher filed trademarks for ‘Ecco’ and ‘Ecco the Dolphin’ in Japan on December 27, 2024.
    • Unclear at this time if this is SEGA protecting old IP or a new entry is planned
  • Fortnite will soon let Godzilla stomp all over its map
    • In addition to a battle pass skin, Godzilla will be in the main battle royale map as a giant roaming boss
    • Hatsune Miku is planned for the Music Festival mode
  • Nintendo delays public release of Nintendo Alarmo
    • In a post on social media, Nintendo revealed Alarmo will remain a timed exclusive for those Nintendo Switch Online members until "early" March. "After that, it will be available to buy from selected retailers in certain countries"
  • Powerwash Simulator abandons VR Version to support "Job Security"
    • Team working on VR version have been moved to other areas of the business instead of making layoffs.
    • The game will remain available on VR storefronts, though it will no longer receive any further updates, support or DLC.
    • Non VR versions of Powerwash Simulator are uneffected.

Business

  • Robocraft 2 developer Freejam shuts down

Rumors

  • Nintendo Switch 2 revealed planned for January 16
    • Another Switch 2 rumour, so please take with a grain of salt.
    • The current rumour states: Reveal of event Jan 15th, reveal (hardware only) Jan 16th, planned release May / June.
    • Unlike many, many other rumours about Switch 2, this one has significant backing from many people from across the game news sphere. Still a rumour until confirmed otherwise though.
    • Many announcements for other things, such as the Nine Inch Nails world tour have been pushed back due to the fires in LA. Unclear at this time if the same will happen here (or if they'll be used as an excuse if the rumoured dates pass with no announcements)
  • Godfall dev Counterplay Games has quietly closed down
    • Counterplay was reportedly co-developing an unannounced project with Jackalyptic Games it was 'disbanded' sometime last year. Anonymous employee at Jackalytpic claimed via LinkedIn that the partnership ended as Counterplay closed its doors.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• House of the Dead 2: Remake release window finally revealed
    • Coming to Nintendo Switch, Xbox and PS platforms, Steam and GOG in Spring 2025
    • Online reception blasts the fact it looks like a "fake mobile game you see in movies"
  • Sleeping Dogs film adaptation canned, says Star Wars' Donnie Yen
  • Games Done Quick has raised over $54 million for charity with AGDQ 2025 bringing in $2.5 million

Business

  • Lords of the Fallen publisher CI Games publicly shuns "DEI", says it won't "embed social or political agendas"
    • "Our games are developed to maximise player enjoyment and commercial success."
  • Infinity Nikki generates $16m in player spending on mobile
    • Revenue reportedly driven by players in China who contributed $10.1 million making up 63% of global player spending.
    • Infinity Nikki made $10 million in its first two weeks, reaching $16 million on January 3.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Ubisoft has delayed Assassin’s Creed Shadows again ‘to incorporate player feedback’
    • Shadows, which was previously delayed from last November to February 2025, will now be released on Match 20
  • Six minutes of Transformers: Reactivate gameplay footage leaks online
  • Game Boy is the next Nintendo console officially getting the Lego treatment
    • Releasing October 2025
  • A Wallace and Gromit collab is coming to Powerwash Simulator
    • Expected to release in March
  • Xbox Developer Direct returns this month with ‘a surprise studio’
    • January 23rd 10am PT / 6pm GMT

Free Games / Subscription

  • Amazon reveals 16 titles heading to Prime Gaming in January
    • Including Zombie Army 4 and BioShock 2

Business

  • Hitman World of Assassination reaches over 75m players

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• McDonalds launches Sonic 3 themed Happy Meals in the UK today
    • McDonalds Japan is currently running an extensive Evangelion campaign
  • Minecraft YouTuber to present new Classic FM video game music series
    • Next Level with DanTDM launches on Saturday 11th January at 9pm for an hour, and will run each week until Easter.
    • The first episode will focus on legendary soundtracks from games such as The Legend of Zelda, Final Fantasy 7, The Elder Scrolls, and of course Minecraft.
  • Final Fantasy 7 director would ‘love’ another movie based on the game

Business

  • Squid Game: Unleashed reaches over 10m installs
    • Battle royale multiplayer has become Netflix's fifth most downloaded game
    • For launch, is temporarily free to all (even those without a Netflix account), though this is only the case for a limited period
    • Most of Squid Game: Unleashed's downloads are from Apple's App Store at 7.4 million installs, with 3.3 million from Google Play.
    • The United States was the top country for installs at 1.8 million, followed by Brazil with 1.1 million downloads.
  • Plaion CEO Klemens Kundratitz steps down 31 years after co-founding Koch Media
    • Deputy CEO Phil Rogers will assume leadership
  • Splash Damage confirms layoff plans as Transformers game is cancelled
    • The London-based studio behind Gears Tactics, Wolfenstein: Enemy Territory and Dirty Bomb announced the news on Wednesday in an X post.
    • Unclear which party decided to end development on Transformers: Reactive
    • Undisclosed amount of people now "at risk of redundancy"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Horrific abuse of workers at Indonesian external development studio Brandoville revealed in new People Make Games video
    • Brandoville is a company used for art and animation support in games such as Age of Empires 4 and Assassin's Creed Shadows.
    • At the centre of the video is Christa Sydney, who worked at the company from 2019 until 2024 and says she was subjected to mental and physical abuse by commissioner Kwan Cherry Lai, wife of Brandoville CEO Ken Lai, who ran day-to-day operations.

Free Games / Subscription

  • January’s PlayStation Plus Essential games are now available
    • Suicide Squad: Kill the Justice League (PS5), Need for Speed Hot Pursuit Remastered (PS4) and The Stanley Parable: Ultra Deluxe (PS4/5)
  • Xbox Game Pass titles for January
    • Arriving:
      • Road 96 (Cloud, Console, and PC) – January 7
      • Lightyear Frontier (Game Preview) (Xbox Series S/X) – January 8
      • My Time at Sandrock (Console) – January 8
      • Robin Hood – Sherwood Builders (Xbox Series S/X) – January 8
      • Rolling Hills (Console) – January 8
      • EA Sports UFC 5 (Cloud and Xbox Series S/X) EA Play – January 14
      • Diablo (PC) – January 14
    • Leaving (Jan 15th):
      • Common’Hood (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Escape Academy (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Exoprimal (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Figment (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Insurgency Sandstorm (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Those Who Remain (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Business

  • Rocksteady cut further jobs at the end of 2024
    • It's unclear how many people were impacted by this second redundancy round in just three months
  • Halo Infinite lead’s new company says it’s in trouble
    • Jar of Sparks, the studio founded in 2022 by Xbox veteran Jerry Hook in partnership with publisher NetEase, says it is halting development on its debut game in the hope of securing new funding.
  • Court documents reveal Call of Duty games cost ‘up to $700 million’ to develop
    • Three games budgets have been revealed in the document:
      • Black Ops 3 - Three year dev time, "over $450 million in development costs over the game’s lifecycle"
      • 2007 Modern Warfare - “Infinity Ward developed the game over several years and has spent over $640 million in development costs throughout the game’s lifecycle.”
      • Black Ops Cold War - “Treyarch and Raven Software took years to create the game with a team of hundreds of creatives. They ultimately spent over $700 million in development costs over the game’s lifecycle.”

Rumors

  • Former Annapurna Interactive staff reportedly taking over Private Division portfolio following Take-Two sale
    • According to the rumour, Private Division's buyer was Texas-based private equity firm Haveli Investments. Haveli reportedly then struck a deal with ex-Annapurna employees to fund their new company and place them in charge of distributing the former Private Division titles. These are include the Kerbal Space Program series, upcoming Lord of the Rings farming game Tales of the Shire, and Pokémon developer Game Freak's new action-adventure IP.
    • Bloomberg adds the new arrangement will likely result in a number of layoffs among the approximately 20 Private Division employees Haveli inherited as part of its purchase, although specifics are reportedly still to be finalised.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Ghost of Tsushima is getting an anime adaptation
    • Co-production between Aniplex and Crunchyroll
    • Series will debut in 2027
  • Two more live-action films based on PlayStation games (Helldivers 2 and Horizon: Zero Dawn) have been announced
    • Columbia Pictures and PlayStation Productions are at the early stages of developing Horizon: Zero Dawn
    • Sony Pictures and PlayStation Productions are working on Helldivers 2 movie
  • The first Tokyo Xtreme Racer in 18 years lands on Steam early access this month
    • Steam early access debut later this month, on 23rd January
  • Acer unveils dubiously portable Nitro Blaze 11 handheld gaming PC with 10.95-inch screen
    • Plus 8.8-inch Blaze 8
  • Meta stops selling Quest Pro headsets after two-year run
  • NVIDIA launches GeForce RTX 50 “Blackwell” series
    • GeForce RTX 5090 - $1,999
    • GeForce RTX 5080 - $999
    • GeForce RTX 5070 Ti - $749
    • GeForce RTX 5070 - $549 (Nvidia claims performance equivalent to 4090 - most likely via DLSS)
  • Nvidia is bringing a native GeForce Now app to Steam Deck

Business

  • Shift Up (Stellar Blade Dev) Is Doing So Well It’s Given All 311 Staff a PS5 Pro and $3,400 Bonus
  • Tencent Designated as a Chinese Military Company by US
    • The designation itself does not carry any legal consequences such as sanctions, but inclusion on the list is not ideal for companies looking to conduct business in the United States.
  • Dynasty Warrior M servers to shut just 15 months after launch
    • Dynasty Warriors M will end its service on February 20th, 2025
  • WWE 2K23 gets 'final takedown' after 2K discontinues online service
  • Sonic the Hedgehog movie franchise tops $1 billion at global box office

Other Notes

  • Don't forget, you can add the Game News Round-Up to your own Discord server by joining this server

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Awesome Games Done Quick 2025 has started
  • Marvel Rivals is adding four new characters
    • Fantastic Four characters; The Thing, Sue Storm, The Human Torch and Mr. Fantastic are set to be revealed today in a trailer drop at 8:00 AM PST / 16:00 UTC
    • Season 1 of Marvel Rivals will bring balance changes and is set to launch this weekend
  • Call of Duty: Black Ops 6 players lament "battle passes in between battle passes" with Squid Game event
  • Notch has "basically announced" a spiritual successor to Minecraft

Business

  • Resident Evil 4 Remake has sold over 9 million units worldwide

Rumors

  • Images of Joycons for Nintendo Switch 2 appear online
    • According to Internet sleuths, the difference between this rumour compared to previous rumours is the fact that the serial number on the images of these controllers can be validated with a Warranty Check on the Nintendo Technical Support website
  • Cloud Imperium Games undergoes Major Shake-up To Hit Deadlines
    • According to the rumour, several top-level executives have been laid off, and other top people at the company have been shifted to different roles

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Season 1 of Marvel Rivals includes the Fantastic Four
    • Trailer releases January 6th 8:00 AM PST / 16:00 UTC
  • Atari announces GameStation Go handheld

Business

  • Tango Gameworks rebrands following Krafton acquisition
    • Now known as Tango Gameworks Inc.
    • The only IP that went with them in the deal is Hi-Fi Rush.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/01/2025
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 January 2025.
item.content:encoded:
• Team17 co-founder Martyn Brown has passed away
  • Nintendo Music adds Mario 64 tunes
  • Over 25 years of classic Halo content has leaked online
    • The leaked content appears to have originated from a Halo Studios collaboration with fan modders, which aimed to restore cut content from past Halo titles, including the Halo 2 E3 2003 demo released last month.
    • The mod team Digsite had been working with 343 since last summer – allegedly for no pay – to restore content, including multiplayer maps originally developed for the PC port of Halo Combat Evolved, and cut content from classic Halo games.
    • Includes the original Halo reveal beta, when Halo was that a third person game

Free Games / Subscription

  • January’s PlayStation Plus Essential Games announced
    • Suicide Squad: Kill the Justice League, Need for Speed Hot Pursuit, and The Stanley Parable: Ultra Deluxe will be available from January 7 for PlayStation Plus members.

Business

  • Sumo Group CEO Carl Cavers receives OBE in New Year's Honours list
    • For services to the video games industry
  • Stardew Valley has topped 41 million lifetime sales
  • SNK opens new studio to 'evolve' fighting game franchises
  • PlayStation’s boss says it’s open to reviving classic franchises
    • The platform holder intends to offer a broad range of experiences from both its first-party studios, and by working with external developers.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Cyberpunk 2077 characters now available in Fortnite
    • For a limited time, grab female V and Johnny Silverhand (Keanu's second appearance in Fortnite) in a bundle
    • Other items such as the mantis blades and a vehicle inspired by Cyberpunk available

Business

  • Sega says it’s weighing up launching its own game subscription service
  • Sega isn’t planning more mini consoles: ‘We are not a retro company’
    • “We really appreciate our legacy, we value it, but at the same time, we want to deliver something new – otherwise we’ll become history. That’s not what we’re aiming for.”
  • Aonic secures over €150m in funding
    • Aonic has received a €152 million investment after entering an agreement with Metric Capital Partners and Active Ownership.
    • Metric Capital Partners have provided €100 million of the funding, while the remaining €52 million has been converted from Active Ownership's shareholder loan.

Other Notes

  • Thats a wrap on the Game News Round-Up 2024! From me and the rest of the Game News Round-Up team, thank-you for being a part of this little thing we've made and we hope you continue to enjoy the Game News Round-Up in the new year and beyond. 2025 is set to be an insane year for video-games (new hardware, massive games like GTA6 and other surprises on the way!), so I hope you all continue to enjoy the wild world of game news with us!

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• The year's Steam Replay reveals 85% of Steam users in 2024 spent time playing games released in 2023 or earlier
  • XDefiant's final patch includes content that would have released in future seasons if it hadn't been cancelled
    • The content itself includes three new factions, including the Assassin Creed's crossover promised a little while back. The Wolves and Omega Force factions are also available, even though they were supposed to release in seasons four and five, respectively.
    • 13 new maps, including a Rayman map that was going to be part of an epic event takeover in Season 4. It even has it’s own mode with Lum Hunt
  • Fully playable Wii build of Star Wars: Battlefront 3 Wii build leaks online
    • Reportedly found on a Wii test kit discovered at an e-waste recycling center and lovingly restored by fan group Free Radical Archive, the Star Wars: Battlefront 3 build is dated just a week before work on the game came to a halt. As such, it’s more than eight months further along in the development timeline than an Xbox 360 prototype that got leaked in 2016.

Free Games / Subscription

  • Wizard of Legend is free on Epic Game Store today
    • Epic's annual free-game-a-day begins today

Business

  • Hothead Games has shut down after almost 20 years
    • The Vancouver-based studio was established in 2006 by former Radical Entertainment staffers Steve Bocska, James Ceraldi and Joel DeYoung. Initially, Hothead focused on developing and publishing digital console and PC titles, but eventually pivoted to focus on creating free-to-play mobile games (with mixed results).
  • Glen Schofield says EA recently turned down his Dead Space 4 proposal

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Helldivers 2 developers respond to criticism of Killzone 2 crossover cost
    • Arrowhead gifting planned second wave of collaboration items for free.
  • Lost Soul Aside launches in 2025, nine years after Devil May Cry and Final Fantasy 15 inspired action game's reveal
  • ZeniMax will pivot Elder Scrolls Online to seasonal content in 2025
    • After eight annual expansions, Elder Scrolls Online is shifting to a seasonal structure so ZeniMax Online has a lighter load and can launch content when it's ready.

Business

  • Sony is now Kadokawa’s largest shareholder
    • Sony is now Kadokawa's largest shareholder, but it has not acquired the company.
  • Tencent removes directors from Epic Games after Justice Dept. inquiry
    • The Justice Department voiced concerns that Tencent was being anti-competitive by appointing its own directors to Epic, which it only has a minority stake in

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Crush 40 singer sues Sega over Sonic Adventure 2's 'Live and Learn' theme song
    • Frontman Johnny Gioeli claims Sega has spent 23 years using the hit song 'Live & Learn' in dozens of media outside Sonic Adventure 2 without properly compensating him for it. As he is not a gamer, he was unaware of its continued use in Sonic media. A fan raised the issue to him.
    • Sonic 3 (the movie) is unaffected as a deal was agreed for its usage there
    • Johnny Gioeli apparently owns the master recording and composition rights of the song
  • The Game Awards breaks viewership record with 154 million livestreams
    • Up 31% year-on-year
  • The Sims creator Will Wright discusses his forthcoming AI life sim Proxi
    • Proxi is a dreamlike life sim where players seemingly train an AI to create worlds from their memories. Players record a memory through text, known as a "mem", which the game interprets and forms into an animated scene using assets created by the developers (and eventually players too).
    • These scenes can then be edited by players to tweak the details. Then players "train your Proxi's mind with each new memory" by placing them on a memory timeline and mindmap to create connections.

Business

  • Activision quietly recast Black Ops 6 Zombies actors during SAG-AFTRA strike
    • Pre-release versions of Call of Duty: Black Ops 6 Zombies cutscenes contain different voiceover performances than those heard in the final game.
    • Black Ops 6 isn't a "struck" production as work on it began before the strike.
  • Moon Beast Productions nets $4.5 million to 'redefine' ARPGs
    • The San Francisco studio was formed by a pair of former Diablo leads in 2021
    • The investment round was led by 1AM Gaming with backing from 1UP Ventures, The Mini Fund, Overwolf, Versus Ventures, and a network of angel investors such as Gaingels, Cohh Carnage, and Mark Pincus.
  • 11 Bit Studios cuts staff after canceling console-focused 'Project 8' game
    • The Frostpunk 2 creator said Project 8 had too many 'unresolved issues and challenges' to continue development, which initially started back in 2018

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• James Wan is adapting indie horror Pacific Drive for TV
  • Jingle Jam raises £2.7 million after impressive 2024 fundraising event
    • Over 800 creators raised money for the likes of War Child, Campaign Against Living Miserably (CALM), Autistica, and Sarcoma UK
  • Marvel Rivals Winter Celebration features Splatoon-esque arcade mode starring Jeff the Land Shark
    • Cross-progression is also in the works
  • Amazon renews video game anthology series Secret Level for Season 2
    • Amazon said Secret Level enjoyed the most-watched animated series debut of all time for Prime Video within its first week
    • Unofficial data from insights company Luminate said the show was viewed for 155.3 million minutes in the US during its launch week, which works out at about 1.4 million views based on its runtime
  • Dungeons & Dragons: Dark Alliance is being delisted
    • Servers will be shutdown on the 2nd Feb, 2025
    • Released in June 2021 for Xbox One, Xbox Series S/X, PS4/5 and PC. It was also made available to Xbox Game Pass subscribers at launch.
  • Elden Ring Nightreign can be played in 3-player co-op or single player, but not as a duo

Business

  • Marvel Rivals hits 20 million players

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• League of Legends skins won't get custom VO while SAG-AFTRA strikes
    • The video game voice actors strike is entering its fifth month, and Riot Games is making "temporary changes" to how it handles voices for in-game Leage of Legends skins. These changes come months after SAG-AFTRA called for Formosa Interactive, the popular MOBA's voiceover studio, to be struck back in September.
    • Since League's PC version is a struck title, union actors aren't allowed to record lines for it while the strike is active. As such, skins for champions with actors based in the United States will use already-recorded "base voiceovers (VO)" rather than lines done by different actors. When the strike ends, the studio will update those affected skins with new lines from their original actors "as soon as scheduling and availability will allow."

Business

  • Dark Passenger raises $3 million to fund debut multiplayer game
    • Dark Passenger is made up of several ex-CD Projekt Red developers, who previously worked on The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt and Cyberpunk 2077. Other staff hail from fellow Warsaw-based studios, such as People Can Fly, Reikon, and Flying Wild Hog.
  • Elden Ring has now sold 28.6m copies, outselling the Dark Souls series combined prior to release
    • The game has sold an additional 3.6m copies since the release of Shadow of the Erdtree

Rumors

  • Dbrand has posted what it claims to be an image of Switch 2 inside its latest case
    • According to industry chatter, the reveal of the Switch 2 may have been moved to January to combat these "leaks" from accessory manufacturers.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Ciri has been recast in The Witcher 4
    • Ciara Berkeley has been cast as Ciri in The Witcher 4, replacing the characters' previous actor
  • Netflix’s Squid Game: Unleashed will be free for everyone at launch
    • For a limited time, Squid Game: Unleashed won't be locked behind a subscription paywall
    • Developed by internal studio Boss Fight Entertainment, Squid Game: Unleashed is a “party royale” game.
  • Steel Paws is a new mobile game from Yu Suzuki

Business

  • Sega’s president calls Sonic Superstars a ‘disappointment’
    • “This time we have Sonic X Shadow Generations and the next Sonic movie, so we’re pushing things in the transmedia direction – movies and games working in harmony to make things bigger. But the core [of the experience] is the game. The game needs to be very good or else there is no transmedia effect.”
  • Zenimax Online Studios workers unionise
    • Union of 461 workers has been recognised by Microsoft
    • The union, called ZOS United-CWA, has been recognised by parent company Microsoft and is made of 461 members, including "web developers, designers, engineers, and graphic artists," the announcement said.
    • A majority of the unionised workers are based in Maryland, but they are also located across the US in California, New York, Pennsylvania, Texas, and Wisconsin.
    • This is the latest union to be created via CWA's Campaign to Organize Digital Employees (CODE-CWA), which the organisation says has seen over 6,000 workers unionise since 2020.
  • Capcom says it’s working on ‘re-activating other dormant IPs’ like Onimusha and Okami

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
Highlights

  • Co-op standalone game Elden Ring Nightreign announced
    • Releases 2025 for PlayStation 4/5, Xbox Series and PC
  • Ico and Shadow of the Colossus creator Fumito Ueda reveals his next game, Project: Robot
    • Final title to be confirmed
    • Veteran team of Ico, Shadow of the Colossus and The Last Guardian team members
  • Onimusha Way of the Sword coming in 2026
    • First Onimusha game since 2006
    • Developed by Capcom for PS5, Xbox Series S/X and PC
  • Clive (Final Fantasy XVI) is the next Tekken 8 DLC Character
    • 72 Hour Early Access December 16th
  • Hideki Kamiya’s new studio, Clovers, is working with Capcom on an Okami sequel
  • Naughty Dog’s next game, Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet, has been announced
    • Music by Trent Reznor and Atticus Ross
