    • Directed by Neil Druckmann
    • No release date
  • Mafia: The Old Country gets Summer 2025 Release Date and new Trailer
  • Sonic Racing CrossWorlds has been announced
    • No release date
    • PS4/5, Xbox One / Series S/X, Switch, Steam, Epic Game Store
  • Overcooked studio’s next game is Stage Fright, published by Hello Games
    • Warframe 1999 expansion out today
  • A new third-person Turok game, Turok Origins, is in development
    • Turok Origins is planned for release on PS5, Steam and Xbox Series S/X
  • New Virtua Fighter game announced
    • Developed by Ryu Ga Gotoku Studio
  • Ryo Ga Gotoku reveals its latest game, Project Century
  • Gearbox has shown off the first Borderlands 4 gameplay trailer
    • Releases in 2025
    • Planned for release on PS5, Xbox Series S/X, Steam and Epic Game Store
  • Wargaming announces Steel Hunters, a free-to-play mech hero shooter
  • First The Outer Worlds 2 gameplay debuted
    • Due 2025 on Xbox Series, PC, and PlayStation 5
  • It Takes Two studio Hazelight’s latest co-op game, Split Fiction officially revealed
    • Releases March 6
  • Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth is releasing on PC January 23rd
  • The Witcher 4 unveiled at The Game Awards
    • Game focuses on Ciri
    • The footage was rendered using an “unannounced RTX GPU”.
  • The Last of Us Part 2 Remastered is coming to PC April 3rd
  • Thick as Thieves is the next game from Deus Ex and Thief leads’ studio OtherSide
    • Set for release 2026 for PC, Xbox Series S/X and PS5
    • OtherSide is led by Warren Spector (Deus Ex), Greg LoPiccolo (Thief) and Paul Neurath (Thief)
  • Pac-Man set to star in Metroidvania Shadow Labyrinth based on the Pac-Man Secret Level episode
    • Set for release in 2025 for Nintendo Switch, PS5, Xbox Series S/X, and Steam
  • Infinity Nikki surpasses 20 million downloads
  • Dave the Diver: In the Jungle announced
  • Sifu developer's (Sloclap) next game is a football game called Rematch
    • Rematch will be a 5v5 online multiplayer game where players take to the pitch in third-person
    • Release date set for summer 2025 for Steam, PS5 and Xbox Series S/X

Game Awards

  • Game of the Year: Astro Bot
    • Best Game Direction: Astro Bot
    • Best Narrative: Metaphor: ReFantanzio
    • Best Art Direction: Metaphor: ReFantanzio
    • Best Score & Music: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth
    • Best Audio Design: Senua’s Saga: Hellblade 2
    • Best Performance: Melina Juergens, Senua’s Saga: Hellblade 2
    • Innovation In Accessibility: Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown
    • Games for Impact: Neva
    • Best Ongoing Game: Helldivers 2
    • Best Community Support: Baldur's Gate 3
    • Best Independent Game: Balatro
    • Best Debut Indie Game: Balatro
    • Best Mobile Game: Balatro
    • Best VR/AR Game: Batman: Arhkam Shadow
    • Best Action Game: Black Myth: Wukong
    • Best Action/Adventure Game: Astro Bot
    • Best RPG: Metaphor: ReFantazio
    • Best Fighting Game: Tekken 8
    • Best Family Game: Astro Bot
    • Best Sim/Strategy Game: Frostpunk 2
    • Best Sports/Racing Game: EA Sports FC 25
    • Best Multiplayer Game: Helldivers 2
    • Best Adaptation: Fallout
    • Most Anticipated Game: Grand Theft Auto VI
    • Content Creator of the Year: CaseOh
    • Best Esports Game: League of Legends
    • Best Esports Athlete: Faker
    • Best Esports Team: T1

Other News

  • 99 jobs cut at Warner Bros Games Montreal
    • Given 8 weeks notice
    • Unconfirmed rumours are circulating that the layoffs primarily impact subcontractors working in the Keywords QA team (hired through Keywords), though Warner Bros nor Keywords have responded publicly about the news at this time
  • Ex-Bungie dev Chris Barrett sues Bungie and Sony for "falsely destroying his reputation"
    • Lawsuit asserts Barrett was terminated to avoid $45m payment
  • Pokémon Trading Card Game Pocket surpasses 60m downloads
  • Epic Games announces deal with Telefónica
    • Partnership will see Epic Games Store pre-installed on Telefónica Android devices in the UK, Spain, Germany, Spanish-speaking Latam.
  • Free Balatro DLC Friends of Jimbo 3 is out now
    • Features face cards from Shovel Knight, Divinity: Original Sin 2, Warframe, Don't Starve, 1000xResist, Enter The Gungeon, Cult of the Lamb and Potion Craft: Alchemist Simulator

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Pokémon partners with Wallace and Gromit animation studio Aardman
    • Project releases in 2027

Free Games / Subscription

  • Tetris (NES) and Tetris DX (GBC) are now on Switch Online
  • The Epic Games Store will give away 16 free games over Christmas
    • Starts December 19th
  • December’s PlayStation Plus Game Catalog and Classics titles revealed
    • Extra and Premium
      • Sonic Frontiers | PS4/5
      • Forspoken | PS5
      • Rabbids: Party of Legends | PS4
      • WRC Generations | PS4/5
      • F.I.S.T.: Forged In Shadow Torch | PS4/5
      • Jurassic World Evolution 2 | PS4/5
      • Coffee Talk | PS4/5
      • Coffee Talk Episode 2: Hibiscus & Butterfly | PS4/5
      • A Space for the Unbound | PS4/5
      • Phogs | PS4
      • Biped | PS4/5
    • Premium
      • Star Wars: Tales from the Galaxy’s Edge | PS VR2

Business

  • Nintendo now owns 100% of Xenoblade studio Monolith Soft

Other Notes

  • Tonight is The Game Awards. There will be a special edition Game News Round-Up after the show, as is tradition. I hope you look forward to it!

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Cyberpunk 2077 update adds new customization and photo mode features
  • Fortnite commits to Rock Band-like Festival musical mode support through "2025 and beyond"
    • After Rocket Racing mode placed on back burner
    • Festival celebrates its first anniversary this weekend with access to play all of the mode's 285 tracks for a limited time

Free Games / Subscription

  • December’s first PlayStation Plus Classics games have been released
    • Sly 2: Band of Thieves, Sly 3: Honor Among Thieves, and Jak and Daxter: The Precursor Legacy

Business

  • People Can Fly is ‘suspending or laying off’ 120 employees
    • People Can Fly announces job cuts and plans to scale back its self-publishing strategy
    • Project Victoria has been suspended, Project Bifrost's team has been downscaled and some PCF's support teams have been restructured.
  • Build A Rocket Boy acquires PlayFusion
    • PlayFusion CEO and CTO Mark Gerhard appointed co-CEO at Build A Rocket Boy
  • FTC starts issuing refunds to Epic Games users following settlement
    • Over $72 million will be sent in refunds, with the average payment being around $114

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Suicide Squad’s current season will be its last, offline mode coming this week
    • Final seasonal update launches January 2025
    • Long-promised offline mode releases Tuesday, December 10
  • Cyberpunk 2077 is getting a surprise update 2.2
    • The update will be detailed in a live stream today, which will be broadcast on Twitch and YouTube at 8am PT / 4pm GMT / 5pm CET.
    • Not related to New Game + or a PS5 Pro patch
    • The studio claimed previously that 2.1 would be the last significant patch for Cyberpunk 2077
  • Lego Fortnite is adding a new ‘social roleplay experience’ called Brick Life
    • Launching on December 12, it will let users explore a Lego city, build homes, socialise and work different jobs.
    • 32 player mode
  • Palworld ditches Pokéball-style summoning mechanic amid Nintendo legal battle
  • Bandai Namco's cancelled MMORPG Blue Protocol is coming back as Star Resonance
    • Bandai Namco's shuttered MMORPG Blue Protocol project has been extended a new lifeline courtesy of Tencent-funded developer Bokura
    • Some of the original development team has joined Bokura
    • As yet, there's no firm release date either in China, Japan, or beyond. Blue Protocol's servers are due to go dark on 18th January 2025.
    • A closed beta test is already planned for players on Android devices and PC, although sadly, that's only open to Chinese players for now.

Business

  • Capcom is holding a game development competition for Japanese students using its RE Engine
    • The Capcom Games Competition could lead to "commercialisation" of winning entries
  • The entire itch.io site was temporarily taken down by Funko
    • Funko was apparently using ‘brand protection software’ (BrandShield) which uses AI to flag a disputed page, and filed a “bogus” report with itch.io’s domain registrar.
    • The site is now back
  • Infinity Nikki has topped 10 million downloads in just four days
  • Marvel Rivals opening weekend sees 10m players join in

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Argonaut delays Croc return
    • Originally intended to release this month, it will now release in Q1 2025.
  • Evercade manufacturer announces partnership with SNK
    • Blaze will be releasing officially licensed Neo Geo products in 2025/2026.
  • Fortnite goes first-person for new 5v5 mode Ballistic
    • Ballistic arrives on Wednesday 11th December with one map - Skyline 10 - plus a limited number of weapons and items. These will all be expanded over time based on player feedback, Epic Games has said.
  • Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 release date brought forward
    • Will now release a week earlier (4th Feb 2025)
  • Like a Dragon devs open up on sharing source code, inspiring future programmers
    • 'Anyone can become a game programmer as long as they have learned the basics of programming,' said technical director Yukata Ito
  • Ananta announcement trailer released
    • Formally "Project Mugen"
    • Pre-Registrations currently at 6,732,942 users
  • The Game Awards 10th Anniversary This Thursday
    • 7:30pm ET / 4:30pm PT / 12:30am GMT
    • As per usual, there will be a special edition of the Game News Round-Up post-show

Business

  • Tantalus Keywords Australia CEO steps down
    • Tom Crago will remain in the industry as "a mentor and sometime-advisor" for Keywords

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• The Thing: Remastered lands surprise release date via Xbox website
    • If the Xbox website is to believed, the game is launching today
  • Sony showcases demo of PSVR2 controller-free hand tracking
    • Shown off at Siggraph Asia 2024
    • The demo states this feature "tracks the position and posture" of the users' hands, and the "library is available with the latest SDK of PlayStation 5".

Business

  • Friday the 13th studio IllFonic lays off staff as it 'realigns to a refined strategy'
    • The studio also worked on Killer Klowns from Outer Space
    • Unspecified number of layoffs
  • Deceive Inc studio Sweet Bandits shuts down
    • "After a long and difficult road of trying to get Deceive Inc in a state where it could thrive, we have reached the breaking point," co-founder says
  • Chivalry developer Torn Banner Studios lays off staff
    • All affected employees will receive severance packages and have been allowed to keep their work computers. Piggott added that the company is "working hard to help people find positions at other studios where we can."
    • The news follows the early access launch for the studio's latest game, zombie shooter No More Room In Hell 2. Despite the cuts, Piggott emphasised that the studio is "fully committed as we move forward with a full 1.0 release in 2025."
  • Smilegate makes strategic investment in Dan Houser's Absurd Ventures
    • Through the agreement, Smilegate - the company behind Amazon Game's MMORPG, Lost Ark - will work with Absurd Ventures' new IP. The company said it will "benefit from Houser and his team’s proven narrative, AAA game and business expertise in the Western market, while Absurd will benefit from Smilegate’s expertise in the Asian market."
  • Playgama raises $3 million to build global HTML5 platform ecosystem
    • With funding primarily from firms The Open Platform and s16vc, the company is hoping to get in HTML5's "renaissance" in games. In the announcement on December 3, it outlined its plans for its PaaS ecosystem, which will include a unified SDK to allow for publishing across multiple platforms, a QA tool that consolidates those platforms' requirements into a single list, and tools for monetization, marketing, and payments.
  • MyGames sells off hypercasual games publisher Mamboo
    • After years of using Mamboo to court hypercasual players, MyGames is putting its focus back on 'mid-core' releases.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 4 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
Free Games / Subscription

  • December’s PlayStation Plus Essential games are now live
    • It Takes Two, Aliens: Dark Descent and Temtem
  • New Xbox Game Pass titles for console, PC and Cloud revealed

Business

  • Ubisoft shutting studios, laying off 277 employees as it pulls plug on live-service shooter XDefiant
    • A month and a half after denying reports it was poised to pull the plug on its free-to-play live-service shooter XDefiant - which launched back in May - Ubisoft has confirmed it's doing just that, resulting in the closure of three production studios and - 277 employees losing their jobs
    • Studios closing: San Francisco, Osaka. "Ramping Down" Studios: Sydney
    • Season 3 will launch as planned and servers will remain online until 3rd June, 2025
  • Phasmophobia has sold 1m copies on consoles
    • Torn Banner Studios cuts staff after No More Room in Hell 2's EA launch: <https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/torn-banner-studios-cuts-staff-after-no-more-room-in-hell-2-s-ea-launch>
    • The developer affirmed it was committed to fixing No More Room in Hell 2's issues and growing the game into a full, 1.0 release for a 2025 launch

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Sony's PlayStation 30th anniversary PS5 update turns your console retro for a limited time
    • Your console will also show the classic Sony Computer Entertainment logo, followed by a 30th Anniversary screen, on startup - with accompanying PS1 startup sound.
    • System sounds / theme can be set to PS1, PS2, PS3 or PS4
    • Fans online are criticising the fact this is a limited time feature

Free Games / Subscription

  • Crash Team Racing Nitro-Fueled is heading to Game Pass this week
    • December 4th

Business

  • Project Party developer Lilith Games lays off 40 (out of 100) employees
    • AFK Arena studio almost halves headcount following lukewarm beta test of life sim Project Party
    • Lilith Games was founded in 2013 in Shanghai, China. Its first game, Soul Hunters, was released in 2014, and it expanded into publishing and game development in 2016. Lilith Games launched its own publishing brand, Farlight Games, back in 2022.
  • Vision of Mana director quits NetEase to join Square Enix
    • In a statement on X/Twitter, Yoshida thanked NetEase and his development team for their support, but confirmed he quit the Chinese megacorp at the end of October and will join Square Enix in December.
    • Whilst Yoshida had been employed at NetEase subsidiary Ouka Studios, Visions of Mana was published by Square Enix - the company he has now joined.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/12/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• UK counter-terrorism unit demands Steam withdraw controversial shooter from sale
    • Fursan al-Aqsa: The Knights of the Al-Aqsa Mosque describes itself as "address[ing] the Israel x Palestine conflict from a Palestinian perspective"
    • A counter-terrorism spokesperson said: "The CTIRU works closely with a range of technology, social media and online service providers, but we do not comment on specific content or any communication we may have with specific platforms or providers."
  • Fortnite Remix Finale event smashes 14m all-time concert record
    • 14.3 million people were in-game for Fortnite's Remix Finale event, according to the game's public player stats, while millions more were watching online via Twitch and YouTube.
    • The only other in-game event to ever get higher was the Marvel season finale featuring Galactus, again back during the lockdown era of 2020, when 15.3 million people were in-game.
  • Marvel Rivals confirms 5 more characters ahead of launch
    • Wolverine, Iron Fist, Black Widow, Cloak and Dagger, and Squirrel Girl will be added to the roster
    • All of the game’s heroes launch heroes, and any added at a later date, will be free, according to NetEase
    • Launches December 6th
  • Riot will now ban content creators for infringements even if they’re not committed in-game

Business

  • Star Citizen funding passes $750m
  • Dave the Diver sells 5m units

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Palworld announces major update, Terraria crossover despite ongoing legal issues
  • Plague Inc gets surprise sequel where you rebuild humanity following devastating zombie apocalypse
    • After Inc is available to install now on iOS and Android. It will be coming to Steam in 2025

Free Games / Subscription

  • Epic's latest free game now live and two more for next week officially revealed
    • Brotato is free to download right now until next Thursday, 5th December
    • Next week, Bus Simulator 21 and Lego Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga will be free to claim

Business

  • Scopely opens new studio (Mirai) in Saudi Arabia led by EA veteran Charity Joy
    • Joy worked at EA for three years, joining as franchise senior producer on EA Sports in 2021 before moving to Criterion as executive producer in 2022. She then became general manager in November 2023 before leaving in October 2024.
    • She also worked for The Walt Disney Company as production manager on Club Penguin in 2007. She went on to become creative director in 2014.
  • Codecks nets €1 million in funding for game dev project management tool
    • Codecks uses a card-and-deck system that lets developers to track project milestones and share decks with outside devs.
    • The tool is in use by companies such as Unity, 505 Games, and Devolver Digital.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Baldur’s Gate 3 will add cross-play, 12 subclasses and a photo mode next year

Free Games / Subscription

  • December’s PlayStation Plus Essential and Classic games announced
    • December’s Plus Essential games are It Takes Two (PS4/5), Aliens: Dark Descent (PS4/5) and Temtem (PS5). They’ll be available to all PlayStation Plus members from December 3 until January 6.
    • December’s additions to the Classics Catalog will include PS2 games Sly 2: Band of Thieves, Sly 3: Honor Among Thieves, and Jak and Daxter: The Precursor Legacy. They will be available from December 10.
  • Three more Mega Drive / Genesis games have been added to Switch Online
    • ToeJam & Earl: Panic on Funkotron, Vectorman, Mercs

Business

  • Mac Walters' Worlds Untold studio on "pause" as it seeks new funding partner
    • Mac Walters' Worlds Untold - which had been backed by NetEase - announced it was "pausing operations" until it can locate further funding.
    • NetEase said it remains committed to "a strong global presence" despite pulling funding from Worlds Untold, whose project it has praised.
  • Block Blast reaches 40m daily active players
    • Over 40 million active players every day and more than 150 million active players each month across the globe

Rumors

  • Balatro has made almost $4.4m from its mobile release alone
    • The feat has apparently been achieved without microtransactions
  • Leaked image suggests Valve's rumoured new Steam Controller taking design cues from Steam Deck

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• New Cyberpunk Netflix animation confirmed as 2077 tops 30 million sales
    • Live-action Cyberpunk project is still in the conceptual phase
  • Star Wars Hunters is coming to PC in 2025
    • A Steam playtest will be held from December 13-16, ahead of its Early Access release on January 27, 2025.

Business

  • CD Projekt Group Q3 Results
    • Sales revenue: Down 49% year-on-year to €52.8 million ($55.2 million)
    • Net profit: Down 61% year-on-year to €18.1 million ($19 million)
    • Cyberpunk 2077 to date has "topped" 30 million copies while Phantom Liberty has sold eight million units.
  • PlayStation veteran Shuhei Yoshida to retire in January
    • The firm's development leader to step down after 31 years
  • PS2 officially surpasses 160m sales, retaining its best-selling console crown
    • Another 10m units sold since 2011
  • Nintendo Switch eShop and online services are ending in China in 2026
    • On March 31, 2026, the Chinese Nintendo eShop will stop selling games and any other software. After this date, all software, including paid and free titles, will no longer be available to purchase or claim. On May 15, 2026, players will no longer be able to download titles or redeem codes. DLC will also become unavailable at this time.
    • In China, the Nintendo Switch is sold in collaboration with Tencent, the Chinese tech giant that owns dozens of developers and other tech firms.

Rumors

  • Ubisoft reportedly developing Animal Crossing-style social sim with Minecraft-like building
    • Former Gotham Knights creative director Patrick Redding said to be at the reins
    • Codename Alterra has been in the works for around 18 months. Alterra is said to have risen from the ashes of an earlier Ubisoft Montreal project, canceled after four years of development, with the two games supposedly sharing a focus on voxel creation.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• New Sonic the Hedgehog 3 trailer features beloved Chao Garden and Keanu's Shadow
  • 2K has removed its launcher from all PC games
    • BioShock, Mafia and Civilization publisher 2K has officially shut down its PC games launcher and removed the platform from all of its games.
    • Additionally, the publisher has also removed the “2K Launcher Beta”, a completely separate launcher, from Sid Meier’s Civilization V.
  • IO pulls Hitman content featuring Conor McGregor following court ruling
    • McGregor was last week ordered to pay $260,000 in damages after a jury at a civil trial at Ireland’s high court found that he’d assaulted Nikita Hand, who had accused him of raping her.
    • Hitman World of Assassination’s limited-time Elusive Target mission The Disruptor, which features the likeness and voiceover work of McGregor, was released this summer and returned again this month.
      • The mission, which was due to be available from November 15 until December 8, was also included as part of the free Hitman World of Assassination starter pack.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 25/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Virtua Fighter 5 is getting a Steam remaster called Virtua Fighter 5 R.E.V.O.
    • Remaster has 4K graphics and rollback netcode
    • No release date yet
  • Samsung launches free mobile cloud gaming platform for Galaxy devices
    • There will be 23 games available at launch, including free-to-play hits such as Monopoly Go, Candy Crush Saga, Homescapes and Honor of Kings.
    • All games will be freely available, with Samsung's vice president and head of game services Jong Woo telling GamesIndustry.biz: "Unlike other cloud services, there is no content gated behind a paywall and no monthly subscriptions for users to access these games via our cloud streaming tech."
    • Games are accessed via links distributed via Galaxy Store listings, Samsung Gaming Hub, or ads. The Galaxy Store, which comes pre-installed on all Samsung phones and tablets, will serve as the runtime client.

Business

  • CMA calls for investigation into Apple and Google's UK mobile market dominance
    • CMA may use its powers under the new Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act (DMCC) to examine possible anti-competitive behaviour from the two companies given their dominance of the UK's mobile markets.
    • However, it proposes that no further action be taken on cloud gaming given "primary concerns have been addressed".
    • Following a preliminary investigation, an independent inquiry group has recommended a review into the UK mobile ecosystem and assess if the two megacorps have Strategic Market Status (SMS) which potentially gives them a self-preferential position in the market.
  • Stalker 2 sells 1m copies in two days
    • The 1 million number does not include Game Pass players

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 22/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Guilty Gear Strive photo mode update makes fighters disappear when viewed at inappropriate angles
    • The game's rating on PS4 and PS5 was recently made higher in Japan (CERO B - 12+ to CERO C - 15+)
  • Rollercoaster Tycoon Classic is coming to Switch next month
    • The compilation includes Rollercoaster Tycoon 1 and 2
    • December 5 release date
  • Pac-Man Live Experience opening in Manchester next year
    • Set to open at Manchester's Arcade Arena on 22nd March 2025, the offering is described by its creators as a "multi-sensory adventure, seamlessly blending the real world with augmented reality". It's being put together by Little Lion Entertainment, which previously worked on live experiences for The Crystal Maze Live Experience and Tomb Raider.
    • Tickets are available now, in 30-minute slots, at Early Bird pricing. Adult tickets are £25. Child tickets are also £25. There's no word yet on how much the experience will cost once Early Bird pricing ends.
    • The experience is played in teams of eight, with two mazes operating concurrently. The full experience lasts 50 minutes, of which you should expect to spend around 30 minutes playing (though presumably less if the ghosts catch you).
  • Fortnite is now selling digital shoes for your character skin, with Nike Air Jordans costing around £7
  • Stardew Valley: Symphony of Seasons concert tour announced, coming to the UK in 2026
    • It will be in Glasgow on 10th February 2026, before heading to Manchester on 12th February and London on 14th February.
    • Features a 35 piece orchestra
  • AFK Journey named Google Play's Game of the Year
    • Meanwhile, Supercell racks up two wins with Clash of Clans and Squad Busters

Free Games / Subscription

  • Donkey Kong Land is now available on Nintendo Switch Online

Business

  • Itch wants asset creators to disclose when they've used generative AI
  • Lego and UNICEF team on game design toolbox prioritizing child safety
    • The RITEC Design Toolbox was conceived as a means for designers to 'ensure their products are aligned with best practices for children's well-being.'
  • Bandai Namco subsidiary Bandai Namco Online to be dissolved and merged with parent company
    • Following the closures of the short-lived Gundam Evolution and Blue Protocol, Bandai Namco is fully absorbing its online game-focused studio
    • Existing IP will transfer to Bandai Namco
    • It's unclear if staff will be transferred to the parent company along with its games.
    • Bandai Namco said the merger comes as a result of changes across the industry but expects to continue to deliver high quality games that "exceed" the expectations of fans like Idolish7, SD Gundam Operations, and Gundam Tribe.
    • The merger will complete on April 1, 2025.
  • Hyper Light Breaker dev Heart Machine lays off unknown number of staff
    • But studio says early access launch of upcoming open-world roguelite will not be impacted
  • Valve updates guidelines for season passes on Steam
    • Developers and publishers must commit to a release date for season pass content
    • As listed on Steamworks, developers and publishers must commit to a release date for season pass content.
    • It suggests if a developer is not "ready to clearly communicate" about launch timing, then a season pass shouldn't be offered on the platform.
    • Valve also stipulates that each piece of content in a season pass must have a detailed description of what's included.
    • For those ready to offer a season pass, release schedules can be detailed by quarter and year (such as January to March 2025), season, or precise dates.
    • A release date can only be rescheduled once if there is a delay, and so long as that date is "up to three months later than originally selected."

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Fortnite Crew will soon include OG, Lego and Music passes too
    • Doubles as confirmation that the upcoming permanent OG mode will have its own battle pass
  • CD Projekt says "no current plans" for Cyberpunk 2077 upgrade on PS5 Pro

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Cloud Gaming now lets players stream select games they own
    • 50 titles are currently supported and the feature is available on PC, phone, tablet, handheld, and most devices with access to a web browser.
    • Supported titles at launch include Baldur’s Gate 3, Cyberpunk 2077, Modern Warfare 2, Final Fantasy XIV, Hogwarts Legacy, The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt, Star Wars Outlaws and Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2.

Business

  • Unknown 9 developer Reflector Entertainment lays off 18% of staff
    • Studio says affected employees "aren't assigned to roles that our active projects require"
    • Reflector Entertainment is the developer behind Unknown 9: Awakening, the first in a series of action adventure games with a transmedia universe built around it. Other Unknown 9 outlets include a podcast audio drama, novels and a comic book series.
      • The studio landed Bandai Namco as publisher, with the Japanese company acquiring Reflector back in 2020 ahead of Unknown 9's release.
      • Unknown 9: Awakening was originally due for release this summer, but was delayed to October 18, 2024. It released to mixed reviews, and it unclear how well it has sold.
  • FromSoftware parent Kadokawa confirms buyout interest from Sony
    • Sony sent the firm a letter of intent to acquire its shares, but "no decision has been made at this time," CEO says
    • Rumours circulating online state that this is to prevent a hostile takeover from Kakao (a large South Korean company), who has been buying shares in Kadokawa since 2020.

Rumors

  • Kwalee lays off "at least 10" people
    • Rumour states most of the redundancies affect Kwalee's marketing team
    • Earlier this year, Kwalee laid off an unknown number of staff following a restructure to better align itself to "significant growth opportunities.". Kwalee confirmed that less than 40 people had lost their jobs, and clarified it was planning to grow its headcount from 346 workers last year to between 410 and 420 this year.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Final Fantasy 14 Mobile officially revealed
  • F-Zero X soundtrack added to Nintendo Music app
  • PlayStation Portal is getting cloud streaming today
    • PS Plus Premium members will be able to test cloud streaming of 120 PS5 games on the Portal
  • A new trailer for the Minecraft live-action movie has been released
  • Control 2 will be an action RPG, Remedy says
  • Minecraft theme park rights won by Alton Towers and Legoland owner Merlin
    • Minecraft-themed lands will appear in Merlin theme parks from 2026
    • The first attractions won’t be full-scale Minecraft theme parks, but will instead be included as themed areas in existing Merlin locations.

Free Games / Subscription

  • New Xbox Game Pass titles for console, PC and Cloud announced
    • Arriving:
      • Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024 (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S|X) – November 19 on Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
      • Stalker 2: Heart of Chornobyl (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S|X) – November 20 on Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
      • Little Kitty, Big City (Console) – November 20. Now with Game Pass Standard
      • PlateUp! (Console) – November 20. Now with Game Pass Standard
      • Nine Sols (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S|X) – November 26 on Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
      • Aliens: Dark Descent (Cloud, Console, and PC) – November 27 on Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass, Game Pass Standard
    • Leaving:
      • Conan Exiles (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Coral Island (PC)
      • Hello Neighbor 2 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Remnant: From The Ashes (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Rollerdrome (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Soccer Story (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Spirit of the North (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • The Walking Dead: The Final Season (PC)
      • While the Iron’s Hot (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Business

  • Starfield reaches 15m players
  • Farming Simulator 25 has sold 2m copies in a week
  • Alan Wake 2 sells over 1.8m units
  • PlayStation Studios has confirmed its new head of mobile
    • Olivier Courtemarche has been co-head of mobile at PlayStation Studios since June 2023, but has now become the full head of mobile.

Rumors

  • Pokemon TCG Pocket has reportedly passed $120 million in earnings

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Time to Vote for The Game Awards
    • Game of the Year contenders are: Astro Bot, Balatro, Black Myth: Wukong, Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree, Final Fantasy VII Rebirth, Metaphor: ReFantazio
    • 23 days until live show
  • Rainbow Six Siege is getting PC and console cross-play next month
    • Technically cross-cross play as cross-play between consoles has been available since late 2022.
  • Assassin’s Creed Syndicate is getting a 60 FPS update for PS5 and Xbox Series S/X
    • Launching on November 19, the 60 FPS patch will also add 4K resolution on PS5, PS5 Pro and Xbox Series X.
  • Lego Horizon Adventures set confirmed for 2025
    • Aloy & Varl Vs Shel-Walker & Sawtooth will be released next year

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass is set to lose these 6 games at the end of the month
    • While the Iron's Hot
    • Spirit of the North Enhanced Edition
    • Soccer Story
    • Rollerdrome
    • Conan Exiles
    • Coral Island
    • Remnant: From the Ashes

Business

  • Indie RPG dev Thought Pennies cuts staff after losing project funding
    • The four-year-old studio is letting employees go after it spent years building staff and working on 'something amazing.'
  • Play Ventures raises $140m in third gaming fund
    • Firm expects to invest in up to 25 early-stage games companies worldwide
    • Play Ventures will focus on mobile free-to-play games and infrastructure and AI-enhanced gaming tools funding with the $140m.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Final Fantasy 7 remake part 3’s plot ‘is complete’ as the game enters full production
  • The Game Awards confirms that DLC, remakes and remasters will be eligible for Game of the Year
    • Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree is eligable for Game of the Year at this year's Game Awards
  • Apex Legends considering Fortnite-like monthly subscription service
    • Survey disseminated to players seems to be testing the waters
  • Ubisoft's Splinter Cell movie cancelled
    • Ubisoft's Splinter Cell film has been in production limbo since it was announced in 2012. Variety later reported that Tom Hardy was to be cast as Sam Fisher.
  • Half-Life 2’s 20th anniversary documentary shows off unseen Episode 3 footage
    • Half-Life 2: Episode 3 footage has been shown off for the first time
  • Patapon spiritual sequel Ratatan gets new trailer confirming platforms
    • Ratatan is coming to all major platforms next year (PS4/5, Xbox Series S/X, Switch and PC)
    • The game was crowdfunded earlier this year, hit its ¥20 million ($137,000 / £108,000) funding goal within an hour of its launch and went on to raise over 10 times that initial goal.

Business

  • SteamWorld creator Thunderful announces 80-100 more layoffs as part of second round of "restructuring"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 15/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 15 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Gearbox, Bethesda and Epic veterans reveal their new multiplayer ‘suburban invasion’ game
    • Ruckus Games raises $19m in funding round led by Krafton. Bitkraft Ventures, Transcend, and Hypergryph also contributed to the round, among others.
    • Ruckus was founded in 2021 by Gearbox and Riot alumni, and is led by CEO Paul Sage, formerly creative director on Borderlands 3.
    • The currently unnamed project is described as a “session-based suburban invasion game combining RPG elements, crafting, town building and more”.
  • Microsoft reveals its new marketing campaign: ‘This is an Xbox’
    • Xbox's new slogal explains how its games can be played on numerous devices
  • Valve accused of "allowing the proliferation of hate" as report finds 1.8m instances of extremist or hateful content on Steam
    • ADL's Center on Extremism says it has discovered "millions of examples of extremist and hateful content"
    • The ADL highlighted that Valve's automated content moderation tools can be easily bypassed using altered words and ASCII art.
  • Epic Games is officially cool with the Internet Archive preserving early Unreal games
    • Epic Games has given the green light for the Internet Archive to host installation files for Unreal and its immediate follow-up, Unreal Tournament.

Business

  • Embracer divests mobile studio Easybrain in $1.2 billion deal
    • Miniclip will become Easybrain's new owner when the deal concludes in early 2025

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Steam's new API lets devs grant easier build access to players
    • Previously, players would have to access those beta branches on their own in the 'Settings' tab; now, developers can add a button and text to a game's menu that shows what a build a player has and invite them to join the beta branch. Inside that branch, players will "see new UI immediately next to the play button in Steam that shows the current beta they have set, and easily lets them opt back out if they so choose."
    • Once selected, Steam will reboot and relaunch the game into beta mode and its player-selected build. That build will be further reflected in the game's Steam library page.
  • World of Warcraft’s Midnight expansion will add player housing
    • Coming Summer 2025
    • Blizzard previously experimented with a similar concept when it introduced Garrisons to World of Warcraft via the Warlords of Draenor expansion in 2014. The feature arriving with Midnight, however, appears to hew closer to community expectations by matching the player housing experiences in other MMOs like Final Fantasy XIV and The Elder Scrolls Online.
  • Blizzard has released remastered versions of Warcraft 1 and 2
    • Also includes a 2.0 version of Warcraft 3 Reforged, which intends to fix the massive amounts of issues from the original Reforged release
    • Blizzard has “added modern controls” for Warcraft 1, while both games include various UI and UX improvements.
    • All three titles are available as part of the Warcraft Remastered Battle Chest, which is priced at $39.99.

Free Games / Subscription

  • November’s PlayStation Plus Game Catalog and Classics titles revealed
    • Releases November 19th
      • Extra and Premium
        • Grand Theft Auto V | PS4/5
        • Dying Light 2: Stay Human | PS4/5
        • Like a Dragon: Ishin | PS4/5
        • MotoGP 24 | PS4/5
        • The Sims 4 Island Living (Add-on only) | PS4
        • Digimon Survive | PS4
        • Overcooked! All You Can Eat | PS4/5
        • Stick Fight: The Game | PS4
        • Clash: Artifacts of Chaos | PS4/5
        • Killer Frequency | PS4/5
        • Hungry Shark World | PS4
        • Chivalry 2 | PS4/5
      • Premium
        • Synapse | PS VR2
        • Blood Omen: Legacy of Kain | PS4/5
        • Blood Omen 2 | PS4/5
        • Resistance: Fall of Man
        • Resistance 2

Business

  • Ubisoft appoints new chief people officer
    • Sébastien Froidefond
    • Froidefond's expertise "spans HR strategy, performance management, culture building and employee engagement." He joins Ubisoft following a position as human resources Americans lead at Lactalis Americas.
    • Previously, he has worked at senior HR positions across a number of sectors, including beauty, pharma, and life sciences.
  • Nexon's Q3 Results
    • Revenues: ¥135.6 billion ($874 million), up 12.7% year-on-year
    • Net income: ¥27 billion ($174 million), down 23% year-on-year
  • Hundreds of Bethesda employees strike over remote work and outsourcing policies
    • Hundreds of Bethesda employees are striking today over remote working and outsourcing concerns they claim Microsoft has failed to address
    • Workers in Maryland and Texas are holding a one-day strike after filing an unfair labor complaint against parent company ZeniMax in October
    • According to the union, ZeniMax workers are currently required to go to the office twice a week, but it claims many are being denied their remote work requests.
    • Additionally, it’s seeking to limit the percentage of quality assurance testers ZeniMax outsources in comparison to the number of full-time workers present in its bargaining unit.
  • Lollipop Chainsaw RePop’s sales greatly exceeded expectations, parent company claims
    • Dragami Games' parent company Extreme has revised its annual forecast for the year as a result
    • Revised its sales forecast to ¥11.65 billion ($75.4 million), up from ¥11 billion ($74 million). It also revised its net profit to 1.08 billion ($6.9 million), up from ¥710 million ($4.6 million).
    • In all, it forecasts a sales increase of 5.9%, an operating profit increase of 50%, an ordinary profit increase of 52.4% and a net profit increase of 52.1% for the year, compared to its previous forecast.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• The Last of Us season two's 2025 release window narrowed down by HBO
    • Coming "Spring"
  • Three years later, GTA Trilogy gets PC and console fixes from improved mobile port
  • Fortnite OG is returning permanently in December
    • Returns December 6

Business

  • Rime developer Tequila Works files for insolvency
  • More layoffs at Payload Studios
    • The exact number of staff affected is unclear at this stage, but CEO confirms redundancy round
  • Metalstorm developer Starform raises $6 million
    • Funding round, led by Bitkraft, to go towards further development of its air-combat arena shooter
  • Modern Times Group to acquire Plarium for $620m
    • Acquisition of the Raid: Shadow Legends developer expected to close during Q1 2025
  • Neowiz signs $8m publishing deal with game dev Zakazane
    • Korean firm will globally publish the studio's upcoming neo-Western RPG
  • Lightspeed launches new Japan studio led by Hideaki Itsuno
    • Capcom veteran will oversee the development of original AAA games
  • Shift Up is considering releasing Stellar Blade for PC in 2025
    • Stellar Blade's developer expects the game to perform better than it has on PS5

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Super Nintendo World's new Donkey Kong Country theme park expansion opening December 11th
  • Stellar Blade's Nier: Automata DLC drops later this month
    • 20th November

Business

  • Death Stranding reaches 19m players globally
  • NetEase execs arrested for alleged money laundering and bribery
    • Corruption charges reportedly amount to roughly $139m
    • An unknown number of external individuals linked to the anti-corruption charges are also being investigated, with NetEase adding that 27 external companies have been blacklisted due to connections with the conduct of those arrested.
  • Raw Fury appoints ex-Avalanche chief Pim Holfve as new CEO
    • Holfve takes over the position from Raw Fury founder Jónas Antonsson, who will now lead its parent company, Combined Effect.
  • Ubisoft is being sued for making The Crew unplayable
    • A pair of Californian gamers are suing Ubisoft for closing down racing game The Crew.
    • The suit also notes that Ubisoft previously shut down online servers for old games while preserving their offline features, as was the case with Assassin’s Creed 2 and Assassin’s Creed 3.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Awesome Games Done Quick reveals full charity speedrun schedule ahead of January's return
    • Raising money for Prevent Cancer Foundation
    • Awesome Games Done Quick 2025 runs from 5th-12th January next year
  • This year’s Grammy nominees for best video game soundtrack have been announced
    • Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora – Pinar Toprak, composer
    • God of War Ragnarök: Valhalla – Bear McCreary, composer
    • Marvel’s Spider-Man 2 – John Paesano, composer
    • Star Wars Outlaws – Wilbert Roget, II, composer
    • Wizardry: Proving Grounds of the Mad Overlord – Winifred Phillips, composer
  • Insomniac pays tribute to Spider-Man 2 actor Tony Todd, who has died
    • Although Todd is best known for his movie career – most notably as the iconic horror villain Candyman, and funeral home owner William Bludworth in the Final Destination movies – he also performed in a number of video games over the years.
    • Todd will posthumously appear in Indiana Jones and the Great Circle, which is set for release on December 9.

Business

  • Kojima Productions acquires Death Stranding's IP rights
    • The studio now has full ownership of the IP from original publisher 505 Games
    • This is also the second IP that 505 Games has relinquished this year. In February, Remedy Entertainment took full ownership of its supernatural title Control as part of its push to turn that game into a larger franchise—which presently spans a sequel, a multiplayer spinoff, and potential film or TV adaptation.
  • Take-Two looking at 'select mobile M&A opportunities' after Zynga integration
    • Take-Two Interactive's mobile division accounted for more than 50 percent of the company's total net bookings during Q2 and over 64 percent of recurrent consumer spending.
  • Infinity Nikki tops 30m pre-registrations

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• GeForce Now introducing 100 hours a month playtime limit, but subscribers can pay extra for more
    • Won't kick in until 2026 for existing members
  • Mass Effect is officially getting a TV adaptation at Amazon
    • Fast & Furious 9 (AKA F9: The Fast Saga) scribe Daniel Casey on writing and executive producer duties - Previously wrote the 2018 movie Kin and performed rewrites on 10 Cloverfield Lane
  • Over 60 classic Sega games are being delisted from digital stores
    • Select titles will still be playable via Nintendo Switch Online
    • The titles and bundles being delisted largely come from Sega’s Sega Mega Drive/ Sega Genesis Classics collection. Select titles from these bundles on Steam and Xbox, and the entire bundles on PlayStation and Nintendo Switch, will no longer be available for purchase as of Friday, December 6.

Business

  • Unity Q3 Financial Results
    • Total revenue: $447 million (down 18% year-over-year)
    • Create Solutions revenue: $132 million (up 5% year-over-year)
    • Grow Solutions revenue: $298 million (down 5% year-over-year)
    • Net loss: $125 million (flat when compared to same quarter last year, and down from $126 million in Q2)
  • Warner Bros says MultiVersus is one reason its games division is ‘substantially underperforming’
    • The fighting game was blamed for a $100 million hit on Year-On-Year Q3 gaming revenue
  • FromSoftware parent Kadokawa expands into console and PC publishing
    • FromSoftware's parent company, Kadokawa Corporation, is "developing a structure" to "expand the scope" of in-house publishing for console and PC games.
    • In its latest financial report, the company also said it hopes to "maximise the lifetime value" of games like Elden Ring with DLC and "repeat" sales.
  • Digital Bros rebrands Hook Indie Publishing label as 505 Pulse
    • The label says the change "reinforces the indie division's synergy with its larger publishing arm, 505 Games"
  • Call of Duty Mobile reaches 1bn downloads
  • Sega has sold Humankind developer Amplitude
    • The studio's most recent game was last year's Endless Dungeon
    • Buyer and amount were not disclosed
  • Sony Games Division Q3 Results
    • Total revenue: $7 billion (up 12% year-over-year)
    • Operating income: $900 million (up 184%)
    • PS5 consoles shipped: 3.8 million (down 29% year-over-year)
    • Software sales: 77.7 million units (up 10.1 million year-over-year)

Rumors

  • Macaulay Culkin has joined the cast for season two of Fallout

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Steam’s new game recording feature is now available for everyone
    • The built-in system for creating and sharing gameplay footage works on Steam Deck too
    • Works with any game that supports the Steam overlay, including non-Steam titles.
    • It features a background recording mode which continuously saves gameplay as well as an on demand mode with manual start and stop.
    • When enabled by the player, it will capture gameplay footage through the Steam client but won’t capture video of the desktop or other programs, unless the user chooses to include the audio from other programs, for example voice chats.
  • 20 years later, Konami confirms fan suspicions Metal Gear Solid 3's Eva was voiced by The Little Mermaid
    • The identity of Eva's voice performer has long puzzled Metal Gear Solid 3 fans, given the person listed in the game's credit - one Suzetta Miñet - didn't appear to be a real person at all.
    • In the newly released third instalment of Konami's Metal Gear Solid Legacy Series documentary, charting the history of the franchise, Benson finally confirmed her Snake Eater involvement.
    • "When we recorded... in 2003, just the various projects that I am affiliated with in my career are sort of family based, children-based, things like that."
  • Grand Theft Auto 6 still on track for fall 2025 release
  • Netflix Removing Interactive Specials
    • Originally believed to be ALL interactive specials, we have now learnt that the majority of specials will be removed December 1st
    • The specials that will remain post the December 1st date are: "Black Mirror: Bandersnatch, Unbreakable Kimmy Schmidt: Kimmy vs. the Reverend, Ranveer vs. Wild with Bear Grylls, and You vs. Wild"
  • Sega's developing another Virtua Fighter game
    • As it continues "legacy" revivals
  • Death Stranding shadowdropped on Xbox

Business

  • Take-Two Interactive Q2 Results
    • Net revenue: $1.35 billion (up 4% year-on-year)
    • Net loss: £365.5 million (down 39% year-on-year)
    • Total net bookings: $1.47 billion (up 2%)
  • Take-Two has sold its indie label Private Division
    • It was claimed in May that Take-Two had laid off the “vast majority” of Private Division staff, and that it was shutting its Kerbal Space Program 2 developer Intercept Games and OlliOlli World studio Roll7.
    • On Wednesday, the publisher confirmed that it had indeed closed the two studios ahead of a sale.
    • Who the studio was sold to and how much for has not been disclosed
  • ‘Disappointing’ Borderlands movie benefitted game sales, says Take-Two
    • Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick has labelled this summer’s Borderlands movie “disappointing”
    • The company will continue to "selectively" license its IP for adaptations
    • In February 2022, it was announced that Take-Two had partnered with Netflix to produce a live action film adaptation of BioShock, which is set to be directed by Francis Lawrence (I Am Legend, The Hunger Games).
      • During a panel at San Diego Comic-Con this summer, producer Roy Lee said the scope of the BioShock film had been “reconfigured” after Netflix cut its budget.
      • “The new regime has lowered the budgets,” he said (via Variety). “So we’re doing a much smaller version. … It’s going to be a more personal point of view, as opposed to a grander, big project.”

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Nvidia tells users to urgently update their GPU drivers to fix eight ‘highly severe’ vulnerabilities
    • The security risks affect Nvidia’s GeForce, RTX, Quadro, NVS and Tesla products, and if not patched could lead to unauthorised attacks on users’ PCs.
    • There are two main types of vulnerabilities exposed by the risks identified. The first “could allow a privileged attacker to escalate permissions”, while the other is “a vulnerability in the user mode layer, where an unprivileged regular user can cause an out-of-bounds read”.
    • Nvidia says this can result in “remote code execution, denial of services, escalation of privileges, information disclosure and data tampering” if not addressed.
  • Hogwarts Legacy sequel will tie in with the new Harry Potter HBO series
  • Switch 2 will be backwards compatible with Switch, Nintendo confirms
    • Even though the console is yet to be officially announced, Nintendo president Shuntaro Furukawa confirmed the device will be backwards compatible and that Nintendo Switch Online will be available on the console.
    • Nintendo reiterated it still intends to announce its next console hardware before the end of its current fiscal year, which concludes on March 31, 2025.

Free Games / Subscription

  • November’s PlayStation Plus Essential games are now available
    • Hot Wheels Unleashed 2 – Turbocharged (PS4/5)
    • Ghostwire: Tokyo (PS5)
    • Death Note Killer Within (PS4/5)

Business

  • Nintendo Financial Results (For six months ended September 30)
    • Net sales: ¥523.3 billion ($3.4 billion, down 34% year-on-year)
    • Operating profit: ¥121.5 billion ($798.3 million, down 57%)
    • Ordinary profit: ¥147.1 billion ($966.5 million, down 61.3%)
    • Hardware sales: 4.72 million (down 31%)
    • Software sales: 70.28 million (down 27.6%)
  • Kingdom Come Deliverance has sold 8m units
  • Study finds 21 percent of European devs laid off in past year
    • According the Big Games Industry survey, HR and QA have been hit hardest by layoffs within the European games industry between 2023 and 2024.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
Free Games / Subscription

  • New Xbox Game Pass titles for console, PC and Cloud dated
    • Arriving:
      • StarCraft: Remastered (PC) – November 5
      • StarCraft II: Campaign Collection (PC) – November 5
      • Metal Slug Tactics (Cloud, Console, and PC) – November 5
      • Go Mecha Ball (Console) – November 6
      • Harold Halibut (Xbox Series S/X) – November 6
      • The Rewinder (Console) – November 6
      • Turnip Boy Robs a Bank (Console) – November 6
      • Goat Simulator Remastered (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) - November 7
      • Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024 (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) - November 19
    • Leaving (November 15th):
      • Dicey Dungeons (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Dungeons 4 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Goat Simulator (PC)
      • Like a Dragon: Ishin! (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Like a Dragon: The Man Who Erased His Name (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Persona 5 Tactica (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Somerville (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Business

  • Atari acquires Transport Tycoon IP
    • Chris Saywer's classic management game added to publisher's growing portfolio of retro brands
    • Atari already holds the publishing rights to Chris Sawyer's 2004 spiritual successor Locomotion, as well as sister franchise RollerCoaster Tycoon.
  • Radical Forge raises £2.6m
    • Investment to go towards team expansion, the release of Southfield, and more
    • Money raised via NPIF II – Mercia Equity Finance (which is a part of the Northern Powerhouse Investment Fund II).
  • Supercell acquires Space Ape
    • The Finnish company already owned 62% of Space Ape, with this acquisition due to close by spring 2025, with a London office operational by then as well.
  • Bethesda to shut down The Elder Scrolls Legends
    • Servers for card game to be retired in January 2025, has also been delisted on Steam
  • Remedy Q3 Results
    • Revenue: €17.9 million (up 129%)
    • Operating loss: €2.4 million (compared to operating loss of €5.5 million for same quarter last year)
  • Nintendo has lowered its Switch forecast after sales declined 31%
    • The company made no mention of its next console in its latest earnings report

Rumors

  • Netflix is eliminating its interactive shows in December
    • Matt Layzell, creator of the streamer's animated series Battle Kitty, claimed on Instagram that his show and others like it would be removed as Netflix "wipes the slate clean of their interactive programs."
    • Would include Black Mirror: Bandersnatch and Minecraft: Story Mode
    • Supposedly happens December 1st
  • Ubisoft’s NFT RPG has reportedly been plagued by a single player auto-winning every game
    • The player has two accounts
    • Apparently the accounts have been banned but are still auto-winning
    • As the game is strictly PVP, this renders the game unplayable
    • Players report they are being automatched with one of the two accounts and auto lose before the match even starts
  • Pokemon TCG Pocket is currently earning 3x more per day than Pokemon Go, estimates suggest
    • Data suggests the mobile game has made $12 million in the first four days of release

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 4 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• House of the Dead film in the works from Resident Evil, Monster Hunter director
    • House of the Dead was previously "adapted" by Uwe Boll
    • Pawl WS Anderson will write and direct
  • Final Fantasy 14 Crossroads release date announced
    • Crossroads is the first major update to the MMO since the release of Dawntrail
    • Tuesday, 12th November
  • More than 10m Fortnite players watch Snoop Dogg, Ice Spice headline concert
    • More than 10 million players in-game and millions more watching via YouTube and TikTok
    • The event was a livestreamed concert held for real in New York's Times Square, where Ice Spice and Snoop Dogg performed in front of a large crowd
  • Square Enix fans can expect its games to "release simultaneously on each platform more and more"
    • Yoshida also teased that he would like a re-release of Vagrant Story and Final Fantasy Tactics.

Business

  • Alan Wake 2 has now recouped "most" of the money it cost to make
    • Yet to still start earning royalties
  • IWGB Game Workers launches first manifesto
    • Union for UK-based video game developers calls for equality, inclusion, and fair pay
    • Key Initiatives:
      • Union recognition: Have all members recognised by their employer
      • Working hours and overtime: End reliance on overtime, ensure that "no overtime" rules cannot be enforced, voluntary overtime is fairly compensated, introduce a four-day work week, guarantee mental health days as part of sick leave
      • Compensation: Mandate annual pay increases, improve baseline pay for all industry workers, equitable shares of studio profits, end the gender pay gap, equal parental leave, receive residuals and royalties, improve pension schemes
      • Accountability and transparency: Hold C-suite executives accountable in event of mergers, acquisitions, studio closures, and redundancies, mandate salary transparency, demand "open book" accounting for studio finances
      • Job security: Better contractual protections, ensure clearly laid out redundancy processes, improve redundancy packages, campaign for AI regulations, end abuse of fixed-term contracts
      • Equity and inclusion: Ensure universal accessible working options, improve disability, neurodiversity, LGBTQ+, and mental health support, ensure policies for handling sexual misconduct, harassment, and bullying are followed, endorse inclusive language guidelines, compulsory training in DEI best practices
      • Training and education: Enhance learning and development schemes, mandate sexual misconduct and antitrust training for C-suite executives and management
      • Contractual terms: Remove unreasonable non-compete and non-disparagement clauses, guarantee credits, guarantee ownership of personal side projects
  • Roblox Q3 Results
    • Revenue: $919 million, up 29% year-over-year
    • Bookings: $1.1 billion, up 34% year-over-year
    • Net losses: $240 million, compared to $278 million during the same quarter last year
  • Phasmophobia has sold 20m copies

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 01/11/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 1 November 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Red Barrels partners with Lionsgate Studios for movie adaptation of horror series Outlast
    • It and Strange Darling producer Roy Lee will lead the production
  • The Room studio Fireproof Games returns with spooky, 80s-set VR puzzle-adventure Ghost Town
    • Coming to Quest, PSVR2, and PC
  • PUBG studio unveils top-down PvP shooter Project ARC, with 'real-time shared line of sight'
    • There's no word of a release for Project Arc just yet or platforms beyond Steam
  • The first gameplay footage of Ghostface in Mortal Kombat 1 has been revealed

Business

  • Meta's AR/VR Reality Labs division records further losses of $4.4 billion
    • And it expects operating losses to "increase meaningfully year-over-year"
    • This is the third reported loss of the year in this segment, with Reality Labs reporting a loss of $3.8 billion during its first quarter, and $4.5 billion in the second.
    • For its Reality Labs business, Meta said Q3 revenue was up 29% to $270 million, which was "driven by hardware sales."
    • Reality Labs expenses were also up 19% year-on-year to $4.7 billion, which Meta said was driven primarily by higher headcount-related expenses and infrastructure costs.
  • Call of Duty’s development structure ‘won’t change under Microsoft
    • 15 different Activision studios are currently working on the franchise

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 31/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 31 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Two box sets celebrating the games of Tetsuya Mizuguchi announced
    • Sounds and Visions: Enhance at 10 and The Enhance Soundworks Collection Boxed Set are available to pre-order now ahead of their release next year.
    • Produced by Enhance, Lost in Cult, and Cook & Becker, Sounds and Visions: Enhance at 10 and The Enhance Soundworks Collection Boxed Set will be limited to 1010 and 505 copies respectively.
    • The Sounds & Visions: Enhance at 10 Boxed Set contains a book, CD, glass sculpture and glass prints
    • The Enhance Soundworks Collection Boxed Set features 9 Vinyl Records covering Rez Infinite, Lumines, Tetris Effect and Humanity
  • Clock Tower: Rewind released
    • Enhanced version of the original Clocktower, marking the games first proper international localization
    • Released for Xbox One / Series S / X, PS4/5, Nintendo Switch and PC
    • Has received positive reviews, though fans have called out the lack of advertising for the release

Free Games / Subscription

  • November’s PlayStation Plus Essential games have been announced
    • Hot Wheels Unleashed 2 – Turbocharged (PS4/5)
    • Ghostwire: Tokyo (PS5)
    • Death Note Killer Within (PS4/5)
      • Day one release.
      • It's a "Werewolf" (read: Among Us) take on the Death Note IP
  • Nintendo Music app launching later today, featuring decades of Nintendo soundtracks
    • Exclusive to Nintendo Switch Online members (basic tier)
    • For iOS and Android
    • Due to the way video-game songs are constructed, some songs can be "extended" (looped) for a custom set user time period (up to 60 minutes)
    • Users will be given recommendations based on their Nintendo Switch play history, and it will also be possible to hide certain soundtracks in order to avoid spoilers.
    • More songs will be added to the future
    • Fan response has been mixed. The app itself has been praised but the "fear" Nintendo will start copyrighting music videos on YouTube has caused an overall negative reaction to the news

Business

  • GameSpot staff endure another round of job cuts
  • ProbablyMonsters lays off "about 50 people" as Battle Barge project cancelled
  • Dozens of staff laid off at Inflexion as part of restructuring
    • The news comes after Inflexion Games shut down its UK division last week.
    • At least 22 people have been affected
  • Fingersoft lays off 14 employees
    • Fingersoft was founded in 2012 by Toni Fingerroos, releasing its debut title, Hill Climb Racing, that same year. Its spin-off, Lego Hill Climb Adventures, launched in May 2024.
  • Team17 appoints Rashid Varachia as CFO and COO
    • The firm's current CFO Mark Crawford to step down on October 31
  • Unity appoints former King exec Steve Collins as CTO
    • Collins replaces Marc Whitten, who resigned from his role as chief product and technology officer in June
  • PlayStation’s Wolverine director has taken over Xbox’s Perfect Dark
    • Brian Horton, who was previously named publicly as the creative director of Insomniac’s Marvel Wolverine title in 2021, left the studio in the summer and has joined his fellow Crystal Dynamics alumni at Xbox’s The Initiative
    • Brain Horton previously helmed Marvel’s Spider-Man: Miles Morales and 2015’s Rise of the Tomb Raider
    • Wolverine is now being led by the design duo behind 2021’s Ratchet & Clank: Rift Apart, creative director Marcus Smith, and game director Mike Daly
  • Ubisoft Q1 Results
    • Sales: €672 million (down 19.6% year-on-year)
    • Net bookings: €642 million (down 21.9%)
    • Digital net bookings: 527 million (down 26.5%)
    • Back-catalogue net bookings: 494 million (down 28.95%)
  • EA Q2 Results
    • Revenue: $2.05 billion, up 8% year-on-year
    • Net income: $234 million, down 26% year-on-year
    • Net bookings: $2.08 billion, up 15% year-on-year
  • Xbox Financial Results (Three months ended September 30, 2024)
    • Gaming Revenue: Up 43% year-over-year
    • Xbox Hardware Revenue: Declined 29% year-over-year
    • Content and Services Revenue: Increased 61%, with 53 points of net impact from the Activision Blizzard acquisition
    • Game Pass: Set a new Q1 record for total revenue and average revenue per subscriber
  • Black Ops 6 launch was ‘the biggest Call of Duty release ever’, Microsoft claims
    • “Last week’s launch of Black Ops 6 was the biggest Call of Duty release ever, setting a record for day one players as well as Game Pass subscriber adds on launch day,” he said.
    • “And unit sales on PlayStation and Steam were also up over 60% year-over-year. This speaks to our strategy of meeting gamers where they are by enabling them to play more games across the screens they spend their time on.”

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Pokémon Trading Card Game Pocket Launches Worldwide on iOS and Android
  • Nintendo announces Xenoblade Chronicles X remaster for Switch
    • The new version of the Wii U title will feature additional content

Business

  • Capcom Financial Results
    • For the six months ended September 30, 2024, Capcom reported:
      • Net sales: ¥56.4 billion ($368 million), down 25% year-on-year
      • Operating income: ¥20.7 billion ($135 million), down 39% year-on-year
      • Net income: ¥15.2 billion ($99 million), down 40% year-on-year
  • The Sims 4 gained more than 15m new players in the past year, despite being over a decade old
  • Sony shuts down Concord dev Firewalk and mobile studio Neon Koi
    • Both studios are being closed down so PlayStation can 'ensure we are meeting near and long-term business priorities.'
    • Concord "will not return"
    • Concord is still planned to show up as an episode of the upcoming TV show Secret Level

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Oregon Trail is taking the road to a movie adaptation
    • MECC's Oregon Trail game is becoming a movie over at Apple
  • Epic is simplifying Fortnite’s pass progression systems
    • All passes will progress with XP

Free Games / Subscription

  • N64’s Shadow Man and Turok 2 have been added to Nintendo Switch Online
    • Both games are available via the Mature app

Business

  • Germany launches new grant to fund gaming start-ups
    • Successful bids can secure €2,750 (almost $3,000) per month over an 18-month period to help "focus on the founding phase and drive forward the development of their games."
  • Dead Island 2 reaches 10m players

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Netflix's animated Tomb Raider: The Legend of Lara Croft series is returning for Season 2
  • STEINS;GATE REBOOT Game Announced at 15th Anniversary Event
    • A new version of the original Steins;Gate game with new graphics and additional story elements
    • This is the third time we've received an updated version of the original Steins;Gate game, following Steins;Gate ELITE (a remaster that used the anime visuals)
    • No word on the Steins;Gate successor Steins;???

Business

  • 10 years in, DayZ just hit a new record of 500k concurrent players
    • Thanks to new expansion Frostline
  • Romero Games has added further worker benefits as the studio celebrates 10 years
    • The studio added Menopause Leave and Clothing Allowance in the past year
    • Romero Games is based in Galway, Ireland. The team currently includes more than 75 developers and plans to expand into a larger office in 2025.
  • U.S. Copyright Office rejects DMCA exemption to support game preservation
    • The Video Game History Foundation decried the 'lobbying efforts by rightsholder groups' that have 'impeded' proper efforts to archive old games at libraries.
  • Sonic x Shadow Generations sells over 1 million copies at launch
  • 20 Xbox franchises have made $1 billion in lifetime revenue, says Microsoft CEO

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 25/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• First Three Episodes of Like a Dragon: Yakuza have released on Amazon Prime Video
    • Reaction so far has been overall mixed to negative with reviewers criticizing the fact it's "nothing like the games"
  • Balatro's back with another collaboration card pack
    • Friends of Jimbo Pack 2 features cameos from The Binding of Isaac and Slay the Spire, alongside Cyberpunk 2077 and Stardew Valley
    • The Free update is available now on PC, PS, Switch, Android and iOS
  • The PC version of the Croc remaster is exclusive to GOG, will come with the original version
    • The remaster will feature enhanced HD graphics, modern controls (with an option to keep the original’s tank controls), an updated camera and retro video modes. However, players who prefer retro aesthetics will also have access to the original 1997 - PC version of the game, tweaked to work on modern hardware.
    • As well as the PC version on GOG, the Croc remaster will also be released on PS4/5, Xbox One / Series S/X and Switch
  • Yooka-Replaylee is coming to PS5, Xbox Series S/X and ‘Nintendo platforms’
    • No release date confirmed yet
  • PlayStation Stars will no longer give credit for money spent on subscriptons
    • Points will also only last just over a year

Business

  • Beyond Good & Evil 2 still in development, now with new creative director
    • Industry veteran Fawzi Mesmar is joining the Beyond Good and Evil 2 team as creative director. Taking to Linkedin, Mesmar announced his new role at Ubisoft Montpellier, having worked with the team previously during his last three years as Ubisoft’s VP of Editorial.
    • Mesmar is filling the role left by previous creative director Emile Morel, after he passed away suddenly last year. Prior to working at Ubisoft, Fawzi Mesmar worked at DICE as head of design, as well as design jobs at King and Gameloft.
  • Indie boxing game Undisputed sells one million units in a week
    • Undisputed was initially released via Early Access in January 2023, before launching on PC, PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series S/X on October 11.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Ubisoft confirms new Rayman project currently in the "exploration phase" following reports of a remake
    • With Michel Ancel consulting
  • Fishing is coming to Fallout 76, along with your own Dogmeat and cat pets for your camp
  • Warcraft 30th Anniversary Direct stream announced for November
    • November 13, viewable on Twitch, YouTube and TikTok at 10am PT / 6pm GMT.
  • "The Remarkable Life of Ibelin" uses World of Warcraft to recreate disabled boys digital life
    • The documentary explores the life of Mats Steen, who had Duchenne muscular dystrophy, and his vibrant virtual life in World of Warcraft.
    • The film is now in UK cinemas and will be available on Netflix from October 25.

Free Games / Subscription

  • Switch Online subscribers can play three games for free for the next week (Game Trials)
    • All regions will have access to two games – Cursed to Golf and A Little to the Left. North America will also get Vampire Survivors, while Europe gets Minecraft Dungeons.
    • Switch Online members will be able to download them today from 10am PT / 1pm ET / 6pm BST, and will then have full access to them until October 30 at 11.59pm PT (2.59am ET on October 31, 7.59am BST on October 31).

Business

  • Keywords Studios and EQT, together with CPP Investments and Temasek, announce acquisition completion
  • Arma 2 sells over 47m units in 15 years
  • 2K's Californian Motion Capture Studio files for union election
  • NCSoft lays off staff following ‘broader internal reorganisation’
    • The company is reportedly working on a Horizon MMO with Sony

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Warhammer 40K: Space Marine 2 getting an animated sequel in Amazon's new Secret Level series
    • Clive Standen to voice Titus
    • Secret Level is a new 15-episode animated anthology series from the creators of Netflix's Love, Death & Robots, with each episode featuring "original stories set within the worlds of some of the most beloved video games". Those games include Armored Core, Concord, Crossfire, Dungeons & Dragons, Exodus, Honor of Kings, Mega Man, New World: Aeternum, Pac-Man, Sifu, Spelunky, The Outer Worlds, Unreal Tournament, a variety of PlayStation Studios games, and Warhammer 40K.
  • Sea of Thieves temporarily yanks nearly all its big new stealth features following "significant issues"
    • Just days after the launch of Sea of Thieves' stealth-focused Season 14, developer Rare has yanked nearly all its new features - including the ability to crouch and don disguises - acknowledging "significant issues" arising from their addition to the game.
  • Assassin’s Creed Shadows Collector’s Edition price cut by $50
    • Following Ubisoft's cancellation of the game's season pass and early access

Business

  • Inflexion Games' UK division is reportedly closing down
    • Audio director Jon Newman confirmed the cuts on LinkedIn, saying: "It is with a heavy heart that I am sharing the sad news that the UK subsidiary of Inflexion Games is closing, and with it, the roles of the audio team based here in the UK as well as others in the UK are affected."
    • Inflexion Games worked on Nightingale and was previously owned by Improbable
  • Indie horror Crow Country tops 100,000 sales
  • Bungie creative team joins PlayStation to support live service games
    • Bridget O’Neill, who previously served as senior director of Bungie Creative Studios, discussed the restructuring in a LinkedIn update this week: “Today was my team’s last day at Bungie, but not our last day working on Destiny (and Marathon), Bungie Creative Studios is joining PlayStation Studios to build the foundation for a creative team that can support all PlayStation live service games. As part of this move, I am taking on a new role as Senior Director of Creative at PlayStation Studios.”
    • The studio previously said it would integrate 155 roles into Sony Interactive Entertainment
  • Netflix has shut down its triple-A ‘Team Blue’ studio
    • The studio had the overwatch executive producer and a God of War art director among its ranks

Other Notes

  • Before I get messages about the Prince of Persia story floating around - it is being misreported. Ubisoft Montpellier does not seem to be closing down - the project has concluded and the team is being moved onto to different projects. The headline that the team has been closed is not an accurate headline.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 22/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Mike Brennan, Digital Extremes artist, passed away at age 50
    • Best known as "Mynki," Brennan provided art for Warframe, Dark Sector, and The Darkness II during his 14-year tenure at Digital Extremes
  • Lord of the Rings star Elijah Wood lands first mo-cap role in Batman: Arkham Shadow as iconic villain Scarecrow
