  • Alan Wake 2’s free anniversary update adds highly requested features
    • Available today
    • Update includes ability to invert X axis on mouse and controller (previously was just Y axis), gyro aiming on PS5 and various Gameplay Assist features
  • Vampire Survivors is getting Castlevania DLC later this month
    • Vampire Survivors: Ode to Castlevania will be the games biggest DLC to date and will cost $3.99 / €3.99 / £2.99
    • Releases October 31st

Business

  • Hi-Rez Studios shutting down Realm Royale Reforged and Divine Knockout in February
    • Realm Royale Reforged and Divine Knockout shutdown on February 17, 2025
  • Bandai Namco to publish medieval fantasy RPG from former Witcher devs
    • The studio is called Rebel Wolves and the game is the first entry in the "Dawnwalker saga"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Marvel's Spider-Man 2 gets January 2025 release date on PC
    • 30th January
  • Spiritfarer studio's 33-player co-op roguelike 33 Immortals has been delayed into next year
    • "2025" release date
  • Bloober Team claims it’s finished making ‘shitty’ games
    • "We want to find our niche, and we think we found our niche"
  • Marvel’s Spider-Man 2 will receive ‘no additional story content,’ Insomniac confirms
  • Bethesda will host a Fallout Day broadcast event on Wednesday
    • Wednesday, October 23 at Noon PT / 3p ET / 8pm BST

Business

  • The BFI National Lottery Innovation Challenge has announced it will provide £510,000 to four innovative projects in the UK games industry
    • Investment will support four projects focused on creating tools and datasets for use in the wider industry
      • The Games Insight Database (Into Games CIC)
      • UK Video Games Econometrics (UKIE and Open Knowledge across Research and Entertainment)
      • Business development accelerator Aurora (Code Coven)
      • Access to Market: Self Publishing Toolkit (Film London)
  • Ubisoft Milan joins Ubisoft France strike over return to office
    • "It is not economically sustainable and unfair on a human level," says Italian union Fiom Cgil
  • European Court of Justice rules against Sony in lawsuit over Action Replay cheats
    • But legal experts say this is not necessarily a major win for cheat providers
    • In a case that dates back to 2012, Sony sued Datel in German courts of its Action Replay for PlayStation Portable, using examples of the cheats this product provides for titles such as MotorStorm: Arctic Edge (for instance, infinite boosts).
    • "For procedural reasons, the Court only ruled on one single ground: whether merely changing variables of a computer program qualified as an unauthorized modification. When publishers of multiplayer games act against providers of cheat software today, they typically rely on other legal instruments which are not affected by this decision, e.g. breach of EULAs and unfair competition."
  • Tales of Kenzera: Zau studio puts team on redundancy notice as it seeks funding

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• FBC Firebreak is Remedy’s 3-player Control multiplayer game
    • Scheduled to release in 2025 for PC via Steam and Epic Games Store, Xbox Series S/X and PlayStation 5
    • Will be added to Game Pass on release day
  • Universal Florida's Super Nintendo World park gets May 2025 opening date
  • Prison Architect studio's Blindfire is a multiplayer shooter played in pitch dark
    • Double Eleven, the long-time steward of Paradox Interactive's Prison Architect series before the two companies parted ways earlier this year, has announced its next project: multiplayer shooter Blindfire, which launches into early access for Xbox Series S/X and PC today.
    • A round starts out in darkness. As the round unfolds, light elements are slowly brought into play, starting from the outer walls of the arena to its centre - flushing out players camping in secluded corners and forcing everyone into closer confines.
  • Alan Wake 2 The Lake House will release next week
    • The Lake House will be released on October 22.
    • This DLC unfolds parallel to the main game, putting you in the shoes of FBC agent Kiran Estevez, portrayed by Janina Gavankar.
    • The Lake House will launch alongside the physical version of the game.
  • 2K reveals Project Ethos, a free-to-play, 3rd-person, roguelike hero shooter
    • Community playtests for the game will be held this weekend
    • “Shape your playstyle every match through Evolutions, powerful, semi-randomized, upgrades unique to each hero,” reads a description for the game.
  • Bloober Team’s next game, Cronos: The New Dawn has been announced
    • The game is set for release across PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series S/X in 2025.
  • Pirate Yakuza in Hawaii will now release one week earlier
    • The game, which was originally planned for release on February 28, will now be released on February 21, 2025.
  • Subnautica 2 has been announced, and it’s coming to Game Pass
    • Launches on PC via Xbox Game Preview in 2025

Free Games / Subscription

  • Banjo-Tooie is coming to Nintendo Switch this month

Business

  • Keywords Studios To Acquire Certain Affinity
    • Certain Affinity was founded in 2006, focused primarily on providing high-end co-development services on AAA multiplayer titles. It employs over 180 people in the US and Canada, anchored out of their offices in Austin, Texas, and Toronto, Ontario.
  • Resident Evil 4 Remake tops 8 million copies sold
  • Warhammer Space Marine 2 drives record results for Pullup Entertainment
    • The company said the game has "already attracted more than 4.5 million unique players to date, placing it among the top five launches of the year."
  • It Takes Two sells 20m units
  • IO Interactive will publish Build a Rocket Boy's 'proof of concept' game for Everywhere platform
    • IO CEO Hakan Abrak explained that its publishing deal with BARB specifically concerns MindsEye, which he called "a full triple-A universe unto itself." However, he noted both it and Everywhere are made on Unreal Engine 5, and the latter is still in development.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Plants Vs Zombies 3 is shutting down for 'major overhaul'
    • From today, players will no longer be able to download the game or make in-app purchases
    • "No longer playable from November 15th"
    • EA released a blog post stating that it will continue to work on the game, offering no target date for a re-launch. "We're excited to re-tool the game and bring you an even better PvZ3 experience soon! In the meantime, keep an eye on our social channels for updates."
  • 2K studio 31st Union to reveal debut game ‘Project Ethos’ today
    • It's a third-person roguelike hero shooter
    • 9:00 AM PT | 5pm GMT+1
  • Pre-orders for Analogue 3D, a reimagining of the N64, open next week
    • The console costs $250, with controllers from 8BitDo available separately for $40
    • It’s said to be compatible with every original N64 game ever made, accepts cartridges from all regions, and includes four original-style controller ports.
  • Astro Bot’s speedrun levels will release weekly beginning Thursday
    • Each level will feature two new cameo bots

Business

  • Silent Hill 2 sold 1 million copies three days after launch
    • The original game took a month to reach the same milestone
  • 700+ Ubisoft France staff walk out on a three-day strike in dispute over home working and pay
    • Union says diktat to be in the office at least three days a week is "the straw that broke the camel's back"
  • Jusant, Lost Records studio Don't Nod says 69 jobs at risk as part of "reorganisation project" amid "deteriorating" financial results

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Xbox Partner Preview digital showcase back this week with 25 minutes of games
    • Including Like A Dragon and Alan Wake 2 DLC
    • 6pm in the UK/10am PT on Thursday 17th October
    • Will feature "over a dozen" new trailers across its 25-minute-ish runtime
  • Leaked trailer for Amazon's Secret Level reveals Arnold Schwarzenegger and Keanu Reeves
  • Final Fantasy 11 responsible for the birth of Discord, its creator says
    • Discord creator Stanislav Vishnevskiy discussed the origins of the platform: he was "obsessed" with Final Fantasy 11 and developed a prototype software to use while playing.
  • Steam Next Fest is now live, featuring ‘thousands of free demos’
    • The digital event is running until October 21st
  • Mobile game Destiny: Rising announced by NetEase and Bungie
    • “Experience top-tier sci-fi shooting action in FPP or TPP as you embark on a new adventure in an alternate Destiny timeline. Play as these unique characters to defend future Earth and reclaim humanity’s future!”
    • Free-to-play
    • Pre-registration is now open ahead of the launch of its first closed alpha test, which is scheduled to begin on November 1 in the US and Canada.
  • Tron: Catalyst is a new isometric action game from Bithell Games
    • Disney Tron: Catalyst is a story-driven isometric action game coming to PC, PS5, Xbox Series S/X and Nintendo Switch via Devolver Digital’s Big Fan Games publishing label.
    • "Coming 2025"
  • Games Done Quick raises over $45,000 for hurricane relief
  • PlayStation horror Until Dawn's live-action movie hits cinemas next April
    • 25th April 2025
  • Warhammer 40K: Space Marine 2 shows off formidable new Hierophant Bio-Titan Tyranid in Season 2 trailer
    • Coming "very, very soon"
  • Hamlet film made in GTA Online coming to US & UK cinemas and global streaming services
    • Courtesy of UK distributor Tull Stories, the film (Grand Theft Hamlet) will be in cinemas across the UK and Ireland from 6th December 2024.
    • That follows its UK premiere at the London Film Festival later this month, and other film festival appearances at Bath Film Festival (21st October), Cambridge Film Festival (24th & 25th October), and Leeds Film Festival (8th & 9th November).
  • PlayStation The Concert to tour over 200 cities in 2025 and 2026

Free Games / Subscription

  • Games leaving PlayStation+ on 18th November
    • GTA: San Andreas - The Definitive Edition
    • Red Dead Redemption 2
    • The Sims 4 - City Living Expansion Pack
    • Dragon's Dogma: Dark Arisen
    • Mobile Suit Gundam Extreme vs Maxiboost On
    • Superliminal
    • Eiyuden Chronicle: Rising
    • Klonoa Phantasy Reverie Series
    • Teardown
    • Kingdom Hearts HD 1.5 + 2.5 ReMIX
    • Kingdom Hearts HD 2.8 Final Chapter Prologue
    • Kingdom Hearts 3
    • Kingdom Hearts Melody of Memory
    • Chorus
    • What Remains of Edith Finch
    • Overcooked 2
    • Blasphemous
    • Moving Out
  • New Xbox Game Pass titles for console, PC and Cloud announced
    • Arriving:
      • South Park: The Fractured but Whole (Cloud, Console, and PC) – October 16 on Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass, Game Pass Standard
      • Donut County (Cloud, Console, and PC) – October 17 on Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass, Game Pass Standard
      • MechWarrior 5: Clans (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series X|S) – October 17 on Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
      • Call of Duty: Black Ops 6 (Cloud, Console, and PC) – October 25 on Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
      • Call of Duty: Modern Warfare III (Cloud) – October 25 on Game Pass Ultimate
      • Call of Duty: Warzone (Cloud) – October 25 on Game Pass Ultimate
      • Ashen (Cloud, Console, and PC) – October 29 on Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass, Game Pass Standard
      • Dead Island 2 (PC) – October 31 on Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
      • StarCraft: Remastered (PC) – November 5 on Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
      • StarCraft II: Campaign Collection (PC) – November 5 on Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
    • Leaving on October 31st:
      • Frog Detective: The Entire Mystery (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Headbangers (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Inkulinati (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Lonely Mountain’s Downhill (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Mineko’s Night Market (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Business

  • Against the Storm has sold 1.2m copies
    • In addition to the milestone, Publisher Hooded Horse also announced that Workers & Resources: Soviet Republic sold over 600,000 units, Norland over 300,000 copies since its EA launch in July, and more
  • Rare's Craig Duncan to become head of Xbox Game Studios as Alan Hartman retires
    • Hartman leaves after a 36-year career that included forming Turn 10 and launching the Forza series
    • Duncan has led Rare for the best part of 14 years, overseeing the studio's Kinect titles and the launch of its live service hit Sea of Thieves, which has had more than 40 million players since it launched in 2018.
  • Riot Games eliminates 32 roles
    • Riot Games has confirmed 27 layoffs from its League team, and five further redundancies from its "publishing side."
  • Dragon Ball: Sparking Zero sells 3m copies in 24 hours

Rumors

  • Bandai Namco is reportedly cutting workforce following cancellation of "several" titles
    • It's alleged 200 of its 1300 employees have been moved into these "expulsion rooms" – or "oidashi beya" – and almost half of these have since voluntarily resigned.
    • Bandai Namco has reportedly also cancelled several titles, including games featuring the One Piece and Naruto licenses, as well as a contract project with Nintendo

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Game Freak acknowledges massive Pokémon data breach, as employee info appears online
    • Current, Former and Contract employees have been affected
    • A lot of information has been included in the gigaleak, including but not limited to:
      • Beta contents for old Pokémon games
      • Source files for certain older Pokémon games
      • Marketing materials, design documents and product specs
      • Plans for upcoming TV shows and movies
      • A weird story about a woman getting pregnant from a pokemon and the townspeople killing it [Editor's Note: I wished that one was a joke.]
      • Nintendo Switch 2's development code name ("Ounce")
      • Details of cancelled and unannounced games such as a "Splatoon-like" battle-focused multiplayer developed in collaboration with ILCA
      • Meetings notes, such as notes from the meeting where they decided why Ash's story needed to end in the anime
  • Epic says Fortnite’s Rocket Racing is ending ‘themed updates’
    • The Psyonix-developed game launched less than a year ago
    • “Future updates will be taking new roads with refreshed Ranked periods, renewed Quests, and additional Car items you can take to the track!”
  • Sakurai ends YouTube channel with a tribute to Satoru Iwata
  • Tomb Raider IV-VI Remastered announced, launching in February
    • Developed and published by Aspyr, this new title will be released on Nintendo Switch, PlayStation 4/5, Xbox One / Series S/X, and PC via Steam and the Epic Games Store on Feb. 14, 2025.
    • "IV, V and VI" refers to Tomb Raider: The Last Revelation (1999), Tomb Raider: Chronicles (2000), Tomb Raider: The Angel of Darkness (2003)

Business

  • Tomb Raider franchise tops 100 million copies sold
  • Metaphor: ReFantazio sells 1 million units on its first day, Atlus claims
    • The new IP from the Persona creators released on Friday

Rumors

  • Xbox Cloud Gaming will reportedly let players stream any game in their library from November

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Age of Empires 3 to be delisted after 17 years
    • Players who own the game can still play campaign and skirmish content after October 30
  • Brighter Shores, the new MMO from RuneScape's creator, sees its early access release date moved back
    • Moved back one day because the original release date coincided with the US Election
    • Will now release 6th November
  • PS1 game Star Wars Episode I: Jedi Power Battles is coming to PC and consoles in January
    • The 2000 game, which was originally released on the PS1 before coming to Dreamcast later that year, is coming to PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Switch, PS5 and - Steam on January 23, 2025 at a price of $19.99 / £15.99.
    • The enhanced re-release adds new playable characters and other new features
    • The original game gave some characters the wrong colour of lightsaber, and the new toggle will let players fix this should they wish. For example, Mace Windu’s lightsaber in the game is blue, but players can now change it back to its movie-accurate purple.

Free Games / Subscription

  • Prime Gaming’s 27 ‘free’ games for October include BioShock Remastered and Doom Eternal
    • Available Now
      • Hive Jump 2: Survivors (GOG)
      • Scarf (Amazon Games App)
      • Tomb Raider: Legend (GOG)
      • The Eternal Cylinder (Epic Games Store)
      • Spirit of the North (Epic Games Store)
      • No Straight Roads (Epic Games Store)
      • BioShock Remastered (GOG)
      • Doom Eternal (Microsoft Store)
      • DreadOut 2 (Amazon Games App)
      • Ghostbusters: Spirits Unleashed – Ecto Edition (Epic Games Store)
      • Priest Simulator: Vampire Show (Epic Games Store)
      • The Gap (Amazon Games App)
    • October 17
      • Mystery Box: Hidden Secrets (Legacy Games)
      • Vlad Circus: Descend Into Madness (Amazon Games App)
      • Through the Darkest of Times (Amazon Games App)
      • Killing Floor 2 (Epic Games Store)
      • Zombies Ate My Neighbours and Ghoul Patrol (Amazon Games App)
    • October 24
      • Pumpkin Jack (GOG)
      • The Gunk (GOG)
      • Stasis: Bone Totem (Epic Games Store)
      • Gargoyles Remastered (Amazon Games App)
      • Monster Train (GOG)
      • Morbid: The Seven Acolytes (Epic Games Store)
    • October 31
      • A Plague Tale: Innocence (GOG)
      • Death’s Door (Epic Games Store)
      • Haunted Hotel: Personal Nightmare – Collector’s Edition (Amazon Games App)
      • Scorn (GOG)
      • Coromon (GOG)

Business

  • Microids launches new third-party publishing division with Kwalee collaboration
    • Microids CEO says this "allows us to extend our expertise to new partners"
  • Konami replaces EA as FIFA's esports partner
    • Japanese publisher's eFootball will be used in two FIFA eWorld Cups later this year
    • The football association previously ran this competition in collaboration with Electronic Arts, but this partnership came to an end when the pair split in 2022 after 29 years.
    • The Konami deal will begin with two FIFAe World Cup tournaments within eFootball later this year, one on mobile and the other on console. Qualifying matches begin today, and a champion will be named at the end of each tournament.

Rumors

  • NaturalMotion facing potential layoffs in US and UK
    • Sources claim staff have already been let go, Birmingham studio reportedly closed

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo playtesting mystery new Switch Online feature this month with up to 10K people
    • To get in involved, participants must be at least 18 years of age and have a Nintendo Account registered in either Japan, the USA, the United Kingdom, France, Germany, Italy, or Spain. Sign-ups are due to open via Nintendo's application form on 10th -- October at 6pm PDT and will close on 15th October. The playtest itself runs from 23rd October to 5th November.
    • Unclear at this time what feature is being playtested
  • Genshin Impact's latest update isn't fully voiced in English due to "recording arrangements"
    • SAG-AFTRA strike the suspected cause
  • Universal and Sega are making a Shinobi movie
    • Extractor director Sam Hargrave will direct the film
  • Resident Evil 2 is coming to iPhone, iPad and Mac this year
    • December 31, 2024
    • It will be supported on iPhone 15 Pro models, iPhone 16, and every iPad and Mac with the M1 chip or later.

Free Games / Subscription

  • October’s PlayStation Plus Game Catalogue and Classics titles announced
    • Extra + Premium
      • Dead Island 2 | PS4/5
      • Two Point Campus | PS4/5
      • The Dark Pictures Anthology: The Devil in Me | PS4/5
      • Gris | PS4/5
      • Return to Monkey Island | PS5
      • Ghostbusters: Spirits Unleashed | PS4/5
      • Firefighting Simulator The Squad | PS4/5
      • Overpass 2 | PS5
      • Tour de France 2023 |PS4/5
      • Tom Clancy’s Ghost Recon: Wildlands | PS4
    • Premium
      • The Last Clockwinder | PS VR2
      • Dino Crisis | PS4/5
      • Siren | PS4/5
      • R-Type Dimensions EX | PS4
  • Four new Apple Arcade games announced for November 7th
    • Wheel of Fortune Daily
    • Drive Ahead! Carcade
    • Arkanoid vs Space Invaders+
    • Texas Hold’em Poker: Pokerist+

Business

  • Cryptic Studios is laying off staff at the end of October
    • Almost a full year after its last round of layoffs, Cryptic Studios is letting go of more staff. Various workers on LinkedIn revealed they were impacted, and that they will no longer be at the studio after October 31
    • Exact number of people affected is unclear at this time
  • Ubisoft is being sued for allegedly sharing Ubisoft Store and Ubisoft+ user data with Meta
    • The class action complaint claims Ubisoft sends user data to Facebook without permission

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Hotel Barcelona has been delayed to next year
    • The collaboration between Swery65 and Suda51 is now targeting "early 2025"
  • Red Dead Redemption and Undead Nightmare announced for PC
    • The open world western game will be bundled with its zombie-themed expansion Undead Nightmare when it hits PC on October 29, 2024.
    • The title will be available via the Rockstar Store, Steam and the Epic Games Store.
    • Port is created by Double Eleven and features " native 4K resolution at up to 144hz, ultrawide and super ultrawide support, HDR10 support, fully Keyboard and Mouse support, DLSS 3.7 and FSR 3 support"
  • LittleBigPlanet 3 and LBP DLC are being delisted from the PlayStation Store
    • The PS4 platformer and the entire range of DLC for the franchise will be pulled from sale on October 31, 2024

Business

  • Saudi Arabia has reduced its stake in Nintendo
    • The follows (later corrected) reports that it was planning to invest further
    • Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund (PIF) has cut its stake in Nintendo from 8.58% to 7.54%, a move which still makes it one of Nintendo’s largest shareholders
  • NCsoft Amazon Games' Throne and Liberty tops 3m players in launch week
  • Tequilla Works lays off "small number" of staff, cancels unannounced game
    • Rime and Sexy Brutale developer is restructuring the studio around a single project
  • Humble Games calls in Good Games Group for support following restructure
    • Humble Games is bringing in another third-party to support its operations following a restructuring that saw the entire staff laid off.
    • The company announced today that it will be working with Good Games Group, a new indie publisher set up by former Humble Games leaders Alan Patmore and Mark Nash, to "further support previously released titles."
    • This is in addition to the partnership with The Powell Group, which took over publishing operations after the layoffs.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Sega has announced an Alien Isolation sequel
    • The game is in early development at Creative Assembly

Business

  • Google ordered to open its Android app store to competitors
    • A US judge has issued a permanent injunction ordering Google to open its Android marketplace to competitors.
    • The ruling, which will come into force in November, means Google will not be allowed to block the distribution of third-party Android app stores through Google Play.
    • It will also have to grant third-party app stores access to Google Play’s full catalogue of apps.
  • Detroit: Become Human tops 10m sales
    • Milestone reached in 6 years

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Games Done Quick to hold Hurricane Helene disaster fundraiser next week
    • Donations will go towards humanitarian aid organisation, Direct Relief.
  • Halo’s 343 rebrands and confirms ‘multiple games’ coming, using Unreal Engine 5
    • 343 Industries is now "Halo Studios"
    • Currently working on "multiple projects" in Unreal Engine 5
    • The studio claims it's changed its culture and workflow
  • Halo Infinite is getting a third-person mode
    • The new viewpoint is coming to the game next month
    • Third-person controls will be “supported at a mode level”, senior community manager John ‘Unyshek’ Junyszek said. This means players won’t be able to manually switch between first-person and third-person mode – it will be set for them.
    • The third-person view will also be added to Forge mode allowing creators to switch individual players (or all players) between first- and third-person perspectives as desired.
  • The Until Dawn movie has finished filming
    • The film is planned for release in October 2025
  • Astro Bot’s 87-song soundtrack is now available on streaming platforms
    • The soundtrack includes PlayStation homages such as "Bot of War"
    • The majority of tracks were composed by Kenny CM Young. Additional tracks are contributed by Hirofumi Sasaki, Tadashi Yatabe, Kemmei Adachi, and Sachiko Miyano.

Business

  • Epic bringing free games to its mobile store in a bid to entice new users
    • Arriving alongside third-party apps in Q4
    • In total, Epic expects to have "between 10 and 50" third-party applications available on its mobile store by this "holiday season", with Ark: Ultimate Mobile Edition already to confirmed as one of those games
    • When it comes to enticing developers, it's also pushing a similar self-publishing strategy to that seen on PC, alongside a reduced cut of earnings on app sales and in-app purchases - 12 percent - compared to Apple and Google's share. Epic also waives its cut entirely for six months if a developer agrees to release its game as a six-month Epic store exclusive.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 4 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• LucasArts artist Ti-Ning Kwa has passed away
    • Kwa provided art and model work for The Sims sequels and their expansions and Tribes 2 before pivoting to making award-winning, high-end luxury eyewear.
  • Saber Interactive teams with Paramount to develop triple-A Avatar (Nickelodeon's Avatar) project
  • Fallout series reaches 100m viewers worldwide
  • Sims 4 gets embalming, grim reaping, reincarnation, and naked moon rituals in new Life & Death expansion
    • Arrives October 31st
  • Foamstars is now free-to-play
  • Riot teases ‘new playable experiences’ in the Valorant universe
    • Valorant, which has 35 million players per month, is moving to Unreal Engine 5 (currently on Unreal 4.27)
    • Fun fact: Valorant is built on-top of the ShooterGame sample of Unreal Engine 4

Business

  • Hi-Rez Studios lays off staff to 'ensure' Smite 2's long-term success
    • Several teams across Hi-Rez and its subsidiaries have been impacted by layoffs as the company puts more weight behind Smite 2's development.
    • A specific number of reductions was not revealed, but Chisam said Hi-Rez's marketing and publishing teams have been affected. Cosmetic content and system features teams at subsidiaries Evil Mojo (Paladins), G&A, and Titan Forge (Smite) were also impacted.
  • French publisher RIVRS raises €4m to "professionalise and industrialise" UGC
    • "Our proven model puts us in pole position to capture the global UGC market," said co-founder
    • RIVRS was founded in 2021 in Rennes, France, by Romain Hubert and Loïc Deffains. It has already developed ten games, reaching revenues of "nearly €500K for a single title".
  • Antstream Arcade makes "small number" of layoffs
    • Retro games streaming firm attributed job cuts to a "strategic shift in focus"
    • Unclear specifically how many people are affected
    • Antstream was founded in 2013 and launched its games streaming service in 2019. In recent years, it has been expanding that service to more platforms, including Xbox last year and PlayStation earlier this year.
  • Mario + Rabbids director and Red Dead Redemption lead designer found new studio Day 4 Night
    • Day 4 Night's first project, a new IP, is described by Xbox co-founder Ed Fries - whose 1Up Ventures is financing the studio alongside publisher Krafton - as "easily the most creative thing we have seen in quite some time".
  • Xbox co-founder J Allard has joined Amazon
    • He's working on "new ideas" as Vice President of Product (device and services team)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Smash Bros. creator Masahiro Sakurai is ending his acclaimed game design YouTube series this month
    • One "special finale" to follow
  • The Rocky Horror Video Game is a thing, and coming out this month
    • The game is described as a "bonkers classic platformer", which will allow players to relive the "strange journey of Brad and Janet like never before".
    • The Rocky Horror Video Game is set to release across PC via Steam (although it doesn't appear to have a Steam page as yet), Playstation, Xbox, and Nintendo Switch.
  • Palworld headed to mobile, courtesy of Krafton's PUBG Studios
    • Krafton - which is perhaps best known for its work on PUBG - said this agreement will see the company "faithfully reinterpret and implement the main fun elements of the original for the mobile environment".
    • Krafton's subsidiary PUBG Studios will handle development of the mobile version of Pocketpair's monster catching survival game, the company said.

Business

  • Future shutters PlayStation-focused Play magazine
    • The UK's Official PlayStation Magazine (OPM) was retired after 25 years in May 2021, with Future Publishing replacing it with Play, a new publication with the same editorial team but without the official Sony branding.
    • Whilst the rebranding saw the magazine's numbering reset, Play was otherwise the spiritual successor to OPM, "made by the same team of writers, editors, and designers." It ran for four years, bringing the OPM/Play legacy to a close after 29 years.
  • Indie Studio Black Flag shuts down following "communication breakdown" with publisher
    • "We appear to be another casualty of the current downturn in the video game industry," Black Flag says
    • The team said that whilst it could not "get into the specifics of our contract with our publisher," it did not own the Orphan Age IP "in a way that would allow us to shop it to another publisher," and "the market is also heavily dried up for indie publishing."
    • The studio has filed for bankruptcy
  • Epic Games CEO says company is now "financially sound"
    • Tim Sweeney said Epic had "spent the last year rebuilding and executing solidly on all fronts"
    • "We spent the last year rebuilding and really executing solidly on all fronts," he said. "I'm happy to tell you now that the company is financially sound, and that Fortnite and the Epic Games Store have hit new records in concurrency and success."
    • Sweeney also revealed that Fortnite achieved a new peak over the holidays, reaching 110 million monthly active users.
    • Epic Games Store General manager Steve Allison highlighted that the storefront reached 70 million monthly active users last month.

Rumors

  • Star Citizen Developer Cloud Imperium Games 7-Day Work Week Ahead of CitizenCon
    • Cloud Imperium Games, the developer of Star Citizen, has mandated its developers to work seven days a week to meet deadlines for Citizencon on October 19th.
    • An internal email sent to staff yesterday states that the company needs to “double down over the next 18 days to make sure that once again we create an amazing experience for our community.” This includes finalizing patch 3.24.2 for Star Citizen and having a Squadron 42 demo showcase for Chapter 1.
    • The company mandate states:
      • All staff are to be in the office on Friday, October 4th and Friday, October 11th (typically a work-at-home day)
      • All staff are to be in the office on Saturday, October 5th, and Saturday, October 12th (for these days, the company will provide breakfast and lunch, with staff receiving company TOIL (time off in lieu) which can be stored and used for time off at a later date)
      • All staff are to work on Sunday, October 6th, and Sunday, October 13th (this can be a work-at-home day, but the company “encourages” people to be in the office. For these days and for those in the office, the company will provide breakfast and lunch, with staff receiving company TOIL, which can be stored and used for time off at a later date)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/10/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 October 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Unreal 5.5 Preview 1 Released
    • Key features include Megalights - Enables you to use orders of magnitude more lights than ever before—all movable, dynamic, with realistic area shadows and the ability to light volumetric fog. A tech demo showed over over 1,000 shadow-casting lights in real time running on PlayStation 5
    • Many features have moved to production ready (such as the Skeletal Mesh editor)
    • New features include a PCG-esque character creator ("Mutable plugin"), Nanite Skeletal Meshes being more readily available .etc
    • Better curve support in animations / ability to manually select animation data from "choosers"
  • Microsoft is discontinuing its HoloLens mixed reality headsets
    • Microsoft said HoloLens 2 will continue to receive “updates to address critical security issues and software regressions” until December 31, 2027, after which software support will cease.
  • TV Show Secret Level recreated the Unreal Tournament (99) introduction in Unreal Engine 5
  • Switch emulator Ryujinx pulled offline by creator after contact from Nintendo
    • The download links and original repo have vanished
    • "Yesterday, gdkchan was contacted by Nintendo and offered an agreement to stop working on the project, remove the organisation and all related assets he's in control of," co-developer riperiperi explained in the post.

Free Games / Subscription

  • October’s PlayStation Plus Essential games are now live
    • WWE 2K24, Dead Space and Doki Doki Literature Club Plus!
  • New Xbox Game Pass titles for console, PC and Cloud have been revealed
    • Arriving:
      • MLB The Show 24 (Console) – October 2, Game Pass Standard
      • Open Roads (Console) – October 2, Game Pass Standard
      • Sifu (Cloud, Console, and PC) – October 2, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass, Game Pass Standard
      • Mad Streets (Cloud, Console, and PC) – October 7, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass, Game Pass Standard
      • Inscryption (Cloud, Console, and PC) – October 10, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass, Game Pass Standard
    • Leaving (October 15th):
      • Dyson Sphere Program (PC)
      • Everspace 2 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • From Space (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • F1 Manager 2023 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Scorn (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Business

  • Devolver Digital launches publishing label Big Fan Games for licensed games
    • Devolver acquired Good Shepherd—the team responsible for publishing Hellboy Web of the Wyrd and John Wick Hex—back in 2021.
    • Similar to other indie publishing labels, Big Fan will offer services in finance, PR, marketing, and other key areas.
  • Epic Games lowers royalty fee for games released simultaneously on Epic Games Store
    • Prioritise an Epic Games Store release, and your royalties could drop from 5% to 3.5%
    • Includes mobile and console, as long as the title ships on Epic Game Store before or simultaneously - you can take advantage of the deal
  • Crunchyroll alumni launch indie publisher Shorelines Games
    • New label to focus on systems-based titles that "have a very clear narrative throughline"
    • CEO Kawamura previously was general manager at art products company Too Corporation Americas. He's formerly worked at the likes of Unknown Worlds Entertainment, Perfect World, Crunchyroll, and more.
    • COO Zha most recently was CEO of marketing agency Infinitize, having previously held roles at Rakuten Viki, Melwood Global, and Crunchyroll as well.
  • Sharkmob London faces layoffs after game cancelled
    • Sharkmob aims to "try and minimise this impact."
    • Undisclosed amount of people affected

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Final Fantasy 14's Patch 7.1, Crossroads, is coming in November
    • Expected mid-November, the patch, called "Crossroads", will also include new Allied Society quests for Dawntrail, focusing on a new Pelupelu trader.
  • Valve croaks down on Deadlock cheaters by letting you turn them into frogs
    • When a cheater is detected, opponents are given a choice; instant ban or turn the offender into a frog for the remainder of the match (after the match they'll be banned)
  • Kojima says Death Stranding 2’s release date is decided, but he can’t reveal it
    • Speaking during a special stage presentation for Death Stranding 2: On The Beach, the director claimed that the release date for the game has been decided, but couldn’t be announced during the presentation due to “unforeseen circumstances”.
    • “Since we are talking about 2025, it will be released next year, so we will announce it at some point next year. But, due to some unforeseen circumstances, we cannot announce it today. ”
  • Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom named best game at Japan Game Awards
    • It also picked up the Best Sales award and an Award For Excellence

Business

  • Dome Keeper sells 1m units
  • Manor Lords reaches over 2.5m units sold
  • Steam updates subscriber agreements to remove arbitration clause
    • Change now dictates "disputes are to go forward in court instead".
  • Alan Wake, Control developer agrees €15m convertible loan from Tencent
    • This type of loan is an agreement that can be converted into a predetermined number of equity shares at a later date.
    • Tencent's loan is still to be approved by Remedy's Extraordinary General Meeting, which is scheduled to be held by 24th October. Following its approval, Tencent will "have the right to convert the loan into new Remedy shares in accordance with the terms and conditions of the convertible rights" three years after the drawdown.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• 90s classic Little Big Adventure's remake is out November 14th
  • PlayStation raises Horizon Zero Dawn’s PS4 price following remaster upgrade annoucement
    • The decision to change the PS4 version’s price back to $39.99 means anyone choosing to buy that and upgrade will pay a total of $49.98, instead of $29.98.
  • California forces digital stores to admit players don’t own digital content
  • Hit deck-builder Balatro is now available for mobile
  • Street Fighter’s Ken and Chun-Li are officially joining Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves
    • Ken to arrive Summer 2025, Chun-Li to arrive Winter 2025
  • Final Fantasy Pixel Remaster, Trials of Mana and Legend of Mana released on Xbox
    • Trials of Mana and Legend of Mana are both available on Xbox, and both are also available on Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass (but not Game Pass Standard).
    • Final Fantasy Pixel Remaster also comes to Xbox today, but won’t be on Game Pass.

Free Games / Subscription

  • StarCraft: Remastered and StarCraft 2: Campaign Collection coming to Xbox PC Game Pass
    • Arrives November 5th

Business

  • Ghosts of Tabor passes $20m revenue
  • Vampire Survivor developer Poncle opens publishing arm
    • Poncle's publishing arm will offer funding, as well as platform support, localisation, QA, release management, and development advice for games built with "sincerity, passion, and depth."
    • "We want to sign everything!" the team said during the presentation. "But we're very smol!"
    • Poncle also outlined its "insta-nos", which included anything "Survivor-like," anything AI or Web3 related, and free-to-play mobile games.
  • Gris sales top 3 million
  • The Simpsons: Tapped Out is ending service in January after 12 years
    • In-game purchases have already been disabled, and the game will be removed from the App Store on October 31. The game will be fully delisted on January 24, 2025.
    • “It has been a remarkable journey, and we are grateful that we’ve been able to deliver 308 updates, 831 characters and including today’s final farewell 1,463 questlines. As our journey comes to a close, we offer our sincerest thanks to you, the players, who have made this all possible.”
  • Indie publisher Merge Games is closing down after 15 years
    • Merge published numerous games, as well as physical editions of other notable titles such as Dead Cells, Streets of Rage 4, Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Shredder’s Revenge.
    • Merge Games employed 22 people

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Former Bandai Namco president Shukuo Ishikawa dies aged 69
    • Bandai Namco said it was "deeply grateful for the support and kindness shown to him during his lifetime"
  • Meta Quest 3S officially unveiled with £290 price tag and October release date
    • Meta Quest 3S starts at £289.99/$299.99 USD with 128GB of storage, but there's also a 256GB option priced at £379.99/$399.99
    • Meta has confirmed it's phasing out Quest 2, the Quest Pro, and the 128GB version of Quest 3 - leaving just the 512GB Quest 3 (now slashed by $150 to $499.99) as an alternative.
  • All new Ubisoft games to release day one on Steam starting next February
    • For the first time since 2019
  • Monster Hunter Wilds $1300 collector’s edition in Japan comes with a bike
    • Unclear if the bundle will be made available outside of Japan
    • It will retail for ¥181,590 ($1,260).
    • It also includes a baby Seikret plush, a pouch designed to look like a Weapon Bag from the game, a Steelbook and a PS5 copy of the game.
  • Ubisoft delays Assassin’s Creed Shadows, drops traditional Season Pass model
    • The move comes after Star Wars Outlaws failed to meet expectations
    • The game will now be released on February 14, 2025

Business

  • Pixel Gun 3D reaches over $210m in revenue
  • Pokémon Sleep surpasses $120m in revenue
    • Japan remains in top spot for player spending, having reached $88.4 million since launch
  • Palworld’s PS5 release held up in Japan amid Nintendo Pokémon lawsuit
    • While Pocketpair didn’t specify the reason for the delay, it was recently announced that the developer is being sued by fellow Japanese firms Nintendo and The Pokémon Company.
    • In a lawsuit filed last week in the Tokyo District Court, plaintiffs said they are seeking an injunction against infringement and compensation for damages “on the grounds that Palworld, a game developed and released by the Defendant, infringes multiple patent rights.”

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 25/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Monster Hunter Wilds launches February 28, 2025
    • More infomation at TGS2024 Capcom Online Program - September 26th 7am PDT / 3pm BST / 11pm JST
  • Horizon Zero Dawn Remastered announced, coming this October
    • The game will be available for $49.99 on PS5, Steam and the Epic Game Store, while owners of the original game can upgrade to the remaster for $9.99
    • "Same visual fidelity as Horizon: Forbidden West"
    • Developed with nixxies
    • October 31st release date
  • Keanu Reeves will star in Sonic x Shadow Generations movie DLC
    • The DLC will be inspired by scenes from the Sonic the Hedgehog 3 film and arrives ahead of its release on 12th December.
  • Sony reveals Ghost of Yōtei, the follow up to Ghost of Tsushima
    • The game will feature an all-new Ghost protagonist named Atsu and takes place in the lands surrounding Mount Yōtei - that's on the island of Hokkaido in northern Japan.
    • Releases "2025"
  • Lego Horizon Adventures release date confirmed
    • PS5, PC and Nintendo Switch on November 14th
    • Digital Deluxe Edition includes Sackboy, Ratchet outfits
  • Ghost-laden Holy Horror Mansion announced by Professor Layton developer Level-5, coming to "secret" platforms
  • PlayStation announces Chroma Pearl, Indigo and Teal DualSense and console covers
  • Alan Wake 2’s second DLC, The Lake House, is coming in October
    • This expansion unfolds parallel to the main game, putting you in the shoes of FBC agent Kiran Estevez, portrayed by Janina Gavankar.
  • Palworld is out now on PS5
    • The game has been on Xbox and PC since January
  • Astro Bot speedrun free DLC will add 5 new levels and 10 cameo bots
    • "Further surprises on the way"
    • Some of the cameo bots include Helldivers and Eve from Stellar Blade
    • Releases "later this year"
  • Stellar Blade updates
    • Neir Automata x Stellar Blade DLC "later in 2024"
    • Photo mode incoming
    • Soundtrack now available on "Select Streaming Services"
  • Sonic Rumble is coming to PC as well as mobile this winter
    • The 32-player battle royale sees players take control of toy versions of Sonic characters.
  • Legacy of Kain Soul Reaver 1 & 2 Remastered Announced
    • Coming to PS4/5 and PC on December 10th
  • Fatal Fury’s Terry Bogard is now available in Street Fighter 6

Free Games / Subscription

  • PS+ Additions incoming
    • 26th September: Last of Us Part 1 (Premium + Extra)
    • October 1st: WWE 2K 24, Doki Doki Literature Club +, Dead Space (Monthly Games)
    • "Coming Soon": Dino Crisis (PS1), Blood Omen: Legacy of Kain (PS1)

Business

  • No Time To Relax dev Porcelain Fortress raises $3m
    • The team has also grown from four staff to 14 as a result of the fresh funding, with former CCP director for customer acquisitions Ingolfur Aevarsson hired as CEO.
  • Unicorn Overlord sells over 1 million copies in six months
    • Within half a year, Vanillaware's tactical-RPG has reached the 1 million milestone faster than its last two games, 13 Sentinels and Dragon's Crown.
  • Frostpunk 2 has sold 350,000 copies
  • Behaviour Interactive acquires Darkest Dungeon dev Red Hook Studios
    • Red Hook Studios is known for for the Darkest Dungeon series, which has sold nearly seven million copies.
    • Acquisition news comes just days after closure of Midwinter Entertainment and cancellation of Dead by Daylight spinoff Project T
    • Undisclosed amount
  • Actors union SAG-AFTRA calls a strike against League of Legends
    • Production company Formosa accused of attempting to subvert ongoing video-game strike
    • Riot games denies involvement and has said "since becoming a union project five years ago, League of Legends has only asked Formosa to engage with Union performers in the US and has never once suggested doing otherwise"

Rumors

  • Gamurs Group employees reportedly hit with more layoffs
    • Gamurs Group owns various publications such as Prima Games, Twinfinite, WGTC and Destructoid
    • 30 jobs were allegedly cut as part of restructuring efforts

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Cristiano Ronaldo (?!) is being added to Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves
    • Game releases 24th April 2025
  • PlayStation’s State of Play returns tonight with a look at over 20 games
    • The 30-plus minute show will feature PS5 and PS VR 2 games from studios around the world
    • 3pm PT / 6pm ET / 11pm GMT
  • Grezzo’s Zelda: Echoes of Wisdom started out as a dungeon creation game
    • The new Switch game is the partner's studios first brand-new entry in the Zelda Series - previously they worked on remasters and remakes in the Zelda series
  • Double Dragon artist says Arc System Works’ revival ‘shows no respect’ to the original
    • None of the original developers acknowledge it as a Double Dragon game, the artist claims

Business

  • NetEase subsidiary Skybox Labs lays off 25 people
    • Studio said that "despite best efforts, we are not immune to the challenges that have impacted the industry"
    • Founded in 2011, the studio has co-developed titles such as Halo Infinite, Minecraft, and Fallout 76.
  • Airship Syndicate cuts another 16 jobs
    • COO says layoffs come "in light of the challenges facing our industry"
    • In November last year, Wayfinder publisher Digital Extremes announced it was dropping the title amid layoffs. As a result, Airship Syndicate gained full control over the title's publishing.
    • Airship Syndicate is based in Austin, Texas, and was founded in 2014.
  • Nitro Games extends Warframe developer Digital Extremes partnership with new $5.5m deal
    • That brings the total project order to around $19.5m
    • According to PocketGamer, "the extended partnership and additional funding will lead to ongoing content updates, performance improvements, and optimisations to ensure the game continues to run smoothly on mobile devices."
  • Two games addiction lawsuits dismissed
    • The cases, which were against firms including Activision Blizzard, Epic Games, and Roblox, were voluntarily dismissed
    • The cases were filed in Arkansas and Ohio on behalf of minors
  • Stellarblade the film company is suing Sony over Stellar Blade the game
    • Sony, Shift Up, and and Sony's unnamed liability insurance company have been targeted amid claims Stellarblade's business (that's the film company) is being damaged by Stellar Blade (the game's) use of the name.
  • PlatinumGames has acquired The Wonderful 101 trademark from Nintendo
    • A legal document reveals the transfer was agreed last month
  • Don't Nod Financial Results (HY1 2024-2025)
    • Operational Revenue: €14.6 million for the first half of 2024-2025, down 11% from €16.5 million in 2023.
    • Development Revenues: Decreased to €2.0 million.
    • Capitalized Productions: Decreased to €1.1 million.
    • Write Downs: €24 million partial write down for Banishers and Jusant, and €7.6 million write down for paused projects P12 and P13.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Steam hit an all-time record of 38.3 million PC players online
    • Steam hit an all-time peak of 32 million in January 2023 (and 37 million in August 2024)
  • Microsoft shutting down Xbox Ambassadors
    • Xbox Ambassadors help fellow gamers by providing assistance and promoting a positive gaming environment.
    • To join, you needed to have an Xbox Gamertag, be at least 17 years old, have a minimum of 1,500 Gamerscore, and no enforcement actions against your account in the past year
    • Xbox Ambassadors website will go offline on October 15th
  • Netflix's Splinter Cell: Deathwatch animated series is "coming soon"

Business

  • Microsoft Spends $1 Billion Annually To Get Third-Party Games On Game Pass
    • Some developers get "millions of dollars" up front to bring their titles on Game Pass

Rumors

  • Elder Scrolls Online has reportedly earned $15M in monthly revenue for over a decade
  • Assassin's Creed Invictus is inspired by Fall Guys
    • "It will be a sort of 'Fall Guys' – 16 players (for the moment) who compete over several rounds with different 'games'"
  • PlayStation 30th Anniversary Edition of PS5 Slim Digital to Cost $500, DualSense Controller Priced at $80
  • Microsoft's next-gen console plans reportedly include an Xbox Series X successor and a handheld "take on the Steam Deck"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Two Point Museum gets release date, exclusive Sonic pre-order items
    • March 4 on PS5, Xbox Series S/X and PC
  • Tetsuya Nomura says he’s considering retirement, and concluding Kingdom Hearts’ story
    • “I’m making Kingdom Hearts 4 now with the intention of it being a story that I will complete.”
  • PlayStation announces 30th Anniversary Collection, including grey PS5 and PS5 Pro
    • Only 12,300 of the PS5 Pro variant will be available globally
  • Yager uses Fortnite to revive scrapped game The Cycle: Frontier
    • Using Unreal Editor for Fortnite (UEFN), the Berlin studio created an in-game location, dubbed "The Cycle: Prospect Island," which "captures the essence" of its 2022 extraction shooter.
    • Yager is focused on "various co-development ventures and new projects," according to a spokesperson, but said the team wanted to "keep [The Cycle's] spirit alive. This island is a way of honoring the game that many players, and us at Yager, cherished deeply."

Business

  • Midwinter shut down following Project T cancellation
    • Midwinter Entertainment was acquired by Behaviour Interactive from Improbable in 2022 following the release of its debut title, Scavengers
    • "All the employees were offered to relocate to our Canadian studios, as we strongly believe in their expertise. Should they decide not to accept, they will be presented with a generous severance package. We want to thank them for all their hard work on Project T and wish them the very best for the future."
  • Unity 6 will launch globally on October 17

Rumors

  • Concord’s director is reportedly stepping down as the game’s return remains in limbo
    • Ryan Ellis told staff at Firewalk Studios last week that he would no longer be serving as game director on Concord and would instead be stepping down into a support role

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo and The Pokémon Company are suing Palworld developer Pocketpair
    • "This lawsuit seeks an injunction against infringement and compensation for damages on the grounds that Palworld, a game developed and released by the Defendant, infringes multiple patent rights."
  • Minecraft to end PSVR support from March next year
    • Mojang says PlayStation players will still receive updates to play without VR
  • AI is "the very core of our business", says EA CEO
    • With over a hundred AI projects to assist development
    • Free Games / Xbox GP + Gold / PS+ / NSO
  • Nintendo has added 4 more SNES games to Switch Online
    • Battletoads Double Dragon
    • Big Run
    • Cosmo Gang The Puzzle
    • Kunio-kun no Dodgeball da yo Zen’in Shūgō!

Business

  • Kabam shuts down Disney Mirrorverse after 2.5 years
    • Ends on December 16th
  • Ubisoft is calling devs back into the office for three days a week
    • Ubisoft says various global teams were already coming back to the office but will impose new rules to 'align the rest of the group.'
  • Team17 wants to dip into the 'M&A market' less than a year after layoffs
    • The company has spotted a 'clear opportunity to acquire quality assets' including 'established IP.'
    • M&A = Mergers and Acquisitions

Rumors

  • Alleged images of Nintendo’s new Switch have appeared online
    • The images have been debunked and then subsequently undebunked constantly over the past 24 hours. There's strong evidence for both arguments
    • There is no consistency or verdict on if this is real or fake
    • The PCBs in the images hint at the fact that these are old images of prototypes of the Switch 2

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• First-ever official Shovel Knight concert announced
    • Tickets are now on sale for two US performance in early 2025
  • Lego Fortnite heads into uncharted territory with huge Lost Isles expansion
    • This is only the second Lego Fortnite expansion that includes a battle pass (the first being Star Wars in May).
  • Netflix's latest animated Witcher movie delayed into 2025
    • Sirens of the Deep features Doug Cockle
    • Now releases "11th February 2025"
  • The Sims 5 not happening as EA moves "beyond linear, sequential Sims releases"
    • Sims 4 will receive "new expansions, updates, fixes and modernizations"
    • Project Rene has not been cancelled but will now site alongside Sims 4
    • Community creators (packs made with community made assets) will also be provided for Sims 4
    • The Sims 4's release schedule continues this Thursday, 19th September, with the launch of its Artist Studio and Storybook Nursery DLC, while the still-mysterious Life and Death Expansion Pack arrives on 31st October. Additionally, EA previously confirmed MySims and MySims Kingdom are heading to Switch as a two-game bundle on 19th November
  • EA's free-to-play Skate revival is heading into early access next year
    • Skate will be available on PlayStation, Xbox, PC (including Steam), and mobile devices when its early access begins in 2025
  • Epic Game Store (and Fortnite and Fall Guys) now available in EU on iPad
  • Dead by Daylight spin-off Project T has been cancelled
    • The co-op PVE shooter was announced in May
    • The game was in early development at Midwinter Entertainment, which was acquired by Behaviour Interactive in 2022 following the release of its debut title, Scavengers.

Free Games / Subscription

  • Games leaving PS+ "Soon"
    • Ultra Street Fighter 4
    • Gotham Knights
    • LittleBigPlanet 3
    • Dragon Quest XI S
    • Dragon Quest Builders 2
    • Dragon Quest Builders
    • Dragon Quest Heroes 2
    • Dragon Quest Heroes
    • Toukiden: Kiwami
    • The Evil Within
  • September’s PlayStation Plus Game Catalogue and Classics titles are now live
    • Plus Extra and Premium
      • The Plucky Squire | PS5
      • Under The Waves | PS4/5
      • Night in the Woods | PS4/5
      • Chernobylite | PS4/5
      • Wild Card Football | PS4/5
      • Space Engineers | PS4/5
      • Road 96 | PS4/5
      • Ben 10 | PS4
      • Far Cry 5 | PS4
    • Premium
      • Pistol Whip | PS VR2
      • Secret Agent Clank | PS4/5
      • Sky Gunner | PS4/5
      • Mister Mosquito | PS4/5
  • More Xbox Game Pass titles for September revealed
    • Wargroove 2 (Cloud, Console, and PC) – September 19 Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
    • Frostpunk 2 (PC) – September 20 Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
    • Ara: History Untold (PC) – September 24 Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass

Business

  • Scopely cancels unannounced project, begins transitioning team to other work
    • Scopely says it has "every intention" of keeping on employees from the project.
    • In a statement to Game Developer, a Scopely spokesperson indicated no staff had been laid off at time of writing, and said it intends to move impacted developers to other teams within the studio
      • The statement claims employees on the project will be moved onto Scopely's version of "The Bench"
  • Former Capcom, Ubisoft, WayForward, and Riot devs form Pahdo Labs
    • First project, co-op roguelike action RPG Starlight Re:Volver, is set to debut next year
  • TouchArcade to shut down
    • Lack of financial support cited as reason for closure
    • The website ran for 16 years
  • Lighthouse Games moves to new office in Leamington Spa
    • Developer will occupy a new 20,000 square office space across four floors
  • Team17 H1 Results
    • Revenue: £80.6 million, up 11% year-on-year
    • Gross profit: £32.9 million, up 9% year-on-year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Italian police smash counterfeit retro games ring worth £42m (€50m/$55.5m)
    • The pirated material included 12,000 counterfeit "Nintendo, Sega, and Atari" consoles holding 47m fake versions of retro games like Mario Bros., Street Fighter, and Star Wars.
  • First details on EA’s new Battlefield revealed, including a modern setting
    • The game will ditch the 128-player matches and specialists featured in Battlefield 2042
    • While EA hasn’t confirmed a launch window for the game, Respawn and Infinity Ward co-founder Vince Zampella said some players will be able to sample it in some form next year.
  • Nintendo announces end of regular updates for Splatoon 3
    • Two years after launch, Splatoon 3 will now only see themed "events" return

Business

  • IBM wins $44.9m in infringement lawsuit against Zynga
    • Jury found in favour of IBM, which says Zynga's Farmville game breached patents
    • IBM convinced a Delaware jury that mobile developer Zynga infringed its web-based communications patents with its hugely successful mobile game, Farmville, as well as several spin-off titles.
    • Zynga owner Take Two says the patents are now expired and that it will appeal (they weren't expired when the supposed infringing took place)
  • VR studio nDreams is set to lay off up to 17.5% of its staff
    • "For now, our priority is to support our people in this process"
    • On LinkedIn, nDreams lists its headcount as between 201 and 500 employees, intimating anywhere between 35 and 88 jobs are at risk at "all levels" of the organisation.

Rumors

  • Sony has contracted AMD for PlayStation 6, report claims
    • Sony has contracted AMD to design and fabricate the PlayStation 6’s chip, due to the company’s prioritisation of backwards compatibility, it’s claimed.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Watch Dogs movie finishes filming
    • The film was announced nearly a decade ago.
    • It's unclear whether this Watch Dogs film will be an original tale or based on any of the three main Watch Dogs games released so far.
    • No release date yet or hint how long post production will take.
  • Original Flappy Bird creator denounces crypto-backed re-release
    • “No, I have no [relation] with their game. I did not sell anything. I also don’t support crypto.”

Business

  • Penny's Big Breakaway studio Evening Star announces another half a dozen layoffs
    • 6 team members are affected
  • "Core team" of Immortals of Aveum developers joins Rockstar co-founder Dan Houser's Absurd Ventures
    • Following a difficult year for Immortals of Aveum developer Ascendant Studios, a "core team" of around 20 employees has now decamped to Rockstar co-founder Dan Houser's Absurd Ventures, establishing a new studio that'll be known as Absurd Marin.
    • A month after Immortals of Aveum released, Ascendent Studios announced it had made the "painfully difficult, but necessary" decision to lay off 45 percent of its staff, equating to around 40 employees.
      • Earlier this year, it was reported a "large portion" of the remaining team had been furloughed, leaving the future of Ascendant in doubt. But now, Houser's Absurd Ventures has announced it's taken at least some of the studio under its wing.
    • As for Ascendant Studios, no official notice of its closure has been shared, so its current status remains uncertain. But having lost its founder and 20 employees from an already substantially diminished team.
  • Embracer’s potential sale cancelled for Metro’s 4A Games and Pinball dev Zen Studios
    • The pair of studios had been included as an option in the $247 million sale of the Saber Interactive division to a group of private investors. However, on Friday Embracer confirmed that the deal had now been settled and the option to include 4A and Zen in the deal cancelled.
    • The developers will now remain at Embracer. 4A has two ‘AAA’ titles in development, according to Embracer.

Rumors

  • Diablo 4 has reportedly earned Blizzard $150m from microtransactions
    • That's according to senior product manager, Harrison Froeschke, who stated in a since-nuked LinkedIn account that they had led the "monetisation strategy of the store cosmetics, pricing, bundle offers, personalised discounts, and roadmap planning which have driven over $150m [microtransaction] lifetime revenue".

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Flappy Bird is returning to mobile, more than a decade after it was delisted
    • The original creator is not involved.
    • Gametech Holdings LLC waited for the trademark to lapse to take the Flappy Bird trademark from Dong Nguyen Ha without paying him: <https://ttabvue.uspto.gov/ttabvue/v?pnam=Dong%20Nguyen%20Ha%20%20>
    • The team behind it claim to be "super fans of the original", doing the for the love of the game. They have added additional modes and various forms of monetizations to the game.
  • Planet Coaster 2 has release date confirmed
    • 6th November 2024 for PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series S/X
  • Unity has cancelled its controversial Runtime Fee
    • Instead, it will start charging more for Unity Pro and Enterprise subscriptions.
    • While Unity Personal will remain free (and its revenue and funding ceiling has been raised from $100,000 to $200,000), Unity Pro subscriptions will rise 8% to $2,200 per seat annually and Unity Enterprise subscriptions will rise by 25%.
  • PS5’s new system update is out now, adding Welcome hub and Party Share
    • The welcome hub will roll out to the US before rolling out to other territories
    • Party Share, which allows users to share party voice chat links over messenger apps has released.
    • Personalized 3D audio profiles for headphones and earbuds, adaptive charging for controllers (designed to save power), and more remote play settings are also included in the latest update.

Business

  • Annapurna Interactive’s entire workforce has resigned
    • Annapurna Interactive president Nathan Gary and his team resigned following a dispute with the group’s owner, Megan Ellison.
    • Following discussions to spin the video game publishing arm out into an independent entity, of which Ellison pulled out, two dozen members of staff resigned from the company.
    • “All 25 members of the Annapurna Interactive team collectively resigned,’’ Gary said in a joint statement. “This was one of the hardest decisions we have ever had to make and we did not take this action lightly.”
    • Annapurna (the parent company) has been looking to more closely integrate games with its TV and film divisions, and has rehired co-founder Hector Sanchez from Epic Games as president of interactive and new media.
  • Microsoft is cutting another 650 staff from its gaming teams
    • Phil spencer claims "no games, devices or experiences" will be cancelled as a result of the cults
    • The majority of the cuts are in corporate and support roles, focused mainly around the Activision Blizzard buy-out / staff
    • Xbox has cut more than 2,500 jobs since it completed its $69bn acquisition of Activision Blizzard.
  • Borderlands movie only made $31m at global box office
    • Its production budget was reportedly $115 million

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Steam’s improved family sharing is out now for everyone
    • The Steam Families feature lets you share games with multiple people, manage parental controls, and approve requests to buy games.
    • Up to five family members can be invited into a group, which will grant access to a shared family library. Each person can create their own save files and collect their own achievements for games in the family library
    • The marquee development with the new family feature is that multiple people within the group will be able to play games from the shared library at the same time. And if you own multiple copies of a game, then multiple people can be playing that title at once. The caveat with family sharing is that a game developer may opt not to support the feature
  • Super Smash Bros. director "so sorry" to fans playing for thousands of hours
    • "The time players spend in a game is a cost."
  • Dead Island 2 getting zombie-infested co-op horde mode next month
    • In addition to this new horde mode, the forthcoming update will also include New Game Plus. This will allow players to head back to LA (or, Hell A, as the Dead Island 2 team call it) for another crack at the main story, but with an additional skill slot and an increased level cap.
    • This free update will be arriving on 22nd October, and will coincide with the release of Dead Island 2's Ultimate Edition. This edition will include the base game, as well as both story expansions (Haus and SoLA) and a number of weapons and skin packs
  • Xbox is bringing back friend requests
    • Microsoft will begin testing a new friends and followers experience next week
  • Payday 3’s director has been demoted following the game’s troubled first year
    • Former game director Miodrag Kovačević is now serving as a senior game designer on the project, according to his LinkedIn profile, while Andreas Häll Penninger and Almir Listo will be “the creative force behind the upcoming updates”.
    • Penninger was a lead producer on Payday 3 and a producer on its predecessor, while Listo is global brand director and head of community for the series.
  • Konami confirms MGS Master Collection Volume 2, is considering how to handle the ‘really big titles’
    • "It's going to be a little while before we get a clear picture," says Konami producer, in relation to the fact that MGS2 and 3 had pre-existing ports and the remaining games in the series do not have existing ports.
  • Elden Ring’s latest patch nerfs the final boss in Shadow of the Erdtree
    • Other changes include balance adjustments to PvP, equipment, weapon arts, spells, incantations and numerous fixes for bugs and performance issues.

Free Games / Subscription

  • September’s PlayStation Plus Game Catalogue and Classics titles revealed
    • All of the games listed below will be available beginning on September 17.
      • Extra & Premium
        • The Plucky Squire | PS5
        • Under The Waves | PS4/5
        • Night in the Woods | PS4/5
        • Chernobylite | PS4, PS5
        • Wild Card Football | PS4/5
        • Space Engineers | PS4/5
        • Road 96 | PS4/5
        • Ben 10 | PS4
        • Far Cry 5 | PS4
      • Premium
        • Pistol Whip | PS VR2
        • Secret Agent Clank | PS4/5
        • Sky Gunner | PS4/5
        • Mister Mosquito | PS4/5

Business

  • Nexon spins off Dave the Diver dev Mintrocket into a full corporation
    • Mintrocket will still be supported by Nexon, which wants to 'expand' the studio in the wake of Dave the Diver's massive success
    • The "indie" corporation's Dave the Diver was a critical and commercial darling for Nexon last year, and managed to sell 1 million copies over a week after launch. It's now sold 4 million copies, following on from the 3 million milestone in January.
  • Unrecord dev Drama Studios raises $2.5 million in seed round
    • The French studio plans to put its new money toward making a demo for Unrecord and grow its staff before securing a publishing partner
  • Cypher Games raises $10m in latest fundraising round
    • The Turkish developer says it marks a key milestone as it develops its "very first match-3 title to disrupt the mobile gaming industry."
    • Led by The Raine Group, the round secured funding from Play Ventures, 500 Global, Riccardo Zacconi (founder of King), Humam Sakhnini (former vice-chairman of Activision Blizzard) and Akin Babayigit (co-founder of Tripledot).
  • Studio co-founded by Dr Disrespect confirms ‘significant’ layoffs
    • Job losses at Midnight Society come months after the streamer was fired
    • No specific number of job losses published, but the term "significant" was brought up

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• The Crew 2 and The Crew Motorfest will get offline modes, Ubisoft confirms
    • The original series entry went offline in April and is no longer playable
  • Sony announces digital-only PlayStation 5 Pro, costs £699.99/$699.99/€799.99
    • Includes a 2TB SSD, a DualSense wireless controller and a copy of Astro's Playroom pre-installed
    • Does not come with a vertical stand (£24.99) or disc drive (£99.99)
    • Includes improvements to GPU, Hardware raytracing and also includes "PSSR" (PlayStation Spectral Super Resolution, an AI-driven upscaling that uses a machine learning-based technology)

Free Games / Subscription

  • The new Xbox Game Pass Standard tier is now available for all
    • The latest membership option has replaced the "Game Pass for Console" tier
      • Games now NOT AVAIABLE available in the new version of the tier:
        • Age of Mythology: Retold Standard Edition
        • Another Crab's Treasure
        • Botany Manor
        • Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 3
        • Core Keeper
        • Creatures of Ava
        • Diablo 4
        • Dungeons Of Hinterberg
        • Eiyuden Chronicle: Hundred Heroes
        • Flintlock: The Siege of Dawn
        • Flock
        • Forza Motorsport Standard Edition
        • Galacticare
        • Go Mecha Ball
        • Harold Halibut
        • Hauntii
        • Heavy Weapon
        • Humanity
        • Kunitsu-Gami: Path of the Goddess
        • Lightyear Frontier (Game Preview)
        • Little Kitty, Big City
        • Magical Delicacy
        • MLB The Show 24
        • Neon White
        • Octopath Traveller 2
        • Open Roads
        • Payday 3
        • PlateUp!
        • Robin Hood - Sherwood Builders
        • Rolling Hills: Make Sushi, Make Friends
        • Sea of Solitude
        • Senua's Saga: Hellblade II
        • Slime Rancher 2
        • SpiderHeck
        • Star Trucker
        • Starfield
        • Still Wakes the Deep
        • Tchia
        • The Case of the Golden Idol
        • The Rewinder
        • Turnip Boy Robs a Bank
        • Valorant
        • You Suck at Parking - Complete Edition

Business

  • Warhammer 40K: Space Marine 2 hits 2 million players in 24 hours
  • Lost Boys Interactive to lay off 139 staff
    • Staff from 28 US states were affected, and are due to be "paid regular wages equivalent to two months" following the end of their employement.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• PS5 Pro name seemingly confirmed by Sony ahead of imminent reveal
    • Sony accidentally uploaded an unlisted video titled PS5 Pro
    • Technical presentation with Mark Cerny is today at 8am PDT / 11am EDT / 4pm BST
  • Bungie announces new Destiny 2 release model with two ‘medium’ expansions a year
  • Disgruntled Team Fortress 2 players bind 340,000 signatures into a book and hand-deliver it to Valve
    • #SaveTF2 was born in 2022, amid a bot situation so severe, it was threatening to make Valve's classic team-based shooter literally unplayable. The community faced daily battle bots using game-ruining hacks, racist usernames, disruptive noises in voice chat, and worse - and with Valve doing little to help, players even took to fighting bots with bots of their own.
  • Apple says all its new iPhone 16s play AAA games, no iPhone Pro required
    • iOS 18 out next week with new Game mode
  • Minecraft ditching single summer update for more regular releases, mob votes being retired

Business

  • Ubisoft shares plunge again after investor urges company to go private
    • Recent releases including Star Wars Outlaws and xDefinant have reportedly missed expectations
    • Share price fell almost 10% during trading, before closing down 7.13% at €13.67. It has fallen by more than 50% over the last 12 months and now sits close to a ten-year low.
  • Embracer studio Lost Boys Interactive has made another round of layoffs
    • The support studio's credits include Tiny Tina's Wonderlands, Diablo 4 and PUBG
    • Lost Boys, which also suffered a heavy round of layoffs in January, employs over 200 staff across more than 40 US states and Canada, according to its website.
    • Unclear at this time how many people are affected

Rumors

  • PS5 Pro details expected at Technical Presentation
    • Third Party state of play focused on games that benefit from PS5 Pro due September 19th
    • PS5 Pro supports HDR10+ and Dolby Vision on select streaming services and physical media
    • PS5 Pro Digital due Nov 14th for £499.99
    • PS5 Pro "Deluxe" (Disc variant) due December 5th for £589.99
    • Improved Dualsense with better battery life and enhanced triggers

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Roblox rolls out higher-rev share plan for devs making premium games
    • Roblox developers making pay-to-access "experiences" can now earn a higher revenue share than before.
    • This increase applies to games that are purchased with "real currency value" on desktop. If you make a game priced at $9.99, $29.99, or $49.99, your revenue share will respectively rise to 50 percent, 60 percent, and 70 percent.
    • The company says it has plans to expand the increase to "other supported devices" in the future.

Business

  • Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund to transfer its gaming stock to Savvy Games Group
    • Going forward, the country's PIF is set to expand its involvement in games beyond investments to "active collaboration," even though it's unclear exactly what that means at this stage.
    • Over the past few years Saudi Arabia's PIF has been increasing its stake in Nintendo among other Japanese firms, and currently holds 8.58% of shares in the Mario firm.
    • It also acquired stakes in Capcom and Nexon in 2022, which it has since increased to 6.6% and 10.23% respectively. It also owns a 8.97% stake in Koei Tecmo.
    • Outside of Japan, the PIF has stakes in EA (now 9.34%), Take Two (6.52%), and Activision Blizzard.
  • Developers behind 80 games sign deals with SAG-AFTRA
