    • Named studios included Studio Wildcard (the team behind ARK Survival Evolved), Little Bat Games (an indie studio currently working on its debut title, Vampire Therapist), and Francisco Gonzales (the developer of a series of retro-style point-and-click adventures, including Rosewater and Lamplight City).
  • Until Dawn remake developer Ballistic Moon confirms layoffs
    • The UK studio has spent years working on a 'rebuilt and enhanced' version of the horror title.
    • The total number affected still hasn't been revealed

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Konami confirms that Inter and AC Milan will be exclusive to eFootball this year
    • Both will have generic team names, kits and badges in EA Sports FC 25, joining Lazio and Atalanta
  • PlayStation’s 30th anniversary celebrations include Spotify soundtracks and a board game
    • Starting in October and running until January, the company plans to release digital soundtracks on Spotify for the first time.
    • The soundtracks for God of War, God of War II, God of War: Ghost of Sparta, Twisted Metal, Starhawk and Unit 13 will be made available on the streaming service, and can be bought on numerous digital music storefronts.
    • Sony will also launch a new series of physical products called Shapes of Play, which it says has been “crafted by the team behind the design of PlayStation consoles, giving you opportunities to create fun moments with our iconic shapes”.
    • These include Shapes of Play: Battle, a board game “where you can challenge a friend to line up four different shapes of the same color on the board to win”.
    • This will be joined by Shapes of Play: Create (a set of magnetic blocks which can be built into various shapes) and Shapes of Play: Recharge (which appear to be stress balls of some kind).
  • Action-packed Arcane season two trailer teases plenty of chaos from Jinx
    • Coming "November"
  • Balatro out on mobile later this month
    • The game will be available on both the iOS App store and the Google Play store from 26th September.
    • Balatro+ also coming to Apple Arcade (+ games on Apple Arcade have no ads or microtransactions)
  • Alan Wake (2010) will receive an update on September 10th that removes the song Space Oddity from the game due to changes in licensing, and replaces it with a new original song by Petri Alanko, Strange Moons
    • Only the original PC release is affected. Alan Wake Remastered is also unaffected.

Business

  • Street Fighter 6 has sold over 4 million units globally

Rumors

  • Concord episode will reportedly remain in Amazon's upcoming Secret Level series despite recent shutdown

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• A playable prototype of a cancelled PlayStation 2 game (The Last Job aka The Heist) has been found and shared online
    • The game was being developed at Acclaim’s Cheltenham studio, and was said to be around 40% complete when Acclaim went bankrupt and its projects were cancelled.
    • Planned for PS2 and Xbox, the game was to have players taking control of four criminals, each with their own special abilities.
  • The live-action Minecraft movie has received its first trailer
    • Film stars Jack Black and Jason Momoa
    • Release April 2025
  • Helldivers 2's long-awaited third enemy faction briefly appears on in-game map
    • But studio dismisses sightings as "fake news"
  • Cyberpunk 2077 fans pledge $2m to make official board game happen
    • Project hit $100k crowdfunding goal in 10 minutes
  • Japan's National Museum of Nature and Science adds PS2 to Future Technology Heritage registry
    • It's the first console celebrated for its cultural impact

Business

  • Ubisoft’s share price has fallen to a near 10-year low since the release of Star Wars Outlaws
    • Share Price: €15.20, lowest since 2015.
    • Historical High: €102.95 in July 2018.
    • Sales Expectations: Revised from 7.5 million to 5.5 million units by March 2025.
  • Riot adjusts regional pricing of virtual currency across its live games
    • Riot Games is altering regional pricing of its virtual currency, with one country seeing increases over 70%.
    • Announced yesterday, the developer said these changes have been made in order to "better balance prices between regions."
    • The United States will see no change in pricing, while Ukraine will experience increases of between 64% and 74% across all games. For League of Legends and Teamfight Tactics specifically, the Philippines will see a 40% rise while Vietnam will observe a 41% increase.
  • Black Myth: Wukong tops $850 million in gross revenue on Steam, with average playtime of 27 hours

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 4 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Sony is taking Concord offline, ceasing sales and offering refunds
    • Once refunded, players will no longer have access to the game, which cost $40 / £35.
    • Unclear if Concord will return
    • No word right now on what is going to happen to the upcoming Secret Level episode dedicated to Concord
  • Marvel vs. Capcom Fighting Collection now coming to Xbox following "technical discussions" with Microsoft
    • Capcom Fighting Collection 2 is also coming
    • Previously, it was believed there were technical issues with Capcom's engine that meant the games "couldn't" be ported to Xbox
  • Borderlands' Randy Pitchford defends recent flops by comparing Gearbox to The Beatles
    • Replying to a user who had commented on the developer's recent "judgement and shot calling ability" (Borderlands movie and Risk of Rain 2 DLC, Pitchford stated he was "going to keep making stuff. The greatest musical act of all time, The Beatles, had a 25 percent hit rate."
  • Co-creator of Wizardry, Andrew C. Greenberg, has died aged 67
    • Wizardry is cited as the inspiration behind a list of seminal RPGs, including Fire Emblem, Final Fantasy, and Dragon Quest.

Free Games / Subscription

  • September’s PlayStation Plus Essential games are now available
    • Harry Potter: Quidditch Champions (PS4/5)
    • MLB The Show 24 (PS/5)
    • Little Nightmares 2 (PS4/5)
  • New Xbox Game Pass titles for console, PC and Cloud have been revealed
    • Arriving:
      • Star Trucker (Cloud, Console, and PC) – September 3
      • Age of Mythology: Retold (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – September 4
      • Expeditions: A MudRunner Game (Cloud, Console, and PC) – September 5
      • Riders Republic (Cloud, Console, and PC) – September 11
      • Train Sim World 5 (Cloud, Console, and PC) – September 17
    • Leaving (September 15th)
      • Ashes of Singularity: Escalation (PC)
      • FIFA 23 (Cloud, Console, and PC) EA Play
      • Payday 3 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Slime Rancher 2 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • SpiderHeck (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • You Suck At Parking (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Business

  • Tilting Point to lay off around 90 employees
    • Up to 20% of the studio's workforce could be impacted by the redundancies, CEO says
    • The firm currently employs around 450 staff across four US-based offices, two in Europe in Barcelona and Kyiv, and one in Seoul, Korea.
  • Risk of Rain dev Hopoo Games on hiatus as co-founders join Valve
    • Unannounced game 'Snail' now cancelled, other members of the team also working for Half-Life firm

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/09/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 September 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• iOS versions of Resident Evil 4, 7 and Village now need an online connection at startup
    • The update has left some players unable to play the games on the move

Business

  • Helldivers 2 and Dead Island 2 support studio Toadman Interactive confirms layoffs
    • Toadman is owned by Enad Global 7 (EG7) and has worked on notable releases like Helldivers 2, Dead Island 2, Killing Floor Calamity, and Warhammer: End times–Vermintide.
    • "The annual cost savings for Toadman is approximately SEK 63 million ($6.1 million) and will affect approximately 100 full-time and contract resources [headcount] previously engaged with Toadman," reads the report.
    • Some positions at the Immortal Unchained developer's Berlin office are also at risk.
  • Thomas Koegler appointed CEO of Asmodee
    • Former deputy COO will also join Embracer's executive management team
    • Kogler joined the French board game publisher in 2015 where he held various roles, most recently as the company's deputy COO. He will also replace Stéphane Carville on Embracer's executive management team.
  • Rocksteady hit by layoffs after Suicide Squad: Kill the Justice League underperforms
    • One staff member made redundant while on paternity leave
    • There has also been an acknowledgement by Rocksteady's senior management that product quality will now suffer as a result, staff say.
    • Staff members affected by the job cuts include numerous junior staff, but also several team members employed at Rocksteady for more than five years.
  • Nintendo of Europe president steps down after six years
    • Stephan Bole has been replaced by Luciano Pereña, who is now the company's CEO, president and chairman

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Konami acknowledges that Metal Gear Solid 4 is still only playable on PS3
    • When asked about it, producer Noriaki Okamura said "with [Metal Gear Solid] Vol 1 containing MGS 1-3 dot dot dot… you can probably connect the dots"

Business

  • Annapurna is co-financing Control 2, will bring Remedy IP including Alan Wake to film and TV
    • Annapurna will give 50% of the budget for Control 2
    • Remedy will receive a greater share of game sales revenue after initial investments are recouped, while Annapurna will receive a greater share from audiovisual productions
  • Harry Potter: Magic Awakened to shut down
    • Officially close its operations in the Americas, Europe and Oceania territories on October 29, 2024 and will be removed from app stores on August 26, 2024
  • Games Done Quick officially under new ownership
    • Matt Merkle now owns majority of GDQ, leadership team includes Jason Deng and Ashley Farkas
  • Final Fantasy: Brave Exvius will end global service on 30th October
    • The game was released worldwide in 2016, following its Japanese launch in 2015. The Japanese version will remain in service.
    • In-game events are scheduled to be continually updated until the app service end on 30th October.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• A remaster of Croc: Legend of the Gobbos is coming this year
    • Argonauts Games is back, this time as a "Boutique Publisher"
    • Croc: Legend of the Gobbos will come to console and PC in 2024

Free Games / Subscription

  • PlayStation Plus monthly games for September
    • Quidditch Champions (PS4, PS5)
    • MLB The Show 24 (PS4, PS5)
    • Little Nightmares 2 (PS4, PS5)

Business

  • CD Projekt Financial Results (First Half 2024-2025)
    • Revenue Growth: CD Projekt’s revenue reached 424.8 million PLN ($109.8 million), 30.6% YoY.
    • Net Profit: Net profit rose to 170 million PLN, 88.4% increase.
    • Financial Reserves: Reserves grew to over 55 million PLN.
    • Future Projects: Over 150 million PLN allocated for future projects, including the next Witcher game and Cyberpunk sequel.
    • The Witcher 4 is set to enter production soon, Cyberpunk Orion in "Planning" phase
  • Unionised staff at COD studio Raven Software says Activision is stalling over contract talks
    • Unionised staff at Call of Duty studio Raven Software have filed a complaint with the National Labor Relations Board after claims that Activision and Microsoft are stalling over contract negotiations.

Rumors

  • Bungie's former Marathon reboot director Chris Barrett reportedly ousted following inappropriate behaviour
    • Was replaced by Joe Ziegler in March

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Yakuza Kiwami coming to Switch on October 24
    • Yakuza Kiwami first launched for PlayStation 4 and PlayStation 3 on January 21, 2016 in Japan, followed by PlayStation 4 on August 29, 2017 worldwide, PC via Steam on February 19, 2019, Xbox One on April 21, 2020, and Luna on December 22, 2022
    • Will be available via Nintendo eShop for $19.99 on October 24th
  • Blue Protocol will be discontinued on January 18th, 2025 in Japan and will no longer release worldwide
    • Players cite the disappointing end-game loop and loop monetization options as the primary reasons for the cancelled launch.
  • Flame Fatales raises $117,932 for Malala Fund
  • Balatro's free collaboration update adds The Witcher, Vampire Survivors, Among Us, and more
    • Available now
    • Balatro's Friends of Jimbo update, as it's officially known, is available now and gives player the option to swap out the game's usual Kings, Queens, and Jacks with characters from The Witcher, Dave the Diver, Among Us and Vampire Survivors
  • PowerWash Simulator heading to Shrek's fairytale world this autumn
    • Developer FuturLab's Shrek Special Pack promises five new "fairytale" maps inspired by the Dreamworks' currently four-strong animated movie series, with levels revealed so far including Shrek's Swamp and the Dragon's Lair.
    • Coming "this autumn" to all platforms. No price given yet.
  • Pokémon Go finally confirms its gigantic Dynamax feature
    • Gen 8 Starter Pokémon that debuted in Pokémon Sword and Shield will join Pokémon Go at the beginning of the next season. This includes Grass-type monkey Grookey, Fire-type rabbit Scorbunny, Water-type frog thing Sobble, plus Dragon-type - Dreepy and the Stonehenge-esque Stonjourner (which, excitingly for UK players, will be a regional exclusive).
    • Already-released Gen 8 Pokémon Skwovet and Wooloo will become available in their Shiny versions.
    • Dynamax will effectively add a whole new method of releasing Pokémon to the game - in the same way Shadow Pokémon did previously. Only new Pokémon caught from Max Battles, as well as their evolutions, will be able to Dynamax - pushing you to collect these new creatures as they're released.
  • New Sonic the Hedgehog 3 film trailer reveals Keanu Reeves' Shadow
    • The release of Sonic the Hedgehog 3 on 20th December will round off Sega's Year of Shadow, which also includes Lego sets, a tour of the Shadow motorcycle, and the release in October of Sonic x Shadow Generations.
  • Two Wii games from the MySims series are getting re-released on Switch
    • MySims: Cozy Bundle offers MySims and MySims Kingdom for $39.99 / £34.99
    • Launches on Switch November 19
  • Capcom Fighting Collection 2 features 8 games including Capcom vs SNK and Power Stone 1 & 2
    • Capcom vs SNK: Millennium Fight 2000 Pro
    • Capcom vs SNK 2: Mark of the Millennium 2001
    • Project Justice
    • Capcom Fighting Evolution
    • Street Fighter Alpha 3 Upper
    • Plasma Sword: Nightmare of Bilstein
    • Power Stone
    • Power Stone 2
    • The compilation will be released on Switch, PS4 and PC in 2025. As with other recent Capcom compilations, no Xbox version has been announced.
    • All eight games will feature online play with rollback netcode, as well as a training mode.
    • While announcing the new compilation, Capcom also confirmed that Marvel vs Capcom Fighting Collection: Arcade Classics will be released digitally on September 12 and physically on November 22
  • Genshin Impact Update 5.0 Launches Today
    • New Region; Natlan, and new characters added
  • Digital Eclipse is releasing a Tetris collection called Tetris Forever
    • An “accurate recreation” of the original version on the Electronika 60 computer
    • The 1988 Famicom version
    • Tetris 2 + Bombliss
    • Tetris Battle Gaiden
    • Hatris
    • Super Bombliss
    • Super Bombliss DX
    • Super Tetris 2 + Bombliss
    • Super Tetris 3
    • Releases for "all major platforms" at some point in 2024
  • Square Enix’s Foamstars is officially going free to play in October
    • The game underperformed commercially, according to Square Enix
    • The Square Enix game, which was previously being sold on the PlayStation Store for $30 / £25, will be free to all users from October 4.
    • The game, which is still exclusive to Sony‘s consoles, was released earlier this year. The game was available to PlayStation Plus members at launch.

Business

  • Service provider PTW has acquired co-development studio Ghostpunch Games for $13 million
    • The Florida-based studio has worked on notable titles including Risk of Rain 2, Fortnite, and Mortal Kombat 1
    • Notably, PTW believes the studio's vast experience can be combined with its own "deep QA knowledge" to offer new AI-based QA automation and other services to clients in the future.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo Indie World and Partner Showcase Today
    • 3pm UK Time / 7am Pacific / 10am Eastern
    • No Switch successor announcements
    • 40-minute broadcast to focus on third-party and indie games
    • Combined Nintendo Direct Partner Showcase, which focuses on big games from non-Nintendo developers, and an Indie World Showcase, which spotlights projects from smaller creative teams
  • Taiko no Tatsujin: Rhythm Festival launches November 7 on Steam, PS5, and Xbox Series S/X
    • 120 fps mode
    • New collab characters
    • Taiko Music Pass available at launch (700+ song subscription service)
    • 76 built-in songs (Include Decisive Battle and Cruel Angel's Thesis from Evangelion)
    • Pre-Order comes with Namco Game Music Pack
    • No word on if controllers will be sold for this version of the game Update: Special Controllers available: https://x.com/Wario64/status/1828247945008857179
    • First time Taiko no Tatsujin is on Steam (outside of the three song "arcade" version in Yakuza 5)

Business

  • Black Myth: Wukong has sold 10 million copies worldwide
    • Milestone reached in five days since release

Rumors

  • LEGO Horizon Adventures is launching November 14
    • Information accidentally posted early on the Playstation home page entry for the game

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Here's an 18-minute behind-the-scenes documentary about how Amazon's Fallout show was made
  • Heroes of Might & Magic: Olden Era arrives next year
    • Ubisoft is resurrecting Heroes of Might & Magic for the series' 30th anniversary
    • Available as a Steam early access title
  • Valve officially announces Deadlock, weeks after footage started appearing online
  • Avowed is ‘targeting 30 FPS’, says Obsidian art director
    • “You don’t necessarily need that 60 frames”
  • Sonic 3 movie social media account teases Keanu Reeves as the voice of Shadow
    • The official Sonic the Hedgehog movie account appears to be teasing that Keanu Reeves will play Shadow in the upcoming third movie.

Business

  • Gamescom 2024 had 335,000 visitors this year
    • Visitors came from 120 countries
    • Number went up from 320,000 last year
    • The Opening Night Live show doubled got 40 million video views, double that of 2023
  • Visual Concepts lays off staff at Lego 2K Drive South studio

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 22/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Lego confirms six all-new Mario Kart sets are on the way
    • Launching on New Year's Day 2025, Lego says "each set has been designed to ensure that anyone can enjoy the joy of the Super Mario universe" and includes interactive elements such as audio and visual effects.
  • Digital-only Xbox Series X release date announced, pre-orders open
    • The new batch of Xbox consoles will be released on 15th October in select countries then releasing in another batch of countries following on 29th October
      • Xbox Series S 1TB in Robot White - £299.99
      • Xbox Series X 1TB Digital Edition - £429.99
      • Xbox Series X 2TB Galaxy Black Special Edition - £549.99
  • New Xbox accessibility products revealed, including the Adaptive Joystick
    • Xbox Adaptive Joystick, 3D Printable Files for Adaptive Thumbstick Toppers, 8BitDo Lite SE 2.4G Wireless Controller, ByoWave Proteus Controller
  • Nintendo is ending service for another mobile game: Animal Crossing
    • A new version of Pocket Camp will launch in November to preserve Save Data
    • From November 29, 2024 – seven years after Pocket Camp launched – the current app will end service. Players will no longer be able to purchase microtransactions, and the monthly subscription service will also end ahead of this date.
  • Three new Lego Animal Crossing sets are coming next year
    • Lego Animal Crossing Stargazing with Celeste (€9,99/ $9.99 / £8,99)
    • Lego Animal Crossing Leif’s Caravan & Garden Shop (Price: €29,99/ $29.99 / £24,99)
    • Lego Animal Crossing Able Sisters Clothing Shop (Price: €39,99/ $39.99 / £34,99)
  • Game of Thrones star Gwendoline Christie is the narrator of Civilization VII
  • The Game Awards 2024 date confirmed for December
    • The event’s 10th anniversary gala celebration will take place on December 12th
    • Geoff Keighley's comments about looking ahead "at the next 10 years" have sparked Switch 2 rumours
    • Response online has been mixed due to the treatment of developers at last year's event ("please wrap it up") and the aggressive amount of advertisements

Business

  • ‘There’s going to be more change’: Phil Spencer speaks on Indy PS5 news
    • "Xbox is a business"
    • Exec claims Xbox console player numbers this year are "as high as they've ever been"

Rumors

  • Sea of Thieves has reportedly surpassed one million sales on PS5
    • While Xbox hasn’t announced sales figures for the game on PS5, there’s been plenty of evidence to suggest the $40/£40 title has performed strongly since its release in late April.
    • According to Sony, Sea of Thieves was the most downloaded PS5 game in both the US/Canada and Europe in May. In June, it ranked among the top five downloaded games, and in July it remained in the top ten.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Reikon Games' Ruiner to receive film adaptation by Universal Pictures
    • The 2017 sci-fi shooter's film adaptation is being headed up by Wes Ball, director of Sony's upcoming Zelda movie.
  • Black Myth: Wukong opens to 2.2 million Steam players in one day
    • Beats previous day one records set by Elden Ring and Cyberpunk 2077
  • Atsuko Tanaka – the Japanese voice of Chun-Li and Bayonetta – has died
  • The Finals studio's PvPvE extraction shooter ARC Raiders resurfaces, and it's no longer free-to-play
    • Will be available on Steam, Epic, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series S/X when it launches next year
  • Civilization 7 gets February 2025 release date and first gameplay trailer
    • PlayStation 5, Xbox Series S/X, Switch, and PC on 11th February next year
  • 2K announces Mafia: The Old Country for release in 2025
    • Mafia: The Old Country will be released in 2025 for PS5, Xbox Series S/X and PC via Steam.
    • The game, which is set during the origins of the Sicilian mafia, is in development at 2K’s Hangar 13 studio.
  • Peter Molyneux unveils new god game Masters of Albion
    • Ex-Media Molecule boss Mark Healey is also working on the project
    • The game, which according to Molyneux takes elements from all of his previous work, is descroned as an “open-world God game.”
    • “After messing around on mobile – what the hell was I doing? – I thought to myself, I need to come home to PC and console. So I looked at Dungeon Keeper… I’ve taken some things from Black & White, I’ve done the same with Fable, and this is what I’m showing tonight.”
  • Xbox’s Indiana Jones game is officially coming (back?) to PS5
    • The game will be released first for Xbox and PC on December 9, followed by Sony‘s console in Spring 2025
  • Path of Exile 2 early access release date revealed
    • November 15, 2024 early access for PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC via Steam and the Epic Games Store
  • Amazon reveals series based on Mega Man, Spelunky, Unreal Tournament, and more
    • The anthology series (titled Secret Level, made from the team behind Love, Death & Robots) will include episodes based on:
      • Armored Core
      • Concord
      • Crossfire
      • Dungeons & Dragons
      • Exodus
      • Honor of Kings
      • Mega Man
      • New World: Aeternum
      • PAC-MAN
      • Various PlayStation Studios games (such as Ghosts of Tsushima and God of War - possibly some sort of collab episode)
      • Sifu
      • Spelunky
      • The Outer Worlds
      • Unreal Tournament
      • Warhammer 40,000
    • Secret Level will arrive on Prime Video on December 10
  • Dune Awakening is coming to PC early access in 2025
    • Coming to consoles "at a later date"
  • Marvel Rivals is launching in December, and all heroes will be free
    • December 6, 2024 for Steam / Epic Game Store, PS5, Xbox Series S/X
  • Genshin Impact is officially coming to Xbox
    • Launches November 20 for Xbox Series S/X
    • Will also arrive on Game Pass - though not sure how a free game can be any free-er
  • New Monster Hunter Wilds gameplay shown at Gamescom Opening Night Live
  • A new mini console based on the Atari 7800 has been revealed
    • The Atari 7800+ is billed as a scaled down and faithful recreation of the console that first released in 1986.
    • It will launch worldwide on November 29, 2024, and is now available to pre-order for $129.99 / £99.99 / €119.99.
  • Vampire: The Masquerade – Bloodlines 2 has been delayed again (as in, again, again, again, again)
    • Originally set for release in 2020, the new release date given is "first half of 2025"

Free Games / Subscription

  • New Xbox Game Pass titles for console, PC and Cloud have been announced
    • Arriving:
      • Atlas Fallen (Cloud, PC and Xbox Series S/X) - August 22
      • Core Keeper (Cloud, PC and Xbox Series S/X) - August 27
      • Star Trucker (Cloud, Console and PC) - September 3
    • Leaving (August 31st):
      • Atomic Heart (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Guilty Gear Strive (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • NBA 2K24 (Cloud and Console)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Dead Cells receives its final major update today, seven years after release
    • "The End is Near" is the 35th update to the game
    • The original designer of Dead Cells, Sébastien Benard, formed a new studio and has a new roguelike game on the way called Tenjutsu in which players take the role of a renegade yakuza.
    • Earlier this year, Benard called the decision to end Dead Cells development "the worst imaginable asshole move".
  • Final Fantasy 16 gets a September release date for PC
    • Free demo available to try now
    • Originally released for PS5 in June 2023, it will make the jump to PC on September 17, 2024, with the base game priced at $49.99 / £44.99 via Steam and the Epic Games Store.
    • Final Fantasy 16 Complete Edition, which includes the base game plus its story expansions Echoes of the Fallen and The Rising Tide, is priced at $69.99 / £64.99.
  • Funko Fusion gets a PC demo and Mega Man reveal trailer
    • The free PC demo includes the first level of Funko Fusion’s world inspired by 1982 horror classic The Thing.
    • The game will be released on September 13 for PS5, Xbox Series S/X and PC, and on November 15 for PS4 and Nintendo Switch.
  • Gamescom Opening Night Live today
    • 2 p.m. EDT / 11 a.m. PDT / 7 p.m. BST / 8 p.m. CEST

Business

  • Climax opens new studio in Scotland
    • Climax Studios has opened a new location in Edinburgh.
    • The studio opened its doors in February, and has been recruiting a team over the past few months.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Silent Hill 2 Story Trailer
    • Normally wouldn't post game trailers outside of Special Editions of the Game News Round-Up, but this one is being very well received compared to the previous looks at the Silent Hill 2 Remake
  • Nintendo announces Museum-focused Nintendo Direct
    • Focused solely on the upcoming Nintendo Museum in Kyoto, Japan
    • “Join us tomorrow, August 19 at 3pm PT, for a tour of the Nintendo Museum that’s scheduled to open during the fall of 2024 in Kyoto, Japan!”
    • Video will be 10 minutes long
  • Sega’s 1993 fighting game Eternal Champions is becoming a movie
    • Jurassic World and Detective Pikachu co-writer Derek Connolly is writing the film
    • The film is set to be produced by Skydance, which is set to merge with Paramount Global next year.

Business

  • Adam Boyes steps down as co-CEO of Iron Galaxy
    • Chelsea Blasko will take over as the studio's sole CEO
  • Ubisoft cuts 45 jobs across Cary and San Francisco teams
    • The reductions were spread across US offices located in Cary, North Carolina and San Francisco, CA. A Ubisoft spokesperson said the cuts were made to "align [their] organizations with future business and development objectives."
    • Ubisoft's Cary team (typically known as Red Storm Entertainment) was the lead developer on several Tom Clancy titles over the years. More recently, it released Assassin's Creed Nexus VR and cancelled The Division Heartland.
    • Meanwhile, Ubisoft San Francisco was the lead developer on the Rocksmith series. Other titles under its name include South Park: The Fractured But Whole and the free-to-play shooter xDefiant.
  • Tango Gameworks acquisition will transfer around 50 out of original 100 staff
    • This is because others have since found employment elsewhere, and as such there are a number of job openings at the studio

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Games Done Quick's week-long Flame Fatales charity speedrunning event returns this Sunday
    • Raising money for the Malala Fund
    • Starts Sunday, 16th August at 6:15pm BST, with a Horizon Forbidden West Burning Shores 100% speedrun by RE_doc19
    • Event will last for 7 days and ends on Sunday 25th at 4:06am BST with a Super Mario Odyssey Talkatoo% speedrun by CheeseJay
  • Hugely-promising indie The Plucky Squire launches next month, day one on PlayStation Plus
    • An innovative 2D and 3D platformer based in and around the pages of a picture book, The Plucky Squire is the first project from All Possible Futures, the indie development studio set up by former Pokémon artist James Turner.
    • PC, PlayStation 5, Nintendo Switch and Xbox Series S/X on 17th September
    • It'll also be a day one launch via PlayStation Plus, for anyone signed up to Sony's subscription service.
  • Evercade announces its next cartridge, Legacy of Kain Collection
    • Blood Omen: Legacy of Kain, and Legacy of Kain: Soul Reaver
    • The cartridge can be played on all Evercade and Super Pocket devices, including the upcoming Evercade Alpha tabletop arcade machines.
    • Unrelated to the rumours of a Legacy of Kain remastered collection, apparently in development for current gen consoles.
  • SteamOS support coming to ROG Ally and other handhelds
    • Dual-boot support for SteamOS and Windows is also being prepped for Steam Deck OLED
    • No planned release date for either features yet.
  • Epic Games Store is available now on mobile, including Fall Guys and Fortnite
    • iOS (EU only) Install Instructions (open on device): https://store.epicgames.com/en-US/download/ios
    • Android Install Instructions (open on device): https://store.epicgames.com/en-US/download/android

Business

  • Star Wars Eclipse’s lead writer has left Quantic Dream to start a new studio
    • Adam Williams was also the lead writer on Detriot: Become Human
  • Scottish Games Week skips 2024, promises late 2025 return
    • The industry's current issues and a lack of government support have put the annual Scottish event on ice, but next year's comeback is already underway
  • Reforged Studios acquires Ground Shatter and Extra Mile Studios
    • Ground Shatter is led by founder and director James Parker, and is the developer behind roguelike deck-builder Fights in Tight Spaces.
    • Extra Mile Studios is led by Bobby Farmer, and is known for providing co-developer services on Revolution Software's Broken Swords series.
  • New DDM (Digital Development Management) report says the games industry is "showing signs of a recovery"
    • DDM's Games Investment Review reports "sizeable uptick" in investments, recovering ground lost in 2023
    • DDM suggests the half-yearly uptick suggests in just the first half of 2024, investments totalled $8.1bn – almost doubling the $4.5bn investment volume seen for the whole of 2023.
    • However, though Q2 was "great for games investments," the report authors say M&As reached $845m across 40 transactions, which is "a drastic decline QoQ" (-59% in value and -5% in volume over Q1’s $2.1bn across 43 transactions).
  • Embracer Q1 results
    • Net Sales: SEK 7.9 billion ($754 million, down 24% year-on-year)
    • Net Loss: SEK 2.2 billion ($210 million, compared to loss of SEK 2.3 billion / $219.4 million for the same period last year)
    • Net Debt: SEK 14.3 billion ($1.37 billion, compared to SEK 16.8 billion / $1.6 billion as of June 30, 2023)
    • Tabletop games still generating the bulk of Embracer's revenue, net debt decreased to $1.37 billion

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 15/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 15 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Until Dawn remake launches on PS5 / PC October 4th
  • Square Enix could switch to Unreal Engine 5 for Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3
    • The company is exploring whether changing engines would accelerate development
  • Antstream Arcade is coming to PlayStation consoles
    • The subscription service lets players access a streaming library of over 1,300 retro games.
    • The full list of games available currently stands at 1,357, including 200+ Amiga games, nearly 300 arcade games, 350+ C64 games, 50+ Mega Drive games, 40+ SNES games and 350+ ZX Spectrum games.
    • Players can either buy a year’s subscription to the service for $39.99 / £39.99, or a Lifetime Pass Edition for $99.99 / £99.99.
    • It was also announced that the PS1 Net Yaroze games will soon be coming to Antstream, while Pico-8 developers can now submit their games to be included on the service. The first Pico-8 games will be Puzzles of the Paladin and Manbomber.
    • Release date TBA
  • Kingdom Come Deliverance 2 is officially delayed until February 11th
  • Among Us adds Critical Role cosmetics, ahead of Ashley Johnson's role in series
  • Let's Build a Zoo studio is making a game dev management sim where you design your own MMO
    • Let's Build a Dungeon will release on Xbox and Steam at some point in the future

Free Games / Subscription

  • August’s PlayStation Plus Game Catalogue and Classics titles announced
    • Extra and Premium:
      • The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt | PS4/5
      • Wild Hearts Standard Edition | PS5
      • Cult of the Lamb | PS4/5
      • Ride 5 | PS5
      • Watch Dogs 2 | PS4
      • Sword Art Online: Alicization Lycoris | PS4
      • Sword Art Online: Last Recollection | PS4/5
      • Sword Art Online: Fatal Bullet | PS4
      • Sword Art Online: Hollow Realization | PS4
      • Naruto to Boruto: Shinobi Striker | PS4
    • Premium:
      • Vacation Simulator | PS VR2
      • TimeSplitters | PS4/5
      • TimeSplitters 2 | PS4/5
      • TimeSplitters: Future Perfect | PS4/5
      • Sword Art Online: Lost Song | PS4

Business

  • Supercell appoints new CFO
    • Supercell has appointed Gerard Griffin as its new CFO.
    • Griffin has over 25 years of experience in finance and business leadership roles, having previously assumed senior positions at firms like EA, Zynga, NBC, and Sportradar.
    • Outgoing CFO Janne Snellman will remain at Supercell "with a focus on supporting existing and future growth initiatives like working with investee companies."
  • TerraTech developer Payload Studios is laying off 25 percent of its workforce
    • TerraTech and spin-off TerraTech Worlds will be "maintained and developed without interruption," and implored studios with vacancies to consider those impacted by the layoffs.

Rumors

  • Amazon orders new animated video game anthology series, Secret Level
    • The series will reportedly feature Amazon's New World, Spelunky, and "PlayStation titles"
    • Will be made by the same team behind Love, Death & Robots
    • The show will be produced by Amazon MGM Studios and Tim Miller's Blur studio. Dave Wilson has apparently signed on to serve as supervising director and executive producer.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Helldivers 2 studio reveals 60-day plan of action following recent patch outcry
    • Fans are complaining about the nerfing of key weapons... again.
    • This follows an established cycle now for Helldivers 2 where a new content drop comes out, nerfs the most popular weapons, receives player base outcry, promises to do better next time and the cycle repeats.
  • PlayStation lays out Concord live-service roadmap, commits to at least three seasons
    • Season 1's launch in October will add a new map, character, story scenes and more. Season 2 will bring another new map, character and set of story scenes, plus a fresh mode. Season 3 - for now - remains under wraps.
  • WayForward is turning Halloween and Ash vs Evil Dead into 16-bit style platformers
    • RetroRealms: Halloween and RetroRealms: Ash vs Evil Dead are action platform games created in a 16- bit style.
    • Each game will cost $24.99 digitally, or can be purchased physically as a two-in-one release for $49.99.
    • Boss Team Games says that if players buy both games, it will give them access to new features, enabling them to play as Michael Myers or Ash in each other’s worlds.
  • Alien: Romulus pays tribute to Alien Isolation with a telephone easter egg, director says
    • In Alien Isolation, there is a normally a telephone save point before "scary encounters". The film pays tribute to that by placing telephones in scenes just before "scary encounters".

Business

  • Games industry veterans have formed the Sustainable Games Alliance, a non-profit organisation dedicated to "helping the games industry become sustainable."
    • Backed by noted industry professionals such as Unity Technologies founder David Helgason and Remedy co-founder Petri Järvilehto, Sustainable Games Alliance – which is overseen by managing director and co-founder Maria Wagner – is working to make games "the most sustainable form of entertainment on the planet" by creating actionable ways to help the industry measure, understand, and reduce its footprint.
    • To achieve this, the Alliance is developing a free, open, and global industry standard for sustainability reporting. It hopes to simplify compliance with key regulations such as the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS) and the EU’s Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD).
  • Owlcat Games expands into publishing
    • "We have already created our dream game, and now we have the expertise to guide others," Owlcat says
    • Owlcat Games has signed two RPGs by Emotion Spark Studio and Another Angle Games to its new publishing division.
    • Owlcat has also assumed control of META Publishing, which published Pathfinder: Wrath of the Righteous on PC and console back in 2021.
    • Owlcat publishing is focused on RPGs and narrative titles. META Publishing is focused on co-op multiplayer games that focus on "gameplay-first"
  • Marvelous USA and Xseed Games to operate as separate publishing labels
    • Japanese publisher Marvelous is "taking a more holistic view" to its offerings in the West
    • Xseed will continue to publish third-party titles including upcoming games The Big Catch and Moonlight Peaks, while Marvelous USA will focus on publishing Marvelous games such as Story of Seasons and the Rune Factor series.
      • Marvelous USA will also provide its parent company with business, operational, and marketing support to its arcade business unit which is expanding to the West with Naruto Emblem Battle.
  • Keywords acquires Wushu Studios
    • The Liverpool-based studio was founded in 2017 by Alan McDermott and provides work-for-hire services to publishers and developers in addition to porting, remaster, and live support services.
    • Wushu has worked on games including Baldur's Gate 3, Fall Guys, Forza Horizon 5, The Ascent, and State of Decay 2.
    • The sum of the deal has not been made public
  • Ethical Games looking to craft industrywide ethics code, seeks developer input
    • The game industry lacks a solid code of ethics, and the organization hopes to create one with long-term benefits for developers and players alike.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Red Dead Redemption's US PlayStation page talks about the long awaited (but not confirmed) PC version
    • "Experience the epic western adventures that defined a generation — now on PC for the first time ever. Also included is Undead Nightmare, the iconic horror story that transforms the world of Red Dead Redemption into an apocalyptic fight for survival against a zombie horde. Featuring the complete single-player experiences of both games, including bonus content from the Game of the Year Edition, Red Dead Redemption features all of the 2023 console version upgrades plus PC specific enhancements - including support for increased resolutions and framerates, multiple displays, and other accessories, plus spatial surround sound.*
  • A new Sunsoft retro compilation brings three Famicom games to the west for the first time
    • "Sunsoft is Back! Retro Games Collection" will cost $9.99 digitally
    • The three games included are Firework Thrower Kantaro’s 53 Stations of the Tokaido, The Wing of Madoola and Ripple Island.
  • A Halloween (as in the film series not Halloween the time of year) game is in development in UE5, from the creators of Evil Dead: The Game with John Carpenter contributing
    • In a statement Carpenter, who’s said to be “intimately involved” with the game’s development, said: “As a huge gamer myself, I’m thrilled to help bring Michael Myers to life again in this game, and my hope is to scare you silly.”
  • Gearbox boss Randy Pitchford teases Borderlands 4
  • Open-world game Terminator: Survivors has been delayed to 2025
    • Previously scheduled to hit PC on October 24, 2024, before coming to consoles at a later date, the game will now be released in 2025.
    • Terminator: Survivors is set in the world of the first two Terminator films, and will follow an original story which takes place after the second movie, four years following Judgment Day.

Business

  • Enshrouded reaches 3m players

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Fortnite x Marvel: Absolute Doom detailed at D23
    • Fortnite x Marvel: Absolute Doom will begin on August 16. The battle pass will include Marvel characters such as Doctor Doom and Gwenpool
  • Max Payne 1 & 2 Remake is in full production, Control 2 is now in a playable state
    • Condor, Control 2 and Max Payne 1 & 2 are currently in production.
  • Borderlands movie earned $4m on its opening day
    • $4m from a $115 production budget
    • Lionsgate has "financially shielded itself" and recouped 60 percent of its production costs through international pre-sales
  • PlayStation is introducing user reviews on the PS Store
    • PlayStation.com is emailing players and inviting them to share their thoughts on a handful of games, including Helldivers 2, Fortnite, Resident Evil 4, and Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth.
    • Specific players have been invited for specific reviews for now. Unclear if this feature will be rolled out to the masses at this time.
  • Doom Eternal gets official mod support on PC with new mod browser and idStudio creation tools
  • Kingmakers secures movie adaptation ahead of its release
    • Partner Story Kitchen says it will "tell a unique story and establish the IP"

Business

  • Hi-Fi Rush studio Tango Gameworks has been acquired by Krafton and will remain open
    • Unclear specifically how many staff will be coming along as part of the deal, seeing as the studio was originally shut down in late May.
    • Hi-Fi Rush is the only IP coming along with the deal. The Evil Within series and Ghostwire Tokyo remain with Microsoft.
    • Krafton said that the acquisition will have no impact on the availability of Tango's previous releases, and that they will remain available where they are currently.
  • Arkadium launches publishing arm
    • Company establishes $1 million fund to support browser-based developers
  • Remedy Financial Results
    • Q2 2024:
      • Revenue: €10.3 million (up 16.2%)
      • Operating loss: €3.2 million (compared to operating loss of €4.8 million for same quarter last year)
    • H1 2024:
      • Revenue: €21.1 million (up 33.7%)
      • Operating loss: €5.3 million (compared to operating loss of €10.4 million in 2023)
  • Unity Q2 Results
    • Total revenue: $449 million (down 16% year-over-year)
    • Create Solutions revenue: $129 million (up 4%)
    • Grow Solutions revenue: $296 million (down 9%)
    • Net loss: $126 million (compared to $193 million for same quarter last year)

Rumors

  • Looks like Valve is introducing a new review system to filter out "unhelpful" reviews
    • As spotted by a member of the Steam subreddit, Valve appears to have briefly activated a "helpfulness system" to a handful of users that essentially hid joke or silly reviews from view.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Türkiye blocks Roblox due to child safety concerns
    • The ban follows a Bloomberg report last month highlighting the game's issues with child safety
  • Dragon Ball MOBA announced for mobile and Steam
    • The game, which will be released on Steam, iOS and Android, will enter beta later this month.
    • Steam users in Canada, France, Germany, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, the United Kingdom, and the United States can sign up for the beta tests, which will take place on August 20.
  • Vampire Survivors coming to PlayStation later this month
    • The game will be released on PlayStation 4/5 on August 29, 2024.
    • All of the game’s DLC will be released on PlayStation platforms on the same day.
  • The Borderlands movie has started off with a Rotten Tomatoes score of 0%
  • Doom 1 + 2 have be re-released yet AGAIN. This time combined and with a new episode
    • People who owned Doom or Doom 2 on Steam, PSN, Xbox, eShop or GOG can get the enhanced edition at no extra cost (automatically upgraded).
    • Bundle includes: DOOM, DOOM 2, TNT: Evilution, The Plutonia Experiment, Master Levels for Doom 2, No Rest for the Living, Sigil, Legacy of Rust, Deathmatch pack (26 maps)
    • All in all, there are a total of 187 mission maps and 43 deathmatch maps
    • Now on the KEX engine
    • Players will also be able to choose between the original soundtrack, or a new arranged soundtrack by Andrew Hulshult, who composed the music for Brutal Doom and co-composed the music for the Ancient Gods expansion for Doom Eternal.
    • The new edition also supports cross-platform multiplayer for up to 16 players in both Deathmatch and co-op mode.
  • Netflix suffers massive leaks
    • The full list of shows affected has not been revealed but Arcane season 2 has had its first 4 episodes leaked online.

Business

  • Warner Bros is ‘looking at’ licensing its IP to other game studios
  • Bandai Namco profits rose 56% during Q1 FY25
    • For the three months ended June 30, 2024:
      • Net sales UP: 24.8% year-over-year to ¥280 billion ($1.9 billion
      • Profit UP: 56.3% to ¥34 billion ($233 million).
      • Digital entertainment sales UP: 55.8% to ¥106 billion ($725 million).
      • Amusement segment UP: 17% to ¥33 million ($225,760).
  • Take Two Q1 Results
    • Net revenue: $1.34 billion (up 4% year-over-year)
    • Net loss: $262 million (compared to a $206 million loss in the year-ago quarter)
    • Total net bookings: $1.22 billion (up 1% year-over-year)
  • Warner Bros sees 41% year-on-year drop in Q2 gaming revenue
    • One key reason for this drop is the immense success last year of Hogwarts Legacy. It was one of the best-selling games of 2023 and by January 2024 had sold over 24m copies.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• UK actors' union Equity introduces minimum rates to address "systemic low pay" for video game performers
  • Fortnite ditches long-standing promise Battle Pass items won't be available to buy later
    • 18 month wait between an item appearing in the battle pass before able to be sold on the store

Business

  • Meta shuts down Quest developer Ready at Dawn
    • Across its 21-year lifetime, Ready at Dawn developed the Echo series for the Meta Quest, along with Daxter, various God of War spinoffs, and The Order: 1886
  • Sega Financial Results
    • The Numbers (Three months ended June 30, 2024)
      • Net sales: ¥104.7 billion ($710.5 million, down 3.2% year-on-year)
      • Entertainment Contents net sales: ¥72.7 billion ($493.3 million, up 34%)
    • Overall decline in net sales but a solid performance from its video games business
  • Balatro passes 2m sales, with free major update due in 2025
  • Shipyard Games shuts down
    • CEO and co-founder cited lack of investment as the main reason for studio's closure
    • Shipyard Games was founded in 2017. A couple months later, Supercell invested $2.9 million in the studio.
  • Foretold Studios is struggling to pay wages, leaving staff "begging" for help
    • The problems date back to at least last September, with current and former staff accusing CEO Michael Kuvshynov of failing to communicate transparently with staff about alleged cashflow issues, and actively recruiting new hires whilst owing outstanding wages to current and former employees.
    • Leadership says it will "compensate [staff] once we raise money"
  • Remedy appoints new CFO
    • Santtu Kallionpää will join Remedy's core management team in October, relinquishing his role as VP finance and business at Finnish confectionery company, Fazer

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Star Wars Jedi: Survivor is coming to PS4 and Xbox One in September
    • September 17th ($49.99)
    • The PC version of Star Wars Jedi: Survivor will also receive an update in the coming weeks, bringing “enhancements to the game’s technical performance, controls and more, plus a variety of quality-of-life improvements”
  • Fall Guys is now in Fortnite
    • For a limited time, you can play Fall Guys within Fortnite's battle royale, via an enormous obstacle course currently floating above the map. Enter into it and your in-game character will be turned into a customised Fall Guy bean.
    • Outside of Fortnite's battle royale, you can play custom Fall Guys levels made by Mediatonic and additional mini-games made by other creators, who have had access to Fall Guys assets ahead of time.
    • The tie-up appears permanent, with a dedicated Fall Guys row in Fortnite's main Discover menu, and all non-licensed Fortnite skins given a Fall Guys bean character equivalent.
  • Acclaimed biking game Descenders is getting an extreme sports sort-of-sequel
    • "Descenders Next" - 2025
    • Announced so far is snowboarding and mountainboarding
  • Dead by Daylight's Castlevania collaboration adds Dracula and Trevor Belmont later this month
    • Dead by Daylight's Castlevania Chapter is available now for public testing on PC, but it'll get its full release across all platforms on 27th August, when it'll cost around $7.99 USD
  • Five Nights at Freddy’s x Dead by Daylight crossover announced for 2025

Free Games / Subscription

  • August’s PlayStation Plus Essential games are now live
    • Lego Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga (PS4/5)
    • Five Nights at Freddy’s Security Breach (PS4/5)
    • Ender Lilies: Quietus of the Knights (PS4)
  • Xbox Game Pass August line-up announced
    • Arriving
      • Creatures of Ava (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) - 7th August
      • Crash Bandicoot N. Sane Trilogy (Cloud, Console, and PC) - 8th August
      • Mafia: Definitive Edition (Cloud, Console, and PC) - 13th August
    • Leaving (15th August)
      • Airborne Kingdoms (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Offworld Trading Company (PC)
      • Shadow Warrior 3 (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • The Texas Chain Saw Massacre (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Business

  • PlayStation Q1 Results
    • Q1 Numbers:
      • PS5 Sales: 2.4 million units, down from 3.3 million last year
      • Gaming Division Sales: Up 12% year-on-year
      • Operating Income: Up 32%, reaching ¥65.2 billion ($443 million)
    • PS5 Sales: 61.7 million units sold to date
  • Square Enix Q1 Results
    • The company's overall net sales dropped by 18.4 percent year-on-year for the most recent quarter.
    • This is driven in part due to a decline in sales of new games. By comparison, the same quarter in 2023 saw the release of Final Fantasy 16 and the Final Fantasy Pixel Remasters.

Rumors

  • Warner Bros. is reportedly considering selling a stake in its video games business
    • Warner Bros. Discovery exec JB Perrette said earlier this year that the company’s games business revolves around four main intellectual properties, “which are all billion-dollar plus businesses themselves”.
      • The four IP are: Mortal Kombat, Game of Thrones, DC and Harry Potter

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Final Fantasy 7 remake trilogy finale will receive "revamped" Queen's Blood card game
  • The first footage from HBO’s The Last of Us season 2 has been shown
    • The footage shows Cathrine O'Hara's new character
    • Season two of The Last of Us will be released in 2025
  • Shooter-MOBA hybrid Predecessor launches August 20th
    • Moving from open beta to a full release
    • Predecessor is the non-Epic developed spiritual successor to Paragon

Business

  • Jam City is laying off 10% of its employees
    • 85 members of staff impacted by cuts at the mobile game studio
    • Los Angeles-based Jam City is the company behind mobile games including Harry Potter: Hogwarts Mystery, DC Heroes and Villains, Disney Frozen Adventures, Jurassic World Alive, Cookie Jam and Panda Pop.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Paradox Interactive delays Prison Architect 2 indefinitely
    • Originally meant to release on September 2nd
    • Paradox clarified that it "can't commit to a new release date" until it can "re-assess the scope of the work needed to be done."
  • Final Fantasy 14 mobile game is approved in China
    • Other games approved include Rainbow Six Mobile, Dynasty Warriors, and Marvel Rivals
  • THQ Nordic has teased a new Darksiders game
    • Unclear at this time if it's a sequel, remake or remaster.
    • No release date
  • Development build of Call of Duty Black Ops 6 leaks as footage floods social media
  • Game Informer magazine ends after 33 years
    • Game Informer was known for its unprecedented access, revealing dozens of games during its history.
    • Game Informer’s parent company, GameStop, laid off several staff from its magazine operation in 2022.
  • A Civilization VII gameplay showcase is coming later this month
    • The first gameplay footage will be shown during the Opening Night Live show at Gamescom, which starts at 11am PT / 4pm ET / 7pm BST on August 20.
    • Shortly after Opening Night Live finishes, 2K and developer Firaxis will host their own 20 minute Gameplay Showcase video on the Firaxis Twitch channel and the official Civilization YouTube channel.
  • Wreckfest 2 announced
    • No release date
    • Coming to PC / PS5 / Xbox Series S/X

Business

  • Pokémon Scarlet and Violet sells 25m units
  • Silent Hill: The Short Message has been downloaded 3m times
  • Nintendo Q1 Results
    • The Numbers:
      • Operating Profit: ¥54.5 billion ($366 million), down 70.6% year-on-year
      • Net Sales: Video game business dropped 46.4% year-on-year
      • Mobile and IP-related Sales: Decreased 53.8% year-on-year
      • Switch Consoles: Sold 2.1 million in Q1, with a forecast of 13.5 million for the financial year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Xbox annouces Gamescom 2024 plans, including over 50 playable games
    • Xbox will also host exclusive theater presentations of Avowed, Indiana Jones and the Great Circle, and Starfield: Shattered Space.
    • Blizzard‘s Gamescom 2024 presence will also be part of the Xbox booth.

Business

  • Armor Games Studios has seemingly laid off its publishing team
    • Whilst the company has yet to announce the cuts publicly, several colleagues have confirmed the layoffs via their social media and LinkedIn pages.
    • Unknown number of staff affected
  • Roblox reports strong Q2 2024 as CFO steps down
    • For the three months ended June 30, 2024:
      • Revenue: $893.5m, up 31% year-on-year
      • Bookings: $955.2m, up 22% year-on-year
      • DAUs: 79.5m, up 21% year-on-year
      • Hours engaged: 17.4bn, up 24% year-on-year
  • Whiteout Survival passes $1 billion in revenue
    • It's the third fastest 2023 mobile title to achieve this feat, following Monopoly Go and Honkai: Star Rail
    • According to Sensor Tower, the number was hit in 17 months.
  • Nintendo Switch has now sold 143.42 Million Units Worldwide
    • Top 10 Best Selling Switch Games:
      • Mario Kart 8 Deluxe - 62.90M
      • Animal Crossing: New Horizons - 45.85M
      • Super Smash Bros. Ultimate - 34.66M
      • Breath of the Wild - 32.05M
      • Super Mario Odyssey - 28.21M
      • Pokemon Sword/Shield - 26.35M
      • Pokemon Scarlet/Violet - 25.29M
      • Super Mario Party - 20.84M
      • Tears of the Kingdom - 20.80M
      • New Super Mario Bros. U Deluxe - 17.61M

Rumors

  • Obsidian's Avowed reportedly delayed from 2024 to early next year
    • The game is said to be in "good shape"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 01/08/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 1 August 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Xbox Discord update includes friend activity and the ability to watch streams from console
    • Highlights include the ability to watch friends stream directly on their Xbox One / Series S/X. If they’re sharing their screen from PC, mobile, or another Xbox, they’ll be able to watch whatever they’re playing.
    • Users can also now call their friends directly from Xbox consoles. The update will also unify the Xbox friends list and the Discord friends list, if players opt into it.
  • Braid Anniversary Edition has ‘sold like dog sh*t’, creator Jonathan Blow says
  • Suda51 and Shinji Mikami want to make a Complete Edition of Killer7, followed by a sequel
    • The sequel could be called Killer11 or Killer 7: Beyond, Suda says

Free Games / Subscription

  • PlayStation Plus Essential lineup for August 6th
    • Lego Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga (PS4/5)
    • Five Nights at Freddy's Security Breach (PS4/5)
    • Ender Lilies: Quietus of the Knights (PS4)

Business

  • Flintlock: The Siege of Dawn passes 500,000 players
  • Bungie to lay off over 200 staff as it forms new studio within PlayStation
    • 220 staff have been laid off
    • An additional 155 staff members are being placed into other Sony studios "over the next few quarters"
    • Bungie's headcount has fallen from approximately 1200 to approx 850 after multiple rounds of lay-offs.
    • Bungie will move one of its early-development projects - an "action game set in a brand-new science-fantasy universe" - to PlayStation Studios to form a new studio.
    • In the past Sony has said if things don't improve at Bungie, Sony would take over fully (instead of supporting from the side). This appears to have happened.
    • Rumours have mentioned that Herman Hulst is now leading Bungie, though this isn't validated yet.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 31/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 31 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Activision Releases Call of Duty Warzone Caldera Data Set For Academic Use
    • The dataset is available in OpenUSD format
    • Includes various metrics such as player pathing and time samples, aimed at helping developers and researchers improve game design and player experience.
    • This initiative is part of Activision’s broader effort to enhance transparency and collaboration within the gaming community by making valuable data accessible to the public.
  • Jackbox Games new Megapicker launcher currently gives you Drawful 2 free
    • Megapicker launcher (available on Steam) allows you to purchase Jackbox mini-games seperately
    • To celebrate the release, players can redeem Drawful 2 over the next 30 days
  • Helldivers 2's fire-themed Warbond detailed ahead of next week's launch

Business

  • Activision Blizzard again boosted Xbox’s earning results, while hardware declined
    • For the three months ended June 30, 2024, gaming revenue was up 44% year-over-year, Microsoft said on Tuesday, which includes 48 points of net impact from Activision. Without the acquisition, gaming revenue would have been down 4 percent.
    • Xbox hardware revenue declined 42% year-over-year, but Xbox content and services revenue, which includes Xbox Game Pass, was up by 61 percent this quarter. Again, the majority of this (58 points of net impact) came from Activision Blizzard.

Rumors

  • Goat Simulator Remastered Has Been Rated For PS5 In Taiwan
    • If correct, a remaster of Goat Simulator is headed to PS5 and other current-gen platforms

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• The face of Supermassive Games' The Dark Pictures Anthology, Tony Pankhurst (The Curator), dies aged 67
    • Although Pankhurst died in May 2024, Supermassive only today (July 29) shared the news across its social media channels.
    • Unlike some of the other notable faces and names attached to The Dark Pictures Anthology since the series began in 2019, in this instance, Pankhurst provided the facial likeness of The Curator, whilst Pip Torrens provided the voice and motion capture.
  • Removing skill-based matchmaking leads to a drop in players, Activision study suggests
    • The publisher held an experiment in Modern Warfare 3 earlier this year
    • For its experiment, Activision took the North American player base for Modern Warfare 3. For half the players it ran a ‘Deprioritise Skill Test’, which “loosened the constraints” on skill-based matchmaking, meaning less emphasis was placed on a player’s skills when looking for opponents
    • According to Activision’s findings, most of the players in the Deprioritise Skill Test were less likely to come back to the game. While the top 10% highest skilled players returned to the game in increased numbers within two weeks (because they were winning more), when it came to the other 90%, fewer returned to the game than normal.
  • Grasshopper Manufacture to host second annual showcase later this week
    • 31st July
  • Castle Crashers is getting new DLC 16 years after it first released
    • 12 years after it first arrived on Steam, and 16 years after it debuted on Xbox Live Arcade, Castle Crashers is getting new DLC.
    • Although Castle Crashers debuted on Xbox and is still available on Sony consoles and Nintendo Switch, the DLC is only coming to Steam
  • Double Fine drops a 90-minute update to its 20+ hour series documenting the development of Psychonauts 2
    • The episode chronicles the studio "reflecting on the launch of Psychonauts 2 and the PsychOdyssey documentary series while looking to the future and a return to smaller projects".
  • Mortal Kombat 1: Khaos Reigns DLC announced; Animalities, T-1000, Conan, and Scream's Ghostface all on the way

Business

  • Embracer's Plaion will publish Uncharted director’s Marvel 1943: Rise of Hydra
    • The publisher (formerly known as Koch Media) has entered into a “worldwide publishing partnership” with the game’s developer, Skydance Games.
  • Hasbro CEO: "We're going all in on becoming a digital play company"
    • "Our goal is to be shipping one to two new games per year starting as early as late 2025, potentially early 2026," Chris Cocks says
  • Electric Square's Lively Studio faces small number of layoffs
    • Unknown amount of layoffs
    • "Limited to the sister studio" - Electric Square unaffected for now
  • Capcom Q1 FY25 Results
    • For the three months ended June 30, 2024:
      • Net sales: ¥29.6 billion ($192 million), down 32.5% year-on-year
      • Digital Contents net sales: ¥21.4 billion ($138 million), down 43.5% year-on-year
      • Net income: ¥9.97 billion ($64.7 million), down 45.1% year-on-year
      • Arcade Operations up 18%, and Amusement Equipments up 147%

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• There’s just one day left before Xbox 360’s Marketplace closes
    • Around 220 digital games will disappear for good, according to VGC analysis
  • Riot Games' hero shooter Valorant is running an open console beta, and it's live right now
    • The free-to-play 5v5 shooter was initially released for PC in 2020, but support for current-gen console systems finally rolled out last month through closed playtests.
  • Like a Dragon: Yakuza TV show drops a new teaser trailer
    • The show will premiere on 24th October.
  • Epic removes Fortnite from mobile app stores to prepare for own store launch
    • Publisher also pulls games from Samsung Galaxy store due to side-loading settings
  • PSVR 2 app to release on Steam on August 6
    • The app means users will be able to play PlayStation VR 2 titles on PC via Steam

Business

  • Look North World raises $4.5m in overall funding
    • Studio will use investment to further support development of original titles
  • Steam demo overhaul includes standalone store pages, user reviews and wishlist notifications
    • Valve says demos will now behave in the same way as free games on leading PC marketplace
  • Paradox Interactive profits drop 90% due to Life By You cancellation
    • Write-downs of development costs for the game amounted to $19.2 million

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• S&Box, the successor of Garry's Mod (now it's more like Roblox for adults), based on the Source 2 engine, has released
  • 7 Days to Die officially released 1.0 version after 11 years of early access
    • Also available on PS5 and Xbox Series consoles
    • The Telltale versions have been delisted
  • US video game performers announce strike over ongoing AI concerns
    • Video game performers within United States actors union SAG-AFTRA have voted to go on strike starting Friday, 26th July, after "more than than a year and a half of negotiations" with major game companies over the use of AI have failed to reach a deal.
  • A Lego Shadow the Hedgehog bust is coming in October
    • The set will retail for $69.99/£69.99

Business

  • Armored Core 6 Fires of Rubicon hits 3 million sales
  • Improbable achieves profitability for the first time
    • Company was founded in 2012, posted profits of £11 million in 2023
    • 37% year-on-year increase in revenue to £66 million and a cash position of £185 million.

Rumors

  • Legacy of Kain Soul Reaver 1 & 2 remastered leaked from toys at SDCC

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 25/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Voice actor Sam Mowry passed away at age 64
    • Mowry's decades-long career in voice acting includes credits for Dota 2, Legends of Runeterra, Infamous, and Torchlight.
  • Concord won’t have a battle pass
  • EA cancels unpopular Apex Legends battle pass change following outcry
    • It will no longer be removing the ability to obtain premium battle passes with Apex coins
  • Suda 51 and Shinji Mikami's Shadows of the Damned: Hella Remastered out this Halloween
    • Releases on PC via Steam, PS 4/5, Switch, Xbox One/Series S/X "Halloween 2024"

Business

  • Former Epic Games exec Alain Tascan is the new president of Netflix Games
    • Tascan spent almost six years at Epic, serving as the Fornite maker's EVP of game development
  • Capcom aims to increase ratio of female managers to 15%
    • Women currently only make up 21.2% of employees at the firm
      • 13.6% of its core talent are female
  • Mario + Rabbids boss Davide Soliani leaves Ubisoft after 25 years
    • Soliani spent 11 years working on the two Mario + Rabbids games, released on Switch in 2017 and 2022 in conjunction with Nintendo.
    • The developer is yet to reveal what he'll be working on next, beyond embarking on "a new adventure".

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo’s new gashapon toys can be used as extra parts for real controllers
    • The toys are on sale in Nintendo Japan stores
    • It’s currently unknown if Nintendo will offer these items outside of Japan.
  • Roblox reported 13,316 instances of child exploitation last year
    • More than two dozen people arrested for abusing minors, following contact in-game
    • The company denies claims that child endangerment is widespread on the game platform
  • Nintendo nullifies Splatoon 3 World Championship win following racism reports
    • "Team Jackpot's win will be considered vacated"
    • The team will not receive trophies for their win and the in-game banner will be removed via a software update
  • PS5 update adds game session invites with a URL and a new voice command
    • Sony said it’s also improved system software performance and stability, as well as the messages and usability on some screens.
  • Helldivers 2’s ‘biggest update yet’ revealed ahead of August release
    • "Escalation of Freedom" includes fresh enemies and mission objectives, plus a new difficulty level
  • Fortnite Lego sets announced
    • Battle Bus (£90), Peely Bone (£90), Supply Llama (£35) and Durrr Burger (£13)
  • Capcom says it’s ‘considering’ how to make Mega Man games on a regular basis
    • The last major entry in the franchise was released in 2018 (Mega Man 11)
  • Ubisoft publishes letter to ‘Japanese community’ ‘concerned’ by Assassin’s Creed Shadows
    • This is a complicated topic with a lot of nuance. The TL;DR is:
      • Yasuke, the "black samurai" featured in the game was known to be a real life person
      • Whether or not he was actually a samurai is unclear, with rumours suggesting he was a samurai coming primarily from Thomas Lockley, who apparently falsified Wikipedia entries since 2015 to promote his 2019 book
      • One camp is calling people racist for having a problem with Yasuke's portrayal in Assassin's Creed whereas another camp is claiming people are trying to revise history and eradiate Japan's culture
      • The Japanese Government has launched an investigation about the game and Yasuke in general
      • Thomas Lockley has removed all social media presence

Free Games / Subscription

  • Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 3 is heading to Xbox Game Pass this week
    • Joins service on 24th July (today)
    • Xbox Game Pass's price hike comes into effect in September

Business

  • Humble Games confirms layoffs amid 'restructuring', denies full shutdown
    • Staff laid off have claimed all staff members have been laid off on social media
    • Ongoing and future projects are said to be unaffected, and the restructure has "no impact" on Humble Bundle's operations.

Rumors

  • Lively Studio (owned by Keywords) facing layoffs of unknown quantity

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• NetherRealm is shutting down free-to-play mobile game Mortal Kombat: Onslaught
    • Onslaught will be pulled from iOS and Android stores today, 22nd July, but in-app purchases will continue until 23rd August. The game will be playable until 21st October this year.
    • News of Mortal Kombat: Onslaught's impending closure comes after claims from NetherRealm employees last week that the studio's mobile division had been laid off.
  • Elon Musk's Tesla Cybertruck will be available to buy in Fortnite
  • Heihachi Mishima is coming to Tekken 8
    • Arriving Autumn 2024
    • Heihachi has "died" more times than I can count at this point, somehow always surviving being thrown down cliffs or into lava - a running joke within the Tekken community
  • Capcom shows off gameplay of Terry Bogard in Street Fighter 6
    • Terry is one of two Fatal Fury characters planned for the game
    • Terry is currently planned for autumn 2024.
    • Terry is the second character in Street Fighter 6’s second year of content. He’ll be joined by Mai Shiranui, also from Fatal Fury, and Elena, a Street Fighter veteran

Business

  • Netflix has over 80 games currently in development and plans to launch one each month
    • So far, Netflix has launched over one hundred games, though many of these are small in scope.
  • Warner Bros. has acquired MultiVersus studio Player First Games
    • Season 2 of the platform fighter launches this week

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 22/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Guilty Gear Strive is getting Lucy from Cyberpunk: Edgerunners as a guest fighter next year
    • Queen Dizzy from Guilty Gear X, in October.
    • Venom from Guilty Gear X, early 2025
    • Unika, a new character, later in 2025
    • Lucy (Cyberpunk Edgerunners), 2025
  • Samurai Jack is joining MultiVersus next week alongside ranked mode
    • Samurai Jack joins the roster on July 23rd
  • Final Fantasy 14 players can visit Gong Cha bubble tea stores to earn in-game mount
    • Wednesday, July 17, 2024 and Wednesday, August 28, 2024 and purchase three teas in a single transaction to receive a redemption code for the Porxie King mount

Business

  • 241 workers at Bethesda Game Studios have unionised
    • "We, a majority of developers at Bethesda Game Studios Dallas, Rockville, and Austin, are ecstatic to announce the formation of our union with CWA Union. Together as #OneBGSUSA, we advocate for the betterment of every developer at BGS, setting the new standard for our industry."
    • First Microsoft studio to fully unionize
  • Develop is heading to Liverpool for Develop: North
    • The one-day event will take place on November 14, 2024, at The Spine in Liverpool. Registration will open in September, when more details will be announced.
  • Ubisoft Financial Results
    • Q1 Numbers
      • Sales: €323.5 million (up 12.0% year-on-year)
      • Net bookings: €290.0 million (up 8.3%)
      • Digital net bookings: 257.2 million (up 6.6%)
      • Back-catalogue net bookings: 249.5 million (up 1.5%)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Former Rare Studios artist Brett Jones has passed away
    • Along with his work on GoldenEye and Perfect Dark, Jones did art and VFX for Guardians of the Galaxy, Star Wars, and Doctor Who
    • A crowdfunding campaign is currently underway to get Jones' work displayed at the Atkins Gallery in Hinckley, Leicestershire.
  • Genshin Impact orchestral concert tour arrives in London this October
    • Alongside the Wembley show on 29th October, the tour will also arrive in Berlin and Paris in December.
  • Splitgate 2 announced, coming to PC and consoles next year
    • Although Splitgate 2 isn’t coming to PC, PS4/5, Xbox One / Series S/X until 2025, a companion app for the game has been released today on iOS and Android, and contains collectables which can be used to unlock content in the game when it’s out
  • ‘Halo’ Canceled After Two Seasons at Paramount+

Business

  • Keywords launches AI-powered FAQ translation tool it says will reduce costs by 100 percent
    • Helpshift, a subsidiary of Keywords, will bundle the feature into its customer support platform
  • FTC claims Xbox’s Game Pass price hike is ‘consumer harm’ it warned of
    • “Microsoft’s price increases and product degradation—combined with Microsoft’s reduced investments in output and product quality via employee layoffs, are the hallmarks of a firm exercising market power post-merger.”
    • The FTC’s letter goes on to note that, “Microsoft’s price increases coincide with adding Call of Duty to Game Pass’s most expensive tier, and discontinuing the Console tier will happen shortly before releasing CoD’s newest game.”
  • Take Two warns investors that review bombings can hurt profits
    • Grand Theft Auto and NBA 2K publisher Take Two Interactive has added review bombing to the list of risks that could impact the publisher’s success, warning investors that the hostile campaigns may “lead to loss of players and revenues, additional advertising and marketing costs, and reputation harm.”
  • Blizzard's John Hight and former Bungie COO Holly Barbacovi join Hasbro
    • Barbacovi will serve as CPO, and Hight as president of Wizards of the Coast and Digital Gaming
  • Pacific Drive tops 600,000 sales globally

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Star Wars: Galaxy of Heroes is now available on PC
    • EA originally released Star Wars: Galaxy of Heroes for mobile in November 2015.
