    • The PC version of the game features options for higher resolutions, supports antialiasing and doubles the framerate to 60fps.
  • Amazon’s Fallout show has received 16 Emmy nominations
    • They include a nomination for Outstanding Drama Series, which pits the show against the likes of 3 Body Problem, Shogun, The Crown, Slow Horses and Mr & Mrs Smith.
    • Walton Goggins is also nominated for Outstanding Lead Actor In A Drama Series for his portrayal of The Ghoul.
    • Its showrunners say Season 2 only scratches the surface of the story they want to tell
  • Orcs Must Die! Deathtrap announced for Xbox Series S/X and PC
    • The fifth game in Robot Entertainment's series is coming next year ("early 2025")
  • Nintendo’s teaser leads to new Famicom Detective Club game reveal
    • Emio – The Smiling Man: Famicom Detective Club releases August 29th
    • First new entry (minus the two recent remakes) in series in 35 years
  • Sega sues social media user for "excessive slanderous and insulting comments" against employee
    • Sega has revealed it's taken legal action against a social media user for harassing one of its employees, warning that it will continue to pursue legal remedies or criminal proceedings should similar occurrences happen in the future.
    • The company - which published a Consumer Harassment Policy last year - reiterated its stance on social media harassment in a post on its Japanese website. Providing context, it explained it has been dealing with an individual who has made "excessive slanderous and insulting comments on social media against one of our employees" for a "long time".
    • After the individual failed to improve their behaviour, the company was granted access to the person's information through the court. Since then, it's reached a settlement, with the individual agreeing to "pay damages to our employee, delete the slanderous and insulting comments, and refrain from such actions in the future."

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Until Dawn studio's Dead by Daylight spin-off The Casting of Frank Stone out in September
    • Supermassive Games' narrative-focused single-player Dead by Daylight spin-off, The Casting of Frank Stone, launches for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series S/X, and PC on 3rd September
  • Dead by Daylight cross-progression and 2v8 updates incoming
    • Behaviour Interactive has confirmed cross-progression is coming to Dead by Daylight on 22nd July, enabling players to share their progress, inventory (excluding platform-exclusive items), paid currency, and DLC between owned versions of the game on PlayStation, Xbox, Steam, the Epic Games Store, and Windows Store using a Behaviour Account.
      • Progression and inventory can still be shared between platforms via Switch but paid currency and DLC bought on Nintendo's console can't be shared elsewhere. However, paid currency and DLC bought on a different platform can be used on Switch
    • Lara Croft available now
    • 2v8 game mode releases on 25th July, enabling eight survivors to face off against two killers across five classic maps
  • Gamescom 2024 will feature more exhibitors than ever, it claims
    • Show kicks off August 20th
    • Koelnmesse also claims the show will feature larger international participation than in previous years

Free Games / Subscription

  • July’s PlayStation Plus Game Catalogue and Classics titles are out now
    • Extra and Premium
      • Remnant II – Standard Edition | PS5
      • Crisis Core: Final Fantasy VII Reunion | PS4, PS5
      • Mount & Blade II: Bannerlord | PS4, PS5
      • The Jackbox Party Pack 9 | PS4, PS5
      • Pathfinder: Wrath of the Righteous | PS4, PS5
      • No More Heroes 3 | PS4, PS5
      • Travis Strikes Again: No More Heroes Complete Edition | PS4
      • Deadcraft | PS4
      • Steep | PS4
    • Premium
      • Job Simulator | PS VR2
      • Summoner | PS4, PS5
      • Ratchet and Clank Size Matters | PS4, PS5
      • Jeanne d’Arc | PS4, PS5
  • New Xbox Game Pass titles for console, PC and Cloud have been confirmed
    • Arriving:
      • Magical Delicacy (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 16
      • Flock (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 16
      • Flintlock: The Siege of Dawn (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S|X) – July 18
      • Dungeons of Hinterberg (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S|X) – July 18
      • Kunitsu-Gami: Path of the Goddess (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 19
    • Leaving
      • A Short Hike (Cloud, Console, and PC) - July 31
      • Train Sim World 4 (Cloud, Console, and PC) - July 31
      • Venba (Cloud, Console, and PC) - July 31

Rumors

  • Resident Evil 7 on iOS has reportedly been purchased by fewer than 2,000 people
    • The numbers claim Resident Evil 7 on iOS has earned Capcom $28,140 since launch

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• A pre-release build of Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2 has leaked
    • The sequel isn't scheduled for release until September
    • In late June, Saber and Focus said a Space Marine 2 open beta had been cancelled to focus on optimization, polish and bug fixing
    • Earlier this month, Focus announced the game had "gone gold"
  • Moomins coming to Journey dev's Sky: Children of the Light this year
    • Animated series The Two Embers due 2025
  • Elden Ring DLC Shadow of the Erdtree removes Latin American translation staff from credits
    • The revised credits now fully credit translators from Europe. Localisation staff have been listed, as well as a Special Thanks including all individual translators, all under Bandai Namco Europe
      • However, Latin America and Thai translators are only listed by the name of the agency, unlike those for Chinese and Korean
    • The original credits for the game credits Brazilian Portuguese and Latin American Spanish translators individually, but the new credits remove these names and instead includes a generic Special Thanks message.

Rumors

  • Crash Bandicoot artist claims a fifth game was cancelled
    • "Some day folks will hear about the Crash 5 that never was, and it’s gonna break hearts”

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 15/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 15 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• The International Olympic Committee (IOC) has announced a 12-year agreement to host the Olympic Esports Games in Saudi Arabia
    • The inaugural Olympic ESports games will take place next year
  • Ubisoft says it’s ‘actively working’ on Driver franchise projects
    • Live-action series has been shelved
    • Unclear at this time if the "franchise projects" are more games or extended media.

Business

  • Details of unannounced Disney projects, including games, leak online after another Slack hack
    • 1.1TB of internal documents and messages were taken from Disney's Slack.
    • Whilst Disney has yet to comment formally on the alleged hack, details from various internal files – including upcoming collaborations with Epic Games' Fortnite, and the Aliens: Fireteam Elite sequel have started to pop up on social media.
  • Dying Light 2 dev Techland is making "most roles" office-based or hybrid, including staff who live abroad
    • CD-Action sources claim the studio's current offices are not large enough to accommodate much more than ten percent of its 500 headcount. It's thought around half of those developers had, up until this point, work remotely.
    • "Employees who live near our offices will be asked to work from the office three days a week. For those working remotely from abroad, we're asking them to come to the office regularly to foster stronger connections.
      • According to Polish site CD-Action, "regularly" equates to eight working days every two months.
  • The First Descendant clocks up 10m players in just one week
  • Nintendo has been accused of miscrediting external translators, according to employees working for service providers Localsoft and Keywords

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Tencent is shutting down its free-to-play shooter Synced after just a year
    • Servers will be "retired" a year to the day the shooter was launched (September 8th)
  • Frontier has announced Planet Coaster 2 for release this year
    • Releasing in autumn 2024 for PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series S/X
    • Planet Coaster 2 will introduce several new features including new water attractions.

Free Games / Subscription

  • Amazon Prime Gaming is giving away 3 more PC games, including Suicide Squad
    • On Tuesday, July 16, Prime members will be able to claim Suicide Squad: Kill the Justice League, Chivalry 2, and Rise of the Tomb Raider at no additional cost.
    • Available for 48 hours
    • Redeemable on Epic Games Store

Business

  • Embracer has secured a new €600m ($652m) revolving credit facility with a two-year tenor
    • This new facility replaces its existing €420m ($456m) revolving credit facility, which matures in May 2025
    • Seven banks were involved in the transaction: BNP Paribas, Citibank, N.A., London Branch, DNB Bank ASA, Sweden Branch, J.P. Morgan SE, Nordea Bank Abp, filial i Sverige, SEB, and Swedbank AB (publ).
  • "Small number of roles" face potential redundancies at Splash Damage
    • Roles at risk are mostly in operations, with development largely unaffected
  • Stellar Blade developer Shift Up raises $320m in IPO
    • Largest gaming IPO in South Korea in over two years, and the first of at least $50 million since 2021, when PUBG developer Krafton raised $3.8 billion.

Rumors

  • UK Store GAME to end in-store pre-orders. Game Wallet to also end
    • As of 1st August, no new in-store pre-orders will be accepted and all existing pre-orders will be refunded for products set to be released after 31st January 2025.
    • Any existing pre-order or deposit for a product set to be launched before the end of January 2025 will still be honoured. There's no change to online pre-orders.
    • In a week's time, on 18th July, GAME Wallet customers will no longer be able to add new funds or gift cards. After 21st August, all GAME Wallet accounts will be closed.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Arc System Works is working on a new 3D Double Dragon
    • The fighting game developer acquired the IP back in 2015 and later released Double Dragon 4
    • Arc System Works is the developer of many popular fighting games, including Guilty Gear Strive, Dragon Ball FighterZ, and Granblue Fantasy Versus.

Free Games / Subscription

  • July’s PlayStation Plus Game Catalogue and Classics titles confirmed
    • Extra and Premium (July 16)
      • Remnant II – Standard Edition | PS5
      • Crisis Core: Final Fantasy VII Reunion | PS4, PS5
      • Mount & Blade II: Bannerlord | PS4, PS5
      • The Jackbox Party Pack 9 | PS4, PS5
      • Pathfinder: Wrath of the Righteous | PS4, PS5
      • No More Heroes 3 | PS4, PS5
      • Travis Strikes Again: No More Heroes Complete Edition | PS4
      • Deadcraft | PS4
      • Steep | PS4
    • Premium (July 16)
      • Job Simulator | PS VR2
      • Summoner | PS4, PS5
      • Ratchet and Clank Size Matters | PS4, PS5
      • Jeanne d’Arc | PS4, PS5

Business

  • Terraria passes 58.7 million lifetime sales

Rumors

  • Riot Games reportedly cancelled plans for a Smash Bros. style fighter
    • Riot Games has reportedly cancelled plans to release a Super Smash Bros. style fighter codenamed Pool Party
    • Executives were put off by the performance of Multiversus
    • 2XKO still due next year

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Xbox Game Pass getting big changes, ditching day one releases for basic tier
    • Xbox Game Pass Core - $9.99 / no day one games
    • PC Game Pass - $11.99 / day one games
    • Xbox Game Pass for Console (existing subs) - $10.99 / day one games (will be phased out 12th September)
    • Xbox Game Pass Standard (new only) - $14.99 / no day one games
    • Xbox Game Pass Ultimate - $19.99 / day one games
  • Vampire Survivors coming to Apple Arcade ad-free this August
    • Legacy of the Moonspell and Tides of the Foscari paid expansions included

Business

  • Pokémon Sleep earns $100m in first year revenue
    • The sleep-tracking app saw 9.8 million downloads during the same period
  • Monster Hunter Stories 2: Wings of Ruin tops 2m copies sold
  • Palworld Entertainment Established by Pocketpair, Sony Music Entertainment, and Aniplex
    • This new joint venture will "expand and develop new businesses associated" with the hit game Palworld.

Rumors

  • New PlayStation Plus Game Catalogue titles leak
    • July 17th
      • Crisis Core: Final Fantasy VII Reunion
      • Remnant II
      • Pathfinder: Wrath of the Righteous – Enhanced Edition
      • Mount & Blade II: Bannerlord
      • No More Heroes 3
      • Travis Strikes Again: No More Heroes Complete Edition

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Next BioShock developer "ramping up"
    • Senior cinematic designer Jeff Spoonhower took to LinkedIn to highlight more than 30 positions now open at 2K Cloud Chamber, the Montreal-based studio tasked with crafting a fresh entry in the beloved BioShock series.
  • The Xbox TV app is now available on Amazon Fire sticks
    • Game Pass Ultimate required
  • Disney announces Pixel RPG for mobile
    • Developed by GungHo Entertainment
    • Game will be free-to-play
    • Characters confirmed for the game based on the website are Pooh, from Winnie the Pooh, Genie from Aladdin, Princess Aurora from Sleeping Beauty, Stitch from Lilo and Stitch, Maleficent from Sleeping Beauty and Ariel from The Little Mermaid.
    • Classic Disney characters like Mickey Mouse and Donald Duck will also be included. Players will design their own avatars using costume pieces inspired by Disney characters.
  • Paradox Interactive delays Cities Skylines 2 console version for a third time
    • Paradox says it has "not yet met the stability and performance targets"
  • Respawn doubles Apex Legends' battle pass price in systemic overhaul
    • Beginning with the upcoming Season 22, the three-month passes can only be purchased with real-world money. Players could use in-game AC currency to buy said passes, but that process will be phased out.
      • Further complicating matters is that each Season will be split into two battle passes. The first Premium pass will cost $10 per half season, while the Premium+ pass (which offers extra in-game rewards) will be $20.
      • So unless players stick with the free track, they may pay $20 or $40 for a full season. Per Respawn, the change was done to make things "more approachable and realistic for our global player community."

Business

  • Embracer-owned Gothic, Risen and ELEX developer Piranha Bytes closes
    • Pithead Studio has been founded by Piranha Bytes' Björn Pankratz and Jennifer Pankratz to continue their former studio's focus on role-playing games
  • Kotaku Australia is being shuttered by Pedestrian Group
    • Pedestrian Group currently licenses the Kotaku brand and others including Gizmodo, Vice, Refinery29 and Lifehacker (which are also being axed) so it can deploy them in Australia.
    • Rowley described Pedestrian Group–which is owned by media conglomerate Nine–as a "highly successful business" in that email, but indicated the company has decided to move away from licensed brands.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Summer Games Done Quick 2024 raises £1.9m ($2.5m) for Doctors Without Borders/Médecins Sans Frontières
    • Lifetime charitable total for the event is now £40m

Business

  • Gacha Sensation Zenless Zone Zero Surpasses 50 Million Downloads Across All Platforms
    • The game had 40 million pre-registrations prior to launching

Rumors

  • Final Fantasy XIV is reportedly coming to mobile via Tencent

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• After years in development hell, Ubisoft's Watch Dogs movie finally begins shooting
    • An update from Ubisoft on social media last night showed a set photo with the caption: "Lights_Camera_Action.exe".
  • PlayStation is fully returning to Tokyo Game Show
    • Sony hasn't had a major presence at the show since 2019

Free Games / Subscription

  • Nintendo has added 7 more NES games to Switch Online
    • Urban Champion
    • Golf
    • Donkey Kong Jr. Math
    • Mach Rider
    • The Mystery of Atlantis
    • SOLAR JETMAN
    • COBRA TRIANGLE

Business

  • Co-development studio Tose reports losses due to multiple cancelled games
    • Co-development studio Tose has reported a nine-month loss of ¥367 million ($2.28 million) due to numerous projects being cancelled by its clients.
    • Established in 1979, Tose has a long history as a studio that specialises in working with other publishers and developers.
    • Some of the titles it worked on recently: Paper Mario: The Thousand-Year Door remake, Final Fantasy Pixel Remaster, Crisis Core: Final Fantasy 7 Reunion, Scarlet Nexus, WarioWare: Move It and Dragon Quest Monsters: The Dark Prince.
    • It has lowered its sales forecast for the year by 12.5%, saying it expects things to remain uncertain for a while, but notes that it is working on improving its project management and plans to recover its momentum by steadily launching the games it still has in active development.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 4 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Zenless Zone Zero is now available
    • The latest title from miHoYo - creators of Genshin Impact, Honkai Impact and Honkai Star Rail
    • Recently, miHoYo built a fusion reactor
  • UK man jailed for four months for carrying 6-inch Zelda Master Sword in public
  • Personal data of Spike Chunsoft staff may have been leaked in ransomware hack
    • Kadokawa, which also owns FromSoftware, was hacked last week by ransomware gang BlackSuit, which claimed to have private information on the company’s employees, and threatened to publish it if it wasn’t paid enough.
    • Kadokawa said the leak likely included the personal information of all Dwango employees, internal documents and personal information on some employees from affiliated companies.

Business

  • Keywords Studios reaches £2.2bn acquisition agreement with Swedish investment company EQT
    • The deal is expected to complete in Q4 2024
  • Squad Busters reaches $24m in first month revenue
  • Adult Swim delists four more indie games, stating developers are "not available" to take over as publisher
    • Mega Coin Squad, Super House of Dead Ninjas, Traverser, and Zenzizenzic
    • Super House of Dead Ninja dev said it "did reach out but didn't receive a reply"
  • More co-op games were released on Steam last year than in any other year, report finds
    • According to the report, a total of 799 co-op games were released on Steam in 2023, more than in 2022 (701 games), 2021 (650 games) and 2020 (647 games).
    • The report notes that in 2024 nearly half of all Steam games sold – 46% to date – feature a co-op element.

Rumors

  • Netflix's Horizon Zero Dawn series potentially not moving forward due to issues with Showrunner Steve Blackman

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• EA is "sunsetting" three last-gen Battlefield games
    • Battlefield 3 (Xbox 360 & PlayStation 3), Battlefield 4 (Xbox 360 & PlayStation 3), Battlefield: Hardline (Xbox 360 & PlayStation 3)
    • On Wednesday, July 31st they will be removed from digital storefronts and you will no longer be able to purchase them and all extra related content such as DLC
    • Online services go offline November 7th 2024.
    • Battlefield 3 will still be available for play on PC. Battlefield 4, and Battlefield: Hardline will still be available for play on PC, Xbox One, and PlayStation 4.
  • Dungeons & Dragons rules update includes changes prompted by game's lead designer playing Baldur's Gate 3
    • "Cloud of Daggers" is now "movable"
  • PlayStation Stars loyalty scheme returning after a month of unavailability
    • The service, which allows you to change points you've earnt for prizes, such as virtual collectables or PSN vouchers, has been down since early June
    • It's being rolled out again in phased, regional rollouts. Specific times are unknown.
  • Nintendo sues Switch modchip seller and Switch piracy Reddit moderator
    • The company has filed complaints against Ryan Daly, who operates a store called Modded Hardware, and r/SwitchPirates moderator Archbox, whose real name is James Williams.
    • In addition to being a moderator on Reddit, Nintendo claims Archbox has been involved with three "pirate shops" (which have since been closed) and cliams that he's involved with a fourth active one.
  • MultiVersus update adds Agent Smith, free-for-all and spectator mode
    • Version 1.05 also makes rifts easier and rebalances fighters.

Free Games / Subscription

  • July’s PlayStation Plus Essential games are now live
    • This month’s PlayStation Plus Essential games are Borderlands 3 (PS4/5), NHL 24 (PS4/5) and Among Us (PS4/5).
    • Members have until August 6 to add these titles to their game library.
    • Subscribers will also be able to claim the exclusive Genshin Impact PlayStation Plus Pack starting on July 16
  • New Xbox Game Pass titles for console, PC and Cloud have been revealed
    • Arriving:
      • Journey to the Savage Planet (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 3
      • Nickelodeon All-Star Brawl 2 (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 3
      • Cricket 24 (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 9
      • The Case of the Golden Idol (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 9
      • Neon White (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 11
      • Tchia (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series S/X) – July 11
      • Magical Delicacy (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 16
      • Flock (Cloud, Console, and PC) – July 16
    • Leaving:
      • Coffee Talk Episode 2: Hibiscus & Butterfly (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Figment 2: Creed Valley (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Sins of a Solar Empire: Rebellion (PC)
      • TOEM (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • The Wandering Village (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Business

  • Guilty Gear Strive reaches 3m players worldwide
  • Tales of Kenzera: Zau studio is hit with layoffs ("Just over a dozen")
    • Tales of Kenzera: Zau was released earlier this year. Published as an EA Original title, the game received generally positive reviews at release, with a Metacritic score of 76.

Rumors

  • More Xbox Layoffs happening / happened this week.
    • One employee had their role eliminated whilst on vacation

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Dead Rising Deluxe Remaster is coming in September, using the RE Engine
    • The game will include auto-saving, improve UI and more
    • September 19, 2024 for Xbox Series S/X, PlayStation 5 and Steam
    • Capcom also confirmed that the game will be remastered using the RE Engine, Capcom’s versatile engine behind its recent string of hits
    • The game’s digital deluxe edition will include 17 extra costumes for Frank, and 17 music tracks that will be played throughout the in-game mall:
      • Ashley Graham
      • Blodia
      • Chainsaw Villager
      • Chris Redfield
      • Clown
      • Creepy Rabbit
      • Ken
      • Lan Hikari
      • Leon S. Kennedy
      • Licker
      • Mega Man
      • Nemesis
      • Rikuo
      • Sheriff
      • Tron Bonne
      • War Photographer
      • Sodom
  • Resident Evil 7 director Koshi Nakanishi will direct Resident Evil 9
  • Nintendo says it wants to avoid Switch 2 scalping by making enough to meet demand
  • Sega’s Crazy Taxi reboot is a ‘massively multiplayer driving game'
  • Acclaimed theme park management sim Parkitect hits consoles this week
    • Parkitect: Deluxe Edition arrives for Xbox One / Series S/X and PlayStation 4/5 this Wednesday, 3rd July.
  • Star Citizen developer ordered to pay £27k to disabled worker in return-to-office discrimination claim
    • The case, filed by Star Citizen senior programmer Paul Ah-Thion, revolves around a return-to-work policy that also involved work from the company's new central - Manchester office location - some 15 miles from its previous Wilmslow location.
    • Ah-Thion, who previously worked in-office at Wilmslow, brought the case after his requests to work remotely rather than travel into central Manchester were denied and he was subsequently terminated
    • CIG's penalty of £27,748 includes more than £14k of lost earnings, and £12k compensation for injury to feelings.

Business

  • Capcom makes animation production company Minimum Studios a subsidiary
    • The Taiwan-based studio was established in 2018, and specialises in animation production for game development
    • The Taiwanese studio has worked on Resident Evil games and Dragon's Dogma 2
  • Arc System Works has opened a European branch
    • Arc System Works Europe is based in Paris, and will focus on the “marketing of products by Arc System Works Co, their products’ promotions, customer service, licensing operations, localisation, and new IP / new business development”.
  • World of Tanks Blitz passes $1bn in lifetime revenue

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 01/07/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 1 July 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2 cancels public beta to "prepare for the full launch"
  • Helldivers 2 loses 90% of its PC player base in just five months
    • 30-day average of 41,860 concurrent players on Steam
    • Majority of players are on PS5
  • Team Fortress 2 aimbots are being quietly banned by Valve
  • Awesome Games Done Quick has returned

Business

  • A Way Out has sold 9 million units

Rumors

  • Timesplitters could be headed to PlayStation Plus, new ratings suggest
    • Free Radical Design's classic FPS has been classified for PS4/5 in Taiwan
  • A newly filed Sony patent might lend further credence to the recent rumors about PlayStation 3 backwards compatibility being in the works for PlayStation 5
    • Filed on 27 June 2024 and titled "Backward Compatibility Testing of Software in a Mode That Attempts to Induce Skew," the patent describes a mechanism that allows a device (presumably the PS5) to run in a "timing testing mode" that purposefully induces skew.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Xbox Cloud Gaming coming to Amazon Fire TV in select markets
  • Frostpunk 2 release delayed to "guarantee the best possible experience" at launch
    • 11 bit studios is now planning to release Frostpunk 2 on 20th September.
  • Star Wars: Bounty Hunter gets a remake courtesy of Aspyr
    • Set for release on 1st August
    • Aspyr has released a number of Star Wars re-releases and remakes, including most recently Star Wars: Battlefront Classic Collection
  • Ubisoft CEO confirms multiple Assassin's Creed games are getting remakes

Free Games / Subscription

  • Amazon Prime is giving away an additional 6 ‘free’ PC games in July
    • Baldur's Gate: Enhanced Edition
    • Cat Quest 2
    • Maneater
    • Midnight Fight Express
    • Masterplan Tycoon
    • YouTubers Life 2

Business

  • Layoffs at Gameloft Toronto
    • Affects 49 people.
  • Layoffs at Chinese developer Perfect World
  • Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree sells 5m units
  • More users choosing to play games on Steam with controllers
    • Since 2018 daily average controller use has increased from 5% to 15%
    • Xbox is the most popular controller among players 59% of sessions compared to 26% for PlayStation controllers and 10% on Steam Deck
  • Bethesda Montreal files for unionisation
    • Bethesda Montreal is the second ZeniMax studio to form a union

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Dead Rising Deluxe Remaster announced
  • Forza Horizon 4 will be delisted later this year
    • Players who bought the game will still be able to download and play it after this
  • The original 1997 PC port of Resident Evil is now on GOG, with 2 and 3 coming soon
    • Players can buy a bundle which gives access to the sequels as they arrive
  • Steam launches new background gameplay recording and sharing feature into beta

Free Games / Subscription

  • July’s PlayStation Plus Essential games have been revealed
    • Borderlands 3
    • NHL 24
    • Among Us

Business

  • Bandai Namco Online faces insolvency post-Blue Protocol launch
    • The publisher's live-service and MMO-specific studio was already losing money, and 2023's launch of Blue Protocol may have exacerbated that team's issues.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Atari 50 getting 39 new games in DLC detailing Intellivision "console war"
    • Expanded Edition out in 25th October.
  • Disney Epic Mickey: Rebrushed is releasing in 24th September

Free Games / Subscription

  • Amazon Prime is giving away 15 ‘free’ PC games
    • Now available:
    • Deceive Inc (Epic Games Store)
    • Tearstone: Thieves of the Heart (Legacy Games)
    • The Invisible Hand (Amazon Games App)
    • Call of Juarez (GOG)
    • June 27:
    • Forager (GOG)
    • Card Shark (Epic Games Store)
    • Heaven Dust 2 (Amazon Games App)
    • Soulstice (Epic Games Store)
    • July 3:
    • Wall World (Amazon Games App)
    • Hitman Absolution (GOG)
    • Call of Juarez: Bound in Blood (GOG)
    • July 11:
    • Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Shredder’s Revenge (Epic Games Store)
    • Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic 2 – The Sith Lords (Amazon Games App)
    • Alex Kidd in Miracle World DX (Epic Games Store)
    • Samurai Bringer (Amazon Games App)

Business

  • Pokémon never followed up on Palworld legal threat, developer reveals

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 25/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Fortnite's new mode saw over a million players this weekend
    • Tilted Towers returns again in Reload, a take on Warzone's Resurgence.
  • Online shooter Boundary shuts down as dev and publisher clash over rights
  • Resident Evil 2 Remake becomes the franchise's top-seller at nearly 14 million sales
  • Diablo 4 is adding a wave-based survival mode in Season 5

Business

  • Studio co-founded by Dr Disrespect cuts ties, following Twitch ban accusations
  • EU opens fresh investigation into Apple following further non-compliance claims
  • Pullup Entertainment acquires minority stakes in Uppercut and Rundisc
    • The games firm set publish upcoming projects for both studios through subsidiary Focus Entertainment Publishing
  • Second Wave developer folds after missing wage payments and amassing $1.7M in debt
  • GAME denies report it will stop selling physical games, other than fulfilling pre-orders

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo won't confirm studio behind Mario & Luigi: Brothership
    • Stating however that "some of the original developers" were involved
  • Xbox's Matt Booty implies Tango Gameworks closure was partly due to leadership change
    • Shinji Mikami left Tango Gameworks after 12 years in early 2023
  • Sony has reportedly made cuts to VR development, with only 2 titles currently planned
  • Embracer reportedly axed a Red Faction Guerrilla sequel

Business

  • Crash Bandicoot N. Sane Trilogy has sold 20m units

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Beyond Good & Evil: 20th Anniversary Edition will be released next week.
    • Ubisoft confirms that the game will include “new Treasure Hunt”, an “anniversary gallery” and “new content”, as well as a “re-orchestrated soundtrack”.
    • The game will run at up to 4K and 60 fps depending on the platform
  • Microsoft has started reimbursing players who bought cancelled Redfall DLC
  • Demon's Souls (2020) developer Bluepoint Games still working on an original title for PlayStation
    • The studio was also behind titles such as Uncharted's Nathan Drake Collection, Gravity Rush Remastered, and Shadow of the Colossus remaster

Business

  • Embracer Group repays $300m to lenders
    • Repayment comes from net proceeds received from Take-Two's purchase of Gearbox Entertainment
    • Full repayment is due by May 2025, the group still held debts of $1.5 billion.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Dragon Quest 3 HD-2D Remake studio says it regrets announcing the game so early
    • The remake was originally announced in May 2021
  • Fallout 1 and 2 won’t be getting remade, Todd Howard says

Business

  • Apple facing $1bn daily fines over alleged European Commission's Digital Markets Act breaches
  • PlayStation is skipping Gamescom again this year
    • **Sony **most recently appeared at Gamescom in 2019
  • Amazon's Luna adds games from GOG and launches in 3 more countries

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 19/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 19 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Ace Attorney Investigations Collection coming to Switch
    • Ace Attorney Investigations 2: Prosecutor’s Gambit will be released in English for the first time
  • Nintendo finally reveals Metroid Prime 4, with a 2025 release date
    • The Nintendo Switch sequel was first announced in 2017
  • Marvel vs. Capcom Fighting Collection: Arcade Classics announced for Switch
  • Stray is officially coming to Nintendo Switch
  • Super Mario Party Jamboree is coming to Switch this year
  • Dragon Quest 3 HD-2D Remake dated, 1 & 2 HD-2D Remake announced
  • Donkey Kong Country Returns HD is coming to Nintendo Switch
  • Final Fantasy creator’s Fantasian Neo Dimension announced for Switch
    • Square Enix is bringing Fantasian to Switch, PS5, PS4, Xbox series S/X and PC this winter
  • Mario and Luigi: Brothership announced for Switch
  • A second Uncharted movie is officially in the works
    • The Tom Holland movie generated over $400m at the box office
  • Saber Interactive reveals A Quiet Place: The Road Ahead
  • Amazon's next free-to-play fantasy MMO Throne and Liberty is out in September
  • Zelda finally playable protagonist in new standalone game The Legend of Zelda: Echoes of Wisdom

Free Games / Subscription

  • Perfect Dark, Turok and more are coming to Nintendo Switch Online today
    • Perfect Dark (includes online multiplayer)
    • Turok
    • Metroid: Zero Mission
    • The Legend of Zelda: A Link to the Past Four Swords (includes online multiplayer)

Business

  • Paradox is closing its Tectonic studio following Life by You’s cancellation
    • The studio, which employs 24 people, was founded in 2019 and headed by former Sims boss Rod Humble
  • Xbox marketing officer Jerret West leaves for Roblox

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 18/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 18 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Embracer is closing Alone in the Dark studio Pieces Interactive
    • Sweden-based studio worked on over 10 games since 2007
  • Lords of the Fallen sequel set for 2026
    • **CI Games** also confirms **Epic ** has exclusive distribution on PC
  • Todd Howard confirms at least one more Starfield expansion after Shattered Space
  • Paradox Interactive has cancelled Sims-like Life By You
    • The game was indefinitely delayed back is May.
  • Masahiro Sakurai says he’s recorded his final YouTube video
    • The legendary designer for the **Super Smash Bros.** series has been sharing design tips since 2022
    • If you haven't watched them, what have you been doing! Go watch them https://www.youtube.com/@sora_sakurai_en

Business

  • Xbox’s Sea of Thieves was PS5’s most downloaded game in May
    • Rare's 2018 swashbuckler was released on **PS5 ** at the end of April
  • Paper Mario: The Thousand-Year Door debuts at No.1 in Japan
    • Remake of the 2004 GameCube title sold over 115,000 units in three days
  • GTA trilogy reaches 30m downloads on Netflix

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Jackbox Games to launch unified game library in July
    • The Jackbox Megapicker will let Steam players launch any and all Party Pack games they have, no switching necessary.
  • PlayStation adding Discord voice chat direct from console
    • Support will roll out over the coming weeks, starting in Japan/Asia, followed by Europe, Australia/New Zealand, the Middle East, and the Americas

Free Games / Subscription

  • The Epic Games Store’s latest free titles have been confirmed
    • June 13-20: Redout2
    • June 13-20: Duke Ravengard’s Champions of Renown Pack for Idle Champions of the Forgotten Realms
    • June 20-27: Freshly Frosted

Business

  • Wizard With a Gun developer Galvanic Games is closing down
  • Deadline for EQT Group's proposed acquisition of Keywords Studio extended
    • Private equity firm within group that is considering cash offer for services empire has until June 28 to formally announce
  • Tango Gameworks employee shares pictures from the Xbox studio’s final day
    • The Evil Within, Ghostwire: Tokyo and Hi-fi Rush studio has closed its doors, meaning ZeniMax Asia is no more.
  • Warcraft’s general manager has left Blizzard after 12 years
    • Warcraft franchise general manager and SVP John Hight has revealed that he’s resigning from Blizzard after more than a decade with the company.
  • SAG-AFTRA says strike likely if publishers disagree with AI protections
    • Negotiations are being held up by a lack of consensus on the controversial technology and what it means for voice actors.

Rumors

  • Gears of War: E-Day could be released in 2025, it’s claimed

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• PS5 now lets players join Discord voice chat directly from console.
    • Previously players needed to join discord either from a PC or mobile device
  • Xbox published strategy game Ara: History Untold gets a 24th September release date
  • Kingdom Hearts series is now on Steam

Business

  • V Rising was the UK’s best-selling game in May
    • Game sales overall for May a dropped by 21% compared with May 2023.
    • Console sales dropped by 10% on the month before and nearly 33% down on May last year. PS5 was the No.1 selling console, but saw sales down 13% over April.
  • Epic Games Store and Fortnite coming to iOS in Japan next year
    • This is following the passing of a new competition law in Japan.
    • Fortnite will now return to iOS in Japan and UK next year, and EU this year
  • Elden Ring ships 25m units worldwide

Rumors

  • Final Fantasy Tactics Remaster 'is real and happening'
    • Final Fantasy Tactics Remaster was part of the 2021 Nvidia Geforce Now leak
  • PlayStation trophies for Beyond Good & Evil 20th Anniversary Edition have emerged online.
    • Original release of Beyond Good & Evil was 11th November 2003

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Metallica headlines Fortnite, as it introduces a new music-themed battle mode and fresh concert

Free Games / Subscription

  • June’s PlayStation Plus Game Catalogue, Classics and PS VR2 titles announced
    • Monster Hunter Rise | PS4, PS5
    • Football Manager 2024 | PS5
    • Crusader Kings III | PS5
    • Monster Energy Supercross – The Official Videogame 6 | PS4, PS5
    • After Us | PS5
    • Anno 1800 | PS5
    • Police Simulator: Patrol Officers | PS4, PS5
    • Far Cry 4* | PS4
    • Lego The Hobbit | PS4
    • Lego The Incredibiles | PS4
    • Kayak VR: Mirage | PS VR2
    • Lego Star Wars II: The Original Trilogy | PS4, PS5
    • Ghosthunter | PS4, PS5
    • Daxter | PS4, PS5

Business

  • Take-Two officially owns Gearbox Entertainment
    • Just over two months after the deal was first announced, Take-Two Interactive and Embracer Group have officially completed the transfer of Gearbox Entertainment.
  • Valve sued for allegedly overcharging 14 million UK Steam players
    • The Competition Appeal Tribunal claims Valve's 30 percent revenue cut from game purchases lets it hold a monopoly and breach the UK's competition law.
  • Sumo Group closing Timbre Games
    • Sumo says studio "impacted" as part of current cuts
  • Armello, Jumplight Odyssey studio League of Geeks going into "hibernation" following layoffs
    • "We're not sure when (or if) we will resurrect this great beast"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 12/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 12 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• EpicDB (a SteamDB equivalent) launched and appears to be leaking many unannounced projects under codenames
    • Some of the listings include:
      • FFXVI (Skobeloff)
      • FFIX Remake (Momo) with a Tetra Master Starter Pack & Thief's Knives bonus
      • Turok from Saber
      • The Last of Us Part 2 (Utah)
      • + Many More
  • Netflix now describes Arcane season two as "the final chapter"
    • Riot has confirmed show's end, but other projects in pipeline

Business

  • Resident Evil Village passes 10m sales
  • Sackboy and Texas Chain Saw Massacre maker Sumo Group reducing staff by 15%
    • Putting nearly 270 employees at risk
    • The Tencent-owned British games company is thought to employ over 1,650 people
  • Japanese studio Gumi wants 80 people to accept voluntary redundancy
    • The mobile and web3 game maker said it must downsize to achieve 'sustainable growth.'

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 11/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Anno 117: Pax Romana announced for release in 2025
    • 2025 for PS5, Xbox Series S/X and PC
    • Based in Rome
  • Ubisoft’s Prince of Persia The Sands of Time remake gets a 2026 release window
    • A couple weeks prior to its original release date, it was taken from Ubisoft India and moved back to pre-production.
    • Ubisoft Montreal and Ubisoft Toronto are now the development teams working on the title
  • XDefiant Season 1 revealed ahead of July launch
    • The July 2nd season release brings a new faction, GSK from Team Rainbow, plus three free new weapons.
  • More Resident Evil games are coming to iPhone, iPad and Mac
    • Resident Evil 7 Biohazard will be available via the App Store from July 2, 2024, for iPhone 15 Pro, iPhone 15 Pro Max, and all iPads and Macs with the M1 chip or later
    • Resident Evil 2 in development for the same platforms

Business

  • ‘I think we should have a handheld too’, says Xbox boss Phil Spencer

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Black Ops 6 will require a persistent internet connection for all game modes
    • Keeps install size to just 309.85 GB, Activision says
    • Base version is £70, available day one on Xbox Game Pass
  • Starship Troopers: Extermination is leaving early access just in time for a console release
    • PC via Steam, PS5, and Xbox Series S/X on 11th October, 2024
    • Features Johnny Rico from the movie
  • Fable gets a gameplay trailer and a release window
    • The game will be released in 2025 for Xbox Series S/X and PC
    • Eidos Montreal confirms it has been helping with the development of Fable
  • New Avowed story trailer confirms Obsidian’s RPG is still set for a 2024 release
    • PC and Xbox Series S/X "later in 2024"
  • Doom: The Dark Ages is an origin story for the Doom Slayer's rage
    • "2025" for Xbox Series S/X, PC (via Steam) and PS5
  • Fallout 76: Skyline Valley launches next week
    • Skyline Valley presents the multiplayer survival RPG's first map expansion
    • Releasing June 12th
    • Free update to all players
    • This gives players two days to complete the controversial battle pass - causing a stir in the F76 community
      • Players have realized it was not possible for many of them to finish the battle pass without Fallout 1st - the subscription service
    • Players can opt for a Ghoul-ish makeover starting early 2025
  • Valorant on PS5 won’t support gyro aiming, to ensure Xbox players aren’t at a disadvantage
    • "We'd be heavily favbouring PS5 over Xbox, which we don't want to do"
  • Starfield’s story expansion Shattered Space gets a trailer, but no release date yet
    • However, creation kit, new locations, gear, bounties and community created content has been added via the June update
  • World of Warcraft: The War Within launches in August
    • August 26, 2024
  • Metal Gear Solid Δ: Snake Eater gets its first story trailer
    • No release date
  • Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024 gets a release date
    • November 19
    • Xbox Series S/X and PC, Xbox Game Pass on day one
  • Perfect Dark gets first gameplay trailer, but doesn’t yet have a launch window
  • Indiana Jones and the Great Circle gets a new trailer, still set for a 2024 release
    • "Later this year"
    • Xbox Series S/X and PC, Xbox Game Pass on day one
  • The Diablo 4 Vessel of Hatred expansion launches in October
    • Vessel of Hatred releases October 8th
  • Gears of War: E-Day is a prequel told through the eyes of Marcus Fenix
    • The game will depict the locust invasion on Emergence day
    • Fans have been left confused as the Gears 5 storyline finished on a cliff-hanger
    • No release date
  • Age of Mythology: Retold is coming to PC and Xbox in September
    • "Definitive edition treatment" to the 2002 RTS
    • September 4 on Xbox Series S/X, PC and Steam

item.title: Game News Round-Up Special Edition — Summer Games Fest - 08/06/2024
item.description: Special edition games-industry round-up: Summer Games Fest (8 June 2024).
item.content:encoded:
• Among Us developer launches indie fund Outersloth to give others a helping hand
  • Astro’s Playroom has received new content and trophies ahead of Astro Bot’s release
    • The free update adds new gatchas and new "special bots"
  • Nightdive revives a cult 2002 survival horror with The Thing: Remastered
    • The remaster will be released on PS4/5, Xbox One / S/X, Switch, and PC (via Steam) later this year
  • Lego Horizon Adventures has been officially confirmed for PS5, Switch and PC
    • Created by Studio Gobo; a Keywords studio and Guerrilla Games
    • PS5, Switch and PC holiday 2024
  • Harry Potter: Quidditch Champions dated and confirmed for PlayStation Plus day one
    • PS4/4, Xbox One /X/S, Nintendo Switch and PC via Steam and the Epic Games Store on September 3rd
  • Stanley Parable and Tacoma creators fight back against onslaught of coffee games with tea-themed Wanderstop
    • "Coming 2024" for PS5 and PC (Steam)
  • Sonic x Shadow Generations get release date
    • October 25th
    • Pre-orders get a SA1 skin for Sonic
    • Shadow doesn't seem to have guns this time, but he gets edgy wings instead (Editor's Note: a decent compromise?)
  • Alan Wake 2's deliciously bizarre Night Springs DLC out this weekend, physical version finally due later this year
    • Physical version releases "this fall"
    • Night Springs DLC launches Saturday, allows you to play as Jesse Faden (Control), Shaun Ashmore (Playing Himself?) and Rose, the Waitress
  • StarCraft 2 dev David Kim’s RTS hybrid Battle Aces debuts at Summer Game Fest
    • Mixes "RTS elements with deckbuilding"
    • No release date given
  • Horror film production company Blumhouse marks its arrival with SIX game announcements
    • “We’re going to do independent games, we’re going to look for creators and give them a platform, and encourage these creators to be weird and subversive, and find the most F’ed up, scariest things they can, and put them into really cool games.”
  • Digital Eclipse is making a new Power Rangers beat ’em up
    • Mighty Morphin Power Rangers: Rita's Rewind will be released later this year
  • Terry and Mai from Fatal Fury are coming to Street Fighter 6
    • Joined by M Bison and Elena
  • Civilization VII has officially been announced for 2025
    • Civilization VII will be released in 2025, and will be released on PC, PS4/5, Xbox One / S/X and Switch

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• 3DO horror comedy FPS Killing Time is being remastered by Nightdive Studios
    • Killing Time: Resurrected launches later this year on PS4/5, Xbox One / Series S/X, PC (Steam / GOG)
    • Nightdive has also hinted on social media that they are remastering "The Thing"
  • Yooka-Laylee is getting a remaster called Yooka-Replaylee
    • The new version offers overhauled art and animations, as well as “enhanced performance and resolution”.
    • Improvements have been made to the game’s controls and camera, two of the areas where the original game received the most criticism.
    • No release date given
  • Card battler Rabbids: Legends of the Multiverse is one of four new Apple Arcade games today
    • Mobile card-based battling game starring the Rabbids

Free Games / Subscription

  • Xbox Game Pass June titles announced
    • Arriving:
      • Octopath Traveler (Cloud, Console, and PC) - Available now
      • Octopath Traveler 2 (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series X/S) - Available now
      • Depersonalization (PC) – 12th June
      • Isonzo (Cloud, Console, and PC) – 13th June
      • The Callisto Protocol (Cloud, Console, and PC) - 13th June
      • Still Wakes the Deep (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series X|S) – 18th June
    • Leaving:
      • Bramble: The Mountain King (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • High on Life (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Rune Factory 4 Special (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • Spacelines from the Far Out (Cloud, Console, and PC)
      • The Bookwalker (Cloud, Console, and PC)
  • Marvel’s Midnight Suns is now free on Epic Games Store

Business

  • Mediatonic alumni form Kindred Spirit Games
    • Co-founders Megan Ralph and Joel Herber made the announcement on Linkedin, saying the studio was created six months ago. Ralph is the firm's design director and Herber its technical director.

Rumors

  • Summer Game Fest trailers can cost devs up to $550K
    • On the low end, developers will pay $250,000 for a one-minute trailer.
      • The price goes up depending on length, from $350,000 for 90 seconds to a high of $550,000 for two and a half minutes.
    • Major publishers get "earned editorial placements" for free

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Destiny developer "truly sorry" as The Final Shape launch issues see players missing crucial story cutscenes
  • Hades 2's second patch is here, bringing plenty of Daedalus Hammer changes with it

Business

  • Squad Busters generates $1.1m at launch
    • The game apparently saw approximately 8.8 million downloads when the title released on May 29
    • The daily revenue peak for Squad Busters is estimated to be $1.5 million
  • Ubisoft vet David Polfeldt opens new studio in Barcelona
    • Bespoke Pixel has raised $25 million in funding
    • Polfeldt left Massive in 2022 to work as a strategic advisor for PlayerUnknown and Disney before co-founding Bespoke Pixel.

Rumors

  • Looks like Sony's live-service sci-fi shooter Concord will launch with a £35 price tag
    • Much like Helldivers 2
    • Australian PlayStation Store currently lists Concord's Standard Edition with a pre-order price of $59.95 AUD and its Digital Deluxe Edition at $94.95.
    • That matches Helldivers 2's Australian price tag, strongly suggesting Concord will retail for £34.99/$39.99 (£49.99/$59.99 for the Digital Deluxe Edition) in the US and UK

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 05/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 5 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Hitman 3 VR: Reloaded launches this summer as a Meta Quest 3 exclusive
  • Octopath Traveler 2 reportedly coming to Xbox, Windows PC and Game Pass this month

Free Games / Subscription

  • June’s first wave of PlayStation Plus games have been released.
    • Ghostbusters: Rise of the Ghost Lord
    • Walkabout Mini Golf
    • Synth Riders
    • Before Your Eyes
    • The Walking Dead: Saints & Sinners – Chapters 1 & 2
    • Tomb Raider Legend
    • Star Wars: The Clone Wars
    • Sly Cooper and the Thievius Raccoonus.
    • Dredge (PS4, PS5)
    • Lego Marvel Super Heroes 2 (PS4)
    • June 5: Cricket 24 (PS4, PS5)
    • June 7: Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas – The Definitive Edition (PS4, PS5)

Business

  • Dead by Daylight studio Behaviour is laying off up to 95 employees
    • The cuts could impact approximately 7% (up to 95 employees) of its staff, with most based in Montreal
  • Ikea will pay players an hourly wage to work in its virtual Roblox store
    • 10 successful applicants will be paid the London living wage to work in its digital shop

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 04/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 4 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Ubisoft Toronto will co-develop Prince of Persia: Sands of Time's remake
  • Live action Like A Dragon series coming to Amazon Prime video
    • The first three episodes will be released on October 25, 2024, followed by the other three on November 1.
    • Episodes will be subtitled and dubbed in 30 languages
  • Summer Game Fest ‘largely focused’ on announced games coming this year
    • Geoff Keighley attempts to temper expectations for this week's live show
    • The show will begin on June 7 at 2pm PT / 5pm ET / 10pm GMT and run for about two hours, he said

Business

  • Content Warning sells 2.2 million copies, nets 8.8M players in two months
    • The co-op horror game got off to a good start by launching as a free-to-keep game, and its fortunes have only improved since
  • Bloober Team's publishing deal with Private Division reportedly gutted
    • Bloober Team is currently looking for a replacement publisher for "Project C"
    • This follows (currently unverified) rumours that Private Division is closing
  • Just Cause maker Avalanche announces 50 layoffs and closure of two studios
    • Roughly 9% of its workforce is being cut, with its New York and Montreal offices shutting

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/06/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 June 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Take-Two still wants to bring its biggest IP to mobile
    • Two years since it completed its takeover of Zynga, Take-Two is still trying to figure out a way to bring its biggest franchises to mobile.
  • Spyro and Crash studio Toys for Bob partners with Xbox on its next game
    • The developer recently left Activision to go independent
  • $400k worth of stolen Playdate consoles make heroic return after getting dumped outside a restaurant

Rumors

  • Metal Gear Solid Delta: Snake Eater might not release until 2025

Other Notes

  • Summer Game Fest Schedule This Week
    • Friday
      • SummerGameFest Live Show
      • Day of Devs
      • Devolver Direct
    • Saturday:
      • Future of Play
      • Wholesome Direct
      • Latin American Games
      • Women Led Games
    • Sunday:
      • Xbox Games Showcase
    • Monday:
      • Ubisoft Forward

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 31/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 31 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Redfall’s final update has been released, adding offline play
    • Features include Pausing in Single-Player, new weapon and revamped Nests system
    • The update is called 1.451.3.0
    • 0451 is a common "Immersive Sim" easter egg that pays homage to System Shock.
  • PlayStation’s sci-fi multiplayer FPS Concord gets first gameplay footage and release date
    • Debut game from Washington-based Firewalk Studios
    • The game will be released on PS5 and PC on August 23, with a beta taking place in July
  • Pac-Man’s 64-player battle royale game has yet to reach 64 concurrent players on Steam
    • Pac-Man Mega Tunnel Battle was originally a Google Stadia exclusive released in November 2020, but when Stadia officially shut down in January 2023 it naturally ended with it.
    • Bandai Namco released an updated version, titled Pac-Man Mega Tunnel Battle: Chomp Champs, on PC and consoles on May 9
    • The game also supports cross-play, meaning those Steam players could theoretically still find a 64-player game by joining those playing on other platforms.
  • Silent Hill 2 remake has a release date
    • PS5 / Steam: October 8, 2024
  • Astro Bot is getting his own full-price platformer
    • Releases September 6
    • The game will see Astro making use of 15 new abilities, making full use of the DualSense controller as you explore 6 galaxies and over 80 levels
  • Monster Hunter Wilds gets its first gameplay trailer
    • Releases 2025
  • Netflix announces Minecraft animated series
    • Series to be co-developed with animation studio WildBrain
    • Seemingly unrelated to the Jack Black led live action movie.

Business

  • Amazon signs debut title from Playground alumni Maverick Games
    • The unannounced driving game will be open world and narrative-led, and release across PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC
    • Co-creator of TV show Skins, Jamie Brittain, is on board as the game's lead writer.
  • Fallout 76 nets 20 million players

Rumors

  • Just Cause movie is on the way
  • YouTube has investigated employees breaching their contractual agreements on two different occasions in the past 18 months due to employees accessing content on the backend (Leaking game information)
    • The State of Play last night was leaked ahead of time, once again.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Sony apologises to Neil Druckmann following ‘heavily edited’ interview
    • Druckmann posted his unedited comments to social media following backlash
    • “We apologize to Neil for misrepresenting his words and for any negative impact this interview might have caused him and his team. In coordination with Naughty Dog and SIE, we have removed the interview.”
  • MultiVersus relaunches to more than 114,000 concurrent players on Steam
  • PlayStation State of Play Tonight
    • 3pm PT / 6pm ET / 11pm BST
    • "30 minutes covering PS5 and PS VR 2 titles"
    • In total, 14 titles will be shown, including some upcoming PlayStation Studios titles.
  • Silent Hill Transmission Tonight
    • One hour after the PlayStation State of Play
    • 4pm PT / 7pm ET / Midnight 31st BST

Free Games / Subscription

  • June’s ‘free’ games with Amazon Prime Gaming have been announced
    • May 30: The Lullaby of Life [GOG code]
    • June 6: Star Wars Battlefront II (Classic, 2005) [GOG code]
    • June 6: Weird West Definitive Edition [Epic Game Store code]
    • June 6: Genesis Noir [Amazon Games app]
    • June 13: Everdream Valley [Amazon Games app]
    • June 13: Mythforce [Epic Game Store code]
    • June 13: Blast Brigade vs. the Evil Legion of Dr. Cread [GOG code]
    • June 13: Projection: First Light [Amazon Games app]

Business

  • Gameloft Romania cuts 136 jobs and closes Cluj studio
  • Evercade has sold one million cartridges
  • PlayStation 5 "most profitable generation to date", Sony says
    • Operating income for PS5 gen reaches $10bn, beating PS4's $9bn
    • PS5 has hit $106bn in sales, $1 billion below PS4
    • The PS5 and PS4 both have 49 million users per month, as of 30th April
    • 50% of this is PS5 users, 50% is PS4
    • Of the 3.8bn hours played, 2.4bn of those were on PS5
    • PS5 users are spending 176 percent more on add-on content, 57 percent more on services and 34 percent more on peripherals than the previous generation of console did in the first four years of its cycle
    • PS5 users are also spending 12 percent less on full game content than those on PS4 did

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Black Ops 6 live action trailer released as Game Pass launch announced
    • Activision will fully reveal the game during a showcase on June 9
    • Confirmed to be day one Game Pass
  • Lego The Legend of Zelda: Great Deku Tree officially launching in September
    • It will cost €299.99 / £259.99/ $299.99 (2,500 pieces)
  • Genshin Impact developer's upcoming urban action RPG lands summer release date
    • Zenless Zone Zero launches July 4th
    • Pre-registration numbers over 35 million
  • YouTube launches in-app Playables arcade
    • The video platform is experimenting with instant and quick-to-play games for users on its browser and phone apps
    • Previously soft-launched earlier in the year
    • At the moment, the arcade will have over 75 games that are instantly playable whether on computer, iOS, or Android

Free Games / Subscription

  • Days of Play 2024 adds new PS2 classics and PS VR2 games to PS Plus
    • SpongeBob Squarepants: The Cosmic Shake
    • AEW Fight Forever
    • Streets of Rage 4
    • Ghostbusters: Rise of the Ghost Lord
    • Walkabout Mini Golf
    • Synth Riders
    • Before Your Eyes
    • The Walking Dead: Saints & Sinners – Chapters 1 & 2
    • Tomb Raider Legend (PS2)
    • Star Wars: The Clone Wars (PS2)
    • Sly Cooper and the Thievius Raccoonus (PS2)
    • Dredge | PS4, PS5 (available May 29)
    • Lego Marvel Super Heroes 2 | PS4 (available May 31)
    • Cricket 24 | PS4, PS5 (available June 5)
    • Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas – The Definitive Edition | PS4, PS5 (available June 7)

Business

  • CD Projekt Q1 Results
    • 226.8 million PLN in revenue, up 30 percent from 2023's start

Rumors

  • Resident Evil Zero and Code Veronica remakes currently in development, leak claims

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 28/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Silent Hill stream planned for May 30th
    • 4 p.m. PDT on the SILENT HILL YouTube channel

Rumors

  • Doom: The Dark Ages to be revealed at Xbox Games Showcase
    • "Medieval Inspired Doom World"
  • A new Astro Bot game will be revealed ‘in weeks’
    • Rumor is that the title could be called simply, ‘Astro Bot’, and could be announced within the next 15 days

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 27/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo is opening an official store in San Francisco next year
    • It's second U.S store will be located in Union Square
  • New Fortnite season adds Fallout’s T-60 power armor and Wastelander Magneto

Free Games / Subscription

  • 12 games are leaving PlayStation Plus in June
    • Brothers: A Tale of Two Sons (PS4)
    • DC League of Super-Pets: The Adventures of Krypto and Ace (PS4)
    • Dodgeball Academia (PS4)
    • Grand Theft Auto V (PS4/5)
    • Indivisible (PS4)
    • Monster Jam Steel Titans (PS4)
    • MotoGP 23 (PS4/5)
    • SpongeBob SquarePants: Battle for Bikini Bottom Rehydrated (PS4)
    • The Talos Principle: Deluxe Edition (PS4)
    • The Wild at Heart (PS4)
    • Unturned (PS4)
    • Baja: Edge of Control HD (PS4)

Business

  • Bungie wins court battle against Destiny cheat site AimJunkies
    • AimJunkies, aka Phoenix Digital, was instructed to pay the Destiny 2 developer $63,210 in damages – that's the revenue the company is thought to have earned selling the cheats – setting a new precedent in what's thought to be the first lawsuit wherein a jury has ruled on a game-cheating case

Rumors

  • A new Astro Bot game will be revealed ‘in weeks’, it’s claimed

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 24/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 24 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• MultiVersus director says dataminers do ‘a disservice to the players’
    • "They don't get the grand reveal that we have planned"
  • Activision has officially confirmed Black Ops 6 is this year’s Call of Duty
  • Warhammer 40,000 retro shooter Boltgun is getting an expansion in June
    • New enemies, campaign and more on 18th June
  • Indie publisher Devolver Digital announces summer showcase
    • "Totally normal festivities" start Satuday 8th June, 1am UK time
  • Nightingale introduces highly-requested offline mode in new early access update
  • Overwatch PvE mode dropped after just three months
    • "It hasn't resonated with players"

Free Games / Subscription

  • Farming Simulator 22 is the next Epic Store free game

Business

  • Atari has acquired the Intellivision brand, ending the first ever console war
    • The deal will not include the Amico.
    • Instead, Intellivision Entertainment LLC will continue as a separate, rebranded company, and will continue working on the Amico, using a licence from Atari to release Intellivision games on it.

Rumors

  • xDefiant reportedly netted 1 million players within 2.5 hours of launch
    • It's said the game reached 3 million players and now has 300,000 concurrent players on PC (via Ubisoft Connect), Xbox Series S/X, and PlayStation 5

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 23/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 23 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Returning MultiVersus beta players will receive free premium battle pass
    • Users who played in the previous "beta" will have to login by June 11th to claim
    • In addition, any returning players will receive a special edition Snow Suit Finn character variant, a special edition Rising Stars ringout, and a special edition Banana Guard announcer pack.

Business

  • Relic partners with investment firm to go fully indie again
    • The Vancouver studio split from Sega Europe in late March and has now secured funding to stay indie for the foreseeable future.
    • It's entered a partnership with investment firm Emona Capital that will let it "return to its roots."
  • Helldivers 2 developer Arrowhead names former Paradox exec as new CEO
    • Former Paradox Interactive chief business development officer Shams Jorjani has joined the Swedish studio to oversee business operations
    • Outgoing CEO Johan Pilestedt will remain with the studio as chief creative officer and explained the switch will enable him to spend more time with the dev team.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 22/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 22 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
Business

  • PlayStation London Studio has officially closed its doors
    • Founded in 2002 and based in Soho, London, the first-party studio worked on The Getaway series and was responsible for the Singstar franchise.
    • It also developed numerous games for PlayStation’s EyeToy camera, Wonderbook, and PlayStation VR titles VR Worlds and Blood & Truth.
  • IGN Entertainment acquires Eurogamer, GI, VG247, Rock Paper Shotgun and more
    • New owner makes redundancies across the Gamer Network portfolio
    • Gamer Network’s publications are GamesIndustry.biz, Eurogamer (including six local language editions), Rock Paper Shotgun, VG247 and Dicebreaker.
    • The business also holds shares in Outside Xbox, Digital Foundry and Hookshot (which operates Nintendolife, PushSquare, PureXbox and Time Extension).
  • Amazon Games is opening its first European studio, helmed by a Ubisoft veteran
    • Amazon Games Bucharest
    • The studio will be led by Cristian Pana, former managing director at Ubisoft Bucharest.
    • Pana has over 20 years of experience and previously worked on Far Cry, The Division, and Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora.
  • Little Kitty Big City reaches 1m players on Game Pass
    • Double Dagger Studio's debut sold 100,000 units in two days following its May 9 launch

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 21/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 21 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Kingdom Hearts is coming to Steam
    • June 13th
    • KINGDOM HEARTS HD 1.5 + 2.5 ReMIX
    • KINGDOM HEARTS HD 2.8 Final Chapter Prologue
    • KINGDOM HEARTS III + Re Mind DLC
    • KINGDOM HEARTS INTEGRUM MASTERPIECE (bundle that includes all the above compilations)
  • MultiVersus launch trailer reveals Jason Voorhees and Agent Smith as playable characters
    • Jason will be available at launch, while Agent Smith will be released later in the season
    • MultiVersus will officially return on May 28

Business

  • Nintendo is set to fully acquire Miami-based Shiver Entertainment
    • Shiver is the studio responsible for multiple high-profile Nintendo Switch ports, such as Hogwarts Legacy and Mortal Kombat 1
    • Embracer currently owns Shiver
  • Bandai Namco Financial Results
    • The Numbers:
      • Net sales: ¥1.05 trillion ($6.74 billion, up 6%)
      • Operating profit: ¥90.6 billion ($582 million, down 22%)
      • Net profit: ¥101.4 billion ($651.4 million, up 12.3%)
      • Digital (incl. video games)
      • Net sales: ¥372.6 billion ($2.39 billion, down -3.4%)
      • Profit: ¥6.2 billion ($39.8 million, down 87%)
    • Forecast:
      • Net sales: ¥1.08 trillion ($6.9 billion, up 2.8%)
      • Operating profit: ¥115 billion ($738.7 million, up 27%)
      • Net profit: ¥81 billion ($520.3 million, down 20%)
      • Digital (incl. video games)
      • Net sales: ¥370 billion ($2.38 billion, down -0.6%)
      • Profit: ¥30 billion ($192.7 million, up 384%) <- ???
  • Ardán launches development fund for Irish indie studios
    • The IndieDev program will grant €15,000 to the seven studios in the island of Ireland to generate ideas and create game prototypes

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 20/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Redfall will receive a final update adding offline play
    • Game Update 4, that brings revamped Neighborhood and Nest systems, Single Player Pausing, Offline Mode, and more
  • Fallout is coming to Fortnite
  • Hades 2 in early access "at least through the end of this year", says creative director

Business

  • Ghost of Tsushima becomes PlayStation's biggest single-player launch on PC
    • The game has attracted over 73,000 concurrent players
    • The only other PlayStation-published game with a higher peak is multiplayer phenomenon Helldivers 2, with a whopping 458,000 peak
    • Has a slight advantage since it came to the storefront on launch day, as opposed to being ported years later.

Rumors

  • Total War: Star Wars reportedly in development at Creative Assembly
  • Player1 reportedly lays off staff, closes down Insomnia Gaming Festival
    • Unclear at this time on who exactly was laid off. Some sources say it's the entire sales and marketing team whereas others claim it's the entire company
    • Insomnia's collapse leaves EGX and WASD as the UK's main consumer shows.
    • WASD took place in April in London, while EGX is co-locating with MCM in October this year.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 17/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• 2K to reveal new game in one of its "biggest and most beloved" franchises next month
    • June 7th. Expected to be Borderlands related.
  • Grand Theft Auto 6 narrows release window to "fall" 2025
  • Friday the 13th villain Jason Voorhees is set to appear in more games
    • A new Enterprise called Jason Universe will bring the slasher icon to a range of media
    • Its X account (formally known as Twitter) is currently only following one other account on the social media platform, which is Fortnite.

Free Games / Subscription

  • The Epic Games Store will reportedly offer 4 free AAA games over the next month
    • Right now you can claim Dragon Age Inquisition – Game of the Year Edition

Business

  • Phoenix Labs lays off over 100 staff, cancels all upcoming projects
    • One of the cancelled games was nearing an announcement
  • GTA 5 has surpassed 200 million copies sold, according to Take Two

Rumors

  • Details on New 'Deadlock' Valve Game Leaked
    • Deadlock will reportedly be a 6 vs 6 third-person hero shooter
    • The game will feature a large map with four lanes to attack that have tower defence mechanics as well as various items and abilities to utilize.
    • Fantasy setting “mixed with steampunk. Magicians, weird creatures and robots.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 16/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 16 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Ubisoft's free-to-play shooter The Division Heartland cancelled three years after being announced
  • Assassin's Creed Shadows trailer reveals first look at dual protagonists
    • The long-leaked Japan-based Assassin's Creed title (prev. codename "Red") has finally been revealed.
    • Launches on 15th November for PC (via the Ubisoft / Epic Game Store) Mac, PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series S/X
    • Three days early access for those with Ubisoft+ or for those who buy a pricier collector's edition
    • £59.99 base game (£69.99 on consoles)
    • £94.99 Gold Edition adding the season pass and three days "early access"
    • £114.99 Ultimate Edition adding five skill points, the Sekiryu character pack and hideout pack, plus the Red Dragon photo filter
  • Final Fantasy 14 TTRPG is so popular there's a second print before it's even out
    • Priced at £50.99
    • Remains unclear whether the game is based on an existing system like Dungeons & Dragons, or something new.
  • Hi-Fi Rush studio breaks silence to announce one last patch
    • “We’re working to release the physical edition of Hi-Fi Rush with our partners at Limited Run Games, as well as a final patch to address some minor issues releasing later today.”
  • Mortal Kombat 2 film ties down release date
  • UK government responds to Stop Killing Games campaign
    • There is "no requirement in UK law" preventing publishers from rendering older games unplayable
    • The petition will continue to run until October 16, 2024 and if it passes 100,000 signatures, it will be considered for debate in Parliament. At the time of writing, 22,890 people have signed.

Free Games / Subscription

  • Red Dead Redemption 2 headlines PlayStation Plus Premium and Extra games for May
    • 21st May:
    • PS+ Extra:
    • Red Dead Redemption 2 (PS4)
    • Deceive Inc. (PS5)
    • The Sims 4 City Living (PS4, requires The Sims 4)
    • Crime Boss: Rockay City (PS5)
    • The Settlers: New Allies (PS4)
    • Stranded: Alien Dawn (PS4, PS5)
    • Cat Quest 1 & 2 (PS4)
    • The LEGO Movie 2 Videogame (PS4)
    • Watch Dogs (PS4)
    • PS+ Premium:
    • 2Xtreme (PS4, PS5)
    • G-Police (PS4, PS5)
    • Worms Pinball (PS4, PS5)
  • Super Mario Land, Alleyway and Baseball added to Nintendo Switch Online

Business

  • Ubisoft Financial Results
    • Sales: €2.3 billion ($2.5 billion), an increase of 27% year-over-year
    • Net bookings: €2.32 billion ($2.52 billion), an increase of 34% year-over-year
    • Operating income: €313.6 million ($341 million), compared to a loss of €586 million ($638 million) profit the previous year.
  • Former Bandai Namco worker arrested for suspected embezzlement
    • Ex-employee is said to have sold upwards of 4,000 company-owned smartphones worth around $2.57 million
  • Marc Whitten to leave Unity
    • Unity's chief product and technology officer Marc Whitten will resign from his role on June 1, and will leave the firm by the end of 2024

Rumors

  • Microsoft bosses reportedly pushing for no "red line" around which Xbox games launch on PlayStation

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 15/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 15 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Dead by Daylight roguelite gets a surprise release, and 2 million copies are free
    • Titled What the Fog, the game was revealed on Tuesday during a Dead by Daylight eighth anniversary broadcast
    • Two million copies of What the Fog are being given away for free to players who sign in to or create a Behaviour account
    • The game is also available on Steam for $4.99 / £4.29
    • Dead by Daylight’s anniversary broadcast was also used to announce Dungeons & Dragons and Castlevania chapters for the multiplayer action horror game, plus a 2V8 game mode that pits two killers against eight survivors on a much larger map
    • The Dungeons & Dragons chapter launches on June 3, while more info on the Castlevania chapter will be revealed on August 6
  • Amazon has greenlit a Tomb Raider TV series from Phoebe Waller-Bridge
    • The British actress, screenwriter and producer, who won Emmy, Golden Globe and BAFTA awards for her work on Fleabag, will serve as writer and executive producer on the TV show

Free Games / Subscription

  • New Xbox Game Pass titles for console, PC and Cloud have been revealed
    • Arriving:
    • Brothers: A Tale of Two Sons (Cloud, Console, and PC) – May 14
    • Chants of Sennaar (Cloud, Console, and PC) – May 15
    • EA Sports NHL 24 (Cloud) EA Play – May 16
    • Immortals of Aveum (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series X|S) EA Play – May 16
    • Senua’s Saga: Hellblade II (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series X|S) – May 21
    • Galacticare (Cloud, PC, Xbox Series X|S) – May 23
    • Hauntii (Cloud, Console, and PC) – May 23
    • Moving Out 2 (Cloud, Console, and PC) – May 28
    • Humanity (Cloud, Console, and PC) – May 30
    • Lords of the Fallen (Cloud, PC, and Xbox Series X|S) – May 30
    • Firework (PC) – June 4
    • Rolling Hills (Cloud, Console, and PC) – June 4
    • Leaving:
    • Chicory: A Colorful Tale (Cloud, Console, and PC)
    • Farworld Pioneers (Cloud, Console, and PC)
    • JoJo’s Bizarre Adventure: All-Star Battle (Cloud, Console, and PC)
    • Pac-man Museum Plus (Cloud, Console, and PC)
    • Little Witch in the Woods (Cloud, Console, and PC)
    • Railway Empire II (Cloud, Console, and PC)

Business

  • Fortnite to appeal €1.1m fine over "unfair commercial practices aimed at children"
    • ACM says that Fortnite used ads aimed at encouraging kids to make purchases with slogans such as "get it now", and "misleading" sale countdown timers that gave the impression certain items would be removed from sale before they ultimately were.
