  • Two Joy-Con lawsuits against Nintendo are dismissed
    • Diaz vs. Nintendo and Carbajal vs. Nintendo we moved for the dismissal by both the Mario maker and parents involved
  • Xbox's "Transparency Report" H2
    • From the six months that ended December 31, 2023, Xbox users issued 27.44 million reports (Up from 10.31 million in H2 2023)
    • 12.74 million (47%) user reports regarding communication with others
    • 11.34 million (41%) reports involving unsportsmanlike conduct (cheating)
    • 3.36 million (12%) reports related to user-generated content
    • 7.32 million proactive enforcements for cheating and inauthentic accounts
    • 138,000 recordings captured by the Xbox voice reporting feature
    • 98% of users showed better behavior after receiving a strike
    • 674 reports to the National Center for Missing and Exploited Children
    • 2,076 referrals to Crisis Text Line
    • 179,000 players appealed against suspensions
    • 7% of appeals were successful

Rumors

  • The Lego Zelda Great Deku Tree will be released in September for €300, it’s claimed
    • The set will include two different designs for the tree, one based on The Legend of Zelda Ocarina of Time, and another based on The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
    • It’s claimed that if owners want to change the design, the tree will have to be completely rebuilt.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 14/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Developer Double Eleven taken off Prison Architect series after nine years
    • Paradox notes that Prison Architect 2 passed certification and the contract was fulfilled, but "...We could not find a commercial agreement that worked for both parties moving forward and mutually agreed to part ways."

Business

  • Sony’s gaming division Financial Results
    • The Numbers:
    • Year Ended March 31 2024:
      • **Sales**: ¥4.26 trillion (17% year-on-year)
      • **Sony’s Network services revenue**: ¥545.5 billion (up from ¥464.6 billion)
      • **Software sales (PS4/5)**: 286.4 million units (22.2 million increase year-on-year)
    • Highlights:
      • Digital software accounted for 70% of annual game sales, compared to 67% last year
      • PS5 has sold 59.3 million units to date
      • Helldivers 2 is now PlayStation’s fastest-selling game launch ever, with sales “far exceeding expectations” at 12 million copies sold across PC and PS5 in its first 12 weeks
  • PlayStation names Hermen Hulst and Hideaki Nishino as its new CEOs
    • Hulst is CEO of the newly named Studio Business Group, which includes all of PlayStation’s first-party teams, plus covers the development of PlayStation IP onto other mediums, such as TV and film.
    • Hulst was already head of PlayStation Studios. He was previously the co-founder of Horizon and Killzone developer Guerrilla Games, which was acquired by Sony in 2005.
    • Hideaki Nishino will lead the Platform Business Group, which includes console hardware, technology, accessories, PlayStation Network and third-party relations (covering major publishers and indie studios).
    • He was already SVP of Platform Experiences. He’s been part of the Sony business since 2006, holding numerous roles at Sony Network Entertainment, Sony Corporation and SIE.
  • Warner Bros. transferring ownership of titles under Adult Swim Games label back to some devs
    • The move comes months after the firm said it was delisting titles from the publishing division
  • Square Enix Financial Results
    • The Numbers:
    • **Net sales**: ¥356 billion ($2.28 billion, up 3.8% year-on-year from ¥343 billion)
    • **Digital entertainment sales**: ¥248 billion ($1.59 billion, up 2.6% YoY from ¥245 billion)
    • **Operating income**: ¥32.5 billion ($208 million, down 26.6% YoY from ¥44.3 billion)
    • **Profit attributable to owners of parent**: ¥15 billion ($96.2 million, down 69.7% YoY from ¥49.2 billion)
    • The Aftermath:
    • Square Enix Has Said:
      • FF16 sales fell short of expectations
      • FF7 Rebirth fell short of expectations
      • Foamstars fell short of expectations
      • Remains confident FF16 can achieve its goal over the original 18-month sales plan. Also, sales of Rebirth and Foamstars aren't necessarily bad.
      • Reorganized development team into five console teams, one smartphone game team
      • Has been relied too much on an individual's creativity
      • Will promote sharing game making how-how among teams and will seek best balance of individual creativity and organizational discipline
      • DQ12 remains under development
    • Investors Reacted:
    • Sold shares heavily, stocks fell to the day's trading limit
    • Square Enix Reacted:
    • Layoffs
    • Employees working in publishing, IT, and the Square Enix Indie Collective are losing their jobs.
      • American and United Kingdom offices are affected and are expected to be further affected

Rumors

  • Red Dead Redemption 1 finally heading to PC, new datamine suggests

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 13/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 13 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Square Enix will make AAA games multiplatform as part of its ‘aggressive’ new business plan
    • It implies that the publisher's days of platform exclusives may be ending
  • Microsoft to launch mobile game store in July
  • Netflix launches Nextworld, a ‘digital theme park’ on Roblox with games and items
    • The hub includes games based on Stranger Things, One Piece and Rebel Moon
  • EA has teams looking at "thoughtful implementations" of ads "inside its game experiences"
  • The Rogue Prince of Persia delayed to avoid popularity of Hades 2

Free Games / Subscription

  • Destiny developer Bungie's original sci-fi shooter Marathon hits Steam for free
    • The rest of the trilogy is on the way

Business

  • V Rising hits 100,000 concurrent players on Steam
    • It moved into full release last week
  • Little Kitty Big City sells 100,000 units in 48 hours
  • Unity restructuring increases losses to $291m in Q1 2024
    • Q1 Results:
    • Total revenue: $460 million (down 8% year-on-year)
    • Create Solutions revenue: $133 million (up 17%)
    • Grow Solutions revenue: $294 million (down 4%)
    • Net loss: $291 million (compared to $254 million for same quarter last year)

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 10/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Hellblade 2 is only two weeks away, and fans feel Xbox should be making more noise
    • Senua's Saga: Hellblade 2 releases 21st May
    • Xbox marketing mouthpiece Aaron Greenberg told fans that Hellblade 2's "global paid media campaign started yesterday", and noted there was a "special Iceland creator event" happening now - where the company has apparently flown some YouTubers to visit the game's setting (not to the supermarket).
  • Dead by Daylight teases dark fantasy era with Dungeons & Dragons themed collaboration
    • "Learn more on May 14th"
  • Devolver believes in "selling games first" over day one launches in Game Pass-esque subscriptions, says co-founder
    • "We're looking at subscription more for the longer tail."
  • Nintendo is ending X/Twitter support for Nintendo Switch
    • Beginning on June 10, users will no longer be able to upload images to the service directly from Nintendo Switch machines, and the Nintendo Switch will no longer allow players to send friend requests to social media friends using X integration

Business

  • Capcom announces seventh consecutive year of record results
    • The Numbers:
    • FY Ending March 31st:
      • Net Sales: were 152,410 million yen ($977M), which is up 21.0% year-over-year
      • Operating Income: was 57,081 million yen ($366M) which is up 12.3% year-over-year
      • Ordinary Income: was 59,422 million yen ($381M), which is up 15.7 % year-over-year
      • Net Income: 43,374 million yen ($278M) up 18.1 % year-over-year
    • Highlights:
    • Street Fighter 6 sold 3.3 million units
    • Dragon’s Dogma 2 sold 2.6 million (series now at over 11 million units)
    • Resident Evil 4 Remake sold 7.02 million units (series now at 36.29 million units)
    • 15 titles achieved over 1 million sales during this financial period.
      • Monster Hunter franchise has crossed 100 million cumulative sales
  • Roblox Q1 Financial Results
    • The Numbers:
    • Revenue: $801 million, up 22% year-on-year
    • Bookings: $924 million, up 19% year-on-year
    • Net losses: $270 million, compared to $269 million in the same quarter last year
  • Warner Bros CEO says ‘disappointing’ Suicide Squad release ‘overshadowed’ its overall Studios financials

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 09/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Nintendo World Championships: NES Edition announced for Switch with 'over 150 challenges from 13 NES games'
    • Specifically concerned with speedrunning the game features 150 "byte-sized" challenges that can be tackled across several modes across 13 games:
    • Balloon Fight
    • Donkey Kong
    • Excitebike
    • Ice Climber
    • Kid Icarus
    • Kirby’s Adventure
    • Metroid
    • Super Mario Bros.
    • Super Mario Bros. 2
    • Super Mario Bros. 3
    • Super Mario Bros. The Lost Levels
    • The Legend of Zelda
    • Zelda 2: The Adventure of Link
    • Will be available digitally for £24.99 via Switch's eShop on 18th July, and a £49.99 physical Deluxe Set releases at the same time.
  • Sega unveils Fall Guys-like Sonic Rumble as Sonic Mania Plus hits mobile
    • Editor's note: The framerate of the trailer, did they not have better footage?!
    • Available "this winter"
    • Until then, Sonic Mania Plus is now available on iOS and Android via a Netflix subscription
  • EA CEO says there’s a “real hunger” among developers to use AI to speed up development
  • Mark Hamill will voice The Joker in Multiversus
    • MultiVersus will officially return on May 28
  • Shin Megami Tensei 5 joins Download Festival
    • Download has called it a "first of its kind experience" and is the latest in a growing crossover between the worlds of music and gaming.
    • Game releases the first day of the festival

Business

  • Dead Island 2 reaches "over" 7 million players in it's first year
  • Nintendo Financial Results
    • FY (Ended March 31st 2024)
    • Net sales: ¥1.7 trillion ($11 billion, up 4.4% year-on-year)
    • Operating profit: ¥528.9 billion ($3.4 billion, up 4.9%)
    • Ordinary profit: ¥680.5 billion ($4.4 billion, up 13.2%)
    • Gross profit: ¥954.3 billion ($6.2 billion, up 7.8%)
    • Forecast (March 2025)
    • Net sales: ¥1.35 trillion ($8.7 billion, down 19.3%)
    • Operating profit: ¥400 billion ($2.6 billion, down 24.4%)
    • Ordinary profit: ¥420 billion ($2.7 billion, down 38.3%)
  • Krafton Q1 Results
    • Sales: $488 million (666 billion KRW), up 24% year-on-year
    • Operating Profit: $227 million (310.5 billion KRW), up 10% year-on-year
    • Net Profit: $255 million (348.6 billion KRW), up 30% year-on-year

Rumors

  • Microsoft's Perfect Dark reboot reportedly in "a very rough state" and a "big, protracted mess"

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 08/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 8 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Witcher 3’s official mod editor launches this month
    • It will be available free for everyone who owns the game on PC
    • The Witcher 3 REDkit will be released May 21st
  • Redfall will get no further updates, players who paid for DLC will get ‘make good offers’
    • “The game and its servers will remain online for players to enjoy and we will provide make-good offers to players who purchased the Hero DLC.”
    • The DLC referred to in the email was included in the $99.99 Bite Back Edition of the game. This included the ‘Hero Pass’, which was originally promised to include two extra heroes. That content has now been cancelled.

Free Games / Subscription

  • May’s PlayStation Plus Essential games have been released
    • Now Available:
    • EA Sports FC 24 (PS4/5)
    • Ghostrunner 2 (PS5)
    • Tunic (PS4/5)
    • Destiny 2: Lightfall (PS4/5)

Business

  • Support dev PTW opens mocap studio to grow partnership with Imaginarium Studios
    • PTW, a support developer that's previously worked on Starfield and Exoprimal, has opened a new studio exclusively for motion capture.
    • Located in the same building as audio developer SIDE London, it's meant as an expansion of PTW's partnership with Imaginarium.
    • The aim is to make it so full-body capture and voice recording can be done simultaneously in the same place
  • Activision to pay $23.4m for patent infringement
    • Acceleration Bay's legal battle with the Call of Duty maker ends after eight years
    • The case filed in the US District Court for the District of Delaware claims that patents for networking and broadcasting were used in Activision's multiplayer title.
    • The publisher owes Acceleration Bay $18 million in copyright infringement for World of Warcraft and $5.4 million for two Call of Duty titles.
    • Editor's Note: I tried to look into the specific patents and what was infringing in the two games mentioned, but I could not find any public mention on the specific infringements
    • While the case against Activision Blizzard has been decided in favour of the tech incubator, it has also filed similar patent infringement suits against EA, Take-Two, and Amazon Web Services.
    • Activision will appeal.
  • In less than a day, Hades 2 Early Access has doubled Hades’ all-time peak player count on Steam
    • It should be noted that the first Hades game was originally an Epic Games Store exclusive, where it was released as an Early Access title in 2018, and didn’t come to Steam until 2019.
    • However, its peak on Steam wasn’t reached until the game came out of Early Access and officially ‘launched’ in September 2020.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 07/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 7 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Remedy has cancelled "Project Kestral"
    • According to Remedy Entertainment, the cancelation of “Kestrel” (previously "Vanguard") will allow other ongoing game development projects to gain experienced developers reassigned from “Kestrel.”
    • Additionally, investment needs for the project have been removed, and Remedy Entertainment’s overall recruitment needs have been reduced.
  • Hades 2 Early access is out now
    • Available in early access on Steam and Epic Games Store
    • The full release date of the final game has not been given at this time
  • Nintendo confirms Switch Successor will be announced "this fiscal year"
    • A Switch Nintendo Direct to be held this June
    • Don't expect Switch Successor news at the Nintendo Direct
  • Beat Saber drops Meta Quest 1 support in November
    • On November 2, Multiplayer will be disabled for the game on Meta Quest 1 (but the game will remain playable)
    • Leaderboards "might" be shut off later down the line.
  • Xbox is shutting down Arkane Austin, Tango Gameworks, Alpha Dog Studios and Roundhouse Games (will be merge with Zenimax Online Studios)

Business

  • Story Kitchen teams with Dreamworks Animation to adapt video games to film and TV
    • The Kung Fu Panda studio has a first-look option on any game-based animated movie Story Kitchen works on.
    • Story Kitchen is currently working on a live-action Dredge film and Vampire Survivors animated series (They aren't included in this deal)
    • Story Kitchen's noted projects as of late include an adaptation of It Takes Two for Amazon, Lionsgate's Streets of Rage movie, and Netflix's Tomb Raider anime

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 06/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 6 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Limited Run apologises for selling CD-Rs as authentic 3DO games
    • The company recently sold a $64.99 ‘Collector’s Edition’ of 1995 horror game D, consisting of a physical three-disc release for the 3DO console.
    • However, some players receiving the game discovered that it doesn’t work on their console, because it’s a CD-R instead of a pressed disc.
    • "“We tested the discs on the 3DO consoles in our office and all five discs from the game worked as expected. It was only upon shipping to our customers that we came to realise that some users were experiencing difficulty. We will not be selling or shipping further copies of this game, or any future 3DO titles until the situation has been resolved."
  • Sony overturns Helldivers 2 PSN requirement following backlash
    • On Friday, the publisher announced Helldivers 2 players on Steam would soon be required to link to a PSN account for safety and security. It said this was always going to be a requirement for players on PC, but due to issues at launch, linking was made optional for a period.
    • The news was immediately met with intense backlash, which resulted in an influx of negative reviews from Helldivers 2 players on Steam.
    • Many asked for a refund as Helldivers 2 was pulled from sale in 177 countries where PSN is not available.
    • Unclear if the 177 countries that were removed from Steam will have Helldivers 2 reinstated.
    • Many online have claimed this has ruined all goodwill of PlayStations PC efforts

Business

  • Gray Zone Warfare sells over 500,000 copies in three days

Rumors

  • Warhammer 40K: Space Marine 2 will include a PvP mode according to art book leak
    • The PvP mode will be Marine x Marine "only" with classes, alongside banner and clan customisation for PvP and a hub-type space from which you can pick up missions and upgrade your character.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 03/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 3 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• THQ Nordic’s fourth annual digital showcase is coming in August
    • Featuring Gothic 1 remake, Titan Quest II and "much more"
    • Friday August 2, 2024, at 12pm PDT / 3pm EDT / 8pm BST / 9pm CEST
  • Ubisoft free-to-play FPS XDefiant finally gets a release date
    • The game will go live on May 21, on PS5, Xbox Series S/X and PC
  • Nintendo takes down more than 8,500 clones of Switch emulator Yuzu with a single DMCA notice
  • Knuckles had the most-viewed opening weekend of any Paramount+ original series to date
    • The show clocked 4 million hours of viewing in its first three days
  • Bloodstained's final two Kickstarter stretch goals arrive next week, five years after release
    • Chaos Mode and VS Mode both in 1.5 update

Free Games / Subscription

  • May’s ‘free’ games with Amazon Prime Gaming have been revealed
    • May 2nd:
    • Tomb Raider Game of the Year Edition [GOG Code]
    • Lego Star Wars III: The Clone Wars [GOG Code]
    • May 9th:
    • Dark City: International Intrigue [Amazon Games App]
    • Fallout 3: Game of the Year Edition [GOG Code]
    • Nine Witches: Family Disruption [Amazon Games App]
    • Electrician Simulator [Epic Games Store]
    • May 16th:
    • 100 Doors Games: Escape from School [Legacy Games Code]
    • The Forgotten City [Amazon Games App]
    • May 23rd:
    • Spirits of Mystery: Whisper of the Past [Amazon Games App]

Business

  • Paladin Studios shuts down after nearly 19 years
    • The gamer maker attributed its decision to a failure of not landing enough work
    • Based in The Hague, Netherlands, Paladin launched in 2005. It has since released games such as Amazing Katamari Damacy, My Tamagotchi Forever, and Galaga Wars.
    • The studio closed with 45 employees. Paladin also said it will continue providing technical support for its games library.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 02/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 2 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• New PS5 shareable links will let recipients join multiplayer sessions without being PSN friends
    • Players will be able to create links either directly on their PlayStation 5 console or using the PlayStation App.
      • The resulting link can then be shared as a simple URL or a scannable QR code via the social media or messaging platform of their choice.

Business

  • Unity appoints former EA and Zynga executive as its new CEO
    • Matthew Bromberg will take on the role later this month
      • Bromberg previously served as group general manager of BioWare, before taking global responsibility for EA’s mobile business during a four-year spell with the company that ended in July 2016.
      • He left to serve as chief operating officer of leading mobile game developer and publisher Zynga, where he worked until 2022.
  • Belka Games reduces staffers by 20% (33 people)
  • Firesprite lays off Horizon: Call of the Mountain Game director Alex Barnes
    • Barnes, who'd been with the company for seven years, directed its 2023 game Horizon: Call of the Mountain.
    • <Rumor> Beyond the layoffs, Firesprite's next project was reportedly canceled as part of the reevaluation.
    • It's claimed the UK studio was making a new live-service Twisted Metal game that was said to be in early development.
  • Game workers in Quebec are making a huge unionization push
    • Game Workers Unite Montreal has joined forces with Canadian trade union CSN to help workers establish unions at major studios in the region

Rumors

  • Rumor, Multiple Sources> Take-Two reportedly closing Private Division studios Roll7 and Intercept Games
    • Roll7 is the London-based developer behind OlliOlli and Rollerdrome
    • Seattle-based Intercept Games developed Kerbal Space Program 2
    • Take-Two wouldn't confirm whether Intercept Games has been impacted by the cuts—despite multiple Kerbal Space Program developers indicating they recently left the studio, with one expressly stating they were "laid off." A company spokesperson did, however, explain that its Private Division publishing label will continue to support Kerbal Space Program 2.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 01/05/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 1 May 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Xbox Games Showcase and “Redacted” Direct announced for June 9
    • June 9, 2024, starting at 10am PT / 1pm ET / 6pm BST
    • The "Redacted Direct" will start immediately after the Xbox Games Showcase
      • Editors Note: SPOILERS: It's clear "Redacted" is the next Call of Duty: Black Ops title.
  • Starfield’s Shattered Space story expansion is launching this fall
    • Ahead of that, the game's next update will include ship building and city map features
    • No exact release date atm

Free Games / Subscription

  • Fallout 4’s free next-gen update now available for PlayStation Plus Collection owners
    • After much confusion and many mixed messages, both PS+ and PS Collection users should now have access to the PS5 update of Fallout 4
  • New Xbox Game Pass titles for console, PC and Cloud have been announced
    • Arriving:
    • Have A Nice Death (Cloud, Console, and PC) – April 30
    • Tomb Raider: Definitive Edition (Cloud, Console, and PC) – May 2
    • Kona II: Brume (Cloud, Console, and PC) – May 7
    • Little Kitty, Big City (Cloud, Console, and PC) – May 9
    • Brothers: A Tale of Two Sons (Cloud, Console, and PC) – May 14
    • Leaving:
    • May 15th
      • Eastern Exorcist (Cloud, Console, PC)
      • Eiyuden Chronicle Rising (Cloud, Console, PC)
      • Fuga: Melodies of Steel 2 (Cloud, Console, PC)
      • Ghostlore (Cloud, Console, PC)
      • Just Cause 4 Reloaded (Cloud, Console, PC)
      • Norco (Cloud, Console, PC)
      • SD Gundam Battle Alliance (Cloud, Console, PC)
      • Supraland Six Inches Under (Cloud, Console, PC)

Business

  • Deliver Us Mars developer Keoken Interactive lays off team after failing to find funding at GDC
    • 13 people affected, only the two co-founders remain
    • The pair wrote that the team had been let go due to "nothing substantial materialising directly after our visit to GDC," adding: "We’ve unfortunately exhausted all our possible options for publishing, work for hire, and co-development."
  • Hasbro's $1 billion bet on internal game development
    • Instead of licensing out properties, Hasbro is attempting to build internal teams.
    • The company has four AAA studios right now:
    • North Carolina-based Atomic Arcade, which is working on a Snake Eyes GI Joe game
    • Montreal-based Invoke Studios is working on a Dungeons & Dragons game
    • Austin, Texas-based Skeleton Key is "doing something spooky
    • Another Austin, Texas studio, called Archetype, where BioWare veteran and Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic lead designer James Ohlen is working on a new franchise called Exodus
  • Square Enix abandons £112m of in-development projects in an effort to improve quality
    • Will be "more selective" moving forward

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 30/04/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 30 April 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Todd Howard says Bethesda is focused on finding ways to increase its output
    • The company hasn't ruled out partnering with external developers on future games
    • Remedy’s Control multiplayer game has entered full production: https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/remedys-control-multiplayer-game-has-entered-full-production/
      • "The core loop is engaging, and the game brings a unique Remedy angle to the genre"
    • Tekken 8’s second DLC character and upcoming free updates have been revealed: https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/tekken-8s-second-dlc-character-and-upcoming-free-updates-have-been-revealed/
      • Lidia from Tekken 7 will be joining the game in the Summer
    • Amazon's Fallout TV show was watched by 65m people in its first two weeks: https://www.eurogamer.net/amazons-fallout-tv-show-was-watched-by-65m-people-in-its-first-two-weeks
      • Second biggest Amazon show (behind Rings of Power)
      • Amazon hasn't qualified what it considers a viewer in its figures, so it's unclear how much of the series any of those counted have actually watched.

Business

  • Tencent has increased its shareholding in Remedy to almost 15%
    • The Chinese tech giant has tripled its shares in Remedy since late 2022
    • Manor Lords sells 1m copies in 24 hours: https://www.eurogamer.net/manor-lords-sells-1m-copies-in-24-hours-reveals-whats-coming-next

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 29/04/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 29 April 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Veteran LucasArts lead Matthew Shell has passed away
    • Shell was credited on over 60 games over 20 years
  • Lego Animal Crossing getting new set featuring K.K. Slider this August
    • The Town Hall and Dodo Airlines Dock sets will be released August 1st
  • ArcheAge to go offline in North America and Europe June 27
    • The XL Games MMO sunsets after nine years due to declining active players

Business

  • Capcom revises earnings forecast following "favorable" Dragon’s Dogma 2 sales
    • Previously, the figure was ¥140 billion ($892 million), and now the forecast is set to ¥152.4 billion ($971 million). The change represents an increase of 9%.

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 26/04/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 26 April 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• People Can Fly has cancelled co-op action RPG Project Dagger
    • The company had been planning to self-publish the game after it was dropped by Take-Two
    • It said it had decided to cancel the project after a revaluation of the scope and commercial potential of the game
  • Stellar Blade director says half-second ‘input lag’ when parrying and dodging is deliberate
    • Triggering the move instantly "would not fit the game's style of animation", Kim Hyung-Tae says
  • Players who got PS4 Fallout 4 through PS Plus facing issues getting the PS5 upgrade
    • At the time of release, PS Plus players were unable to redeem the PS5 version.
    • - Both Bethesda and Sony claimed this was a bug
    • - Some users have now been given access to the PS5 version whilst others are still left waiting.
  • BlizzCon is cancelled again after last year's first in-person event since 2019
    • But may return "in future years".

Business

  • Frostpunk has sold 5m copies in six years

item.title: Game News Round-Up - 25/04/2024
item.description: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 25 April 2024.
item.content:encoded:
• Garry's Mod Steam Workshop Hit By Nintendo Takedown Demand
    • In the post on Steam, Facepunch Studios urged players and creators to help them remove every trace of anything Nintendo-themed from Garry's Mod.
    • It was originally thought that the DMCA requests coming from 'Nintendo' were false and were being circulated by a malicious operator, but Facepunch Studios confirmed that the requests are legit and come directly from the legal team at Nintendo.
    • Many users are now wondering what took Nintendo so long to make this formal request.
  • Kazuma Kaneko, longtime Atlus artist, departs after 35-year run
    • During his Atlus tenure, Kaneko touched everything from Persona to Shin Megami Tensei and Soul Hackers to even Devil May Cry.
    • The artist has jumped ship over to COLOPL.
  • Steam amends refund policy, nixing advanced access loophole in the process
    • In the past, the "refund timer" would reset on an early access game's full release, allowing players with hundreds of hours to get an automatic refund when a game moved to full release.
  • Suspect charged for cancelling Nintendo Japan events by threatening to 'kill everyone' for 'shitty games'

Free Games / Subscription

  • Switch Online's N64 app gets two more games today
    • Extreme-G and Iggy's Reckin' Balls

Business

  • Previously thought to be only over one million players in a single day on Fallout 76, there has been almost five million players across all Fallout games in a single day
    • **EGX and MCM **come together for 2024: <https://www.gamesindustry.biz/egx-and-mcm-come-together-for-2024>
    • The two events will co-locate to create 'the biggest pop culture weekend in the UK'
    • Normally GamesIndustry Biz supports EGX with the business offering (Meetings with investors .etc)
    • They have hinted that their offering will also be part of the EGX-MCM event.
  • CI Games hits record fiscal revenue in 2023
    • CI Games has generated over $60 million in consolidated net revenue for its fiscal year ended December 31, 2023.
    • The figure represents a 400% year-over-year growth, the highest in the company's history.
    • CI Games attributed 83% of its sales revenue to Lords of the Fallen, launched October 13, 2023.
    • Meanwhile, 17% was generated from its back catalog of titles, and Sniper Ghost Warrior Contracts 2 accounted for the remaining 9% of revenue.
